See RONR (12th ed.) §12:25 — “It should be noted that many of the rules governing the different forms of amendment are particular applications of the following principle: After the assembly has voted that certain words (or a certain paragraph) shall, or shall not, form part of a pending resolution, it is not in order, during the same session at which that vote was taken, to make another motion to Amend that raises the same question of content and effect.”

**495.** `[C0319 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the consideration of a motion to insert certain words, or (if they are to be placed at the end of the passage being amended) to add certain words, any necessary perfecting of the new words should be done by secondary amendment _____ the vote is taken on inserting or adding as proposed by the primary amendment.

   *Answer:* before — “In the consideration of a motion to insert certain words, or (if they are to be placed at the end of the passage being amended) to add certain words, any necessary perfecting of the new words should be done by secondary amendment before the vote is taken on inserting or adding as proposed by the primary amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:27 — “In the consideration of a motion to insert certain words, or (if they are to be placed at the end of the passage being amended) to add certain words, any necessary perfecting of the new words should be done by secondary amendment before the vote is taken on inserting or adding as proposed by the primary amendment.”

**496.** `[C0319 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the consideration of a motion to insert certain words, or (if they are to be placed at the end of the passage being amended) to add certain words, any necessary perfecting of the new words should be done by secondary amendment _____ the vote is taken on inserting or adding as proposed by the primary amendment.
   A. Discharge a
   B. or local law
   C. before
   D. vote

   *Answer:* C) before. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:27 — “In the consideration of a motion to insert certain words, or (if they are to be placed at the end of the passage being amended) to add certain words, any necessary perfecting of the new words should be done by secondary amendment before the vote is taken on inserting or adding as proposed by the primary amendment.”

**497.** `[C0319 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the consideration of a motion to _____ certain words, or (if they are to be placed at the end of the passage being amended) to add certain words, any necessary perfecting of the new words should be done by secondary amendment before the vote is taken on inserting or adding as proposed by the primary amendment.

   *Answer:* insert — “In the consideration of a motion to insert certain words, or (if they are to be placed at the end of the passage being amended) to add certain words, any necessary perfecting of the new words should be done by secondary amendment before the vote is taken on inserting or adding as proposed by the primary amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:27 — “In the consideration of a motion to insert certain words, or (if they are to be placed at the end of the passage being amended) to add certain words, any necessary perfecting of the new words should be done by secondary amendment before the vote is taken on inserting or adding as proposed by the primary amendment.”

**498.** `[C0319 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the consideration of a motion to _____ certain words, or (if they are to be placed at the end of the passage being amended) to add certain words, any necessary perfecting of the new words should be done by secondary amendment before the vote is taken on inserting or adding as proposed by the primary amendment.
   A. chairman pro tem
   B. more than a
   C. insert
   D. majority vote

   *Answer:* C) insert. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:27 — “In the consideration of a motion to insert certain words, or (if they are to be placed at the end of the passage being amended) to add certain words, any necessary perfecting of the new words should be done by secondary amendment before the vote is taken on inserting or adding as proposed by the primary amendment.”

**499.** `[C0319 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the consideration of a motion to insert certain words, or (if they are to be placed at the end of the passage being amended) to _____ certain words, any necessary perfecting of the new words should be done by secondary amendment before the vote is taken on inserting or adding as proposed by the primary amendment.

   *Answer:* add — “In the consideration of a motion to insert certain words, or (if they are to be placed at the end of the passage being amended) to add certain words, any necessary perfecting of the new words should be done by secondary amendment before the vote is taken on inserting or adding as proposed by the primary amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:27 — “In the consideration of a motion to insert certain words, or (if they are to be placed at the end of the passage being amended) to add certain words, any necessary perfecting of the new words should be done by secondary amendment before the vote is taken on inserting or adding as proposed by the primary amendment.”

**500.** `[C0331 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In some cases—depending on the length of the resolution and the _____, the nature of the subject matter, the conditions of the assembly, etc.—the question may be clearer to the members if the chair restates only the amendment, rather than employing the three steps outlined above.

   *Answer:* amendment — “In some cases—depending on the length of the resolution and the amendment, the nature of the subject matter, the conditions of the assembly, etc.—the question may be clearer to the members if the chair restates only the amendment, rather than employing the three steps outlined above.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:40 — “In some cases—depending on the length of the resolution and the amendment, the nature of the subject matter, the conditions of the assembly, etc.—the question may be clearer to the members if the chair restates only the amendment, rather than employing the three steps outlined above.”

**501.** `[C0331 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In some cases—depending on the length of the resolution and the _____, the nature of the subject matter, the conditions of the assembly, etc.—the question may be clearer to the members if the chair restates only the amendment, rather than employing the three steps outlined above.
   A. strike out
   B. speed up
   C. Articles of Association
   D. amendment

   *Answer:* D) amendment. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:40 — “In some cases—depending on the length of the resolution and the amendment, the nature of the subject matter, the conditions of the assembly, etc.—the question may be clearer to the members if the chair restates only the amendment, rather than employing the three steps outlined above.”

**502.** `[C0331 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: On the other hand, in circumstances of particular complexity, when confusion is otherwise likely, the chair may find it advisable to employ _____ steps: first, to reread the entire main motion or resolution (or the paragraph or portion that the amendment would affect);

   *Answer:* four — “On the other hand, in circumstances of particular complexity, when confusion is otherwise likely, the chair may find it advisable to employ four steps: first, to reread the entire main motion or resolution (or the paragraph or portion that the amendment would affect);”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:40 — “On the other hand, in circumstances of particular complexity, when confusion is otherwise likely, the chair may find it advisable to employ four steps: first, to reread the entire main motion or resolution (or the paragraph or portion that the amendment would affect);”

**503.** `[C0331 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: On the other hand, in circumstances of particular complexity, when confusion is otherwise likely, the chair may find it advisable to employ _____ steps: first, to reread the entire main motion or resolution (or the paragraph or portion that the amendment would affect);
   A. recommit
   B. Filling Blanks
   C. Optional Headings
   D. four

   *Answer:* D) four. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:40 — “On the other hand, in circumstances of particular complexity, when confusion is otherwise likely, the chair may find it advisable to employ four steps: first, to reread the entire main motion or resolution (or the paragraph or portion that the amendment would affect);”

**504.** `[C0332 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As an example of _____, assume that there are pending: (1) a resolution, “Resolved, That the Society purchase the property adjoining the present Headquarters”;

   *Answer:* secondary amendment by inserting — “As an example of secondary amendment by inserting, assume that there are pending: (1) a resolution, “Resolved, That the Society purchase the property adjoining the present Headquarters”;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:41 — “As an example of secondary amendment by inserting, assume that there are pending: (1) a resolution, “Resolved, That the Society purchase the property adjoining the present Headquarters”;”

**505.** `[C0332 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As an example of _____, assume that there are pending: (1) a resolution, “Resolved, That the Society purchase the property adjoining the present Headquarters”;
   A. secondary amendment by inserting
   B. assembly of an organized society
   C. immediately
   D. chairman

   *Answer:* A) secondary amendment by inserting. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:41 — “As an example of secondary amendment by inserting, assume that there are pending: (1) a resolution, “Resolved, That the Society purchase the property adjoining the present Headquarters”;”

**506.** `[C0332 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As an example of secondary amendment by inserting, assume that there are pending: (1) a resolution, “_____, That the Society purchase the property adjoining the present Headquarters”;

   *Answer:* Resolved — “As an example of secondary amendment by inserting, assume that there are pending: (1) a resolution, “Resolved, That the Society purchase the property adjoining the present Headquarters”;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:41 — “As an example of secondary amendment by inserting, assume that there are pending: (1) a resolution, “Resolved, That the Society purchase the property adjoining the present Headquarters”;”

**507.** `[C0332 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As an example of secondary amendment by inserting, assume that there are pending: (1) a resolution, “_____, That the Society purchase the property adjoining the present Headquarters”;
   A. Resolved
   B. quasi committee of the whole
   C. recorder,
   D. basic right of the individual member

   *Answer:* A) Resolved. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:41 — “As an example of secondary amendment by inserting, assume that there are pending: (1) a resolution, “Resolved, That the Society purchase the property adjoining the present Headquarters”;”

**508.** `[C0332 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and (2) (immediately pending) a primary amendment, “to add the words, ‘and convert it into a parking lot.’” (Details as to probable costs of proposed phases of the project are assumed to be known.) MEMBER X (obtaining the floor): I move to insert in the pending amendment the word “_____” before “parking lot.” (Second.) CHAIR: It is moved and seconded to amend by inserting in the primary amendment the word “landscaped” before “parking lot.” If the word is inserted, the primary amendment will be, “to add to the resolution for the purchase of the property, the words ‘and convert it into a landscaped parking lot.’” The question is on inserting the word “landscaped.”

   *Answer:* landscaped — “and (2) (immediately pending) a primary amendment, “to add the words, ‘and convert it into a parking lot.’” (Details as to probable costs of proposed phases of the project are assumed to be known.) MEMBER X (obtaining the floor): I move to insert in the pending amendment the word “landscaped” before “parking lot.” (Second.) CHAIR: It is moved and seconded to amend by inserting in the primary amendment the word “landscaped” before “parking lot.” If the word is inserted, the primary amendment will be, “to add to the resolution for the purchase of the property, the words ‘and convert it into a landscaped parking lot.’” The question is on inserting the word “landscaped.””. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:41 — “and (2) (immediately pending) a primary amendment, “to add the words, ‘and convert it into a parking lot.’” (Details as to probable costs of proposed phases of the project are assumed to be known.) MEMBER X (obtaining the floor): I move to insert in the pending amendment the word “landscaped” before “parking lot.” (Second.) CHAIR: It is moved and seconded to amend by inserting in the primary amendment the word “landscaped” before “parking lot.” If the word is inserted, the primary amendment will be, “to add to the resolution for the purchase of the property, the words ‘and convert it into a landscaped parking lot.’” The question is on inserting the word “landscaped.””

**509.** `[C0333 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It should be noted that in the preceding example the chair gives the _____ amendment as it would read if the secondary amendment were adopted.

   *Answer:* primary — “It should be noted that in the preceding example the chair gives the primary amendment as it would read if the secondary amendment were adopted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:42 — “It should be noted that in the preceding example the chair gives the primary amendment as it would read if the secondary amendment were adopted.”

**510.** `[C0333 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It should be noted that in the preceding example the chair gives the _____ amendment as it would read if the secondary amendment were adopted.
   A. extend the limits
   B. primary
   C. meeting
   D. subsequently

   *Answer:* B) primary. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:42 — “It should be noted that in the preceding example the chair gives the primary amendment as it would read if the secondary amendment were adopted.”

**511.** `[C0339 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is important to note that: The motion to amend by striking out certain words can be amended only by striking out words _____.

   *Answer:* from the primary amendment — “It is important to note that: The motion to amend by striking out certain words can be amended only by striking out words from the primary amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:49 — “It is important to note that: The motion to amend by striking out certain words can be amended only by striking out words from the primary amendment.”

**512.** `[C0339 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is important to note that: The motion to amend by striking out certain words can be amended only by striking out words _____.
   A. over
   B. Rules
   C. request
   D. from the primary amendment

   *Answer:* D) from the primary amendment. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:49 — “It is important to note that: The motion to amend by striking out certain words can be amended only by striking out words from the primary amendment.”

**513.** `[C0339 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is important to note that: _____ out certain words can be amended only by striking out words from the primary amendment.

   *Answer:* The motion to amend by striking — “It is important to note that: The motion to amend by striking out certain words can be amended only by striking out words from the primary amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:49 — “It is important to note that: The motion to amend by striking out certain words can be amended only by striking out words from the primary amendment.”

**514.** `[C0339 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is important to note that: _____ out certain words can be amended only by striking out words from the primary amendment.
   A. interrupted
   B. makes
   C. speed up
   D. The motion to amend by striking

   *Answer:* D) The motion to amend by striking. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:49 — “It is important to note that: The motion to amend by striking out certain words can be amended only by striking out words from the primary amendment.”

**515.** `[C0341 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The following differences in the effect of a _____—dependent on whether the primary amendment to strike out involves only certain words or a paragraph—should be particularly noted:

   *Answer:* secondary amendment to strike out — “The following differences in the effect of a secondary amendment to strike out—dependent on whether the primary amendment to strike out involves only certain words or a paragraph—should be particularly noted:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:51 — “The following differences in the effect of a secondary amendment to strike out—dependent on whether the primary amendment to strike out involves only certain words or a paragraph—should be particularly noted:”

**516.** `[C0341 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The following differences in the effect of a _____—dependent on whether the primary amendment to strike out involves only certain words or a paragraph—should be particularly noted:
   A. bring business
   B. parliamentary authority
   C. secondary amendment to strike out
   D. amendment to the main question

   *Answer:* C) secondary amendment to strike out. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:51 — “The following differences in the effect of a secondary amendment to strike out—dependent on whether the primary amendment to strike out involves only certain words or a paragraph—should be particularly noted:”

**517.** `[C0341 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When it is moved to _____, the paragraph that would be struck out is opened to improvement by secondary amendment in any of the three forms relating to words (inserting or adding;

   *Answer:* strike out an entire paragraph — “When it is moved to strike out an entire paragraph, the paragraph that would be struck out is opened to improvement by secondary amendment in any of the three forms relating to words (inserting or adding;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:51 — “When it is moved to strike out an entire paragraph, the paragraph that would be struck out is opened to improvement by secondary amendment in any of the three forms relating to words (inserting or adding;”

**518.** `[C0341 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When it is moved to _____, the paragraph that would be struck out is opened to improvement by secondary amendment in any of the three forms relating to words (inserting or adding;
   A. strike out an entire paragraph
   B. Meeting
   C. house of delegates
   D. caucus

   *Answer:* A) strike out an entire paragraph. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:51 — “When it is moved to strike out an entire paragraph, the paragraph that would be struck out is opened to improvement by secondary amendment in any of the three forms relating to words (inserting or adding;”

**519.** `[C0341 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the primary amendment to strike out is voted down, the paragraph then remains in the resolution with any changes that were _____.

   *Answer:* made by secondary amendment — “If the primary amendment to strike out is voted down, the paragraph then remains in the resolution with any changes that were made by secondary amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:51 — “If the primary amendment to strike out is voted down, the paragraph then remains in the resolution with any changes that were made by secondary amendment.”

**520.** `[C0346 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The two parts of this motion cannot be separated, either by secondary amendment or by a _____.

   *Answer:* Division of a Question — “The two parts of this motion cannot be separated, either by secondary amendment or by a Division of a Question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:56 — “The two parts of this motion cannot be separated, either by secondary amendment or by a Division of a Question.”

**521.** `[C0346 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The two parts of this motion cannot be separated, either by secondary amendment or by a _____.
   A. Lay on the Table
   B. Division of a Question
   C. Platform Committee
   D. another member

   *Answer:* B) Division of a Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:56 — “The two parts of this motion cannot be separated, either by secondary amendment or by a Division of a Question.”

**522.** `[C0352 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After a motion to strike out and insert has been adopted, what has been struck out and what has been inserted are subject to the same rules regarding further amendment during the same session, as if the striking out and the inserting had been done by separate motions: The inserted matter cannot be struck out, and the matter that has been struck out cannot be inserted again, except through a reconsideration of the vote on the amendment, or through changes in the wording or the place in a way that presents a new question (under the rules already given for Insert, or Add, and for _____).

   *Answer:* Strike Out — “After a motion to strike out and insert has been adopted, what has been struck out and what has been inserted are subject to the same rules regarding further amendment during the same session, as if the striking out and the inserting had been done by separate motions: The inserted matter cannot be struck out, and the matter that has been struck out cannot be inserted again, except through a reconsideration of the vote on the amendment, or through changes in the wording or the place in a way that presents a new question (under the rules already given for Insert, or Add, and for Strike Out).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:63 — “After a motion to strike out and insert has been adopted, what has been struck out and what has been inserted are subject to the same rules regarding further amendment during the same session, as if the striking out and the inserting had been done by separate motions: The inserted matter cannot be struck out, and the matter that has been struck out cannot be inserted again, except through a reconsideration of the vote on the amendment, or through changes in the wording or the place in a way that presents a new question (under the rules already given for Insert, or Add, and for Strike Out).”

**523.** `[C0352 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After a motion to strike out and insert has been adopted, what has been struck out and what has been inserted are subject to the same rules regarding further amendment during the same session, as if the striking out and the inserting had been done by separate motions: The inserted matter cannot be struck out, and the matter that has been struck out cannot be inserted again, except through a reconsideration of the vote on the amendment, or through changes in the wording or the place in a way that presents a new question (under the rules already given for Insert, or Add, and for _____).
   A. Call for the Orders of the Day
   B. Resolved
   C. originating in the committee
   D. Strike Out

   *Answer:* D) Strike Out. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:63 — “After a motion to strike out and insert has been adopted, what has been struck out and what has been inserted are subject to the same rules regarding further amendment during the same session, as if the striking out and the inserting had been done by separate motions: The inserted matter cannot be struck out, and the matter that has been struck out cannot be inserted again, except through a reconsideration of the vote on the amendment, or through changes in the wording or the place in a way that presents a new question (under the rules already given for Insert, or Add, and for Strike Out).”

**524.** `[C0352 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After a motion to strike out and insert has been adopted, what has been struck out and what has been inserted are subject to the same rules regarding further amendment during the same session, as if the striking out and the inserting had been done by separate motions: The inserted matter cannot be struck out, and the matter that has been struck out cannot be inserted again, except through a reconsideration of the vote on the amendment, or through changes in the wording or the place in a way that presents a new question (under the rules already given for _____, and for Strike Out).

   *Answer:* Insert, or Add — “After a motion to strike out and insert has been adopted, what has been struck out and what has been inserted are subject to the same rules regarding further amendment during the same session, as if the striking out and the inserting had been done by separate motions: The inserted matter cannot be struck out, and the matter that has been struck out cannot be inserted again, except through a reconsideration of the vote on the amendment, or through changes in the wording or the place in a way that presents a new question (under the rules already given for Insert, or Add, and for Strike Out).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:63 — “After a motion to strike out and insert has been adopted, what has been struck out and what has been inserted are subject to the same rules regarding further amendment during the same session, as if the striking out and the inserting had been done by separate motions: The inserted matter cannot be struck out, and the matter that has been struck out cannot be inserted again, except through a reconsideration of the vote on the amendment, or through changes in the wording or the place in a way that presents a new question (under the rules already given for Insert, or Add, and for Strike Out).”

**525.** `[C0352 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After a motion to strike out and insert has been adopted, what has been struck out and what has been inserted are subject to the same rules regarding further amendment during the same session, as if the striking out and the inserting had been done by separate motions: The inserted matter cannot be struck out, and the matter that has been struck out cannot be inserted again, except through a reconsideration of the vote on the amendment, or through changes in the wording or the place in a way that presents a new question (under the rules already given for _____, and for Strike Out).
   A. ordinary committees
   B. Insert, or Add
   C. Conditions for
   D. limit

   *Answer:* B) Insert, or Add. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:63 — “After a motion to strike out and insert has been adopted, what has been struck out and what has been inserted are subject to the same rules regarding further amendment during the same session, as if the striking out and the inserting had been done by separate motions: The inserted matter cannot be struck out, and the matter that has been struck out cannot be inserted again, except through a reconsideration of the vote on the amendment, or through changes in the wording or the place in a way that presents a new question (under the rules already given for Insert, or Add, and for Strike Out).”

**526.** `[C0354 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As already explained in connection with the rules for the motion to _____, the motion to strike out and insert can be used to obtain the effect of striking out or modifying separated words.

   *Answer:* strike out — “As already explained in connection with the rules for the motion to strike out, the motion to strike out and insert can be used to obtain the effect of striking out or modifying separated words.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:66 — “As already explained in connection with the rules for the motion to strike out, the motion to strike out and insert can be used to obtain the effect of striking out or modifying separated words.”

**527.** `[C0354 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As already explained in connection with the rules for the motion to _____, the motion to strike out and insert can be used to obtain the effect of striking out or modifying separated words.
   A. Appeal
   B. privileged motions
   C. strike out
   D. recorder,

   *Answer:* C) strike out. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:66 — “As already explained in connection with the rules for the motion to strike out, the motion to strike out and insert can be used to obtain the effect of striking out or modifying separated words.”

**528.** `[C0354 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As already explained in connection with the rules for the motion to strike out, the motion to _____ can be used to obtain the effect of striking out or modifying separated words.

   *Answer:* strike out and insert — “As already explained in connection with the rules for the motion to strike out, the motion to strike out and insert can be used to obtain the effect of striking out or modifying separated words.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:66 — “As already explained in connection with the rules for the motion to strike out, the motion to strike out and insert can be used to obtain the effect of striking out or modifying separated words.”

**529.** `[C0354 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As already explained in connection with the rules for the motion to strike out, the motion to _____ can be used to obtain the effect of striking out or modifying separated words.
   A. of Privilege
   B. Call
   C. strike out and insert
   D. clerk

   *Answer:* C) strike out and insert. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:66 — “As already explained in connection with the rules for the motion to strike out, the motion to strike out and insert can be used to obtain the effect of striking out or modifying separated words.”

**530.** `[C0354 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If several changes in a paragraph are desired, it is usually better to rewrite the paragraph and offer the new version as a _____, as explained below.

   *Answer:* substitute — “If several changes in a paragraph are desired, it is usually better to rewrite the paragraph and offer the new version as a substitute, as explained below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:66 — “If several changes in a paragraph are desired, it is usually better to rewrite the paragraph and offer the new version as a substitute, as explained below.”

**531.** `[C0355 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As an example of a motion to strike out and insert, assume that the following resolution is pending: “_____, That the Citizens’ Association endorse the Rockville site for the new Community College.”

   *Answer:* Resolved — “As an example of a motion to strike out and insert, assume that the following resolution is pending: “Resolved, That the Citizens’ Association endorse the Rockville site for the new Community College.””. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:67 — “As an example of a motion to strike out and insert, assume that the following resolution is pending: “Resolved, That the Citizens’ Association endorse the Rockville site for the new Community College.””

**532.** `[C0355 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As an example of a motion to strike out and insert, assume that the following resolution is pending: “_____, That the Citizens’ Association endorse the Rockville site for the new Community College.”
   A. dispense with
   B. Resolved
   C. Adopted
   D. of the entire membership

   *Answer:* B) Resolved. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:67 — “As an example of a motion to strike out and insert, assume that the following resolution is pending: “Resolved, That the Citizens’ Association endorse the Rockville site for the new Community College.””

**533.** `[C0356 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After the amendment is debated, the chair puts it to vote, as in the case already illustrated for _____.

   *Answer:* Insert or Add — “After the amendment is debated, the chair puts it to vote, as in the case already illustrated for Insert or Add.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:68 — “After the amendment is debated, the chair puts it to vote, as in the case already illustrated for Insert or Add.”

**534.** `[C0356 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After the amendment is debated, the chair puts it to vote, as in the case already illustrated for _____.
   A. amendment to the amendment
   B. pro tem
   C. Insert or Add
   D. Disposition of

   *Answer:* C) Insert or Add. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:68 — “After the amendment is debated, the chair puts it to vote, as in the case already illustrated for Insert or Add.”

**535.** `[C0357 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (For the replacement of sentences _____ a paragraph, see below.) A substitute offered for a main motion or resolution, or for a paragraph within a resolution, is a primary amendment and can therefore be moved only when no other amendment is pending.

   *Answer:* within — “(For the replacement of sentences within a paragraph, see below.) A substitute offered for a main motion or resolution, or for a paragraph within a resolution, is a primary amendment and can therefore be moved only when no other amendment is pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:69 — “(For the replacement of sentences within a paragraph, see below.) A substitute offered for a main motion or resolution, or for a paragraph within a resolution, is a primary amendment and can therefore be moved only when no other amendment is pending.”

**536.** `[C0357 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (For the replacement of sentences _____ a paragraph, see below.) A substitute offered for a main motion or resolution, or for a paragraph within a resolution, is a primary amendment and can therefore be moved only when no other amendment is pending.
   A. Information
   B. within
   C. four
   D. control of the assembly

   *Answer:* B) within. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:69 — “(For the replacement of sentences within a paragraph, see below.) A substitute offered for a main motion or resolution, or for a paragraph within a resolution, is a primary amendment and can therefore be moved only when no other amendment is pending.”

**537.** `[C0357 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion to Amend by striking out an entire paragraph, section, or article—or a complete main motion or resolution—and inserting a different paragraph or other unit in its place is called a motion to _____, and the paragraph or resolution to be inserted is said to be offered (or proposed) as “a substitute.” A substitute can be offered for a paragraph or a main motion of only one sentence, and in such a case the paragraph proposed as a substitute can contain several sentences.

   *Answer:* substitute — “A motion to Amend by striking out an entire paragraph, section, or article—or a complete main motion or resolution—and inserting a different paragraph or other unit in its place is called a motion to substitute, and the paragraph or resolution to be inserted is said to be offered (or proposed) as “a substitute.” A substitute can be offered for a paragraph or a main motion of only one sentence, and in such a case the paragraph proposed as a substitute can contain several sentences.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:69 — “A motion to Amend by striking out an entire paragraph, section, or article—or a complete main motion or resolution—and inserting a different paragraph or other unit in its place is called a motion to substitute, and the paragraph or resolution to be inserted is said to be offered (or proposed) as “a substitute.” A substitute can be offered for a paragraph or a main motion of only one sentence, and in such a case the paragraph proposed as a substitute can contain several sentences.”

**538.** `[C0357 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A motion to Amend by striking out an entire paragraph, section, or article—or a complete main motion or resolution—and inserting a different paragraph or other unit in its place is called a motion to _____, and the paragraph or resolution to be inserted is said to be offered (or proposed) as “a substitute.” A substitute can be offered for a paragraph or a main motion of only one sentence, and in such a case the paragraph proposed as a substitute can contain several sentences.
   A. Debate
   B. to that
   C. substitute
   D. Limit or Extend

   *Answer:* C) substitute. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:69 — “A motion to Amend by striking out an entire paragraph, section, or article—or a complete main motion or resolution—and inserting a different paragraph or other unit in its place is called a motion to substitute, and the paragraph or resolution to be inserted is said to be offered (or proposed) as “a substitute.” A substitute can be offered for a paragraph or a main motion of only one sentence, and in such a case the paragraph proposed as a substitute can contain several sentences.”

**539.** `[C0357 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion to _____ by striking out an entire paragraph, section, or article—or a complete main motion or resolution—and inserting a different paragraph or other unit in its place is called a motion to substitute, and the paragraph or resolution to be inserted is said to be offered (or proposed) as “a substitute.” A substitute can be offered for a paragraph or a main motion of only one sentence, and in such a case the paragraph proposed as a substitute can contain several sentences.

   *Answer:* Amend — “A motion to Amend by striking out an entire paragraph, section, or article—or a complete main motion or resolution—and inserting a different paragraph or other unit in its place is called a motion to substitute, and the paragraph or resolution to be inserted is said to be offered (or proposed) as “a substitute.” A substitute can be offered for a paragraph or a main motion of only one sentence, and in such a case the paragraph proposed as a substitute can contain several sentences.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:69 — “A motion to Amend by striking out an entire paragraph, section, or article—or a complete main motion or resolution—and inserting a different paragraph or other unit in its place is called a motion to substitute, and the paragraph or resolution to be inserted is said to be offered (or proposed) as “a substitute.” A substitute can be offered for a paragraph or a main motion of only one sentence, and in such a case the paragraph proposed as a substitute can contain several sentences.”

**540.** `[C0358 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In contrast to the rules for striking out and inserting _____, however, when a motion to substitute is under consideration the paragraph to be struck out as well as the paragraph to be inserted can be perfected by secondary amendment in any of the three basic forms (inserting or adding;

   *Answer:* words — “In contrast to the rules for striking out and inserting words, however, when a motion to substitute is under consideration the paragraph to be struck out as well as the paragraph to be inserted can be perfected by secondary amendment in any of the three basic forms (inserting or adding;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:70 — “In contrast to the rules for striking out and inserting words, however, when a motion to substitute is under consideration the paragraph to be struck out as well as the paragraph to be inserted can be perfected by secondary amendment in any of the three basic forms (inserting or adding;”

**541.** `[C0358 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In contrast to the rules for striking out and inserting _____, however, when a motion to substitute is under consideration the paragraph to be struck out as well as the paragraph to be inserted can be perfected by secondary amendment in any of the three basic forms (inserting or adding;
   A. takes precedence of
   B. meeting
   C. words
   D. Designating the Committee Chairman

   *Answer:* C) words. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:70 — “In contrast to the rules for striking out and inserting words, however, when a motion to substitute is under consideration the paragraph to be struck out as well as the paragraph to be inserted can be perfected by secondary amendment in any of the three basic forms (inserting or adding;”

**542.** `[C0360 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For replacing an unbroken part of a paragraph when the part to be replaced consists of (or contains) one or more complete sentences, there is an option between offering the amendment as a motion to strike out the part and insert the new matter (that is, as a motion to _____), or moving it in the form of a substitute for the entire paragraph with only the desired part changed.

   *Answer:* strike out and insert — “For replacing an unbroken part of a paragraph when the part to be replaced consists of (or contains) one or more complete sentences, there is an option between offering the amendment as a motion to strike out the part and insert the new matter (that is, as a motion to strike out and insert), or moving it in the form of a substitute for the entire paragraph with only the desired part changed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:72 — “For replacing an unbroken part of a paragraph when the part to be replaced consists of (or contains) one or more complete sentences, there is an option between offering the amendment as a motion to strike out the part and insert the new matter (that is, as a motion to strike out and insert), or moving it in the form of a substitute for the entire paragraph with only the desired part changed.”

**543.** `[C0360 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For replacing an unbroken part of a paragraph when the part to be replaced consists of (or contains) one or more complete sentences, there is an option between offering the amendment as a motion to strike out the part and insert the new matter (that is, as a motion to _____), or moving it in the form of a substitute for the entire paragraph with only the desired part changed.
   A. session
   B. agree to
   C. strike out and insert
   D. assemblies

   *Answer:* C) strike out and insert. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:72 — “For replacing an unbroken part of a paragraph when the part to be replaced consists of (or contains) one or more complete sentences, there is an option between offering the amendment as a motion to strike out the part and insert the new matter (that is, as a motion to strike out and insert), or moving it in the form of a substitute for the entire paragraph with only the desired part changed.”

**544.** `[C0360 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For replacing an unbroken _____ of a paragraph when the part to be replaced consists of (or contains) one or more complete sentences, there is an option between offering the amendment as a motion to strike out the part and insert the new matter (that is, as a motion to strike out and insert), or moving it in the form of a substitute for the entire paragraph with only the desired part changed.

   *Answer:* part — “For replacing an unbroken part of a paragraph when the part to be replaced consists of (or contains) one or more complete sentences, there is an option between offering the amendment as a motion to strike out the part and insert the new matter (that is, as a motion to strike out and insert), or moving it in the form of a substitute for the entire paragraph with only the desired part changed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:72 — “For replacing an unbroken part of a paragraph when the part to be replaced consists of (or contains) one or more complete sentences, there is an option between offering the amendment as a motion to strike out the part and insert the new matter (that is, as a motion to strike out and insert), or moving it in the form of a substitute for the entire paragraph with only the desired part changed.”

**545.** `[C0360 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For replacing an unbroken _____ of a paragraph when the part to be replaced consists of (or contains) one or more complete sentences, there is an option between offering the amendment as a motion to strike out the part and insert the new matter (that is, as a motion to strike out and insert), or moving it in the form of a substitute for the entire paragraph with only the desired part changed.
   A. Requests and Inquiries
   B. part
   C. order of the day
   D. withdraw

   *Answer:* B) part. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:72 — “For replacing an unbroken part of a paragraph when the part to be replaced consists of (or contains) one or more complete sentences, there is an option between offering the amendment as a motion to strike out the part and insert the new matter (that is, as a motion to strike out and insert), or moving it in the form of a substitute for the entire paragraph with only the desired part changed.”

**546.** `[C0361 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Even if the entire resolution or main motion is replaced, adopting the motion to substitute only _____ the resolution, which remains pending as amended.

   *Answer:* amends — “Even if the entire resolution or main motion is replaced, adopting the motion to substitute only amends the resolution, which remains pending as amended.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:73 — “Even if the entire resolution or main motion is replaced, adopting the motion to substitute only amends the resolution, which remains pending as amended.”

**547.** `[C0361 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Even if the entire resolution or main motion is replaced, adopting the motion to substitute only _____ the resolution, which remains pending as amended.
   A. on a different day
   B. amends
   C. that was referred
   D. impose

   *Answer:* B) amends. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:73 — “Even if the entire resolution or main motion is replaced, adopting the motion to substitute only amends the resolution, which remains pending as amended.”

**548.** `[C0361 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In taking the vote on whether to make the substitution, the chair should first read both the paragraph of the original text and the proposed _____—as they stand at the time as amended.

   *Answer:* substitute — “In taking the vote on whether to make the substitution, the chair should first read both the paragraph of the original text and the proposed substitute—as they stand at the time as amended.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:73 — “In taking the vote on whether to make the substitution, the chair should first read both the paragraph of the original text and the proposed substitute—as they stand at the time as amended.”

**549.** `[C0361 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In taking the vote on whether to make the substitution, the chair should first read both the paragraph of the original text and the proposed _____—as they stand at the time as amended.
   A. rules of order
   B. majority
   C. substitute
   D. Again

   *Answer:* C) substitute. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:73 — “In taking the vote on whether to make the substitution, the chair should first read both the paragraph of the original text and the proposed substitute—as they stand at the time as amended.”

**550.** `[C0361 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In taking the vote on whether to make the substitution, the chair should first read both the paragraph of the original text and the proposed substitute—_____ as amended.

   *Answer:* as they stand at the time — “In taking the vote on whether to make the substitution, the chair should first read both the paragraph of the original text and the proposed substitute—as they stand at the time as amended.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:73 — “In taking the vote on whether to make the substitution, the chair should first read both the paragraph of the original text and the proposed substitute—as they stand at the time as amended.”

**551.** `[C0362 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After a paragraph, section, or version of a resolution has been substituted for another, the substituted paragraph or resolution cannot be amended during the same session except by _____ something that does not modify the paragraph’s existing content—as is true of any paragraph that has been inserted.

   *Answer:* adding — “After a paragraph, section, or version of a resolution has been substituted for another, the substituted paragraph or resolution cannot be amended during the same session except by adding something that does not modify the paragraph’s existing content—as is true of any paragraph that has been inserted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:74 — “After a paragraph, section, or version of a resolution has been substituted for another, the substituted paragraph or resolution cannot be amended during the same session except by adding something that does not modify the paragraph’s existing content—as is true of any paragraph that has been inserted.”

**552.** `[C0362 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After a paragraph, section, or version of a resolution has been substituted for another, the substituted paragraph or resolution cannot be amended during the same session except by _____ something that does not modify the paragraph’s existing content—as is true of any paragraph that has been inserted.
   A. Indefinitely
   B. adding
   C. Motions Relating to Methods of Voting,
   D. stated meeting

   *Answer:* B) adding. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:74 — “After a paragraph, section, or version of a resolution has been substituted for another, the substituted paragraph or resolution cannot be amended during the same session except by adding something that does not modify the paragraph’s existing content—as is true of any paragraph that has been inserted.”

**553.** `[C0364 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a question is being considered _____ seriatim (28), it is in order to move a substitute for any paragraph or section at the time that the paragraph or section is opened to amendment.

   *Answer:* by paragraph or — “When a question is being considered by paragraph or seriatim (28), it is in order to move a substitute for any paragraph or section at the time that the paragraph or section is opened to amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:76 — “When a question is being considered by paragraph or seriatim (28), it is in order to move a substitute for any paragraph or section at the time that the paragraph or section is opened to amendment.”

**554.** `[C0364 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a question is being considered _____ seriatim (28), it is in order to move a substitute for any paragraph or section at the time that the paragraph or section is opened to amendment.
   A. precedence of motions
   B. brought before the assembly
   C. Dilatory Motions
   D. by paragraph or

   *Answer:* D) by paragraph or. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:76 — “When a question is being considered by paragraph or seriatim (28), it is in order to move a substitute for any paragraph or section at the time that the paragraph or section is opened to amendment.”

**555.** `[C0364 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a question is being considered by paragraph or _____ (28), it is in order to move a substitute for any paragraph or section at the time that the paragraph or section is opened to amendment.

   *Answer:* seriatim — “When a question is being considered by paragraph or seriatim (28), it is in order to move a substitute for any paragraph or section at the time that the paragraph or section is opened to amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:76 — “When a question is being considered by paragraph or seriatim (28), it is in order to move a substitute for any paragraph or section at the time that the paragraph or section is opened to amendment.”

**556.** `[C0364 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a question is being considered by paragraph or _____ (28), it is in order to move a substitute for any paragraph or section at the time that the paragraph or section is opened to amendment.
   A. rejected
   B. standing
   C. Consider Seriatim
   D. seriatim

   *Answer:* D) seriatim. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:76 — “When a question is being considered by paragraph or seriatim (28), it is in order to move a substitute for any paragraph or section at the time that the paragraph or section is opened to amendment.”

**557.** `[C0367 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the member wishing to propose the substitute thinks it appropriate, he can try to bring the pending amendments to an immediate vote by moving the _____ (16) on them.

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “If the member wishing to propose the substitute thinks it appropriate, he can try to bring the pending amendments to an immediate vote by moving the Previous Question (16) on them.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:79 — “If the member wishing to propose the substitute thinks it appropriate, he can try to bring the pending amendments to an immediate vote by moving the Previous Question (16) on them.”

**558.** `[C0367 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the member wishing to propose the substitute thinks it appropriate, he can try to bring the pending amendments to an immediate vote by moving the _____ (16) on them.
   A. Previous Question
   B. Fix the Time to Which to
   C. during a meeting
   D. Raise a Question

   *Answer:* A) Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:79 — “If the member wishing to propose the substitute thinks it appropriate, he can try to bring the pending amendments to an immediate vote by moving the Previous Question (16) on them.”

**559.** `[C0367 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The motion to _____ often provides a convenient and timesaving method for handling a poorly framed resolution, or for introducing a different and better approach to the real question raised by a main motion.

   *Answer:* substitute — “The motion to substitute often provides a convenient and timesaving method for handling a poorly framed resolution, or for introducing a different and better approach to the real question raised by a main motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:79 — “The motion to substitute often provides a convenient and timesaving method for handling a poorly framed resolution, or for introducing a different and better approach to the real question raised by a main motion.”

**560.** `[C0367 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The motion to _____ often provides a convenient and timesaving method for handling a poorly framed resolution, or for introducing a different and better approach to the real question raised by a main motion.
   A. substitute
   B. order of business, agenda,
   C. unanimous consent
   D. Consider Seriatim

   *Answer:* A) substitute. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:79 — “The motion to substitute often provides a convenient and timesaving method for handling a poorly framed resolution, or for introducing a different and better approach to the real question raised by a main motion.”

**561.** `[C0369 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Properly applied, the rules for the treatment of motions to _____ automatically operate in fairness to both sides when there is disagreement as to the preferability of the original or the substitute.

   *Answer:* substitute — “Properly applied, the rules for the treatment of motions to substitute automatically operate in fairness to both sides when there is disagreement as to the preferability of the original or the substitute.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:81 — “Properly applied, the rules for the treatment of motions to substitute automatically operate in fairness to both sides when there is disagreement as to the preferability of the original or the substitute.”

**562.** `[C0369 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Properly applied, the rules for the treatment of motions to _____ automatically operate in fairness to both sides when there is disagreement as to the preferability of the original or the substitute.
   A. charge
   B. substitute
   C. original main motion
   D. Robert’s Rules of Order Newly Revised

   *Answer:* B) substitute. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:81 — “Properly applied, the rules for the treatment of motions to substitute automatically operate in fairness to both sides when there is disagreement as to the preferability of the original or the substitute.”

**563.** `[C0370 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As an example of a motion to substitute, assume that the following resolution is pending: “_____, That the Parish Federation undertake the construction and equipping of a new service wing for the Parish House, to be financed as far as possible by a mortgage on the present building.”

   *Answer:* Resolved — “As an example of a motion to substitute, assume that the following resolution is pending: “Resolved, That the Parish Federation undertake the construction and equipping of a new service wing for the Parish House, to be financed as far as possible by a mortgage on the present building.””. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:82 — “As an example of a motion to substitute, assume that the following resolution is pending: “Resolved, That the Parish Federation undertake the construction and equipping of a new service wing for the Parish House, to be financed as far as possible by a mortgage on the present building.””

**564.** `[C0370 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As an example of a motion to substitute, assume that the following resolution is pending: “_____, That the Parish Federation undertake the construction and equipping of a new service wing for the Parish House, to be financed as far as possible by a mortgage on the present building.”
   A. of the board
   B. Division of the Assembly
   C. Resolved
   D. Program

   *Answer:* C) Resolved. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:82 — “As an example of a motion to substitute, assume that the following resolution is pending: “Resolved, That the Parish Federation undertake the construction and equipping of a new service wing for the Parish House, to be financed as far as possible by a mortgage on the present building.””

**565.** `[C0372 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Also, since the adoption of this amendment presumably would make the pending resolution _____ objectionable to those who feel that the proposed project is presently ill advised and who therefore hope that the substitution will be made, most of those members probably vote for the amendment.

   *Answer:* less — “Also, since the adoption of this amendment presumably would make the pending resolution less objectionable to those who feel that the proposed project is presently ill advised and who therefore hope that the substitution will be made, most of those members probably vote for the amendment.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:84 — “Also, since the adoption of this amendment presumably would make the pending resolution less objectionable to those who feel that the proposed project is presently ill advised and who therefore hope that the substitution will be made, most of those members probably vote for the amendment.”

**566.** `[C0372 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Also, since the adoption of this amendment presumably would make the pending resolution _____ objectionable to those who feel that the proposed project is presently ill advised and who therefore hope that the substitution will be made, most of those members probably vote for the amendment.
   A. Resolved by the
   B. less
   C. Question
   D. Any Other Privilege

   *Answer:* B) less. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:84 — “Also, since the adoption of this amendment presumably would make the pending resolution less objectionable to those who feel that the proposed project is presently ill advised and who therefore hope that the substitution will be made, most of those members probably vote for the amendment.”

**567.** `[C0377 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the motion to substitute has been adopted, the resolution now pending is in the position of a paragraph that has been inserted, and it can no longer be amended during the same session except by _____ nonmodifying matter.

   *Answer:* adding — “If the motion to substitute has been adopted, the resolution now pending is in the position of a paragraph that has been inserted, and it can no longer be amended during the same session except by adding nonmodifying matter.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:90 — “If the motion to substitute has been adopted, the resolution now pending is in the position of a paragraph that has been inserted, and it can no longer be amended during the same session except by adding nonmodifying matter.”

**568.** `[C0377 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the motion to substitute has been adopted, the resolution now pending is in the position of a paragraph that has been inserted, and it can no longer be amended during the same session except by _____ nonmodifying matter.
   A. Reconsider and Enter on the Minutes
   B. other than the reporting officer
   C. Lay on
   D. adding

   *Answer:* D) adding. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:90 — “If the motion to substitute has been adopted, the resolution now pending is in the position of a paragraph that has been inserted, and it can no longer be amended during the same session except by adding nonmodifying matter.”

**569.** `[C0381 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The vote that fills a blank does _____ decide the question that contained the blank.

   *Answer:* not — “The vote that fills a blank does not decide the question that contained the blank.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:94 — “The vote that fills a blank does not decide the question that contained the blank.”

**570.** `[C0381 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The vote that fills a blank does _____ decide the question that contained the blank.
   A. standing rules
   B. first paragraph
   C. special rules of order
   D. not

   *Answer:* D) not. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:94 — “The vote that fills a blank does not decide the question that contained the blank.”

**571.** `[C0386 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Motions to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate or for the _____ can be applied to the consideration of suggestions, and when voting on the suggestions has been ordered by adoption of either of these motions, no further suggestions for filling the blank may be made at the time the order goes into effect.

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “Motions to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate or for the Previous Question can be applied to the consideration of suggestions, and when voting on the suggestions has been ordered by adoption of either of these motions, no further suggestions for filling the blank may be made at the time the order goes into effect.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:101 — “Motions to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate or for the Previous Question can be applied to the consideration of suggestions, and when voting on the suggestions has been ordered by adoption of either of these motions, no further suggestions for filling the blank may be made at the time the order goes into effect.”

**572.** `[C0386 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Motions to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate or for the _____ can be applied to the consideration of suggestions, and when voting on the suggestions has been ordered by adoption of either of these motions, no further suggestions for filling the blank may be made at the time the order goes into effect.
   A. paragraph
   B. Raise a Question of Privilege
   C. as they stand at the time
   D. Previous Question

   *Answer:* D) Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:101 — “Motions to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate or for the Previous Question can be applied to the consideration of suggestions, and when voting on the suggestions has been ordered by adoption of either of these motions, no further suggestions for filling the blank may be made at the time the order goes into effect.”

**573.** `[C0386 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Motions to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate or for the _____ can be applied to the consideration of suggestions, and when voting on the suggestions has been ordered by adoption of either of these motions, no further suggestions for filling the blank may be made at the time the order goes into effect.

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “Motions to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate or for the Previous Question can be applied to the consideration of suggestions, and when voting on the suggestions has been ordered by adoption of either of these motions, no further suggestions for filling the blank may be made at the time the order goes into effect.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:101 — “Motions to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate or for the Previous Question can be applied to the consideration of suggestions, and when voting on the suggestions has been ordered by adoption of either of these motions, no further suggestions for filling the blank may be made at the time the order goes into effect.”

**574.** `[C0386 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Motions to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate or for the _____ can be applied to the consideration of suggestions, and when voting on the suggestions has been ordered by adoption of either of these motions, no further suggestions for filling the blank may be made at the time the order goes into effect.
   A. adopted
   B. Limits of Debate
   C. adopt,
   D. Previous Question

   *Answer:* D) Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:101 — “Motions to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate or for the Previous Question can be applied to the consideration of suggestions, and when voting on the suggestions has been ordered by adoption of either of these motions, no further suggestions for filling the blank may be made at the time the order goes into effect.”

**575.** `[C0386 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Motions to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate or for the _____ Question can be applied to the consideration of suggestions, and when voting on the suggestions has been ordered by adoption of either of these motions, no further suggestions for filling the blank may be made at the time the order goes into effect.

   *Answer:* Previous — “Motions to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate or for the Previous Question can be applied to the consideration of suggestions, and when voting on the suggestions has been ordered by adoption of either of these motions, no further suggestions for filling the blank may be made at the time the order goes into effect.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:101 — “Motions to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate or for the Previous Question can be applied to the consideration of suggestions, and when voting on the suggestions has been ordered by adoption of either of these motions, no further suggestions for filling the blank may be made at the time the order goes into effect.”

**576.** `[C0390 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: However, if the pending motion is included in an adopted order for the _____ Question, that order must be carried out even if the blank has not yet been filled—and even if no suggestions have been offered (note, however, that a reasonable opportunity to make suggestions must always be given;

   *Answer:* Previous — “However, if the pending motion is included in an adopted order for the Previous Question, that order must be carried out even if the blank has not yet been filled—and even if no suggestions have been offered (note, however, that a reasonable opportunity to make suggestions must always be given;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:105 — “However, if the pending motion is included in an adopted order for the Previous Question, that order must be carried out even if the blank has not yet been filled—and even if no suggestions have been offered (note, however, that a reasonable opportunity to make suggestions must always be given;”

**577.** `[C0390 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: However, if the pending motion is included in an adopted order for the _____ Question, that order must be carried out even if the blank has not yet been filled—and even if no suggestions have been offered (note, however, that a reasonable opportunity to make suggestions must always be given;
   A. precedence of motions
   B. as thus amended,
   C. Previous
   D. committee

   *Answer:* C) Previous. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:105 — “However, if the pending motion is included in an adopted order for the Previous Question, that order must be carried out even if the blank has not yet been filled—and even if no suggestions have been offered (note, however, that a reasonable opportunity to make suggestions must always be given;”

**578.** `[C0390 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: However, if the pending motion is included in an adopted order for the Previous _____, that order must be carried out even if the blank has not yet been filled—and even if no suggestions have been offered (note, however, that a reasonable opportunity to make suggestions must always be given;

   *Answer:* Question — “However, if the pending motion is included in an adopted order for the Previous Question, that order must be carried out even if the blank has not yet been filled—and even if no suggestions have been offered (note, however, that a reasonable opportunity to make suggestions must always be given;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:105 — “However, if the pending motion is included in an adopted order for the Previous Question, that order must be carried out even if the blank has not yet been filled—and even if no suggestions have been offered (note, however, that a reasonable opportunity to make suggestions must always be given;”

**579.** `[C0390 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: However, if the pending motion is included in an adopted order for the Previous _____, that order must be carried out even if the blank has not yet been filled—and even if no suggestions have been offered (note, however, that a reasonable opportunity to make suggestions must always be given;
   A. Question
   B. makes
   C. recommit
   D. the same

   *Answer:* A) Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:105 — “However, if the pending motion is included in an adopted order for the Previous Question, that order must be carried out even if the blank has not yet been filled—and even if no suggestions have been offered (note, however, that a reasonable opportunity to make suggestions must always be given;”

**580.** `[C0393 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In such cases it is advisable, whenever a logical order is apparent, to arrange the proposed entries so that _____ least likely to be acceptable will be voted on first, and so on.

   *Answer:* the one — “In such cases it is advisable, whenever a logical order is apparent, to arrange the proposed entries so that the one least likely to be acceptable will be voted on first, and so on.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:108 — “In such cases it is advisable, whenever a logical order is apparent, to arrange the proposed entries so that the one least likely to be acceptable will be voted on first, and so on.”

**581.** `[C0393 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In such cases it is advisable, whenever a logical order is apparent, to arrange the proposed entries so that _____ least likely to be acceptable will be voted on first, and so on.
   A. Raise a Question of
   B. the one
   C. Call for the Orders of the Day
   D. in good standing

   *Answer:* B) the one. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:108 — “In such cases it is advisable, whenever a logical order is apparent, to arrange the proposed entries so that the one least likely to be acceptable will be voted on first, and so on.”

**582.** `[C0394 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The character of this measure—to _____ money—indicates that the amounts should be arranged and voted on in order from the highest to lowest.

   *Answer:* spend — “The character of this measure—to spend money—indicates that the amounts should be arranged and voted on in order from the highest to lowest.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:109 — “The character of this measure—to spend money—indicates that the amounts should be arranged and voted on in order from the highest to lowest.”

**583.** `[C0394 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The character of this measure—to _____ money—indicates that the amounts should be arranged and voted on in order from the highest to lowest.
   A. board of trustees.
   B. Objection to the
   C. spend
   D. the chair

   *Answer:* C) spend. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:109 — “The character of this measure—to spend money—indicates that the amounts should be arranged and voted on in order from the highest to lowest.”

**584.** `[C0394 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The question is now on the resolution: ‘_____, That… at a cost not to exceed $300,000.’” Note that if the smallest sum had been voted on first, it might have been adopted, with the result that those who preferred the added advantages possible through a larger expenditure would have been cut off from considering larger sums.

   *Answer:* Resolved — “The question is now on the resolution: ‘Resolved, That… at a cost not to exceed $300,000.’” Note that if the smallest sum had been voted on first, it might have been adopted, with the result that those who preferred the added advantages possible through a larger expenditure would have been cut off from considering larger sums.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:109 — “The question is now on the resolution: ‘Resolved, That… at a cost not to exceed $300,000.’” Note that if the smallest sum had been voted on first, it might have been adopted, with the result that those who preferred the added advantages possible through a larger expenditure would have been cut off from considering larger sums.”

**585.** `[C0394 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The question is now on the resolution: ‘_____, That… at a cost not to exceed $300,000.’” Note that if the smallest sum had been voted on first, it might have been adopted, with the result that those who preferred the added advantages possible through a larger expenditure would have been cut off from considering larger sums.
   A. order of business, agenda,
   B. Resolved
   C. Conditions for
   D. Unfinished

   *Answer:* B) Resolved. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:109 — “The question is now on the resolution: ‘Resolved, That… at a cost not to exceed $300,000.’” Note that if the smallest sum had been voted on first, it might have been adopted, with the result that those who preferred the added advantages possible through a larger expenditure would have been cut off from considering larger sums.”

**586.** `[C0395 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: On the other hand, suppose that the motion or resolution is “to sell the headquarters for an amount not less than $ .” In the case of such a motion—to accept a sum of money in _____—the amounts being considered should be arranged and voted on in order from the smallest to the largest.

   *Answer:* settlement — “On the other hand, suppose that the motion or resolution is “to sell the headquarters for an amount not less than $ .” In the case of such a motion—to accept a sum of money in settlement—the amounts being considered should be arranged and voted on in order from the smallest to the largest.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:110 — “On the other hand, suppose that the motion or resolution is “to sell the headquarters for an amount not less than $ .” In the case of such a motion—to accept a sum of money in settlement—the amounts being considered should be arranged and voted on in order from the smallest to the largest.”

**587.** `[C0395 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: On the other hand, suppose that the motion or resolution is “to sell the headquarters for an amount not less than $ .” In the case of such a motion—to accept a sum of money in _____—the amounts being considered should be arranged and voted on in order from the smallest to the largest.
   A. any
   B. other than
   C. recommit
   D. settlement

   *Answer:* D) settlement. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:110 — “On the other hand, suppose that the motion or resolution is “to sell the headquarters for an amount not less than $ .” In the case of such a motion—to accept a sum of money in settlement—the amounts being considered should be arranged and voted on in order from the smallest to the largest.”

**588.** `[C0395 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: On the other hand, suppose that the motion or resolution is “to sell the headquarters for an amount not less than $ .” In the case of such a motion—to _____ a sum of money in settlement—the amounts being considered should be arranged and voted on in order from the smallest to the largest.

   *Answer:* accept — “On the other hand, suppose that the motion or resolution is “to sell the headquarters for an amount not less than $ .” In the case of such a motion—to accept a sum of money in settlement—the amounts being considered should be arranged and voted on in order from the smallest to the largest.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:110 — “On the other hand, suppose that the motion or resolution is “to sell the headquarters for an amount not less than $ .” In the case of such a motion—to accept a sum of money in settlement—the amounts being considered should be arranged and voted on in order from the smallest to the largest.”

**589.** `[C0395 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: On the other hand, suppose that the motion or resolution is “to sell the headquarters for an amount not less than $ .” In the case of such a motion—to _____ a sum of money in settlement—the amounts being considered should be arranged and voted on in order from the smallest to the largest.
   A. accept
   B. the Rules
   C. disturb the assembly
   D. adjourn

   *Answer:* A) accept. See RONR (12th ed.) §12:110 — “On the other hand, suppose that the motion or resolution is “to sell the headquarters for an amount not less than $ .” In the case of such a motion—to accept a sum of money in settlement—the amounts being considered should be arranged and voted on in order from the smallest to the largest.”

**590.** `[C0399 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The subsidiary motion to _____ or Refer is generally used to send a pending question to a relatively small group of selected persons—a committee—so that the question may be carefully investigated and put into better condition for the assembly to consider.

   *Answer:* Commit — “The subsidiary motion to Commit or Refer is generally used to send a pending question to a relatively small group of selected persons—a committee—so that the question may be carefully investigated and put into better condition for the assembly to consider.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Commit or Refer is generally used to send a pending question to a relatively small group of selected persons—a committee—so that the question may be carefully investigated and put into better condition for the assembly to consider.”

**591.** `[C0399 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The subsidiary motion to _____ or Refer is generally used to send a pending question to a relatively small group of selected persons—a committee—so that the question may be carefully investigated and put into better condition for the assembly to consider.
   A. brought before the assembly
   B. undebatable
   C. preferential voting
   D. Commit

   *Answer:* D) Commit. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Commit or Refer is generally used to send a pending question to a relatively small group of selected persons—a committee—so that the question may be carefully investigated and put into better condition for the assembly to consider.”

**592.** `[C0399 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The subsidiary motion to Commit or _____ is generally used to send a pending question to a relatively small group of selected persons—a committee—so that the question may be carefully investigated and put into better condition for the assembly to consider.
