   *Answer:* Refer — “The subsidiary motion to Commit or Refer is generally used to send a pending question to a relatively small group of selected persons—a committee—so that the question may be carefully investigated and put into better condition for the assembly to consider.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Commit or Refer is generally used to send a pending question to a relatively small group of selected persons—a committee—so that the question may be carefully investigated and put into better condition for the assembly to consider.”

**593.** `[C0399 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The subsidiary motion to Commit or _____ is generally used to send a pending question to a relatively small group of selected persons—a committee—so that the question may be carefully investigated and put into better condition for the assembly to consider.
   A. the same
   B. floor
   C. Refer
   D. Subsidiary motions

   *Answer:* C) Refer. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Commit or Refer is generally used to send a pending question to a relatively small group of selected persons—a committee—so that the question may be carefully investigated and put into better condition for the assembly to consider.”

**594.** `[C0400 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The motion to _____ also has three variations whose object is not to turn the main question over to a smaller group, but to permit the assembly’s full meeting body to consider it with the greater freedom of debate that is allowed in committees—that is, with no limit on the number of times a member can speak.

   *Answer:* Commit — “The motion to Commit also has three variations whose object is not to turn the main question over to a smaller group, but to permit the assembly’s full meeting body to consider it with the greater freedom of debate that is allowed in committees—that is, with no limit on the number of times a member can speak.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:2 — “The motion to Commit also has three variations whose object is not to turn the main question over to a smaller group, but to permit the assembly’s full meeting body to consider it with the greater freedom of debate that is allowed in committees—that is, with no limit on the number of times a member can speak.”

**595.** `[C0400 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The motion to _____ also has three variations whose object is not to turn the main question over to a smaller group, but to permit the assembly’s full meeting body to consider it with the greater freedom of debate that is allowed in committees—that is, with no limit on the number of times a member can speak.
   A. secondary amendment to strike out
   B. Commit
   C. will be
   D. standing rule

   *Answer:* B) Commit. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:2 — “The motion to Commit also has three variations whose object is not to turn the main question over to a smaller group, but to permit the assembly’s full meeting body to consider it with the greater freedom of debate that is allowed in committees—that is, with no limit on the number of times a member can speak.”

**596.** `[C0400 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The motion to Commit also has three _____ whose object is not to turn the main question over to a smaller group, but to permit the assembly’s full meeting body to consider it with the greater freedom of debate that is allowed in committees—that is, with no limit on the number of times a member can speak.

   *Answer:* variations — “The motion to Commit also has three variations whose object is not to turn the main question over to a smaller group, but to permit the assembly’s full meeting body to consider it with the greater freedom of debate that is allowed in committees—that is, with no limit on the number of times a member can speak.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:2 — “The motion to Commit also has three variations whose object is not to turn the main question over to a smaller group, but to permit the assembly’s full meeting body to consider it with the greater freedom of debate that is allowed in committees—that is, with no limit on the number of times a member can speak.”

**597.** `[C0400 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The motion to Commit also has three _____ whose object is not to turn the main question over to a smaller group, but to permit the assembly’s full meeting body to consider it with the greater freedom of debate that is allowed in committees—that is, with no limit on the number of times a member can speak.
   A. Descriptive Characteristics
   B. original
   C. In Brief
   D. variations

   *Answer:* D) variations. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:2 — “The motion to Commit also has three variations whose object is not to turn the main question over to a smaller group, but to permit the assembly’s full meeting body to consider it with the greater freedom of debate that is allowed in committees—that is, with no limit on the number of times a member can speak.”

**598.** `[C0402 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The term _____ is applied to a motion that proposes to refer a question a second time, either to the same committee that previously considered it or to a different one.

   *Answer:* recommit — “The term recommit is applied to a motion that proposes to refer a question a second time, either to the same committee that previously considered it or to a different one.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:4 — “The term recommit is applied to a motion that proposes to refer a question a second time, either to the same committee that previously considered it or to a different one.”

**599.** `[C0402 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The term _____ is applied to a motion that proposes to refer a question a second time, either to the same committee that previously considered it or to a different one.
   A. recommit
   B. spend
   C. Reconsider and Enter on the Minutes
   D. executive secretary,

   *Answer:* A) recommit. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:4 — “The term recommit is applied to a motion that proposes to refer a question a second time, either to the same committee that previously considered it or to a different one.”

**600.** `[C0404 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a motion proposes to assign a task or refer a matter to a committee when no question is pending, such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to _____, but is a main motion.

   *Answer:* Commit — “When a motion proposes to assign a task or refer a matter to a committee when no question is pending, such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to Commit, but is a main motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:6 — “When a motion proposes to assign a task or refer a matter to a committee when no question is pending, such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to Commit, but is a main motion.”

**601.** `[C0404 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a motion proposes to assign a task or refer a matter to a committee when no question is pending, such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to _____, but is a main motion.
   A. Suspend
   B. Motions Relating to Voting
   C. Robert’s Rules of
   D. Commit

   *Answer:* D) Commit. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:6 — “When a motion proposes to assign a task or refer a matter to a committee when no question is pending, such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to Commit, but is a main motion.”

**602.** `[C0405 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The motion to _____ usually should include all necessary details:

   *Answer:* Commit — “The motion to Commit usually should include all necessary details:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:8 — “The motion to Commit usually should include all necessary details:”

**603.** `[C0405 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The motion to _____ usually should include all necessary details:
   A. For Permission (or Leave) to
   B. strike out
   C. Commit
   D. Point of

   *Answer:* C) Commit. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:8 — “The motion to Commit usually should include all necessary details:”

**604.** `[C0406 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Dilatory Motion to _____.

   *Answer:* Commit — “Dilatory Motion to Commit.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:9 — “Dilatory Motion to Commit.”

**605.** `[C0406 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Dilatory Motion to _____.
   A. Commit
   B. a Question Taken from the Table
   C. committees
   D. committee of the whole

   *Answer:* A) Commit. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:9 — “Dilatory Motion to Commit.”

**606.** `[C0407 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a motion to _____ merely lacks essential details—for example, when the motion is made simply “to refer the main question to a committee”—the chair should not rule it out of order.

   *Answer:* Commit — “When a motion to Commit merely lacks essential details—for example, when the motion is made simply “to refer the main question to a committee”—the chair should not rule it out of order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:10 — “When a motion to Commit merely lacks essential details—for example, when the motion is made simply “to refer the main question to a committee”—the chair should not rule it out of order.”

**607.** `[C0407 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a motion to _____ merely lacks essential details—for example, when the motion is made simply “to refer the main question to a committee”—the chair should not rule it out of order.
   A. Commit
   B. undebatable
   C. privileged motions
   D. Which to Adjourn

   *Answer:* A) Commit. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:10 — “When a motion to Commit merely lacks essential details—for example, when the motion is made simply “to refer the main question to a committee”—the chair should not rule it out of order.”

   *Answer:* Commit — “Completing an Incomplete Motion to Commit.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:12 — “Completing an Incomplete Motion to Commit.”

   A. Commit

   C. order
   D. corresponding secretary

   *Answer:* A) Commit. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:12 — “Completing an Incomplete Motion to Commit.”

**610.** `[C0409 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Completing an Incomplete Motion to _____.

   *Answer:* Commit — “Completing an Incomplete Motion to Commit.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:12 — “Completing an Incomplete Motion to Commit.”

   A. regular

**612.** `[C0410 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The first of these methods of selection that receives a majority vote is then inserted into the motion to _____ and the remainder are ignored.

   *Answer:* Commit — “The first of these methods of selection that receives a majority vote is then inserted into the motion to Commit and the remainder are ignored.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:13 — “The first of these methods of selection that receives a majority vote is then inserted into the motion to Commit and the remainder are ignored.”

   A. will be
   B. Refer
   C. Commit
   D. proposed to be reconsidered

   *Answer:* C) Commit. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:13 — “The first of these methods of selection that receives a majority vote is then inserted into the motion to Commit and the remainder are ignored.”

**614.** `[C0411 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the motion to _____ lacks any other detail, the chair proceeds in a similar fashion to obtain completion of the motion.

   *Answer:* Commit — “If the motion to Commit lacks any other detail, the chair proceeds in a similar fashion to obtain completion of the motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:14 — “If the motion to Commit lacks any other detail, the chair proceeds in a similar fashion to obtain completion of the motion.”

   A. Commit
   B. auditors’

   D. interrupted

   *Answer:* A) Commit. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:14 — “If the motion to Commit lacks any other detail, the chair proceeds in a similar fashion to obtain completion of the motion.”

**616.** `[C0411 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the motion to _____ lacks any other detail, the chair proceeds in a similar fashion to obtain completion of the motion.

See RONR (12th ed.) §13:14 — “If the motion to Commit lacks any other detail, the chair proceeds in a similar fashion to obtain completion of the motion.”

**617.** `[C0411 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the motion to _____ lacks any other detail, the chair proceeds in a similar fashion to obtain completion of the motion.
   A. amendment of the first degree
   B. Commit

   D. calling the meeting to order

   *Answer:* B) Commit. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:14 — “If the motion to Commit lacks any other detail, the chair proceeds in a similar fashion to obtain completion of the motion.”

**618.** `[C0411 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As soon as it is completed, if it is a _____ motion to Commit, the chair states the question on it, thus opening it to additional debate during which any member can move—or the chair himself can suggest—that it be amended by adding instructions.

   *Answer:* pending — “As soon as it is completed, if it is a pending motion to Commit, the chair states the question on it, thus opening it to additional debate during which any member can move—or the chair himself can suggest—that it be amended by adding instructions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:14 — “As soon as it is completed, if it is a pending motion to Commit, the chair states the question on it, thus opening it to additional debate during which any member can move—or the chair himself can suggest—that it be amended by adding instructions.”

**619.** `[C0413 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Although it is not necessary to place on a special committee the member who made the motion to _____, it is usual to do so when such a person is interested and qualified.

   *Answer:* Commit — “Although it is not necessary to place on a special committee the member who made the motion to Commit, it is usual to do so when such a person is interested and qualified.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:16 — “Although it is not necessary to place on a special committee the member who made the motion to Commit, it is usual to do so when such a person is interested and qualified.”

**620.** `[C0413 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Although it is not necessary to place on a special committee the member who made the motion to _____, it is usual to do so when such a person is interested and qualified.
   A. Commit
   B. add
   C. Fix the
   D. no more than a quarterly time interval

   *Answer:* A) Commit. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:16 — “Although it is not necessary to place on a special committee the member who made the motion to Commit, it is usual to do so when such a person is interested and qualified.”

**621.** `[C0416 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: On the other hand, any pending amendments, motions for Division of a Question or _____, and adhering debatable appeals (or points of order submitted to the judgment of the assembly) go to the committee with the main motion, and are reported with it.

   *Answer:* Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim — “On the other hand, any pending amendments, motions for Division of a Question or Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim, and adhering debatable appeals (or points of order submitted to the judgment of the assembly) go to the committee with the main motion, and are reported with it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:19 — “On the other hand, any pending amendments, motions for Division of a Question or Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim, and adhering debatable appeals (or points of order submitted to the judgment of the assembly) go to the committee with the main motion, and are reported with it.”

   A. of a Question
   B. chairman
   C. Up Business out of Its Proper Order
   D. Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim

   *Answer:* D) Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:19 — “On the other hand, any pending amendments, motions for Division of a Question or Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim, and adhering debatable appeals (or points of order submitted to the judgment of the assembly) go to the committee with the main motion, and are reported with it.”

   *Answer:* a Question — “On the other hand, any pending amendments, motions for Division of a Question or Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim, and adhering debatable appeals (or points of order submitted to the judgment of the assembly) go to the committee with the main motion, and are reported with it.”. **626.** `[C0417 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Since the purpose of referring a motion to a committee is generally to allow for a more thorough consideration of the matter, greater freedom of action is obtained once the committee commences its deliberations, as follows: The usual prohibition against introducing a motion to _____ that raises the same question of content and effect as one already decided by the assembly at the same session (cf.

   *Answer:* Amend — “Since the purpose of referring a motion to a committee is generally to allow for a more thorough consideration of the matter, greater freedom of action is obtained once the committee commences its deliberations, as follows: The usual prohibition against introducing a motion to Amend that raises the same question of content and effect as one already decided by the assembly at the same session (cf.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:20 — “Since the purpose of referring a motion to a committee is generally to allow for a more thorough consideration of the matter, greater freedom of action is obtained once the committee commences its deliberations, as follows: The usual prohibition against introducing a motion to Amend that raises the same question of content and effect as one already decided by the assembly at the same session (cf.”

   A. society,
   B. Incidental
   C. Order Newly Revised
   D. Amend

**628.** `[C0418 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a question is referred to a committee, any orders limiting or extending the limits of debate (15) or for the Previous Question (16) are thereby exhausted, so that when the question is brought back from the committee, debate in the assembly takes place according to the regular rules, even if at the same session in which the motion to _____ was adopted.

   *Answer:* Commit — “When a question is referred to a committee, any orders limiting or extending the limits of debate (15) or for the Previous Question (16) are thereby exhausted, so that when the question is brought back from the committee, debate in the assembly takes place according to the regular rules, even if at the same session in which the motion to Commit was adopted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §13:21 — “When a question is referred to a committee, any orders limiting or extending the limits of debate (15) or for the Previous Question (16) are thereby exhausted, so that when the question is brought back from the committee, debate in the assembly takes place according to the regular rules, even if at the same session in which the motion to Commit was adopted.”

   A. Commit
   B. plurality vote
   C. presents
   D. adjournment

   *Answer:* A) Commit.    D. landscaped

**636.** `[C0424 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The subsidiary motion to _____ to a Certain Time (or Postpone Definitely, or Postpone) is the motion by which action on a pending question can be put off, within limits, to a definite session, day, meeting, or hour, or until after a certain event.

   *Answer:* Postpone — “The subsidiary motion to Postpone to a Certain Time (or Postpone Definitely, or Postpone) is the motion by which action on a pending question can be put off, within limits, to a definite session, day, meeting, or hour, or until after a certain event.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Postpone to a Certain Time (or Postpone Definitely, or Postpone) is the motion by which action on a pending question can be put off, within limits, to a definite session, day, meeting, or hour, or until after a certain event.”

**637.** `[C0424 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The subsidiary motion to _____ to a Certain Time (or Postpone Definitely, or Postpone) is the motion by which action on a pending question can be put off, within limits, to a definite session, day, meeting, or hour, or until after a certain event.
   A. Articles of Incorporation
   B. body of people
   C. agenda
   D. Postpone

   *Answer:* D) Postpone. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Postpone to a Certain Time (or Postpone Definitely, or Postpone) is the motion by which action on a pending question can be put off, within limits, to a definite session, day, meeting, or hour, or until after a certain event.”

See RONR (12th ed.) §14:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Postpone to a Certain Time (or Postpone Definitely, or Postpone) is the motion by which action on a pending question can be put off, within limits, to a definite session, day, meeting, or hour, or until after a certain event.”

**640.** `[C0424 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The subsidiary motion to _____ (or Postpone Definitely, or Postpone) is the motion by which action on a pending question can be put off, within limits, to a definite session, day, meeting, or hour, or until after a certain event.

   *Answer:* Postpone to a Certain Time — “The subsidiary motion to Postpone to a Certain Time (or Postpone Definitely, or Postpone) is the motion by which action on a pending question can be put off, within limits, to a definite session, day, meeting, or hour, or until after a certain event.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Postpone to a Certain Time (or Postpone Definitely, or Postpone) is the motion by which action on a pending question can be put off, within limits, to a definite session, day, meeting, or hour, or until after a certain event.”

**641.** `[C0425 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In its simple and usual form, the motion to postpone a question makes it a general _____, which cannot interrupt pending business.

   *Answer:* order — “In its simple and usual form, the motion to postpone a question makes it a general order, which cannot interrupt pending business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:2 — “In its simple and usual form, the motion to postpone a question makes it a general order, which cannot interrupt pending business.”

**642.** `[C0425 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In its simple and usual form, the motion to postpone a question makes it a general _____, which cannot interrupt pending business.
   A. order
   B. mass meeting,
   C. primary
   D. parliamentary law

   *Answer:* A) order. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:2 — “In its simple and usual form, the motion to postpone a question makes it a general order, which cannot interrupt pending business.”

**643.** `[C0425 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To give the question being postponed a _____ type of priority that will enable it to interrupt the consideration of other business at the specified time, the motion to Postpone may, by a two-thirds vote, be adopted in a form that makes the question a special order.

   *Answer:* special — “To give the question being postponed a special type of priority that will enable it to interrupt the consideration of other business at the specified time, the motion to Postpone may, by a two-thirds vote, be adopted in a form that makes the question a special order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:2 — “To give the question being postponed a special type of priority that will enable it to interrupt the consideration of other business at the specified time, the motion to Postpone may, by a two-thirds vote, be adopted in a form that makes the question a special order.”

**644.** `[C0425 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To give the question being postponed a _____ type of priority that will enable it to interrupt the consideration of other business at the specified time, the motion to Postpone may, by a two-thirds vote, be adopted in a form that makes the question a special order.
   A. rejecting
   B. preference in being recognized
   C. special
   D. two-thirds vote

   *Answer:* C) special. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:2 — “To give the question being postponed a special type of priority that will enable it to interrupt the consideration of other business at the specified time, the motion to Postpone may, by a two-thirds vote, be adopted in a form that makes the question a special order.”

See RONR (12th ed.) §14:2 — “In its simple and usual form, the motion to postpone a question makes it a general order, which cannot interrupt pending business.”

**646.** `[C0426 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the effect would be to change action already taken by the assembly, as, for example, “to postpone for three weeks the dinner scheduled for October 15,” such a motion is a particular case of the motion to Amend _____ (35).

   *Answer:* Something Previously Adopted — “If the effect would be to change action already taken by the assembly, as, for example, “to postpone for three weeks the dinner scheduled for October 15,” such a motion is a particular case of the motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:3 — “If the effect would be to change action already taken by the assembly, as, for example, “to postpone for three weeks the dinner scheduled for October 15,” such a motion is a particular case of the motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35).”

**647.** `[C0426 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the effect would be to change action already taken by the assembly, as, for example, “to postpone for three weeks the dinner scheduled for October 15,” such a motion is a particular case of the motion to Amend _____ (35).
   A. preferential voting
   B. Improper Amendments
   C. Call of the House
   D. Something Previously Adopted

   *Answer:* D) Something Previously Adopted. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:3 — “If the effect would be to change action already taken by the assembly, as, for example, “to postpone for three weeks the dinner scheduled for October 15,” such a motion is a particular case of the motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35).”

**648.** `[C0426 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a motion proposes to postpone a matter that is not pending—for example, the hearing of a committee’s report— such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to _____, but is an incidental main motion (10).

   *Answer:* Postpone — “When a motion proposes to postpone a matter that is not pending—for example, the hearing of a committee’s report— such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to Postpone, but is an incidental main motion (10).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:3 — “When a motion proposes to postpone a matter that is not pending—for example, the hearing of a committee’s report— such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to Postpone, but is an incidental main motion (10).”

**649.** `[C0426 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a motion proposes to postpone a matter that is not pending—for example, the hearing of a committee’s report— such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to _____, but is an incidental main motion (10).
   A. Requests and Inquiries
   B. of Order Newly Revised
   C. Lay on the
   D. Postpone

   *Answer:* D) Postpone. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:3 — “When a motion proposes to postpone a matter that is not pending—for example, the hearing of a committee’s report— such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to Postpone, but is an incidental main motion (10).”

   C. Other
   D. at a point established in the

   *Answer:* B) meeting. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:5 — “Rules limiting the time to which a question can be postponed are related to the terms meeting and session (8), as follows:”

**655.** `[C0429 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a motion to postpone a question to a regular meeting is already pending, the privileged motion to Fix the _____ (22) can be used to set an adjourned meeting, and the motion to Postpone can then be amended so that the proposed postponement will be to the adjourned meeting.

   *Answer:* Time to Which to Adjourn — “If a motion to postpone a question to a regular meeting is already pending, the privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn (22) can be used to set an adjourned meeting, and the motion to Postpone can then be amended so that the proposed postponement will be to the adjourned meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:7 — “If a motion to postpone a question to a regular meeting is already pending, the privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn (22) can be used to set an adjourned meeting, and the motion to Postpone can then be amended so that the proposed postponement will be to the adjourned meeting.”

**656.** `[C0429 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a motion to postpone a question to a regular meeting is already pending, the privileged motion to Fix the _____ (22) can be used to set an adjourned meeting, and the motion to Postpone can then be amended so that the proposed postponement will be to the adjourned meeting.
   A. Time to Which to Adjourn
   B. mass meeting,
   C. part
   D. strike out

   *Answer:* A) Time to Which to Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:7 — “If a motion to postpone a question to a regular meeting is already pending, the privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn (22) can be used to set an adjourned meeting, and the motion to Postpone can then be amended so that the proposed postponement will be to the adjourned meeting.”

**657.** `[C0429 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If it is desired to postpone a question to a time between regular meetings, it is necessary first to provide for an _____, which is a continuation of the session scheduling it;

   *Answer:* adjourned meeting — “If it is desired to postpone a question to a time between regular meetings, it is necessary first to provide for an adjourned meeting, which is a continuation of the session scheduling it;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:7 — “If it is desired to postpone a question to a time between regular meetings, it is necessary first to provide for an adjourned meeting, which is a continuation of the session scheduling it;”

**658.** `[C0429 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If it is desired to postpone a question to a time between regular meetings, it is necessary first to provide for an _____, which is a continuation of the session scheduling it;
   A. officer
   B. adjourned meeting
   C. who should be engaged well in
   D. counted

   *Answer:* B) adjourned meeting. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:7 — “If it is desired to postpone a question to a time between regular meetings, it is necessary first to provide for an adjourned meeting, which is a continuation of the session scheduling it;”

**659.** `[C0429 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a motion to postpone a question to a regular meeting is already pending, the privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn (22) can be used to set an adjourned meeting, and the motion to _____ can then be amended so that the proposed postponement will be to the adjourned meeting.

   *Answer:* Postpone — “If a motion to postpone a question to a regular meeting is already pending, the privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn (22) can be used to set an adjourned meeting, and the motion to Postpone can then be amended so that the proposed postponement will be to the adjourned meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:7 — “If a motion to postpone a question to a regular meeting is already pending, the privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn (22) can be used to set an adjourned meeting, and the motion to Postpone can then be amended so that the proposed postponement will be to the adjourned meeting.”

**660.** `[C0431 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Neither the motion to _____ nor any amendment to it is in order if the effect would be the same as that of the motion to Postpone Indefinitely—that is, if it would kill the measure.

   *Answer:* Postpone to a Certain Time — “Neither the motion to Postpone to a Certain Time nor any amendment to it is in order if the effect would be the same as that of the motion to Postpone Indefinitely—that is, if it would kill the measure.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:9 — “Neither the motion to Postpone to a Certain Time nor any amendment to it is in order if the effect would be the same as that of the motion to Postpone Indefinitely—that is, if it would kill the measure.”

**661.** `[C0431 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Neither the motion to _____ nor any amendment to it is in order if the effect would be the same as that of the motion to Postpone Indefinitely—that is, if it would kill the measure.
   A. immediately
   B. executive board,
   C. Postpone to a Certain Time
   D. limit

   *Answer:* C) Postpone to a Certain Time. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:9 — “Neither the motion to Postpone to a Certain Time nor any amendment to it is in order if the effect would be the same as that of the motion to Postpone Indefinitely—that is, if it would kill the measure.”

**662.** `[C0431 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Neither the motion to Postpone to a Certain Time nor any amendment to it is in order if the effect would be the same as that of the motion to _____—that is, if it would kill the measure.

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely — “Neither the motion to Postpone to a Certain Time nor any amendment to it is in order if the effect would be the same as that of the motion to Postpone Indefinitely—that is, if it would kill the measure.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:9 — “Neither the motion to Postpone to a Certain Time nor any amendment to it is in order if the effect would be the same as that of the motion to Postpone Indefinitely—that is, if it would kill the measure.”

**663.** `[C0431 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Neither the motion to Postpone to a Certain Time nor any amendment to it is in order if the effect would be the same as that of the motion to _____—that is, if it would kill the measure.
   A. Meeting
   B. previous notice,
   C. when no question is
   D. Postpone Indefinitely

   *Answer:* D) Postpone Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:9 — “Neither the motion to Postpone to a Certain Time nor any amendment to it is in order if the effect would be the same as that of the motion to Postpone Indefinitely—that is, if it would kill the measure.”

**664.** `[C0431 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Neither the motion to Postpone to a Certain Time nor any amendment to it is in order if the effect would be the same as that of the motion to _____—that is, if it would kill the measure.

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely — “Neither the motion to Postpone to a Certain Time nor any amendment to it is in order if the effect would be the same as that of the motion to Postpone Indefinitely—that is, if it would kill the measure.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:9 — “Neither the motion to Postpone to a Certain Time nor any amendment to it is in order if the effect would be the same as that of the motion to Postpone Indefinitely—that is, if it would kill the measure.”

**665.** `[C0432 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Rule Against _____ment of a Class of Subjects.

   *Answer:* Postpone — “Rule Against Postponement of a Class of Subjects.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:10 — “Rule Against Postponement of a Class of Subjects.”

**666.** `[C0432 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Rule Against _____ment of a Class of Subjects.
   A. guilty
   B. Consider Seriatim
   C. Postpone
   D. clerk

   *Answer:* C) Postpone. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:10 — “Rule Against Postponement of a Class of Subjects.”

**667.** `[C0433 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is not in order, either through a subsidiary motion or a main motion, to postpone a class of business composed of several items or subjects, such as reports of officers or reports of committees (see _____, 41);

   *Answer:* Order of Business — “It is not in order, either through a subsidiary motion or a main motion, to postpone a class of business composed of several items or subjects, such as reports of officers or reports of committees (see Order of Business, 41);”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:11 — “It is not in order, either through a subsidiary motion or a main motion, to postpone a class of business composed of several items or subjects, such as reports of officers or reports of committees (see Order of Business, 41);”

**668.** `[C0433 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is not in order, either through a subsidiary motion or a main motion, to postpone a class of business composed of several items or subjects, such as reports of officers or reports of committees (see _____, 41);
   A. functioning of a society or assembly
   B. Order of Business
   C. specification
   D. present and voting

   *Answer:* B) Order of Business. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:11 — “It is not in order, either through a subsidiary motion or a main motion, to postpone a class of business composed of several items or subjects, such as reports of officers or reports of committees (see Order of Business, 41);”

**669.** `[C0434 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and it can be postponed to an adjourned meeting in the manner explained above, after first adopting, if necessary, a motion to _____.

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn — “and it can be postponed to an adjourned meeting in the manner explained above, after first adopting, if necessary, a motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:12 — “and it can be postponed to an adjourned meeting in the manner explained above, after first adopting, if necessary, a motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn.”

**670.** `[C0434 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: and it can be postponed to an adjourned meeting in the manner explained above, after first adopting, if necessary, a motion to _____.
   A. functioning of a society or assembly
   B. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   C. within the control of the assembly
   D. two-thirds vote

   *Answer:* B) Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:12 — “and it can be postponed to an adjourned meeting in the manner explained above, after first adopting, if necessary, a motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn.”

**671.** `[C0434 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ment of a Subject That the Bylaws Set for a Particular Session.

   *Answer:* Postpone — “Postponement of a Subject That the Bylaws Set for a Particular Session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:12 — “Postponement of a Subject That the Bylaws Set for a Particular Session.”

**672.** `[C0434 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ment of a Subject That the Bylaws Set for a Particular Session.
   A. Amend
   B. control of the assembly
   C. Postpone
   D. Objection to the Consideration of a

   *Answer:* C) Postpone. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:12 — “Postponement of a Subject That the Bylaws Set for a Particular Session.”

**673.** `[C0435 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A postponed question becomes an _____ for the session, day, meeting, or hour to which it is postponed.

   *Answer:* order of the day — “A postponed question becomes an order of the day for the session, day, meeting, or hour to which it is postponed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:13 — “A postponed question becomes an order of the day for the session, day, meeting, or hour to which it is postponed.”

**674.** `[C0435 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A postponed question becomes an _____ for the session, day, meeting, or hour to which it is postponed.
   A. extend the limits
   B. Reconsider and Enter on
   C. the
   D. order of the day

   *Answer:* D) order of the day. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:13 — “A postponed question becomes an order of the day for the session, day, meeting, or hour to which it is postponed.”

**675.** `[C0435 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Time at Which a _____d Question Is Taken Up Again.

   *Answer:* Postpone — “Time at Which a Postponed Question Is Taken Up Again.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:13 — “Time at Which a Postponed Question Is Taken Up Again.”

**676.** `[C0435 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Time at Which a _____d Question Is Taken Up Again.
   A. Postpone
   B. subsidiary
   C. unfinished business,
   D. order of the day,

   *Answer:* A) Postpone. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:13 — “Time at Which a Postponed Question Is Taken Up Again.”

**677.** `[C0435 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It cannot be taken up _____ the time for which it is set, except by reconsidering (37) the vote on the motion to Postpone, or by suspending the rules by a two-thirds vote.

   *Answer:* before — “It cannot be taken up before the time for which it is set, except by reconsidering (37) the vote on the motion to Postpone, or by suspending the rules by a two-thirds vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:13 — “It cannot be taken up before the time for which it is set, except by reconsidering (37) the vote on the motion to Postpone, or by suspending the rules by a two-thirds vote.”

**678.** `[C0436 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Orders of the day consist of _____ and special orders.

   *Answer:* general orders — “Orders of the day consist of general orders and special orders.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:14 — “Orders of the day consist of general orders and special orders.”

**679.** `[C0436 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Orders of the day consist of _____ and special orders.
   A. general orders
   B. standing rules
   C. mass meeting,
   D. Assembly

   *Answer:* A) general orders. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:14 — “Orders of the day consist of general orders and special orders.”

**680.** `[C0436 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Orders of _____ day consist of general orders and special orders.

   *Answer:* the — “Orders of the day consist of general orders and special orders.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:14 — “Orders of the day consist of general orders and special orders.”

**681.** `[C0436 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Orders of _____ day consist of general orders and special orders.
   A. same
   B. limiting
   C. Debatability of Motions,
   D. the

   *Answer:* D) the. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:14 — “Orders of the day consist of general orders and special orders.”

**682.** `[C0436 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Orders of the day consist of general orders and _____ orders.

   *Answer:* special — “Orders of the day consist of general orders and special orders.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:14 — “Orders of the day consist of general orders and special orders.”

**683.** `[C0439 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: however, if the chair fails to state the question at the correct time, any member may then demand that the postponed question be taken up as ordered, by making a _____ (18).

   *Answer:* Call for the Orders of the Day — “however, if the chair fails to state the question at the correct time, any member may then demand that the postponed question be taken up as ordered, by making a Call for the Orders of the Day (18).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:17 — “however, if the chair fails to state the question at the correct time, any member may then demand that the postponed question be taken up as ordered, by making a Call for the Orders of the Day (18).”

**684.** `[C0439 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: however, if the chair fails to state the question at the correct time, any member may then demand that the postponed question be taken up as ordered, by making a _____ (18).
   A. Any Other Privilege
   B. Call for the Orders of the Day
   C. event
   D. bylaws

   *Answer:* B) Call for the Orders of the Day. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:17 — “however, if the chair fails to state the question at the correct time, any member may then demand that the postponed question be taken up as ordered, by making a Call for the Orders of the Day (18).”

**685.** `[C0440 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a main motion is postponed, one or more of the subsidiary motions to Postpone Indefinitely, Amend, and Commit or incidental motions for Division of a Question and _____ may be pending.

   *Answer:* Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim — “When a main motion is postponed, one or more of the subsidiary motions to Postpone Indefinitely, Amend, and Commit or incidental motions for Division of a Question and Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim may be pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:18 — “When a main motion is postponed, one or more of the subsidiary motions to Postpone Indefinitely, Amend, and Commit or incidental motions for Division of a Question and Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim may be pending.”

**686.** `[C0440 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a main motion is postponed, one or more of the subsidiary motions to Postpone Indefinitely, Amend, and Commit or incidental motions for Division of a Question and _____ may be pending.
   A. brought before the assembly
   B. Incidental
   C. Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim
   D. substitute

   *Answer:* C) Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:18 — “When a main motion is postponed, one or more of the subsidiary motions to Postpone Indefinitely, Amend, and Commit or incidental motions for Division of a Question and Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim may be pending.”

**687.** `[C0440 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a main motion is postponed, one or more of the subsidiary motions to _____, Amend, and Commit or incidental motions for Division of a Question and Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim may be pending.

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely — “When a main motion is postponed, one or more of the subsidiary motions to Postpone Indefinitely, Amend, and Commit or incidental motions for Division of a Question and Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim may be pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:18 — “When a main motion is postponed, one or more of the subsidiary motions to Postpone Indefinitely, Amend, and Commit or incidental motions for Division of a Question and Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim may be pending.”

**688.** `[C0440 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a main motion is postponed, one or more of the subsidiary motions to _____, Amend, and Commit or incidental motions for Division of a Question and Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim may be pending.
   A. Postpone Indefinitely
   B. would
   C. Strike Out
   D. order

   *Answer:* A) Postpone Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:18 — “When a main motion is postponed, one or more of the subsidiary motions to Postpone Indefinitely, Amend, and Commit or incidental motions for Division of a Question and Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim may be pending.”

**689.** `[C0440 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a main motion is postponed, one or more of the subsidiary motions to Postpone Indefinitely, Amend, and _____ or incidental motions for Division of a Question and Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim may be pending.

   *Answer:* Commit — “When a main motion is postponed, one or more of the subsidiary motions to Postpone Indefinitely, Amend, and Commit or incidental motions for Division of a Question and Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim may be pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:18 — “When a main motion is postponed, one or more of the subsidiary motions to Postpone Indefinitely, Amend, and Commit or incidental motions for Division of a Question and Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim may be pending.”

**690.** `[C0441 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Except for the effect of an unexhausted order limiting or closing debate as just stated, when a question is taken up _____ from the one on which it was postponed, the right of members to debate it begins over again, as if the question had not previously been debated;

   *Answer:* on a different day — “Except for the effect of an unexhausted order limiting or closing debate as just stated, when a question is taken up on a different day from the one on which it was postponed, the right of members to debate it begins over again, as if the question had not previously been debated;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:19 — “Except for the effect of an unexhausted order limiting or closing debate as just stated, when a question is taken up on a different day from the one on which it was postponed, the right of members to debate it begins over again, as if the question had not previously been debated;”

**691.** `[C0441 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Except for the effect of an unexhausted order limiting or closing debate as just stated, when a question is taken up _____ from the one on which it was postponed, the right of members to debate it begins over again, as if the question had not previously been debated;
   A. on a different day
   B. Adjourned Meeting
   C. Insert, or Add
   D. finally, disposed of

   *Answer:* A) on a different day. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:19 — “Except for the effect of an unexhausted order limiting or closing debate as just stated, when a question is taken up on a different day from the one on which it was postponed, the right of members to debate it begins over again, as if the question had not previously been debated;”

**692.** `[C0441 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: But when consideration is resumed at the _____ session—even on another day, as may happen in a convention—all such applicable orders remain in effect.

   *Answer:* same — “But when consideration is resumed at the same session—even on another day, as may happen in a convention—all such applicable orders remain in effect.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:19 — “But when consideration is resumed at the same session—even on another day, as may happen in a convention—all such applicable orders remain in effect.”

**693.** `[C0441 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: But when consideration is resumed at the _____ session—even on another day, as may happen in a convention—all such applicable orders remain in effect.
   A. recorder,
   B. Suspend the Rules
   C. same
   D. privileged motions

   *Answer:* C) same. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:19 — “But when consideration is resumed at the same session—even on another day, as may happen in a convention—all such applicable orders remain in effect.”

**694.** `[C0443 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the amendment to make a special order is rejected, the chair proceeds in the usual manner to take a voice vote on the _____ motion to postpone.

   *Answer:* unamended — “If the amendment to make a special order is rejected, the chair proceeds in the usual manner to take a voice vote on the unamended motion to postpone.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:22 — “If the amendment to make a special order is rejected, the chair proceeds in the usual manner to take a voice vote on the unamended motion to postpone.”

**695.** `[C0443 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the amendment to make a special order is rejected, the chair proceeds in the usual manner to take a voice vote on the _____ motion to postpone.
   A. original
   B. executive committee
   C. voce
   D. unamended

   *Answer:* D) unamended. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:22 — “If the amendment to make a special order is rejected, the chair proceeds in the usual manner to take a voice vote on the unamended motion to postpone.”

**696.** `[C0443 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: But if the resolution _____ been made a special order for the following day at 11 A.M., as in the above example, then at the appointed time the chair says:

   *Answer:* has — “But if the resolution has been made a special order for the following day at 11 A.M., as in the above example, then at the appointed time the chair says:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:22 — “But if the resolution has been made a special order for the following day at 11 A.M., as in the above example, then at the appointed time the chair says:”

**697.** `[C0443 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: But if the resolution _____ been made a special order for the following day at 11 A.M., as in the above example, then at the appointed time the chair says:
   A. confirm
   B. execution
   C. Reference Committee
   D. has

   *Answer:* D) has. See RONR (12th ed.) §14:22 — “But if the resolution has been made a special order for the following day at 11 A.M., as in the above example, then at the appointed time the chair says:”

**698.** `[C0444 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (The other motion serving such a purpose is the _____, 16.

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “(The other motion serving such a purpose is the Previous Question, 16.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:1 — “(The other motion serving such a purpose is the Previous Question, 16.”

**699.** `[C0444 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (The other motion serving such a purpose is the _____, 16.

   C. special rules of order
   D. Previous Question

**700.** `[C0444 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The subsidiary motion to _____ is one of the two motions by means of which an assembly can exercise special control over debate on a pending question or on a series of pending questions.

   *Answer:* Limit or Extend Limits of Debate — “The subsidiary motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate is one of the two motions by means of which an assembly can exercise special control over debate on a pending question or on a series of pending questions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate is one of the two motions by means of which an assembly can exercise special control over debate on a pending question or on a series of pending questions.”

**701.** `[C0444 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The subsidiary motion to _____ is one of the two motions by means of which an assembly can exercise special control over debate on a pending question or on a series of pending questions.
   A. Point
   B. Program
   C. Extend Limits of Debate
   D. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate

   *Answer:* D) Limit or Extend Limits of Debate. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:1 — “The subsidiary motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate is one of the two motions by means of which an assembly can exercise special control over debate on a pending question or on a series of pending questions.”

See RONR (12th ed.) §15:2 — “The motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate can limit debate by: (1) reducing the number or length of speeches permitted, without including specific provision for closing debate;”

**703.** `[C0445 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The motion to _____ can limit debate by: (1) reducing the number or length of speeches permitted, without including specific provision for closing debate;
   A. Division of
   B. adjournment sine die
   C. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate
   D. Subsidiary motions

   *Answer:* C) Limit or Extend Limits of Debate. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:2 — “The motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate can limit debate by: (1) reducing the number or length of speeches permitted, without including specific provision for closing debate;”

**704.** `[C0445 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate can _____ debate by: (1) reducing the number or length of speeches permitted, without including specific provision for closing debate;

   *Answer:* limit — “The motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate can limit debate by: (1) reducing the number or length of speeches permitted, without including specific provision for closing debate;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:2 — “The motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate can limit debate by: (1) reducing the number or length of speeches permitted, without including specific provision for closing debate;”

**705.** `[C0445 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate can _____ debate by: (1) reducing the number or length of speeches permitted, without including specific provision for closing debate;

**706.** `[C0445 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It cannot impose an immediate closing of debate, which requires a different motion—the _____.

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “It cannot impose an immediate closing of debate, which requires a different motion—the Previous Question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:2 — “It cannot impose an immediate closing of debate, which requires a different motion—the Previous Question.”

**707.** `[C0446 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When an assembly adopts a motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, it is said to adopt an “_____” taking such action.

   *Answer:* order — “When an assembly adopts a motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, it is said to adopt an “order” taking such action.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:3 — “When an assembly adopts a motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, it is said to adopt an “order” taking such action.”

**708.** `[C0446 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When an assembly adopts a motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, it is said to adopt an “_____” taking such action.
   A. Disposition of
   B. Standing
   C. order
   D. meetings

   *Answer:* C) order. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:3 — “When an assembly adopts a motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, it is said to adopt an “order” taking such action.”

**709.** `[C0446 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When an assembly adopts a motion to _____ Limits of Debate, it is said to adopt an “order” taking such action.

   *Answer:* Limit or Extend — “When an assembly adopts a motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, it is said to adopt an “order” taking such action.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:3 — “When an assembly adopts a motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, it is said to adopt an “order” taking such action.”

**710.** `[C0446 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When an assembly adopts a motion to _____ Limits of Debate, it is said to adopt an “order” taking such action.
   A. parliamentary law
   B. add
   C. of a Question
   D. Limit or Extend

   *Answer:* D) Limit or Extend. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:3 — “When an assembly adopts a motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, it is said to adopt an “order” taking such action.”

**711.** `[C0446 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When an assembly adopts a motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, it is said to adopt an “_____” taking such action.

   *Answer:* order — “When an assembly adopts a motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, it is said to adopt an “order” taking such action.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:3 — “When an assembly adopts a motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, it is said to adopt an “order” taking such action.”

**712.** `[C0447 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a motion proposing to change the regular limits of debate (for any length of time or during the consideration of one or more particular subjects) is made while no question is pending, such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to Limit or Extend _____, but is an incidental main motion (although it requires a two-thirds vote for its adoption, just as the subsidiary motion does).

   *Answer:* Limits of Debate — “If a motion proposing to change the regular limits of debate (for any length of time or during the consideration of one or more particular subjects) is made while no question is pending, such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, but is an incidental main motion (although it requires a two-thirds vote for its adoption, just as the subsidiary motion does).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:4 — “If a motion proposing to change the regular limits of debate (for any length of time or during the consideration of one or more particular subjects) is made while no question is pending, such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, but is an incidental main motion (although it requires a two-thirds vote for its adoption, just as the subsidiary motion does).”

**713.** `[C0447 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a motion proposing to change the regular limits of debate (for any length of time or during the consideration of one or more particular subjects) is made while no question is pending, such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to Limit or Extend _____, but is an incidental main motion (although it requires a two-thirds vote for its adoption, just as the subsidiary motion does).
   A. Call of the House,
   B. same
   C. ruling
   D. Limits of Debate

   *Answer:* D) Limits of Debate. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:4 — “If a motion proposing to change the regular limits of debate (for any length of time or during the consideration of one or more particular subjects) is made while no question is pending, such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, but is an incidental main motion (although it requires a two-thirds vote for its adoption, just as the subsidiary motion does).”

**714.** `[C0447 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a motion proposing to change the regular limits of debate (for any length of time or during the consideration of one or more particular subjects) is made while no question is pending, such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to _____ Limits of Debate, but is an incidental main motion (although it requires a two-thirds vote for its adoption, just as the subsidiary motion does).

   *Answer:* Limit or Extend — “If a motion proposing to change the regular limits of debate (for any length of time or during the consideration of one or more particular subjects) is made while no question is pending, such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, but is an incidental main motion (although it requires a two-thirds vote for its adoption, just as the subsidiary motion does).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:4 — “If a motion proposing to change the regular limits of debate (for any length of time or during the consideration of one or more particular subjects) is made while no question is pending, such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, but is an incidental main motion (although it requires a two-thirds vote for its adoption, just as the subsidiary motion does).”

**715.** `[C0447 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a motion proposing to change the regular limits of debate (for any length of time or during the consideration of one or more particular subjects) is made while no question is pending, such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to _____ Limits of Debate, but is an incidental main motion (although it requires a two-thirds vote for its adoption, just as the subsidiary motion does).
   A. Electronic Meetings
   B. Limit or Extend
   C. and Example: 1(a), 1(b)
   D. vote

   *Answer:* B) Limit or Extend. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:4 — “If a motion proposing to change the regular limits of debate (for any length of time or during the consideration of one or more particular subjects) is made while no question is pending, such a motion is not the subsidiary motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, but is an incidental main motion (although it requires a two-thirds vote for its adoption, just as the subsidiary motion does).”

**716.** `[C0448 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Any relevant provisions can be included in the motion to _____ by its maker or, while the motion is pending, can be added or modified by amendment (12).

   *Answer:* Limit or Extend Limits of Debate — “Any relevant provisions can be included in the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate by its maker or, while the motion is pending, can be added or modified by amendment (12).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:6 — “Any relevant provisions can be included in the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate by its maker or, while the motion is pending, can be added or modified by amendment (12).”

**717.** `[C0448 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Any relevant provisions can be included in the motion to _____ by its maker or, while the motion is pending, can be added or modified by amendment (12).
   A. Strike Out
   B. Insert, or Add
   C. ruling
   D. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate

   *Answer:* D) Limit or Extend Limits of Debate. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:6 — “Any relevant provisions can be included in the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate by its maker or, while the motion is pending, can be added or modified by amendment (12).”

**718.** `[C0449 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If, at the time the motion to _____ Extend Limits of Debate is made, the immediately pending question is one among a consecutive series of pending debatable questions, the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate can be applied, as noted in Standard Characteristic 2 above, to the immediately pending question, to any consecutive part of the series beginning with the immediately pending question, or to the entire series.

   *Answer:* Limit or — “If, at the time the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate is made, the immediately pending question is one among a consecutive series of pending debatable questions, the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate can be applied, as noted in Standard Characteristic 2 above, to the immediately pending question, to any consecutive part of the series beginning with the immediately pending question, or to the entire series.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:7 — “If, at the time the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate is made, the immediately pending question is one among a consecutive series of pending debatable questions, the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate can be applied, as noted in Standard Characteristic 2 above, to the immediately pending question, to any consecutive part of the series beginning with the immediately pending question, or to the entire series.”

**719.** `[C0449 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If, at the time the motion to _____ Extend Limits of Debate is made, the immediately pending question is one among a consecutive series of pending debatable questions, the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate can be applied, as noted in Standard Characteristic 2 above, to the immediately pending question, to any consecutive part of the series beginning with the immediately pending question, or to the entire series.
   A. Limit or
   B. something to
   C. Approved
   D. over

   *Answer:* A) Limit or. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:7 — “If, at the time the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate is made, the immediately pending question is one among a consecutive series of pending debatable questions, the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate can be applied, as noted in Standard Characteristic 2 above, to the immediately pending question, to any consecutive part of the series beginning with the immediately pending question, or to the entire series.”

**720.** `[C0449 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If, at the time the motion to Limit or _____ is made, the immediately pending question is one among a consecutive series of pending debatable questions, the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate can be applied, as noted in Standard Characteristic 2 above, to the immediately pending question, to any consecutive part of the series beginning with the immediately pending question, or to the entire series.

   *Answer:* Extend Limits of Debate — “If, at the time the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate is made, the immediately pending question is one among a consecutive series of pending debatable questions, the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate can be applied, as noted in Standard Characteristic 2 above, to the immediately pending question, to any consecutive part of the series beginning with the immediately pending question, or to the entire series.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:7 — “If, at the time the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate is made, the immediately pending question is one among a consecutive series of pending debatable questions, the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate can be applied, as noted in Standard Characteristic 2 above, to the immediately pending question, to any consecutive part of the series beginning with the immediately pending question, or to the entire series.”

**721.** `[C0449 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If, at the time the motion to Limit or _____ is made, the immediately pending question is one among a consecutive series of pending debatable questions, the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate can be applied, as noted in Standard Characteristic 2 above, to the immediately pending question, to any consecutive part of the series beginning with the immediately pending question, or to the entire series.
   A. Division of
   B. Extend Limits of Debate
   C. viva voce
   D. the Assembly

   *Answer:* B) Extend Limits of Debate. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:7 — “If, at the time the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate is made, the immediately pending question is one among a consecutive series of pending debatable questions, the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate can be applied, as noted in Standard Characteristic 2 above, to the immediately pending question, to any consecutive part of the series beginning with the immediately pending question, or to the entire series.”

**722.** `[C0449 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If, at the time the motion to _____ is made, the immediately pending question is one among a consecutive series of pending debatable questions, the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate can be applied, as noted in Standard Characteristic 2 above, to the immediately pending question, to any consecutive part of the series beginning with the immediately pending question, or to the entire series.

   *Answer:* Limit or Extend Limits of Debate — “If, at the time the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate is made, the immediately pending question is one among a consecutive series of pending debatable questions, the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate can be applied, as noted in Standard Characteristic 2 above, to the immediately pending question, to any consecutive part of the series beginning with the immediately pending question, or to the entire series.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:7 — “If, at the time the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate is made, the immediately pending question is one among a consecutive series of pending debatable questions, the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate can be applied, as noted in Standard Characteristic 2 above, to the immediately pending question, to any consecutive part of the series beginning with the immediately pending question, or to the entire series.”

**723.** `[C0450 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Unless otherwise specified in the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, its effect on the debate of motions that may subsequently become pending is as follows: An order _____ debate applies not only to the motion(s) on which the limitation is ordered, but also to any debatable subsidiary motions, motions to Reconsider, or debatable appeals that may become pending subsequently while the order is in force.

   *Answer:* limiting — “Unless otherwise specified in the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, its effect on the debate of motions that may subsequently become pending is as follows: An order limiting debate applies not only to the motion(s) on which the limitation is ordered, but also to any debatable subsidiary motions, motions to Reconsider, or debatable appeals that may become pending subsequently while the order is in force.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:8 — “Unless otherwise specified in the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, its effect on the debate of motions that may subsequently become pending is as follows: An order limiting debate applies not only to the motion(s) on which the limitation is ordered, but also to any debatable subsidiary motions, motions to Reconsider, or debatable appeals that may become pending subsequently while the order is in force.”

**724.** `[C0450 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Unless otherwise specified in the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, its effect on the debate of motions that may subsequently become pending is as follows: An order _____ debate applies not only to the motion(s) on which the limitation is ordered, but also to any debatable subsidiary motions, motions to Reconsider, or debatable appeals that may become pending subsequently while the order is in force.
   A. special rules of order
   B. reception
   C. limiting
   D. within the control of the

   *Answer:* C) limiting. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:8 — “Unless otherwise specified in the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, its effect on the debate of motions that may subsequently become pending is as follows: An order limiting debate applies not only to the motion(s) on which the limitation is ordered, but also to any debatable subsidiary motions, motions to Reconsider, or debatable appeals that may become pending subsequently while the order is in force.”

See RONR (12th ed.) §15:8 — “Unless otherwise specified in the motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate, its effect on the debate of motions that may subsequently become pending is as follows: An order limiting debate applies not only to the motion(s) on which the limitation is ordered, but also to any debatable subsidiary motions, motions to Reconsider, or debatable appeals that may become pending subsequently while the order is in force.”

**728.** `[C0452 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the limitation or extension that has been ordered does not provide for closing debate—for example, if the order was “that debate be limited to one speech of five minutes for each member,” as in _____ (a) below—it has no effect on what subsidiary motions can be made.

   *Answer:* Form and Example — “If the limitation or extension that has been ordered does not provide for closing debate—for example, if the order was “that debate be limited to one speech of five minutes for each member,” as in Form and Example (a) below—it has no effect on what subsidiary motions can be made.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:10 — “If the limitation or extension that has been ordered does not provide for closing debate—for example, if the order was “that debate be limited to one speech of five minutes for each member,” as in Form and Example (a) below—it has no effect on what subsidiary motions can be made.”

**729.** `[C0452 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the limitation or extension that has been ordered does not provide for closing debate—for example, if the order was “that debate be limited to one speech of five minutes for each member,” as in _____ (a) below—it has no effect on what subsidiary motions can be made.
   A. part
   B. general orders
   C. Form and Example
   D. debate

   *Answer:* C) Form and Example. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:10 — “If the limitation or extension that has been ordered does not provide for closing debate—for example, if the order was “that debate be limited to one speech of five minutes for each member,” as in Form and Example (a) below—it has no effect on what subsidiary motions can be made.”

   *Answer:* closing — “If the limitation or extension that has been ordered does not provide for closing debate—for example, if the order was “that debate be limited to one speech of five minutes for each member,” as in Form and Example (a) below—it has no effect on what subsidiary motions can be made.”. **731.** `[C0452 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the limitation or extension that has been ordered does not provide for _____ debate—for example, if the order was “that debate be limited to one speech of five minutes for each member,” as in Form and Example (a) below—it has no effect on what subsidiary motions can be made.

   B. Withdraw or Modify a Motion
   C. during a meeting

   *Answer:* A) closing. **732.** `[C0453 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After the adoption of an order that does provide a time for closing debate on the main question—either at a specified hour or after debate for a specified length of time, as in Form and _____ (b) or (f) below—motions to Commit or to Postpone to a Certain Time are not in order, since providing a time for closing debate implies that at that time the question will be voted on.

   *Answer:* Example — “After the adoption of an order that does provide a time for closing debate on the main question—either at a specified hour or after debate for a specified length of time, as in Form and Example (b) or (f) below—motions to Commit or to Postpone to a Certain Time are not in order, since providing a time for closing debate implies that at that time the question will be voted on.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:11 — “After the adoption of an order that does provide a time for closing debate on the main question—either at a specified hour or after debate for a specified length of time, as in Form and Example (b) or (f) below—motions to Commit or to Postpone to a Certain Time are not in order, since providing a time for closing debate implies that at that time the question will be voted on.”

**733.** `[C0453 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After the adoption of an order that does provide a time for closing debate on the main question—either at a specified hour or after debate for a specified length of time, as in Form and _____ (b) or (f) below—motions to Commit or to Postpone to a Certain Time are not in order, since providing a time for closing debate implies that at that time the question will be voted on.
   A. Example
   B. Certificate of Incorporation
   C. house of
   D. by article or section

   *Answer:* A) Example. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:11 — “After the adoption of an order that does provide a time for closing debate on the main question—either at a specified hour or after debate for a specified length of time, as in Form and Example (b) or (f) below—motions to Commit or to Postpone to a Certain Time are not in order, since providing a time for closing debate implies that at that time the question will be voted on.”

**737.** `[C0454 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the allotted time under such an order has expired, no further debate on any pending question is allowed, no further amendments or subsidiary motions other than Lay on the _____ (or a superseding motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate;

   *Answer:* Table — “When the allotted time under such an order has expired, no further debate on any pending question is allowed, no further amendments or subsidiary motions other than Lay on the Table (or a superseding motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:12 — “When the allotted time under such an order has expired, no further debate on any pending question is allowed, no further amendments or subsidiary motions other than Lay on the Table (or a superseding motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate;”
