   *Answer:* Limit or Extend Limits of — “When the allotted time under such an order has expired, no further debate on any pending question is allowed, no further amendments or subsidiary motions other than Lay on the Table (or a superseding motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:12 — “When the allotted time under such an order has expired, no further debate on any pending question is allowed, no further amendments or subsidiary motions other than Lay on the Table (or a superseding motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate;”

**742.** `[C0455 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the assembly wishes only to limit the time devoted to the question without preventing motions to _____ or Postpone from being made during that time, the order can specify, for example, “that debate on the pending resolution be limited to twenty minutes, during which time all applicable secondary motions shall remain in order.”

   *Answer:* Commit — “If the assembly wishes only to limit the time devoted to the question without preventing motions to Commit or Postpone from being made during that time, the order can specify, for example, “that debate on the pending resolution be limited to twenty minutes, during which time all applicable secondary motions shall remain in order.””. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:13 — “If the assembly wishes only to limit the time devoted to the question without preventing motions to Commit or Postpone from being made during that time, the order can specify, for example, “that debate on the pending resolution be limited to twenty minutes, during which time all applicable secondary motions shall remain in order.””

**743.** `[C0455 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the assembly wishes only to limit the time devoted to the question without preventing motions to _____ or Postpone from being made during that time, the order can specify, for example, “that debate on the pending resolution be limited to twenty minutes, during which time all applicable secondary motions shall remain in order.”
   A. For
   B. general parliamentary law
   C. Time to Which to Adjourn
   D. Commit

   *Answer:* D) Commit. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:13 — “If the assembly wishes only to limit the time devoted to the question without preventing motions to Commit or Postpone from being made during that time, the order can specify, for example, “that debate on the pending resolution be limited to twenty minutes, during which time all applicable secondary motions shall remain in order.””

**744.** `[C0455 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the assembly wishes only to limit the time devoted to the question without preventing motions to Commit or _____ from being made during that time, the order can specify, for example, “that debate on the pending resolution be limited to twenty minutes, during which time all applicable secondary motions shall remain in order.”

   *Answer:* Postpone — “If the assembly wishes only to limit the time devoted to the question without preventing motions to Commit or Postpone from being made during that time, the order can specify, for example, “that debate on the pending resolution be limited to twenty minutes, during which time all applicable secondary motions shall remain in order.””. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:13 — “If the assembly wishes only to limit the time devoted to the question without preventing motions to Commit or Postpone from being made during that time, the order can specify, for example, “that debate on the pending resolution be limited to twenty minutes, during which time all applicable secondary motions shall remain in order.””

**745.** `[C0455 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the assembly wishes only to limit the time devoted to the question without preventing motions to Commit or _____ from being made during that time, the order can specify, for example, “that debate on the pending resolution be limited to twenty minutes, during which time all applicable secondary motions shall remain in order.”
   A. made while no question is pending
   B. persuasive
   C. closing
   D. Postpone

   *Answer:* D) Postpone. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:13 — “If the assembly wishes only to limit the time devoted to the question without preventing motions to Commit or Postpone from being made during that time, the order can specify, for example, “that debate on the pending resolution be limited to twenty minutes, during which time all applicable secondary motions shall remain in order.””

**746.** `[C0456 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If it is desired to order a limitation or extension of debate whose effect is other than as described above, this can be specified in the order, such as in _____ (e) below.

   *Answer:* Form and Example — “If it is desired to order a limitation or extension of debate whose effect is other than as described above, this can be specified in the order, such as in Form and Example (e) below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:14 — “If it is desired to order a limitation or extension of debate whose effect is other than as described above, this can be specified in the order, such as in Form and Example (e) below.”

**747.** `[C0456 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If it is desired to order a limitation or extension of debate whose effect is other than as described above, this can be specified in the order, such as in _____ (e) below.
   A. legal
   B. the chair
   C. consideration as if in
   D. Form and Example

   *Answer:* D) Form and Example. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:14 — “If it is desired to order a limitation or extension of debate whose effect is other than as described above, this can be specified in the order, such as in Form and Example (e) below.”

**748.** `[C0459 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Unlike the case of main motions and lower-ranking subsidiary motions (_____, Amend, Commit, Postpone), the adoption of one motion limiting or extending debate in a certain way does not prevent another such conflicting motion from being in order.

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely — “Unlike the case of main motions and lower-ranking subsidiary motions (Postpone Indefinitely, Amend, Commit, Postpone), the adoption of one motion limiting or extending debate in a certain way does not prevent another such conflicting motion from being in order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:17 — “Unlike the case of main motions and lower-ranking subsidiary motions (Postpone Indefinitely, Amend, Commit, Postpone), the adoption of one motion limiting or extending debate in a certain way does not prevent another such conflicting motion from being in order.”

**749.** `[C0459 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Unlike the case of main motions and lower-ranking subsidiary motions (_____, Amend, Commit, Postpone), the adoption of one motion limiting or extending debate in a certain way does not prevent another such conflicting motion from being in order.
   A. mass meeting,
   B. Objection to
   C. Postpone Indefinitely
   D. adjourn

   *Answer:* C) Postpone Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:17 — “Unlike the case of main motions and lower-ranking subsidiary motions (Postpone Indefinitely, Amend, Commit, Postpone), the adoption of one motion limiting or extending debate in a certain way does not prevent another such conflicting motion from being in order.”

**750.** `[C0459 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Unlike the case of main motions and lower-ranking subsidiary motions (Postpone Indefinitely, _____, Commit, Postpone), the adoption of one motion limiting or extending debate in a certain way does not prevent another such conflicting motion from being in order.

   *Answer:* Amend — “Unlike the case of main motions and lower-ranking subsidiary motions (Postpone Indefinitely, Amend, Commit, Postpone), the adoption of one motion limiting or extending debate in a certain way does not prevent another such conflicting motion from being in order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:17 — “Unlike the case of main motions and lower-ranking subsidiary motions (Postpone Indefinitely, Amend, Commit, Postpone), the adoption of one motion limiting or extending debate in a certain way does not prevent another such conflicting motion from being in order.”

**751.** `[C0459 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Unlike the case of main motions and lower-ranking subsidiary motions (Postpone Indefinitely, _____, Commit, Postpone), the adoption of one motion limiting or extending debate in a certain way does not prevent another such conflicting motion from being in order.
   A. Amend
   B. decision
   C. come over from the previous session
   D. Previously Adopted

   *Answer:* A) Amend. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:17 — “Unlike the case of main motions and lower-ranking subsidiary motions (Postpone Indefinitely, Amend, Commit, Postpone), the adoption of one motion limiting or extending debate in a certain way does not prevent another such conflicting motion from being in order.”

**752.** `[C0459 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Unlike the case of main motions and lower-ranking subsidiary motions (Postpone Indefinitely, Amend, _____, Postpone), the adoption of one motion limiting or extending debate in a certain way does not prevent another such conflicting motion from being in order.

   *Answer:* Commit — “Unlike the case of main motions and lower-ranking subsidiary motions (Postpone Indefinitely, Amend, Commit, Postpone), the adoption of one motion limiting or extending debate in a certain way does not prevent another such conflicting motion from being in order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:17 — “Unlike the case of main motions and lower-ranking subsidiary motions (Postpone Indefinitely, Amend, Commit, Postpone), the adoption of one motion limiting or extending debate in a certain way does not prevent another such conflicting motion from being in order.”

**753.** `[C0460 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An order limiting or extending limits of debate is _____ (that is, no longer applies): (1) when all of the questions on which it was imposed have been voted on;

   *Answer:* exhausted — “An order limiting or extending limits of debate is exhausted (that is, no longer applies): (1) when all of the questions on which it was imposed have been voted on;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:18 — “An order limiting or extending limits of debate is exhausted (that is, no longer applies): (1) when all of the questions on which it was imposed have been voted on;”

**754.** `[C0460 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An order limiting or extending limits of debate is _____ (that is, no longer applies): (1) when all of the questions on which it was imposed have been voted on;
   A. parliamentary authority
   B. Form
   C. constitution,
   D. exhausted

   *Answer:* D) exhausted. See RONR (12th ed.) §15:18 — “An order limiting or extending limits of debate is exhausted (that is, no longer applies): (1) when all of the questions on which it was imposed have been voted on;”

**755.** `[C0462 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ is the motion used to bring the assembly to an immediate vote on one or more pending questions;

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “The Previous Question is the motion used to bring the assembly to an immediate vote on one or more pending questions;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:1 — “The Previous Question is the motion used to bring the assembly to an immediate vote on one or more pending questions;”

**756.** `[C0462 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ is the motion used to bring the assembly to an immediate vote on one or more pending questions;
   A. informal consideration,
   B. Previous Question
   C. reception
   D. will be

   *Answer:* B) Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:1 — “The Previous Question is the motion used to bring the assembly to an immediate vote on one or more pending questions;”

**757.** `[C0463 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The motion for the _____ has nothing to do with the last question previously considered by the assembly and has a long history of gradually changing purpose.

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “The motion for the Previous Question has nothing to do with the last question previously considered by the assembly and has a long history of gradually changing purpose.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:3 — “The motion for the Previous Question has nothing to do with the last question previously considered by the assembly and has a long history of gradually changing purpose.”

**758.** `[C0463 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The motion for the _____ has nothing to do with the last question previously considered by the assembly and has a long history of gradually changing purpose.
   A. Question of Privilege
   B. execution
   C. takes effect
   D. Previous Question

   *Answer:* D) Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:3 — “The motion for the Previous Question has nothing to do with the last question previously considered by the assembly and has a long history of gradually changing purpose.”

**759.** `[C0464 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Equal Application of Rules to Nonstandard Forms Such as “Call for the Question.” A motion such as “I call for [or “call”] the question,” “I demand the previous question,” “I move to close [or “end”] debate,” or “I move we vote now” is simply a motion for the _____ made in nonstandard form, and it is subject to all of the rules in this section.

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “Equal Application of Rules to Nonstandard Forms Such as “Call for the Question.” A motion such as “I call for [or “call”] the question,” “I demand the previous question,” “I move to close [or “end”] debate,” or “I move we vote now” is simply a motion for the Previous Question made in nonstandard form, and it is subject to all of the rules in this section.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:6 — “Equal Application of Rules to Nonstandard Forms Such as “Call for the Question.” A motion such as “I call for [or “call”] the question,” “I demand the previous question,” “I move to close [or “end”] debate,” or “I move we vote now” is simply a motion for the Previous Question made in nonstandard form, and it is subject to all of the rules in this section.”

**760.** `[C0464 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Equal Application of Rules to Nonstandard Forms Such as “Call for the Question.” A motion such as “I call for [or “call”] the question,” “I demand the previous question,” “I move to close [or “end”] debate,” or “I move we vote now” is simply a motion for the _____ made in nonstandard form, and it is subject to all of the rules in this section.
   A. Previous Question
   B. Debate,
   C. charge
   D. as thus amended,

   *Answer:* A) Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:6 — “Equal Application of Rules to Nonstandard Forms Such as “Call for the Question.” A motion such as “I call for [or “call”] the question,” “I demand the previous question,” “I move to close [or “end”] debate,” or “I move we vote now” is simply a motion for the Previous Question made in nonstandard form, and it is subject to all of the rules in this section.”

**761.** `[C0465 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Sometimes the mere making of a motion for the _____ or “call for the question” may motivate unanimous consent to ending debate.

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “Sometimes the mere making of a motion for the Previous Question or “call for the question” may motivate unanimous consent to ending debate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:7 — “Sometimes the mere making of a motion for the Previous Question or “call for the question” may motivate unanimous consent to ending debate.”

**762.** `[C0465 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Sometimes the mere making of a motion for the _____ or “call for the question” may motivate unanimous consent to ending debate.
   A. Executive Committee
   B. Carrying Over Unfinished Business
   C. aye
   D. Previous Question

   *Answer:* D) Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:7 — “Sometimes the mere making of a motion for the Previous Question or “call for the question” may motivate unanimous consent to ending debate.”

**763.** `[C0465 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or, if it has already been seconded, he must immediately take a vote on _____ the Previous Question.

   *Answer:* whether to order — “or, if it has already been seconded, he must immediately take a vote on whether to order the Previous Question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:7 — “or, if it has already been seconded, he must immediately take a vote on whether to order the Previous Question.”

**764.** `[C0465 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: or, if it has already been seconded, he must immediately take a vote on _____ the Previous Question.
   A. whether to order
   B. Postpone to a Certain
   C. basic right of the individual member
   D. secret

   *Answer:* A) whether to order. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:7 — “or, if it has already been seconded, he must immediately take a vote on whether to order the Previous Question.”

**765.** `[C0465 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Sometimes the mere making of a motion for the Previous _____ or “call for the question” may motivate unanimous consent to ending debate.

   *Answer:* Question — “Sometimes the mere making of a motion for the Previous Question or “call for the question” may motivate unanimous consent to ending debate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:7 — “Sometimes the mere making of a motion for the Previous Question or “call for the question” may motivate unanimous consent to ending debate.”

**770.** `[C0467 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Voting on a Series of Motions Under the _____ Question;

   *Answer:* Previous — “Voting on a Series of Motions Under the Previous Question;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:9 — “Voting on a Series of Motions Under the Previous Question;”

**771.** `[C0467 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Voting on a Series of Motions Under the _____ Question;
   A. consent
   B. Previous
   C. before
   D. president-elect

   *Answer:* B) Previous. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:9 — “Voting on a Series of Motions Under the Previous Question;”

**772.** `[C0467 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the series includes motions to Postpone Definitely, to Commit, or to _____ and one of these motions is adopted, further voting stops—regardless of how many of the remaining questions were, or were not, included under the order for the Previous Question.

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely — “If the series includes motions to Postpone Definitely, to Commit, or to Postpone Indefinitely and one of these motions is adopted, further voting stops—regardless of how many of the remaining questions were, or were not, included under the order for the Previous Question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:9 — “If the series includes motions to Postpone Definitely, to Commit, or to Postpone Indefinitely and one of these motions is adopted, further voting stops—regardless of how many of the remaining questions were, or were not, included under the order for the Previous Question.”

**773.** `[C0467 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the series includes motions to Postpone Definitely, to Commit, or to _____ and one of these motions is adopted, further voting stops—regardless of how many of the remaining questions were, or were not, included under the order for the Previous Question.
   A. Postpone Indefinitely
   B. speak in debate
   C. temporarily, but not
   D. what the accused is alleged to have

   *Answer:* A) Postpone Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:9 — “If the series includes motions to Postpone Definitely, to Commit, or to Postpone Indefinitely and one of these motions is adopted, further voting stops—regardless of how many of the remaining questions were, or were not, included under the order for the Previous Question.”

**774.** `[C0467 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Voting on a Series of Motions Under the Previous _____;

   *Answer:* Question — “Voting on a Series of Motions Under the Previous Question;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:9 — “Voting on a Series of Motions Under the Previous Question;”

**775.** `[C0468 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a question or series of questions (including motions on which the Previous Question has been ordered) ceases to be _____ before all of the motions affected by the order have been voted on, execution of the order is said to be interrupted.

   *Answer:* the pending business — “If a question or series of questions (including motions on which the Previous Question has been ordered) ceases to be the pending business before all of the motions affected by the order have been voted on, execution of the order is said to be interrupted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:10 — “If a question or series of questions (including motions on which the Previous Question has been ordered) ceases to be the pending business before all of the motions affected by the order have been voted on, execution of the order is said to be interrupted.”

**776.** `[C0468 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a question or series of questions (including motions on which the Previous Question has been ordered) ceases to be _____ before all of the motions affected by the order have been voted on, execution of the order is said to be interrupted.
   A. requirement
   B. four
   C. program
   D. the pending business

   *Answer:* D) the pending business. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:10 — “If a question or series of questions (including motions on which the Previous Question has been ordered) ceases to be the pending business before all of the motions affected by the order have been voted on, execution of the order is said to be interrupted.”

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “If a question or series of questions (including motions on which the Previous Question has been ordered) ceases to be the pending business before all of the motions affected by the order have been voted on, execution of the order is said to be interrupted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:10 — “If a question or series of questions (including motions on which the Previous Question has been ordered) ceases to be the pending business before all of the motions affected by the order have been voted on, execution of the order is said to be interrupted.”

**792.** `[C0472 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In addition, the regular time limits for making a motion to _____ apply (see 37).

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “In addition, the regular time limits for making a motion to Reconsider apply (see 37).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:14 — “In addition, the regular time limits for making a motion to Reconsider apply (see 37).”

   *Answer:* C) Reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:14 — “In addition, the regular time limits for making a motion to Reconsider apply (see 37).”

**794.** `[C0472 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the execution of an order for the _____ was interrupted before any vote was taken under the order, and if the questions come up again during the same session, a motion to reconsider the order (if not made earlier) can be made only in the moment after the chair has announced these questions as the pending business and before any of them are voted on.

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “If the execution of an order for the Previous Question was interrupted before any vote was taken under the order, and if the questions come up again during the same session, a motion to reconsider the order (if not made earlier) can be made only in the moment after the chair has announced these questions as the pending business and before any of them are voted on.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:14 — “If the execution of an order for the Previous Question was interrupted before any vote was taken under the order, and if the questions come up again during the same session, a motion to reconsider the order (if not made earlier) can be made only in the moment after the chair has announced these questions as the pending business and before any of them are voted on.”

**795.** `[C0472 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the execution of an order for the _____ was interrupted before any vote was taken under the order, and if the questions come up again during the same session, a motion to reconsider the order (if not made earlier) can be made only in the moment after the chair has announced these questions as the pending business and before any of them are voted on.
   A. regular meeting
   B. revision
   C. Previous Question
   D. show of hands

   *Answer:* C) Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:14 — “If the execution of an order for the Previous Question was interrupted before any vote was taken under the order, and if the questions come up again during the same session, a motion to reconsider the order (if not made earlier) can be made only in the moment after the chair has announced these questions as the pending business and before any of them are voted on.”

See RONR (12th ed.) §16:15 — “It should be noted that if a motion or series of motions that is under an order for the Previous Question comes up after having been postponed, there can never be a reconsideration of the order.”

   A. event
   B. strike out
   C. Postpone

**800.** `[C0473 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The reason is that the motion to _____ can only have been made before the Previous Question was ordered, so that the order for the Previous Question will always have been partly executed by the vote that caused the postponement.

   *Answer:* Postpone — “The reason is that the motion to Postpone can only have been made before the Previous Question was ordered, so that the order for the Previous Question will always have been partly executed by the vote that caused the postponement.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:15 — “The reason is that the motion to Postpone can only have been made before the Previous Question was ordered, so that the order for the Previous Question will always have been partly executed by the vote that caused the postponement.”

**801.** `[C0474 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In practice, if a motion to reconsider an affirmative vote on the _____ prevails, the vote that adopted the motion to Reconsider is also presumed to have carried out the reconsideration and to have reversed the vote that is reconsidered.

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “In practice, if a motion to reconsider an affirmative vote on the Previous Question prevails, the vote that adopted the motion to Reconsider is also presumed to have carried out the reconsideration and to have reversed the vote that is reconsidered.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:16 — “In practice, if a motion to reconsider an affirmative vote on the Previous Question prevails, the vote that adopted the motion to Reconsider is also presumed to have carried out the reconsideration and to have reversed the vote that is reconsidered.”

   C. Previous Question

   *Answer:* C) Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §16:16 — “In practice, if a motion to reconsider an affirmative vote on the Previous Question prevails, the vote that adopted the motion to Reconsider is also presumed to have carried out the reconsideration and to have reversed the vote that is reconsidered.”

**819.** `[C0479 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In stating the question on this undebatable, unamendable motion, the chair does not pause or ask, “Are you ready for the question?” but _____ the question for a rising vote on the motion for the Previous Question immediately, as shown below.

   D. dilatory

   *Answer:* A) Lay on the Table. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:2 — “By adopting the motion to Lay on the Table, a majority has the power to halt consideration of a question immediately without debate.”

**852.** `[C0490 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Thus, if a resolution with a proposed amendment and a motion to _____ are pending and the resolution is laid on the table, all of these questions go to the table at the same time and, if taken from the table, all will return together.

   *Answer:* Commit — “Thus, if a resolution with a proposed amendment and a motion to Commit are pending and the resolution is laid on the table, all of these questions go to the table at the same time and, if taken from the table, all will return together.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:5 — “Thus, if a resolution with a proposed amendment and a motion to Commit are pending and the resolution is laid on the table, all of these questions go to the table at the same time and, if taken from the table, all will return together.”

**853.** `[C0490 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Thus, if a resolution with a proposed amendment and a motion to _____ are pending and the resolution is laid on the table, all of these questions go to the table at the same time and, if taken from the table, all will return together.

   B. adopt,
   C. Orders of the Day,
   D. Commit

   *Answer:* D) Commit. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:5 — “Thus, if a resolution with a proposed amendment and a motion to Commit are pending and the resolution is laid on the table, all of these questions go to the table at the same time and, if taken from the table, all will return together.”

   A. Lay on the Table

**862.** `[C0496 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (This rule is a consequence of the fact that the rejection of a motion to Lay on the Table or the taking of a question from the table means that the assembly wishes to consider the matter at that time.) Motions to Recess (20) or to _____ (21) that have been made and lost do not justify a new motion to lay the same question on the table, but the renewal might be justified after a vote on an important amendment or on a motion to Commit.

   *Answer:* Adjourn — “(This rule is a consequence of the fact that the rejection of a motion to Lay on the Table or the taking of a question from the table means that the assembly wishes to consider the matter at that time.) Motions to Recess (20) or to Adjourn (21) that have been made and lost do not justify a new motion to lay the same question on the table, but the renewal might be justified after a vote on an important amendment or on a motion to Commit.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:11 — “(This rule is a consequence of the fact that the rejection of a motion to Lay on the Table or the taking of a question from the table means that the assembly wishes to consider the matter at that time.) Motions to Recess (20) or to Adjourn (21) that have been made and lost do not justify a new motion to lay the same question on the table, but the renewal might be justified after a vote on an important amendment or on a motion to Commit.”

**863.** `[C0496 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (This rule is a consequence of the fact that the rejection of a motion to Lay on the Table or the taking of a question from the table means that the assembly wishes to consider the matter at that time.) Motions to Recess (20) or to _____ (21) that have been made and lost do not justify a new motion to lay the same question on the table, but the renewal might be justified after a vote on an important amendment or on a motion to Commit.
   A. Carrying Over Unfinished Business
   B. presiding
   C. Adjourn
   D. Time to Which to Adjourn

   *Answer:* C) Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:11 — “(This rule is a consequence of the fact that the rejection of a motion to Lay on the Table or the taking of a question from the table means that the assembly wishes to consider the matter at that time.) Motions to Recess (20) or to Adjourn (21) that have been made and lost do not justify a new motion to lay the same question on the table, but the renewal might be justified after a vote on an important amendment or on a motion to Commit.”

**864.** `[C0496 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (This rule is a consequence of the fact that the rejection of a motion to Lay on the Table or the taking of a question from the table means that the assembly wishes to consider the matter at that time.) Motions to _____ (20) or to Adjourn (21) that have been made and lost do not justify a new motion to lay the same question on the table, but the renewal might be justified after a vote on an important amendment or on a motion to Commit.

   *Answer:* Recess — “(This rule is a consequence of the fact that the rejection of a motion to Lay on the Table or the taking of a question from the table means that the assembly wishes to consider the matter at that time.) Motions to Recess (20) or to Adjourn (21) that have been made and lost do not justify a new motion to lay the same question on the table, but the renewal might be justified after a vote on an important amendment or on a motion to Commit.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:11 — “(This rule is a consequence of the fact that the rejection of a motion to Lay on the Table or the taking of a question from the table means that the assembly wishes to consider the matter at that time.) Motions to Recess (20) or to Adjourn (21) that have been made and lost do not justify a new motion to lay the same question on the table, but the renewal might be justified after a vote on an important amendment or on a motion to Commit.”

**865.** `[C0497 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If debate has been closed by ordering the _____ or by the expiration of the time to which debate was limited, then up until the moment of taking the last vote under the order, the questions still before the assembly can be laid on the table.

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “If debate has been closed by ordering the Previous Question or by the expiration of the time to which debate was limited, then up until the moment of taking the last vote under the order, the questions still before the assembly can be laid on the table.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:12 — “If debate has been closed by ordering the Previous Question or by the expiration of the time to which debate was limited, then up until the moment of taking the last vote under the order, the questions still before the assembly can be laid on the table.”

   C. Previous Question
   D. the Minutes

   *Answer:* C) Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §17:12 — “If debate has been closed by ordering the Previous Question or by the expiration of the time to which debate was limited, then up until the moment of taking the last vote under the order, the questions still before the assembly can be laid on the table.”

**894.** `[C0510 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____ is a privileged motion by which a member can require the assembly to conform to its agenda, program, or order of business, or to take up a general or special order that is due to come up at the time (14, 41), unless two thirds of those voting wish to do otherwise.

   *Answer:* Call for the Orders of the Day — “A Call for the Orders of the Day is a privileged motion by which a member can require the assembly to conform to its agenda, program, or order of business, or to take up a general or special order that is due to come up at the time (14, 41), unless two thirds of those voting wish to do otherwise.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:1 — “A Call for the Orders of the Day is a privileged motion by which a member can require the assembly to conform to its agenda, program, or order of business, or to take up a general or special order that is due to come up at the time (14, 41), unless two thirds of those voting wish to do otherwise.”

**895.** `[C0510 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A _____ is a privileged motion by which a member can require the assembly to conform to its agenda, program, or order of business, or to take up a general or special order that is due to come up at the time (14, 41), unless two thirds of those voting wish to do otherwise.
   A. Call for the Orders of the Day
   B. main
   C. For
   D. not pending

   *Answer:* A) Call for the Orders of the Day.    D. special order

   *Answer:* C) Call for the Orders of. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:5 — “Times When a Call for the Orders of the Day Is in Order.”

**898.** `[C0513 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Times When a Call for the Orders of _____ Is in Order.

   *Answer:* the Day — “Times When a Call for the Orders of the Day Is in Order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:5 — “Times When a Call for the Orders of the Day Is in Order.”

**899.** `[C0513 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Times When a Call for the Orders of _____ Is in Order.
   A. the Day
   B. rank
   C. promptly after the breach occurs
   D. Reconsider and Enter on

   *Answer:* A) the Day. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:5 — “Times When a Call for the Orders of the Day Is in Order.”

**900.** `[C0514 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In contrast to the privileged _____, an order of the day which such a call may bring before the assembly is itself invariably a main motion, and when it is announced and pending, it is debatable and amendable, and all of the other rules governing main motions apply to it.

   *Answer:* Call for the Orders of the Day — “In contrast to the privileged Call for the Orders of the Day, an order of the day which such a call may bring before the assembly is itself invariably a main motion, and when it is announced and pending, it is debatable and amendable, and all of the other rules governing main motions apply to it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:7 — “In contrast to the privileged Call for the Orders of the Day, an order of the day which such a call may bring before the assembly is itself invariably a main motion, and when it is announced and pending, it is debatable and amendable, and all of the other rules governing main motions apply to it.”

**901.** `[C0514 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In contrast to the privileged _____, an order of the day which such a call may bring before the assembly is itself invariably a main motion, and when it is announced and pending, it is debatable and amendable, and all of the other rules governing main motions apply to it.
   A. Call for the Orders of the Day
   B. or local law
   C. program
   D. yields

   *Answer:* A) Call for the Orders of the Day. See RONR (12th ed.) §18:7 — “In contrast to the privileged Call for the Orders of the Day, an order of the day which such a call may bring before the assembly is itself invariably a main motion, and when it is announced and pending, it is debatable and amendable, and all of the other rules governing main motions apply to it.”

**902.** `[C0519 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To _____ is a device that permits a request or main motion relating to the rights and privileges of the assembly or any of its members to be brought up for possible immediate consideration because of its urgency, while business is pending and the request or motion would otherwise not be in order.

   *Answer:* Raise a Question of Privilege — “To Raise a Question of Privilege is a device that permits a request or main motion relating to the rights and privileges of the assembly or any of its members to be brought up for possible immediate consideration because of its urgency, while business is pending and the request or motion would otherwise not be in order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:1 — “To Raise a Question of Privilege is a device that permits a request or main motion relating to the rights and privileges of the assembly or any of its members to be brought up for possible immediate consideration because of its urgency, while business is pending and the request or motion would otherwise not be in order.”

**903.** `[C0519 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To _____ is a device that permits a request or main motion relating to the rights and privileges of the assembly or any of its members to be brought up for possible immediate consideration because of its urgency, while business is pending and the request or motion would otherwise not be in order.
   A. Incidental
   B. even if it is
   C. after the floor was yielded
   D. Raise a Question of Privilege

   *Answer:* D) Raise a Question of Privilege. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:1 — “To Raise a Question of Privilege is a device that permits a request or main motion relating to the rights and privileges of the assembly or any of its members to be brought up for possible immediate consideration because of its urgency, while business is pending and the request or motion would otherwise not be in order.”

   *Answer:* the assembly or any of its members — “To Raise a Question of Privilege is a device that permits a request or main motion relating to the rights and privileges of the assembly or any of its members to be brought up for possible immediate consideration because of its urgency, while business is pending and the request or motion would otherwise not be in order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:1 — “To Raise a Question of Privilege is a device that permits a request or main motion relating to the rights and privileges of the assembly or any of its members to be brought up for possible immediate consideration because of its urgency, while business is pending and the request or motion would otherwise not be in order.”

**905.** `[C0519 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To Raise a Question of Privilege is a device that permits a request or main motion relating to the rights and privileges of _____ to be brought up for possible immediate consideration because of its urgency, while business is pending and the request or motion would otherwise not be in order.
   A. Lay
   B. precedence of motions
   C. the assembly or any of its members
   D. in the office of president

   *Answer:* C) the assembly or any of its members. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:1 — “To Raise a Question of Privilege is a device that permits a request or main motion relating to the rights and privileges of the assembly or any of its members to be brought up for possible immediate consideration because of its urgency, while business is pending and the request or motion would otherwise not be in order.”

**906.** `[C0521 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is important to understand the distinction between the device _____ and the question of privilege itself.

   *Answer:* Raise a Question of Privilege — “It is important to understand the distinction between the device Raise a Question of Privilege and the question of privilege itself.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:3 — “It is important to understand the distinction between the device Raise a Question of Privilege and the question of privilege itself.”

**907.** `[C0521 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is important to understand the distinction between the device _____ and the question of privilege itself.
   A. Withdraw or Modify a Motion
   B. Pattern of Formality
   C. minutes,
   D. Raise a Question of Privilege

   *Answer:* D) Raise a Question of Privilege. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:3 — “It is important to understand the distinction between the device Raise a Question of Privilege and the question of privilege itself.”

**908.** `[C0521 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a question of privilege is taken up after it has been raised and has been admitted by the chair, however, depending on the form in which it was introduced, it is handled as a _____ (32, 33), or it is treated as a main motion and is debatable and amendable and can have any subsidiary motion applied to it—regardless of whether it interrupted, or awaited the disposal of, the pending business.

   *Answer:* request — “When a question of privilege is taken up after it has been raised and has been admitted by the chair, however, depending on the form in which it was introduced, it is handled as a request (32, 33), or it is treated as a main motion and is debatable and amendable and can have any subsidiary motion applied to it—regardless of whether it interrupted, or awaited the disposal of, the pending business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:3 — “When a question of privilege is taken up after it has been raised and has been admitted by the chair, however, depending on the form in which it was introduced, it is handled as a request (32, 33), or it is treated as a main motion and is debatable and amendable and can have any subsidiary motion applied to it—regardless of whether it interrupted, or awaited the disposal of, the pending business.”

**909.** `[C0521 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a question of privilege is taken up after it has been raised and has been admitted by the chair, however, depending on the form in which it was introduced, it is handled as a _____ (32, 33), or it is treated as a main motion and is debatable and amendable and can have any subsidiary motion applied to it—regardless of whether it interrupted, or awaited the disposal of, the pending business.
   A. request
   B. recess
   C. secret
   D. and Example

   *Answer:* A) request. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:3 — “When a question of privilege is taken up after it has been raised and has been admitted by the chair, however, depending on the form in which it was introduced, it is handled as a request (32, 33), or it is treated as a main motion and is debatable and amendable and can have any subsidiary motion applied to it—regardless of whether it interrupted, or awaited the disposal of, the pending business.”

   *Answer:* raising — “The eight characteristics below apply only to the device of raising a question of privilege;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §19:5 — “The eight characteristics below apply only to the device of raising a question of privilege;”

**912.** `[C0535 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____ is a short intermission in the assembly’s proceedings, commonly of only a few minutes, which does not close the meeting and after which business will immediately be resumed at exactly the point where it was interrupted.

   *Answer:* recess — “A recess is a short intermission in the assembly’s proceedings, commonly of only a few minutes, which does not close the meeting and after which business will immediately be resumed at exactly the point where it was interrupted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:1 — “A recess is a short intermission in the assembly’s proceedings, commonly of only a few minutes, which does not close the meeting and after which business will immediately be resumed at exactly the point where it was interrupted.”

**913.** `[C0535 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A _____ is a short intermission in the assembly’s proceedings, commonly of only a few minutes, which does not close the meeting and after which business will immediately be resumed at exactly the point where it was interrupted.
   A. secondary
   B. bylaws
   C. less
   D. recess

   *Answer:* D) recess. See RONR (12th ed.) §20:1 — “A recess is a short intermission in the assembly’s proceedings, commonly of only a few minutes, which does not close the meeting and after which business will immediately be resumed at exactly the point where it was interrupted.”

See RONR (12th ed.) §20:2 — “The privileged motion to Recess (or to Take a Recess) is a motion that a recess begin immediately, made while another question is pending.”

**924.** `[C0542 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To _____ means to close the meeting (8).

   *Answer:* adjourn — “To adjourn means to close the meeting (8).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:1 — “To adjourn means to close the meeting (8).”

**925.** `[C0542 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To _____ means to close the meeting (8).
   A. assemblies
   B. within the control of the assembly
   C. adjourn
   D. stand at ease

   *Answer:* C) adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:1 — “To adjourn means to close the meeting (8).”

**926.** `[C0543 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The privileged motion to _____ (which is always moved in an unqualified form with no mention of a time either for adjourning or for meeting again) is a motion to close the meeting immediately, made under conditions where some other provision for another meeting exists (so that the adjournment will not have the effect of dissolving the assembly), and where no time for adjourning the present meeting has already been set.

   *Answer:* Adjourn — “The privileged motion to Adjourn (which is always moved in an unqualified form with no mention of a time either for adjourning or for meeting again) is a motion to close the meeting immediately, made under conditions where some other provision for another meeting exists (so that the adjournment will not have the effect of dissolving the assembly), and where no time for adjourning the present meeting has already been set.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:2 — “The privileged motion to Adjourn (which is always moved in an unqualified form with no mention of a time either for adjourning or for meeting again) is a motion to close the meeting immediately, made under conditions where some other provision for another meeting exists (so that the adjournment will not have the effect of dissolving the assembly), and where no time for adjourning the present meeting has already been set.”

**927.** `[C0543 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The privileged motion to _____ (which is always moved in an unqualified form with no mention of a time either for adjourning or for meeting again) is a motion to close the meeting immediately, made under conditions where some other provision for another meeting exists (so that the adjournment will not have the effect of dissolving the assembly), and where no time for adjourning the present meeting has already been set.
   A. Fix the Time to Which to
   B. Suspend
   C. does
   D. Adjourn

   *Answer:* D) Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:2 — “The privileged motion to Adjourn (which is always moved in an unqualified form with no mention of a time either for adjourning or for meeting again) is a motion to close the meeting immediately, made under conditions where some other provision for another meeting exists (so that the adjournment will not have the effect of dissolving the assembly), and where no time for adjourning the present meeting has already been set.”

**928.** `[C0543 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ motion to Adjourn (which is always moved in an unqualified form with no mention of a time either for adjourning or for meeting again) is a motion to close the meeting immediately, made under conditions where some other provision for another meeting exists (so that the adjournment will not have the effect of dissolving the assembly), and where no time for adjourning the present meeting has already been set.

   *Answer:* privileged — “The privileged motion to Adjourn (which is always moved in an unqualified form with no mention of a time either for adjourning or for meeting again) is a motion to close the meeting immediately, made under conditions where some other provision for another meeting exists (so that the adjournment will not have the effect of dissolving the assembly), and where no time for adjourning the present meeting has already been set.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:2 — “The privileged motion to Adjourn (which is always moved in an unqualified form with no mention of a time either for adjourning or for meeting again) is a motion to close the meeting immediately, made under conditions where some other provision for another meeting exists (so that the adjournment will not have the effect of dissolving the assembly), and where no time for adjourning the present meeting has already been set.”

**929.** `[C0543 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ motion to Adjourn (which is always moved in an unqualified form with no mention of a time either for adjourning or for meeting again) is a motion to close the meeting immediately, made under conditions where some other provision for another meeting exists (so that the adjournment will not have the effect of dissolving the assembly), and where no time for adjourning the present meeting has already been set.
   A. stand at ease
   B. Lay on
   C. In Brief
   D. privileged

   *Answer:* D) privileged. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:2 — “The privileged motion to Adjourn (which is always moved in an unqualified form with no mention of a time either for adjourning or for meeting again) is a motion to close the meeting immediately, made under conditions where some other provision for another meeting exists (so that the adjournment will not have the effect of dissolving the assembly), and where no time for adjourning the present meeting has already been set.”

**930.** `[C0543 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: And for the same reason, such a motion has the unique characteristic that, _____ made while no question is pending, it is not debatable or amendable and it remains subject to all of the rules governing the privileged motion to Adjourn (except those that relate to making the motion while business is pending;

   *Answer:* even if it is — “And for the same reason, such a motion has the unique characteristic that, even if it is made while no question is pending, it is not debatable or amendable and it remains subject to all of the rules governing the privileged motion to Adjourn (except those that relate to making the motion while business is pending;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:2 — “And for the same reason, such a motion has the unique characteristic that, even if it is made while no question is pending, it is not debatable or amendable and it remains subject to all of the rules governing the privileged motion to Adjourn (except those that relate to making the motion while business is pending;”

**931.** `[C0544 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In meetings of these organizations, such a motion to _____ is in order regardless of whether business is pending;

   *Answer:* Adjourn — “In meetings of these organizations, such a motion to Adjourn is in order regardless of whether business is pending;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:4 — “In meetings of these organizations, such a motion to Adjourn is in order regardless of whether business is pending;”

**932.** `[C0544 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In meetings of these organizations, such a motion to _____ is in order regardless of whether business is pending;
   A. aye.
   B. Adjourn
   C. Appeal
   D. after the floor was yielded

   *Answer:* B) Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:4 — “In meetings of these organizations, such a motion to Adjourn is in order regardless of whether business is pending;”

**933.** `[C0547 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a special committee has completed the business referred to it, however, it “rises” and reports, which is equivalent to the main motion to adjourn _____ (or without day).

   *Answer:* sine die — “When a special committee has completed the business referred to it, however, it “rises” and reports, which is equivalent to the main motion to adjourn sine die (or without day).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:9 — “When a special committee has completed the business referred to it, however, it “rises” and reports, which is equivalent to the main motion to adjourn sine die (or without day).”

**934.** `[C0547 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a special committee has completed the business referred to it, however, it “rises” and reports, which is equivalent to the main motion to adjourn _____ (or without day).
   A. strike out and insert
   B. sine die
   C. Take from the
   D. Point of Order

   *Answer:* B) sine die. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:9 — “When a special committee has completed the business referred to it, however, it “rises” and reports, which is equivalent to the main motion to adjourn sine die (or without day).”

   A. of Order
   B. after the floor
   C. Adjourn
   D. adopted

   *Answer:* C) Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:10 — “Parliamentary Steps That Are in Order While the Privileged Motion to Adjourn Is Pending, or After the Assembly Has Voted to Adjourn.”

**937.** `[C0549 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If any matters of the types listed above arise after it has been moved to adjourn, the chair should state the facts briefly, or a member who rises and addresses the chair for the purpose may do so—or make the necessary motion or give the desired notice—before the vote is taken on the motion to _____.

   *Answer:* Adjourn — “If any matters of the types listed above arise after it has been moved to adjourn, the chair should state the facts briefly, or a member who rises and addresses the chair for the purpose may do so—or make the necessary motion or give the desired notice—before the vote is taken on the motion to Adjourn.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:11 — “If any matters of the types listed above arise after it has been moved to adjourn, the chair should state the facts briefly, or a member who rises and addresses the chair for the purpose may do so—or make the necessary motion or give the desired notice—before the vote is taken on the motion to Adjourn.”

   A. Adjourn
   B. assembly of an organized society
   C. scribe
   D. Filling Blanks

**939.** `[C0551 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Since a motion to _____ may be voted down because a majority wish to hear one speech or take one vote, this motion must be renewable as soon as there has been any progress in business or even material progress in debate.

   *Answer:* Adjourn — “Since a motion to Adjourn may be voted down because a majority wish to hear one speech or take one vote, this motion must be renewable as soon as there has been any progress in business or even material progress in debate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:13 — “Since a motion to Adjourn may be voted down because a majority wish to hear one speech or take one vote, this motion must be renewable as soon as there has been any progress in business or even material progress in debate.”

**940.** `[C0551 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Since a motion to _____ may be voted down because a majority wish to hear one speech or take one vote, this motion must be renewable as soon as there has been any progress in business or even material progress in debate.
   A. done
   B. Adjourn
   C. amendment to the main question
   D. within the control of the

   *Answer:* B) Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:13 — “Since a motion to Adjourn may be voted down because a majority wish to hear one speech or take one vote, this motion must be renewable as soon as there has been any progress in business or even material progress in debate.”

**941.** `[C0551 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a member who has not properly obtained the floor calls out, “I move to adjourn,” such a call cannot be entertained as a motion except by unanimous consent (see _____, 39).

   *Answer:* Dilatory Motions — “If a member who has not properly obtained the floor calls out, “I move to adjourn,” such a call cannot be entertained as a motion except by unanimous consent (see Dilatory Motions, 39).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:13 — “If a member who has not properly obtained the floor calls out, “I move to adjourn,” such a call cannot be entertained as a motion except by unanimous consent (see Dilatory Motions, 39).”

**942.** `[C0551 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a member who has not properly obtained the floor calls out, “I move to adjourn,” such a call cannot be entertained as a motion except by unanimous consent (see _____, 39).
   A. ballot
   B. Dilatory Motions
   C. subsequently
   D. general

   *Answer:* B) Dilatory Motions. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:13 — “If a member who has not properly obtained the floor calls out, “I move to adjourn,” such a call cannot be entertained as a motion except by unanimous consent (see Dilatory Motions, 39).”

**943.** `[C0551 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Since a motion to _____ may be voted down because a majority wish to hear one speech or take one vote, this motion must be renewable as soon as there has been any progress in business or even material progress in debate.

   *Answer:* Adjourn — “Since a motion to Adjourn may be voted down because a majority wish to hear one speech or take one vote, this motion must be renewable as soon as there has been any progress in business or even material progress in debate.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:13 — “Since a motion to Adjourn may be voted down because a majority wish to hear one speech or take one vote, this motion must be renewable as soon as there has been any progress in business or even material progress in debate.”

**944.** `[C0554 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The following forms may be used for either a privileged or a _____ motion: “I move to adjourn,” or “I move that the meeting [“now”] adjourn.” Additional forms in order as a main motion are: “I move that the club now adjourn to meet at 8 P.M.

   *Answer:* main — “The following forms may be used for either a privileged or a main motion: “I move to adjourn,” or “I move that the meeting [“now”] adjourn.” Additional forms in order as a main motion are: “I move that the club now adjourn to meet at 8 P.M.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:16 — “The following forms may be used for either a privileged or a main motion: “I move to adjourn,” or “I move that the meeting [“now”] adjourn.” Additional forms in order as a main motion are: “I move that the club now adjourn to meet at 8 P.M.”

**945.** `[C0554 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The following forms may be used for either a privileged or a _____ motion: “I move to adjourn,” or “I move that the meeting [“now”] adjourn.” Additional forms in order as a main motion are: “I move that the club now adjourn to meet at 8 P.M.
   A. call
   B. main
   C. Call of the House
   D. amendment

   *Answer:* B) main. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:16 — “The following forms may be used for either a privileged or a main motion: “I move to adjourn,” or “I move that the meeting [“now”] adjourn.” Additional forms in order as a main motion are: “I move that the club now adjourn to meet at 8 P.M.”

**946.** `[C0554 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: on April 10,” or “I move that the convention adjourn _____ [or “adjourn without day”].”

   *Answer:* sine die — “on April 10,” or “I move that the convention adjourn sine die [or “adjourn without day”].””. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:16 — “on April 10,” or “I move that the convention adjourn sine die [or “adjourn without day”].””

**947.** `[C0554 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: on April 10,” or “I move that the convention adjourn _____ [or “adjourn without day”].”
   A. the Day
   B. sine die
   C. the convention
   D. germane

   *Answer:* B) sine die. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:16 — “on April 10,” or “I move that the convention adjourn sine die [or “adjourn without day”].””

**948.** `[C0556 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the motion is lost, the chair, after announcing the result, immediately restates the resolution that was pending when the motion to _____ was made.

   *Answer:* Adjourn — “If the motion is lost, the chair, after announcing the result, immediately restates the resolution that was pending when the motion to Adjourn was made.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:18 — “If the motion is lost, the chair, after announcing the result, immediately restates the resolution that was pending when the motion to Adjourn was made.”

**949.** `[C0556 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the motion is lost, the chair, after announcing the result, immediately restates the resolution that was pending when the motion to _____ was made.
   A. sustaining
   B. Adjourned Meeting
   C. hearing
   D. Adjourn

   *Answer:* D) Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §21:18 — “If the motion is lost, the chair, after announcing the result, immediately restates the resolution that was pending when the motion to Adjourn was made.”

**950.** `[C0558 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The object of the motion to _____ (also referred to as the motion to “fix the time for an adjourned meeting”) is to set the time, and sometimes the place, for another meeting to continue business of the session, with no effect on when the present meeting will adjourn.

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn — “The object of the motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn (also referred to as the motion to “fix the time for an adjourned meeting”) is to set the time, and sometimes the place, for another meeting to continue business of the session, with no effect on when the present meeting will adjourn.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:1 — “The object of the motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn (also referred to as the motion to “fix the time for an adjourned meeting”) is to set the time, and sometimes the place, for another meeting to continue business of the session, with no effect on when the present meeting will adjourn.”

**951.** `[C0558 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The object of the motion to _____ (also referred to as the motion to “fix the time for an adjourned meeting”) is to set the time, and sometimes the place, for another meeting to continue business of the session, with no effect on when the present meeting will adjourn.
   A. special rules of order
   B. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   C. regular
   D. in the brief interval

   *Answer:* B) Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:1 — “The object of the motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn (also referred to as the motion to “fix the time for an adjourned meeting”) is to set the time, and sometimes the place, for another meeting to continue business of the session, with no effect on when the present meeting will adjourn.”

**952.** `[C0559 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion to _____ is in order only if at the time it is offered there is no meeting scheduled for later within the same session.

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn — “A motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is in order only if at the time it is offered there is no meeting scheduled for later within the same session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:2 — “A motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is in order only if at the time it is offered there is no meeting scheduled for later within the same session.”

**953.** `[C0559 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A motion to _____ is in order only if at the time it is offered there is no meeting scheduled for later within the same session.
   A. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   B. financial secretary
   C. limiting
   D. Indefinitely

   *Answer:* A) Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:2 — “A motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn is in order only if at the time it is offered there is no meeting scheduled for later within the same session.”

**954.** `[C0559 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If there is such a meeting, additional meetings within the same session may be set by a motion either to _____ (25) or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), namely, the previously adopted agenda or program for the session.

   *Answer:* Suspend the Rules — “If there is such a meeting, additional meetings within the same session may be set by a motion either to Suspend the Rules (25) or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), namely, the previously adopted agenda or program for the session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:2 — “If there is such a meeting, additional meetings within the same session may be set by a motion either to Suspend the Rules (25) or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), namely, the previously adopted agenda or program for the session.”

**955.** `[C0559 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If there is such a meeting, additional meetings within the same session may be set by a motion either to _____ (25) or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), namely, the previously adopted agenda or program for the session.
   A. Methods of Voting
   B. question is pending
   C. Suspend the Rules
   D. adoption

   *Answer:* C) Suspend the Rules. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:2 — “If there is such a meeting, additional meetings within the same session may be set by a motion either to Suspend the Rules (25) or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), namely, the previously adopted agenda or program for the session.”

**956.** `[C0559 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If there is such a meeting, additional meetings within the same session may be set by a motion either to Suspend the Rules (25) or to Amend Something _____ (35), namely, the previously adopted agenda or program for the session.

   *Answer:* Previously Adopted — “If there is such a meeting, additional meetings within the same session may be set by a motion either to Suspend the Rules (25) or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), namely, the previously adopted agenda or program for the session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:2 — “If there is such a meeting, additional meetings within the same session may be set by a motion either to Suspend the Rules (25) or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), namely, the previously adopted agenda or program for the session.”

**957.** `[C0561 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The privileged motion to _____ can then be used.

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn — “The privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn can then be used.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:4 — “The privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn can then be used.”

**958.** `[C0561 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The privileged motion to _____ can then be used.
   A. over
   B. Motions Relating to Methods of Voting,
   C. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn
   D. Descriptive Characteristics

   *Answer:* C) Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:4 — “The privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn can then be used.”

**959.** `[C0562 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The following eight characteristics apply only to the privileged motion to _____.

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn — “The following eight characteristics apply only to the privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:5 — “The following eight characteristics apply only to the privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn.”

**960.** `[C0562 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The following eight characteristics apply only to the privileged motion to _____.
   A. constitution,
   B. a Question Taken from the Table
   C. Certificate of Incorporation
   D. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn

   *Answer:* D) Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:5 — “The following eight characteristics apply only to the privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn.”

**961.** `[C0562 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The following eight characteristics apply only to the _____ motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn.

   *Answer:* privileged — “The following eight characteristics apply only to the privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:5 — “The following eight characteristics apply only to the privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn.”

**962.** `[C0562 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The following eight characteristics apply only to the _____ motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn.
   A. preferential voting
   B. Robert’s Rules
   C. abstain
   D. privileged

   *Answer:* D) privileged. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:5 — “The following eight characteristics apply only to the privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn.”

**963.** `[C0565 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Whether introduced as a privileged or a main motion, the effect of this motion is to establish an _____—that is, another meeting that will be a continuation of the session at which the motion is adopted.

   *Answer:* adjourned meeting — “Whether introduced as a privileged or a main motion, the effect of this motion is to establish an adjourned meeting—that is, another meeting that will be a continuation of the session at which the motion is adopted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:9 — “Whether introduced as a privileged or a main motion, the effect of this motion is to establish an adjourned meeting—that is, another meeting that will be a continuation of the session at which the motion is adopted.”

**964.** `[C0565 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Whether introduced as a privileged or a main motion, the effect of this motion is to establish an _____—that is, another meeting that will be a continuation of the session at which the motion is adopted.
   A. adjourned meeting
   B. informal consideration,
   C. Take from the Table
   D. society

   *Answer:* A) adjourned meeting. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:9 — “Whether introduced as a privileged or a main motion, the effect of this motion is to establish an adjourned meeting—that is, another meeting that will be a continuation of the session at which the motion is adopted.”

**965.** `[C0565 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Unlike a _____, an adjourned meeting does not require notice, although it is desirable to give such notice if feasible.

   *Answer:* special meeting — “Unlike a special meeting, an adjourned meeting does not require notice, although it is desirable to give such notice if feasible.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:9 — “Unlike a special meeting, an adjourned meeting does not require notice, although it is desirable to give such notice if feasible.”

**966.** `[C0565 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Unlike a _____, an adjourned meeting does not require notice, although it is desirable to give such notice if feasible.
   A. ceases to be
   B. special meeting
   C. Commit
   D. Main Motions,

   *Answer:* B) special meeting. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:9 — “Unlike a special meeting, an adjourned meeting does not require notice, although it is desirable to give such notice if feasible.”

**967.** `[C0566 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Because of the nature of the situations that give rise to use of the privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn, adoption of this motion is often followed by immediate introduction of a motion to _____, or of the privileged motion to Adjourn, depending on the purpose, as shown in the examples below.

   *Answer:* Postpone — “Because of the nature of the situations that give rise to use of the privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn, adoption of this motion is often followed by immediate introduction of a motion to Postpone, or of the privileged motion to Adjourn, depending on the purpose, as shown in the examples below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:10 — “Because of the nature of the situations that give rise to use of the privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn, adoption of this motion is often followed by immediate introduction of a motion to Postpone, or of the privileged motion to Adjourn, depending on the purpose, as shown in the examples below.”

**968.** `[C0566 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Because of the nature of the situations that give rise to use of the privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn, adoption of this motion is often followed by immediate introduction of a motion to _____, or of the privileged motion to Adjourn, depending on the purpose, as shown in the examples below.
   A. Postpone
   B. Motions Relating to Voting
   C. Call of the House,
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* A) Postpone. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:10 — “Because of the nature of the situations that give rise to use of the privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn, adoption of this motion is often followed by immediate introduction of a motion to Postpone, or of the privileged motion to Adjourn, depending on the purpose, as shown in the examples below.”

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn — “Because of the nature of the situations that give rise to use of the privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn, adoption of this motion is often followed by immediate introduction of a motion to Postpone, or of the privileged motion to Adjourn, depending on the purpose, as shown in the examples below.”. **970.** `[C0566 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Because of the nature of the situations that give rise to use of the privileged motion to _____, adoption of this motion is often followed by immediate introduction of a motion to Postpone, or of the privileged motion to Adjourn, depending on the purpose, as shown in the examples below.

   *Answer:* Adjourn — “Because of the nature of the situations that give rise to use of the privileged motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn, adoption of this motion is often followed by immediate introduction of a motion to Postpone, or of the privileged motion to Adjourn, depending on the purpose, as shown in the examples below.”. **972.** `[C0571 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair then puts to vote the motion to _____ Adjourn.

   *Answer:* Fix the Time to Which to — “The chair then puts to vote the motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:15 — “The chair then puts to vote the motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn.”

**973.** `[C0571 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair then puts to vote the motion to _____ Adjourn.

   *Answer:* Adjourn — “The chair then puts to vote the motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §22:15 — “The chair then puts to vote the motion to Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn.”

**975.** `[C0571 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair then puts to vote the motion to Fix the Time to Which to _____.

   D. recording secretary
