**110.** `[C0638 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For example, suppose that the following resolution is pending: “Resolved, That the Society congratulate its member Ernest Dunn on his novel _____, and that three copies be purchased for the Society’s library.” Suppose also that a member wishes to divide the question so as to consider the purchase of the books separately.

   *Answer:* Crestwood — “For example, suppose that the following resolution is pending: “Resolved, That the Society congratulate its member Ernest Dunn on his novel Crestwood, and that three copies be purchased for the Society’s library.” Suppose also that a member wishes to divide the question so as to consider the purchase of the books separately.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:7 — “For example, suppose that the following resolution is pending: “Resolved, That the Society congratulate its member Ernest Dunn on his novel Crestwood, and that three copies be purchased for the Society’s library.” Suppose also that a member wishes to divide the question so as to consider the purchase of the books separately.”

**111.** `[C0640 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a question is indivisible and a member is opposed to a portion of it, he can seek the desired result by moving to _____ (12) the part to which he is opposed.

   *Answer:* strike out — “When a question is indivisible and a member is opposed to a portion of it, he can seek the desired result by moving to strike out (12) the part to which he is opposed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:9 — “When a question is indivisible and a member is opposed to a portion of it, he can seek the desired result by moving to strike out (12) the part to which he is opposed.”

**112.** `[C0640 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a question is indivisible and a member is opposed to a portion of it, he can seek the desired result by moving to _____ (12) the part to which he is opposed.
   A. ballot
   B. Point
   C. strike out
   D. no more than a quarterly time interval

   *Answer:* C) strike out. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:9 — “When a question is indivisible and a member is opposed to a portion of it, he can seek the desired result by moving to strike out (12) the part to which he is opposed.”

**113.** `[C0641 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In such a case, one or more of the several resolutions must receive separate consideration and vote at the request of a single member, and the motion for _____ is not used.

   *Answer:* Division of a Question — “In such a case, one or more of the several resolutions must receive separate consideration and vote at the request of a single member, and the motion for Division of a Question is not used.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:10 — “In such a case, one or more of the several resolutions must receive separate consideration and vote at the request of a single member, and the motion for Division of a Question is not used.”

**114.** `[C0641 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In such a case, one or more of the several resolutions must receive separate consideration and vote at the request of a single member, and the motion for _____ is not used.
   A. Division of a Question
   B. add
   C. Limit or Extend Limits of
   D. Until the chair states the question

   *Answer:* A) Division of a Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §27:10 — “In such a case, one or more of the several resolutions must receive separate consideration and vote at the request of a single member, and the motion for Division of a Question is not used.”

**115.** `[C0645 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the chair does not follow such a course of his own accord and the assembly wishes to do so, the procedure can be ordered by adopting a motion to _____ (or to Consider Seriatim).

   *Answer:* Consider by Paragraph — “If the chair does not follow such a course of his own accord and the assembly wishes to do so, the procedure can be ordered by adopting a motion to Consider by Paragraph (or to Consider Seriatim).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:1 — “If the chair does not follow such a course of his own accord and the assembly wishes to do so, the procedure can be ordered by adopting a motion to Consider by Paragraph (or to Consider Seriatim).”

**116.** `[C0645 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the chair does not follow such a course of his own accord and the assembly wishes to do so, the procedure can be ordered by adopting a motion to _____ (or to Consider Seriatim).
   A. Particular Types of Committee Reports
   B. Consider by Paragraph
   C. Recess
   D. Raise a Question of

   *Answer:* B) Consider by Paragraph. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:1 — “If the chair does not follow such a course of his own accord and the assembly wishes to do so, the procedure can be ordered by adopting a motion to Consider by Paragraph (or to Consider Seriatim).”

**117.** `[C0645 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the chair does not follow such a course of his own accord and the assembly wishes to do so, the procedure can be ordered by adopting a motion to Consider by Paragraph (or to _____).

   *Answer:* Consider Seriatim — “If the chair does not follow such a course of his own accord and the assembly wishes to do so, the procedure can be ordered by adopting a motion to Consider by Paragraph (or to Consider Seriatim).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:1 — “If the chair does not follow such a course of his own accord and the assembly wishes to do so, the procedure can be ordered by adopting a motion to Consider by Paragraph (or to Consider Seriatim).”

**118.** `[C0645 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the chair does not follow such a course of his own accord and the assembly wishes to do so, the procedure can be ordered by adopting a motion to Consider by Paragraph (or to _____).
   A. scribe
   B. characteristics
   C. as suggested by
   D. Consider Seriatim

   *Answer:* D) Consider Seriatim. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:1 — “If the chair does not follow such a course of his own accord and the assembly wishes to do so, the procedure can be ordered by adopting a motion to Consider by Paragraph (or to Consider Seriatim).”

**119.** `[C0645 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Several distinct main motions on _____ cannot be considered seriatim if a single member objects.

   *Answer:* different subjects — “Several distinct main motions on different subjects cannot be considered seriatim if a single member objects.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:1 — “Several distinct main motions on different subjects cannot be considered seriatim if a single member objects.”

**120.** `[C0648 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the chair suggests consideration by paragraph and a member feels that time could be saved by acting on it as a whole, the member can move “that it be considered as a whole.” This motion is governed by standard descriptive characteristics identical to those for _____.

   *Answer:* Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim — “If the chair suggests consideration by paragraph and a member feels that time could be saved by acting on it as a whole, the member can move “that it be considered as a whole.” This motion is governed by standard descriptive characteristics identical to those for Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:5 — “If the chair suggests consideration by paragraph and a member feels that time could be saved by acting on it as a whole, the member can move “that it be considered as a whole.” This motion is governed by standard descriptive characteristics identical to those for Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim.”

**121.** `[C0648 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the chair suggests consideration by paragraph and a member feels that time could be saved by acting on it as a whole, the member can move “that it be considered as a whole.” This motion is governed by standard descriptive characteristics identical to those for _____.
   A. Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim
   B. dilatory
   C. Division of
   D. executive committee

   *Answer:* A) Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:5 — “If the chair suggests consideration by paragraph and a member feels that time could be saved by acting on it as a whole, the member can move “that it be considered as a whole.” This motion is governed by standard descriptive characteristics identical to those for Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim.”

**122.** `[C0650 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the _____ is ordered before the preamble has been considered, it does not apply to the preamble unless expressly so stated.

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “If the Previous Question is ordered before the preamble has been considered, it does not apply to the preamble unless expressly so stated.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:7 — “If the Previous Question is ordered before the preamble has been considered, it does not apply to the preamble unless expressly so stated.”

**123.** `[C0650 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the _____ is ordered before the preamble has been considered, it does not apply to the preamble unless expressly so stated.
   A. the pending business
   B. primary
   C. Previous Question
   D. Debate,

   *Answer:* C) Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:7 — “If the Previous Question is ordered before the preamble has been considered, it does not apply to the preamble unless expressly so stated.”

**124.** `[C0651 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: During the consideration of the separate paragraphs, any motion to Postpone Indefinitely, _____, Postpone, or Lay on the Table can apply only to the entire series or proposition.

   *Answer:* Commit — “During the consideration of the separate paragraphs, any motion to Postpone Indefinitely, Commit, Postpone, or Lay on the Table can apply only to the entire series or proposition.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:8 — “During the consideration of the separate paragraphs, any motion to Postpone Indefinitely, Commit, Postpone, or Lay on the Table can apply only to the entire series or proposition.”

**125.** `[C0651 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: During the consideration of the separate paragraphs, any motion to Postpone Indefinitely, _____, Postpone, or Lay on the Table can apply only to the entire series or proposition.
   A. voice
   B. Question
   C. unique
   D. Commit

   *Answer:* D) Commit. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:8 — “During the consideration of the separate paragraphs, any motion to Postpone Indefinitely, Commit, Postpone, or Lay on the Table can apply only to the entire series or proposition.”

**126.** `[C0651 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: During the consideration of the separate paragraphs, any motion to Postpone Indefinitely, Commit, Postpone, or _____ the Table can apply only to the entire series or proposition.

   *Answer:* Lay on — “During the consideration of the separate paragraphs, any motion to Postpone Indefinitely, Commit, Postpone, or Lay on the Table can apply only to the entire series or proposition.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:8 — “During the consideration of the separate paragraphs, any motion to Postpone Indefinitely, Commit, Postpone, or Lay on the Table can apply only to the entire series or proposition.”

**127.** `[C0651 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: During the consideration of the separate paragraphs, any motion to Postpone Indefinitely, Commit, Postpone, or _____ the Table can apply only to the entire series or proposition.
   A. requirement
   B. Lay on
   C. consent
   D. motions

   *Answer:* B) Lay on. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:8 — “During the consideration of the separate paragraphs, any motion to Postpone Indefinitely, Commit, Postpone, or Lay on the Table can apply only to the entire series or proposition.”

**128.** `[C0651 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: During the consideration of the separate paragraphs, any motion to _____, Commit, Postpone, or Lay on the Table can apply only to the entire series or proposition.

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely — “During the consideration of the separate paragraphs, any motion to Postpone Indefinitely, Commit, Postpone, or Lay on the Table can apply only to the entire series or proposition.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §28:8 — “During the consideration of the separate paragraphs, any motion to Postpone Indefinitely, Commit, Postpone, or Lay on the Table can apply only to the entire series or proposition.”

**129.** `[C0654 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Whenever a member doubts the result of a voice (vivavoce) vote or a vote by show of hands—either because the result appears close or because he doubts that a representative number of the members present have voted— he can call for a _____, thereby requiring the vote to be taken again by rising.

   *Answer:* Division of the Assembly — “Whenever a member doubts the result of a voice (vivavoce) vote or a vote by show of hands—either because the result appears close or because he doubts that a representative number of the members present have voted— he can call for a Division of the Assembly, thereby requiring the vote to be taken again by rising.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:1 — “Whenever a member doubts the result of a voice (vivavoce) vote or a vote by show of hands—either because the result appears close or because he doubts that a representative number of the members present have voted— he can call for a Division of the Assembly, thereby requiring the vote to be taken again by rising.”

**130.** `[C0654 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Whenever a member doubts the result of a voice (vivavoce) vote or a vote by show of hands—either because the result appears close or because he doubts that a representative number of the members present have voted— he can call for a _____, thereby requiring the vote to be taken again by rising.
   A. order of the day
   B. Division of the Assembly
   C. commend
   D. Form and Example

   *Answer:* B) Division of the Assembly. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:1 — “Whenever a member doubts the result of a voice (vivavoce) vote or a vote by show of hands—either because the result appears close or because he doubts that a representative number of the members present have voted— he can call for a Division of the Assembly, thereby requiring the vote to be taken again by rising.”

**131.** `[C0655 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A voice vote retaken by a show of hands is not a Division of _____, since in large assemblies it may be less accurate than a rising vote, and since—even in a small meeting—the rising vote may be more effective in causing a maximum number of members to vote.

   *Answer:* the Assembly — “A voice vote retaken by a show of hands is not a Division of the Assembly, since in large assemblies it may be less accurate than a rising vote, and since—even in a small meeting—the rising vote may be more effective in causing a maximum number of members to vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:2 — “A voice vote retaken by a show of hands is not a Division of the Assembly, since in large assemblies it may be less accurate than a rising vote, and since—even in a small meeting—the rising vote may be more effective in causing a maximum number of members to vote.”

**132.** `[C0655 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A voice vote retaken by a show of hands is not a Division of _____, since in large assemblies it may be less accurate than a rising vote, and since—even in a small meeting—the rising vote may be more effective in causing a maximum number of members to vote.
   A. the Assembly
   B. Postpone to a Certain
   C. Consider Seriatim
   D. refrain

   *Answer:* A) the Assembly. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:2 — “A voice vote retaken by a show of hands is not a Division of the Assembly, since in large assemblies it may be less accurate than a rising vote, and since—even in a small meeting—the rising vote may be more effective in causing a maximum number of members to vote.”

**133.** `[C0655 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A voice vote retaken by a show of hands is not a _____ the Assembly, since in large assemblies it may be less accurate than a rising vote, and since—even in a small meeting—the rising vote may be more effective in causing a maximum number of members to vote.

   *Answer:* Division of — “A voice vote retaken by a show of hands is not a Division of the Assembly, since in large assemblies it may be less accurate than a rising vote, and since—even in a small meeting—the rising vote may be more effective in causing a maximum number of members to vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:2 — “A voice vote retaken by a show of hands is not a Division of the Assembly, since in large assemblies it may be less accurate than a rising vote, and since—even in a small meeting—the rising vote may be more effective in causing a maximum number of members to vote.”

**134.** `[C0655 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A voice vote retaken by a show of hands is not a _____ the Assembly, since in large assemblies it may be less accurate than a rising vote, and since—even in a small meeting—the rising vote may be more effective in causing a maximum number of members to vote.
   A. Division of
   B. secondary amendment by inserting
   C. Raise a
   D. constitution and bylaws

   *Answer:* A) Division of. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:2 — “A voice vote retaken by a show of hands is not a Division of the Assembly, since in large assemblies it may be less accurate than a rising vote, and since—even in a small meeting—the rising vote may be more effective in causing a maximum number of members to vote.”

**135.** `[C0656 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: On an inconclusive voice vote in a very small meeting where all present can clearly see one another, if, instead of calling for a _____, a member asks for a show of hands, this is in the nature of a request, and the chair can retake the vote by this method unless a call for a Division is also made.

   *Answer:* Division — “On an inconclusive voice vote in a very small meeting where all present can clearly see one another, if, instead of calling for a Division, a member asks for a show of hands, this is in the nature of a request, and the chair can retake the vote by this method unless a call for a Division is also made.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:3 — “On an inconclusive voice vote in a very small meeting where all present can clearly see one another, if, instead of calling for a Division, a member asks for a show of hands, this is in the nature of a request, and the chair can retake the vote by this method unless a call for a Division is also made.”

**136.** `[C0656 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: On an inconclusive voice vote in a very small meeting where all present can clearly see one another, if, instead of calling for a _____, a member asks for a show of hands, this is in the nature of a request, and the chair can retake the vote by this method unless a call for a Division is also made.
   A. Division
   B. Fix the Time to
   C. ballot
   D. refrain

   *Answer:* A) Division. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:3 — “On an inconclusive voice vote in a very small meeting where all present can clearly see one another, if, instead of calling for a Division, a member asks for a show of hands, this is in the nature of a request, and the chair can retake the vote by this method unless a call for a Division is also made.”

**137.** `[C0656 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: On an inconclusive voice vote in a very small meeting where all present can clearly see one another, if, instead of calling for a _____, a member asks for a show of hands, this is in the nature of a request, and the chair can retake the vote by this method unless a call for a Division is also made.

   *Answer:* Division — “On an inconclusive voice vote in a very small meeting where all present can clearly see one another, if, instead of calling for a Division, a member asks for a show of hands, this is in the nature of a request, and the chair can retake the vote by this method unless a call for a Division is also made.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:3 — “On an inconclusive voice vote in a very small meeting where all present can clearly see one another, if, instead of calling for a Division, a member asks for a show of hands, this is in the nature of a request, and the chair can retake the vote by this method unless a call for a Division is also made.”

**138.** `[C0656 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: On an inconclusive voice vote in a very small meeting where all present can clearly see one another, if, instead of calling for a _____, a member asks for a show of hands, this is in the nature of a request, and the chair can retake the vote by this method unless a call for a Division is also made.
   A. Division
   B. of Order Newly Revised
   C. receive
   D. debatable

   *Answer:* A) Division. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:3 — “On an inconclusive voice vote in a very small meeting where all present can clearly see one another, if, instead of calling for a Division, a member asks for a show of hands, this is in the nature of a request, and the chair can retake the vote by this method unless a call for a Division is also made.”

**139.** `[C0656 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: On an inconclusive voice vote in a very small meeting where all present can clearly see one another, if, instead of calling for a _____, a member asks for a show of hands, this is in the nature of a request, and the chair can retake the vote by this method unless a call for a Division is also made.

   *Answer:* Division — “On an inconclusive voice vote in a very small meeting where all present can clearly see one another, if, instead of calling for a Division, a member asks for a show of hands, this is in the nature of a request, and the chair can retake the vote by this method unless a call for a Division is also made.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:3 — “On an inconclusive voice vote in a very small meeting where all present can clearly see one another, if, instead of calling for a Division, a member asks for a show of hands, this is in the nature of a request, and the chair can retake the vote by this method unless a call for a Division is also made.”

**140.** `[C0657 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a _____ is demanded, the chair immediately takes the vote again, first by having the affirmative rise, then by having the negative rise.

   *Answer:* Division — “When a Division is demanded, the chair immediately takes the vote again, first by having the affirmative rise, then by having the negative rise.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:5 — “When a Division is demanded, the chair immediately takes the vote again, first by having the affirmative rise, then by having the negative rise.”

**141.** `[C0657 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a _____ is demanded, the chair immediately takes the vote again, first by having the affirmative rise, then by having the negative rise.
   A. Suspend the Rules
   B. special order
   C. Division
   D. dilatory

   *Answer:* C) Division. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:5 — “When a Division is demanded, the chair immediately takes the vote again, first by having the affirmative rise, then by having the negative rise.”

**142.** `[C0659 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When it is clear that there has been a full vote and there can be no reasonable doubt as to which side is in the majority, a call for a _____ is dilatory, and the chair should not allow the individual member’s right of demanding a Division to be abused to the annoyance of the assembly.

   *Answer:* Division — “When it is clear that there has been a full vote and there can be no reasonable doubt as to which side is in the majority, a call for a Division is dilatory, and the chair should not allow the individual member’s right of demanding a Division to be abused to the annoyance of the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:7 — “When it is clear that there has been a full vote and there can be no reasonable doubt as to which side is in the majority, a call for a Division is dilatory, and the chair should not allow the individual member’s right of demanding a Division to be abused to the annoyance of the assembly.”

**143.** `[C0659 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When it is clear that there has been a full vote and there can be no reasonable doubt as to which side is in the majority, a call for a _____ is dilatory, and the chair should not allow the individual member’s right of demanding a Division to be abused to the annoyance of the assembly.
   A. Division
   B. Which to Adjourn
   C. Division of a Question
   D. question again before the assembly

   *Answer:* A) Division. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:7 — “When it is clear that there has been a full vote and there can be no reasonable doubt as to which side is in the majority, a call for a Division is dilatory, and the chair should not allow the individual member’s right of demanding a Division to be abused to the annoyance of the assembly.”

**144.** `[C0659 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When it is clear that there has been a full vote and there can be no reasonable doubt as to which side is in the majority, a call for a _____ is dilatory, and the chair should not allow the individual member’s right of demanding a Division to be abused to the annoyance of the assembly.

   *Answer:* Division — “When it is clear that there has been a full vote and there can be no reasonable doubt as to which side is in the majority, a call for a Division is dilatory, and the chair should not allow the individual member’s right of demanding a Division to be abused to the annoyance of the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:7 — “When it is clear that there has been a full vote and there can be no reasonable doubt as to which side is in the majority, a call for a Division is dilatory, and the chair should not allow the individual member’s right of demanding a Division to be abused to the annoyance of the assembly.”

**145.** `[C0659 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When it is clear that there has been a full vote and there can be no reasonable doubt as to which side is in the majority, a call for a _____ is dilatory, and the chair should not allow the individual member’s right of demanding a Division to be abused to the annoyance of the assembly.
   A. Division
   B. suspects
   C. President-Elect
   D. call

   *Answer:* A) Division. See RONR (12th ed.) §29:7 — “When it is clear that there has been a full vote and there can be no reasonable doubt as to which side is in the majority, a call for a Division is dilatory, and the chair should not allow the individual member’s right of demanding a Division to be abused to the annoyance of the assembly.”

**146.** `[C0661 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The object of motions relating to methods of voting and the polls is to obtain a vote on a question in some form other than by voice, by show of hands, or by _____ (rising);

   *Answer:* Division — “The object of motions relating to methods of voting and the polls is to obtain a vote on a question in some form other than by voice, by show of hands, or by Division (rising);”. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:1 — “The object of motions relating to methods of voting and the polls is to obtain a vote on a question in some form other than by voice, by show of hands, or by Division (rising);”

**147.** `[C0661 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The object of motions relating to methods of voting and the polls is to obtain a vote on a question in some form other than by voice, by show of hands, or by _____ (rising);
   A. Division
   B. as they stand at the time
   C. rejecting
   D. present

   *Answer:* A) Division. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:1 — “The object of motions relating to methods of voting and the polls is to obtain a vote on a question in some form other than by voice, by show of hands, or by Division (rising);”

**148.** `[C0664 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (See also _____, 45:45ff.)

   *Answer:* Roll-Call Vote — “(See also Roll-Call Vote, 45:45ff.)”. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:5 — “(See also Roll-Call Vote, 45:45ff.)”

**149.** `[C0664 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (See also _____, 45:45ff.)
   A. Roll-Call Vote
   B. specification
   C. Division
   D. more than a

   *Answer:* A) Roll-Call Vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:5 — “(See also Roll-Call Vote, 45:45ff.)”

**150.** `[C0665 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After a question has been voted on in any of these ways, and within the time specified in Standard Characteristic 1 above, the assembly can still order that the vote be taken again by some method other than any of the regular ones (see _____ Methods of Voting, 45:17ff.) or that an uncounted vote be retaken as a counted vote.

   *Answer:* Other — “After a question has been voted on in any of these ways, and within the time specified in Standard Characteristic 1 above, the assembly can still order that the vote be taken again by some method other than any of the regular ones (see Other Methods of Voting, 45:17ff.) or that an uncounted vote be retaken as a counted vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:6 — “After a question has been voted on in any of these ways, and within the time specified in Standard Characteristic 1 above, the assembly can still order that the vote be taken again by some method other than any of the regular ones (see Other Methods of Voting, 45:17ff.) or that an uncounted vote be retaken as a counted vote.”

**151.** `[C0665 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After a question has been voted on in any of these ways, and within the time specified in Standard Characteristic 1 above, the assembly can still order that the vote be taken again by some method other than any of the regular ones (see _____ Methods of Voting, 45:17ff.) or that an uncounted vote be retaken as a counted vote.
   A. Something Previously Adopted
   B. event
   C. Other
   D. Consideration of a Question

   *Answer:* C) Other. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:6 — “After a question has been voted on in any of these ways, and within the time specified in Standard Characteristic 1 above, the assembly can still order that the vote be taken again by some method other than any of the regular ones (see Other Methods of Voting, 45:17ff.) or that an uncounted vote be retaken as a counted vote.”

**152.** `[C0665 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After a question has been voted on in any of these ways, and within the time specified in Standard Characteristic 1 above, the assembly can still order that the vote be taken again by some method other than any of the regular ones (see Other _____, 45:17ff.) or that an uncounted vote be retaken as a counted vote.

   *Answer:* Methods of Voting — “After a question has been voted on in any of these ways, and within the time specified in Standard Characteristic 1 above, the assembly can still order that the vote be taken again by some method other than any of the regular ones (see Other Methods of Voting, 45:17ff.) or that an uncounted vote be retaken as a counted vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:6 — “After a question has been voted on in any of these ways, and within the time specified in Standard Characteristic 1 above, the assembly can still order that the vote be taken again by some method other than any of the regular ones (see Other Methods of Voting, 45:17ff.) or that an uncounted vote be retaken as a counted vote.”

**153.** `[C0665 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After a question has been voted on in any of these ways, and within the time specified in Standard Characteristic 1 above, the assembly can still order that the vote be taken again by some method other than any of the regular ones (see Other _____, 45:17ff.) or that an uncounted vote be retaken as a counted vote.
   A. Dilatory Motions
   B. Lay on
   C. Methods of Voting
   D. special

   *Answer:* C) Methods of Voting. See RONR (12th ed.) §30:6 — “After a question has been voted on in any of these ways, and within the time specified in Standard Characteristic 1 above, the assembly can still order that the vote be taken again by some method other than any of the regular ones (see Other Methods of Voting, 45:17ff.) or that an uncounted vote be retaken as a counted vote.”

**154.** `[C0677 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In these cases, a member cannot, as a matter of right, decline such a duty or _____ that he or she be excused from it, but the assembly—except as the bylaws may provide otherwise—can grant the member’s request to be so excused.

   *Answer:* demand — “In these cases, a member cannot, as a matter of right, decline such a duty or demand that he or she be excused from it, but the assembly—except as the bylaws may provide otherwise—can grant the member’s request to be so excused.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:1 — “In these cases, a member cannot, as a matter of right, decline such a duty or demand that he or she be excused from it, but the assembly—except as the bylaws may provide otherwise—can grant the member’s request to be so excused.”

**155.** `[C0677 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In these cases, a member cannot, as a matter of right, decline such a duty or _____ that he or she be excused from it, but the assembly—except as the bylaws may provide otherwise—can grant the member’s request to be so excused.
   A. demand
   B. chairman
   C. Limit or
   D. Request for Information

   *Answer:* A) demand. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:1 — “In these cases, a member cannot, as a matter of right, decline such a duty or demand that he or she be excused from it, but the assembly—except as the bylaws may provide otherwise—can grant the member’s request to be so excused.”

**156.** `[C0677 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In these cases, a member cannot, as a matter of right, decline such a duty or demand that he or she be excused from it, but the assembly—except as the bylaws may provide otherwise—can grant the member’s _____ to be so excused.

   *Answer:* request — “In these cases, a member cannot, as a matter of right, decline such a duty or demand that he or she be excused from it, but the assembly—except as the bylaws may provide otherwise—can grant the member’s request to be so excused.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:1 — “In these cases, a member cannot, as a matter of right, decline such a duty or demand that he or she be excused from it, but the assembly—except as the bylaws may provide otherwise—can grant the member’s request to be so excused.”

**157.** `[C0677 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In these cases, a member cannot, as a matter of right, decline such a duty or demand that he or she be excused from it, but the assembly—except as the bylaws may provide otherwise—can grant the member’s _____ to be so excused.

   *Answer:* functioning of a society or assembly — “A request to be excused from a duty essential to the functioning of a society or assembly is a question of privilege affecting the organization of the assembly;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:7 — “A request to be excused from a duty essential to the functioning of a society or assembly is a question of privilege affecting the organization of the assembly;”

**159.** `[C0682 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A request to be excused from a duty essential to the _____ is a question of privilege affecting the organization of the assembly;
   A. strike out
   B. order of business
   C. functioning of a society or assembly
   D. part

   *Answer:* C) functioning of a society or assembly. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:7 — “A request to be excused from a duty essential to the functioning of a society or assembly is a question of privilege affecting the organization of the assembly;”

**160.** `[C0682 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the case of a resignation _____, unless the bylaws provide otherwise, the assembly cannot proceed to fill the vacancy immediately since notice is a requirement.

   *Answer:* from office — “In the case of a resignation from office, unless the bylaws provide otherwise, the assembly cannot proceed to fill the vacancy immediately since notice is a requirement.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:7 — “In the case of a resignation from office, unless the bylaws provide otherwise, the assembly cannot proceed to fill the vacancy immediately since notice is a requirement.”

**161.** `[C0682 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the case of a resignation _____, unless the bylaws provide otherwise, the assembly cannot proceed to fill the vacancy immediately since notice is a requirement.
   A. from office
   B. rules
   C. the Consideration of a Question
   D. consent calendar

   *Answer:* A) from office. See RONR (12th ed.) §32:7 — “In the case of a resignation from office, unless the bylaws provide otherwise, the assembly cannot proceed to fill the vacancy immediately since notice is a requirement.”

**172.** `[C0689 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A Request for Information (also called a _____) is a request directed to the chair, or through the chair to another officer or member, for information relevant to the business at hand but not related to parliamentary procedure.

See RONR (12th ed.) §33:6 — “A Request for Information (also called a Point of Information) is a request directed to the chair, or through the chair to another officer or member, for information relevant to the business at hand but not related to parliamentary procedure.”

**180.** `[C0704 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The object of the motion to _____ is to make pending again before the assembly a motion or a series of adhering motions that previously has been laid on the table (see 17).

See RONR (12th ed.) §34:1 — “The object of the motion to Take from the Table is to make pending again before the assembly a motion or a series of adhering motions that previously has been laid on the table (see 17).”

**181.** `[C0704 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The object of the motion to _____ is to make pending again before the assembly a motion or a series of adhering motions that previously has been laid on the table (see 17).
   A. Take from the Table
   B. Form and
   C. amendment to the main question
   D. one fifth of

   *Answer:* A) Take from the Table. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:1 — “The object of the motion to Take from the Table is to make pending again before the assembly a motion or a series of adhering motions that previously has been laid on the table (see 17).”

**182.** `[C0707 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Even if no question is pending, a motion to Take from the _____ cannot interrupt a series of motions connected with bringing up a single item of business, but must wait until the complete series is disposed of.

   *Answer:* Table — “Even if no question is pending, a motion to Take from the Table cannot interrupt a series of motions connected with bringing up a single item of business, but must wait until the complete series is disposed of.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:5 — “Even if no question is pending, a motion to Take from the Table cannot interrupt a series of motions connected with bringing up a single item of business, but must wait until the complete series is disposed of.”

   A. Suspend the
   B. Table

   D. consent calendar

**186.** `[C0708 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For example, if amendments and a motion to _____ were pending when a resolution was laid on the table, then when it is taken from the table the question is first on the motion to Commit.

   *Answer:* Commit — “For example, if amendments and a motion to Commit were pending when a resolution was laid on the table, then when it is taken from the table the question is first on the motion to Commit.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:6 — “For example, if amendments and a motion to Commit were pending when a resolution was laid on the table, then when it is taken from the table the question is first on the motion to Commit.”

**187.** `[C0708 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For example, if amendments and a motion to _____ were pending when a resolution was laid on the table, then when it is taken from the table the question is first on the motion to Commit.
   A. Commit
   B. floor
   C. Debatability of Motions,
   D. those present,

   *Answer:* A) Commit. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:6 — “For example, if amendments and a motion to Commit were pending when a resolution was laid on the table, then when it is taken from the table the question is first on the motion to Commit.”

**188.** `[C0708 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For example, if amendments and a motion to _____ were pending when a resolution was laid on the table, then when it is taken from the table the question is first on the motion to Commit.

   *Answer:* Commit — “For example, if amendments and a motion to Commit were pending when a resolution was laid on the table, then when it is taken from the table the question is first on the motion to Commit.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:6 — “For example, if amendments and a motion to Commit were pending when a resolution was laid on the table, then when it is taken from the table the question is first on the motion to Commit.”

**189.** `[C0708 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For example, if amendments and a motion to _____ were pending when a resolution was laid on the table, then when it is taken from the table the question is first on the motion to Commit.
   A. not pending
   B. Commit
   C. no more than a
   D. demand

   *Answer:* B) Commit. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:6 — “For example, if amendments and a motion to Commit were pending when a resolution was laid on the table, then when it is taken from the table the question is first on the motion to Commit.”

**190.** `[C0708 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The same would be true if a motion to _____ to a Certain Time were adhering to a resolution—that is, the motion to Postpone would become immediately pending —except that if the resolution is not taken from the table until after the time of proposed postponement, the motion to Postpone is ignored.

   *Answer:* Postpone — “The same would be true if a motion to Postpone to a Certain Time were adhering to a resolution—that is, the motion to Postpone would become immediately pending —except that if the resolution is not taken from the table until after the time of proposed postponement, the motion to Postpone is ignored.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §34:6 — “The same would be true if a motion to Postpone to a Certain Time were adhering to a resolution—that is, the motion to Postpone would become immediately pending —except that if the resolution is not taken from the table until after the time of proposed postponement, the motion to Postpone is ignored.”

**191.** `[C0713 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: By means of the motions to _____ and to Amend Something Previously Adopted—which are two forms of one incidental main motion governed by identical rules—the assembly can change an action previously taken or ordered.

   *Answer:* Rescind — “By means of the motions to Rescind and to Amend Something Previously Adopted—which are two forms of one incidental main motion governed by identical rules—the assembly can change an action previously taken or ordered.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:1 — “By means of the motions to Rescind and to Amend Something Previously Adopted—which are two forms of one incidental main motion governed by identical rules—the assembly can change an action previously taken or ordered.”

   A. Division of the Assembly

   C. Rescind
   D. majority

   *Answer:* C) Rescind. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:1 — “By means of the motions to Rescind and to Amend Something Previously Adopted—which are two forms of one incidental main motion governed by identical rules—the assembly can change an action previously taken or ordered.”

**196.** `[C0714 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In contrast to the case of the motion to _____, there is no time limit on making these motions after the adoption of the measure to which they are applied, and they can be moved by any member, regardless of how he voted on the original question.

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “In contrast to the case of the motion to Reconsider, there is no time limit on making these motions after the adoption of the measure to which they are applied, and they can be moved by any member, regardless of how he voted on the original question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:3 — “In contrast to the case of the motion to Reconsider, there is no time limit on making these motions after the adoption of the measure to which they are applied, and they can be moved by any member, regardless of how he voted on the original question.”

**197.** `[C0714 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In contrast to the case of the motion to _____, there is no time limit on making these motions after the adoption of the measure to which they are applied, and they can be moved by any member, regardless of how he voted on the original question.
   A. notice
   B. receive
   C. Reconsider
   D. in the affirmative

   *Answer:* C) Reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:3 — “In contrast to the case of the motion to Reconsider, there is no time limit on making these motions after the adoption of the measure to which they are applied, and they can be moved by any member, regardless of how he voted on the original question.”

**198.** `[C0715 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As noted in Standard Descriptive Characteristic 6 above, when previous notice is a requirement for the adoption of a motion to _____ or Amend Something Previously Adopted, no subsidiary motion to Amend is in order that proposes a change greater than that for which notice was given.

   *Answer:* Rescind — “As noted in Standard Descriptive Characteristic 6 above, when previous notice is a requirement for the adoption of a motion to Rescind or Amend Something Previously Adopted, no subsidiary motion to Amend is in order that proposes a change greater than that for which notice was given.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:4 — “As noted in Standard Descriptive Characteristic 6 above, when previous notice is a requirement for the adoption of a motion to Rescind or Amend Something Previously Adopted, no subsidiary motion to Amend is in order that proposes a change greater than that for which notice was given.”

**199.** `[C0715 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As noted in Standard Descriptive Characteristic 6 above, when previous notice is a requirement for the adoption of a motion to _____ or Amend Something Previously Adopted, no subsidiary motion to Amend is in order that proposes a change greater than that for which notice was given.
   A. precedence of motions
   B. Annul
   C. amendment to the amendment
   D. Rescind

   *Answer:* D) Rescind. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:4 — “As noted in Standard Descriptive Characteristic 6 above, when previous notice is a requirement for the adoption of a motion to Rescind or Amend Something Previously Adopted, no subsidiary motion to Amend is in order that proposes a change greater than that for which notice was given.”

See RONR (12th ed.) §35:6 — “The motions to Rescind and to Amend Something Previously Adopted are not in order under the following circumstances:”

**210.** `[C0718 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When previous notice has been given, the motions to Rescind or to _____ may be made as follows:

   *Answer:* Amend Something Previously Adopted — “When previous notice has been given, the motions to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted may be made as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:7 — “When previous notice has been given, the motions to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted may be made as follows:”

   *Answer:* C) Amend Something Previously Adopted. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:7 — “When previous notice has been given, the motions to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted may be made as follows:”

**212.** `[C0718 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When previous notice has been given, the motions to _____ or to Amend Something Previously Adopted may be made as follows:

   *Answer:* Rescind — “When previous notice has been given, the motions to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted may be made as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:7 — “When previous notice has been given, the motions to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted may be made as follows:”

   D. Previously Adopted

   *Answer:* C) Rescind. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:7 — “When previous notice has been given, the motions to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted may be made as follows:”

See RONR (12th ed.) §35:8 — “When no notice of the motion to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted has been given, the motions may be made as follows:”

See RONR (12th ed.) §35:8 — “When no notice of the motion to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted has been given, the motions may be made as follows:”

   A. Something Previously Adopted
   B. postponed
   C. society,
   D. the Consideration of a Question

   *Answer:* A) Something Previously Adopted. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:8 — “When no notice of the motion to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted has been given, the motions may be made as follows:”

**218.** `[C0719 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When no notice of the motion to _____ or to Amend Something Previously Adopted has been given, the motions may be made as follows:

   *Answer:* Rescind — “When no notice of the motion to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted has been given, the motions may be made as follows:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:8 — “When no notice of the motion to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted has been given, the motions may be made as follows:”

**219.** `[C0723 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: On extremely rare occasions when it is desired not only to rescind action but also to express the strongest disapproval, a member may move to Rescind and Expunge from the Minutes (or _____).

   *Answer:* the Record — “On extremely rare occasions when it is desired not only to rescind action but also to express the strongest disapproval, a member may move to Rescind and Expunge from the Minutes (or the Record).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:13 — “On extremely rare occasions when it is desired not only to rescind action but also to express the strongest disapproval, a member may move to Rescind and Expunge from the Minutes (or the Record).”

**220.** `[C0723 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: On extremely rare occasions when it is desired not only to rescind action but also to express the strongest disapproval, a member may move to Rescind and Expunge from the Minutes (or _____).
   A. Point of Information
   B. the Record
   C. voce
   D. on the Table

   *Answer:* B) the Record. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:13 — “On extremely rare occasions when it is desired not only to rescind action but also to express the strongest disapproval, a member may move to Rescind and Expunge from the Minutes (or the Record).”

**221.** `[C0723 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: On extremely rare occasions when it is desired not only to rescind action but also to express the strongest disapproval, a member may move to _____ (or the Record).

   *Answer:* Rescind and Expunge from the Minutes — “On extremely rare occasions when it is desired not only to rescind action but also to express the strongest disapproval, a member may move to Rescind and Expunge from the Minutes (or the Record).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:13 — “On extremely rare occasions when it is desired not only to rescind action but also to express the strongest disapproval, a member may move to Rescind and Expunge from the Minutes (or the Record).”

**222.** `[C0723 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: On extremely rare occasions when it is desired not only to rescind action but also to express the strongest disapproval, a member may move to _____ (or the Record).
   A. Rescind and Expunge from the Minutes
   B. Suspend
   C. question again before the assembly
   D. the Record

   *Answer:* A) Rescind and Expunge from the Minutes. See RONR (12th ed.) §35:13 — “On extremely rare occasions when it is desired not only to rescind action but also to express the strongest disapproval, a member may move to Rescind and Expunge from the Minutes (or the Record).”

**223.** `[C0724 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: By means of the motion to _____ from further consideration of a question or subject, the assembly can take the matter out of a committee’s hands after referring it to the committee and before the committee has made a final report on it, and the assembly itself can consider it.

   *Answer:* Discharge a Committee — “By means of the motion to Discharge a Committee from further consideration of a question or subject, the assembly can take the matter out of a committee’s hands after referring it to the committee and before the committee has made a final report on it, and the assembly itself can consider it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:1 — “By means of the motion to Discharge a Committee from further consideration of a question or subject, the assembly can take the matter out of a committee’s hands after referring it to the committee and before the committee has made a final report on it, and the assembly itself can consider it.”

**224.** `[C0724 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: By means of the motion to _____ from further consideration of a question or subject, the assembly can take the matter out of a committee’s hands after referring it to the committee and before the committee has made a final report on it, and the assembly itself can consider it.
   A. scribe
   B. Postpone Definitely
   C. Division of a
   D. Discharge a Committee

   *Answer:* D) Discharge a Committee. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:1 — “By means of the motion to Discharge a Committee from further consideration of a question or subject, the assembly can take the matter out of a committee’s hands after referring it to the committee and before the committee has made a final report on it, and the assembly itself can consider it.”

**225.** `[C0728 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the committee to which the matter was referred has not yet taken it up and if it is not too late to move to _____ (the day of its committal or the next business-meeting day), the appropriate motion is to reconsider the vote on the motion of referral, which requires only a majority vote.

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “If the committee to which the matter was referred has not yet taken it up and if it is not too late to move to Reconsider (the day of its committal or the next business-meeting day), the appropriate motion is to reconsider the vote on the motion of referral, which requires only a majority vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:6 — “If the committee to which the matter was referred has not yet taken it up and if it is not too late to move to Reconsider (the day of its committal or the next business-meeting day), the appropriate motion is to reconsider the vote on the motion of referral, which requires only a majority vote.”

**226.** `[C0728 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the committee to which the matter was referred has not yet taken it up and if it is not too late to move to _____ (the day of its committal or the next business-meeting day), the appropriate motion is to reconsider the vote on the motion of referral, which requires only a majority vote.
   A. Status of
   B. Reconsider
   C. from office
   D. motion

   *Answer:* B) Reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:6 — “If the committee to which the matter was referred has not yet taken it up and if it is not too late to move to Reconsider (the day of its committal or the next business-meeting day), the appropriate motion is to reconsider the vote on the motion of referral, which requires only a majority vote.”

**227.** `[C0728 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The motion of referral may have been a subsidiary motion to _____ (13) or a main motion, depending on the case, as explained below.

   *Answer:* Commit — “The motion of referral may have been a subsidiary motion to Commit (13) or a main motion, depending on the case, as explained below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:6 — “The motion of referral may have been a subsidiary motion to Commit (13) or a main motion, depending on the case, as explained below.”

**228.** `[C0728 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The motion of referral may have been a subsidiary motion to _____ (13) or a main motion, depending on the case, as explained below.
   A. Standing
   B. Commit
   C. Call
   D. member

   *Answer:* B) Commit. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:6 — “The motion of referral may have been a subsidiary motion to Commit (13) or a main motion, depending on the case, as explained below.”

**229.** `[C0729 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion to do this can be moved as a substitute (see 12) for a pending motion to _____, or it can be introduced as an incidental main motion when no question is pending.

   *Answer:* Discharge a Committee — “A motion to do this can be moved as a substitute (see 12) for a pending motion to Discharge a Committee, or it can be introduced as an incidental main motion when no question is pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:7 — “A motion to do this can be moved as a substitute (see 12) for a pending motion to Discharge a Committee, or it can be introduced as an incidental main motion when no question is pending.”

**230.** `[C0729 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A motion to do this can be moved as a substitute (see 12) for a pending motion to _____, or it can be introduced as an incidental main motion when no question is pending.
   A. Which to Adjourn
   B. president
   C. Indefinitely
   D. Discharge a Committee

   *Answer:* D) Discharge a Committee. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:7 — “A motion to do this can be moved as a substitute (see 12) for a pending motion to Discharge a Committee, or it can be introduced as an incidental main motion when no question is pending.”

**231.** `[C0729 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion to do this can be moved as a substitute (see 12) for a pending motion to _____, or it can be introduced as an incidental main motion when no question is pending.

   *Answer:* Discharge a Committee — “A motion to do this can be moved as a substitute (see 12) for a pending motion to Discharge a Committee, or it can be introduced as an incidental main motion when no question is pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:7 — “A motion to do this can be moved as a substitute (see 12) for a pending motion to Discharge a Committee, or it can be introduced as an incidental main motion when no question is pending.”

**232.** `[C0729 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A motion to do this can be moved as a substitute (see 12) for a pending motion to _____, or it can be introduced as an incidental main motion when no question is pending.
   A. clerk,
   B. Discharge a Committee
   C. Previously Adopted
   D. Insert or Add

   *Answer:* B) Discharge a Committee. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:7 — “A motion to do this can be moved as a substitute (see 12) for a pending motion to Discharge a Committee, or it can be introduced as an incidental main motion when no question is pending.”

**233.** `[C0730 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: No motion to _____ is needed when a committee’s final report on a referred question or subject has been received by the assembly, since the committee is then automatically discharged from further consideration of the matter.

   *Answer:* Discharge a Committee — “No motion to Discharge a Committee is needed when a committee’s final report on a referred question or subject has been received by the assembly, since the committee is then automatically discharged from further consideration of the matter.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:8 — “No motion to Discharge a Committee is needed when a committee’s final report on a referred question or subject has been received by the assembly, since the committee is then automatically discharged from further consideration of the matter.”

**234.** `[C0730 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: No motion to _____ is needed when a committee’s final report on a referred question or subject has been received by the assembly, since the committee is then automatically discharged from further consideration of the matter.
   A. Point of
   B. Discharge a Committee
   C. temporarily, but not
   D. Taking

   *Answer:* B) Discharge a Committee. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:8 — “No motion to Discharge a Committee is needed when a committee’s final report on a referred question or subject has been received by the assembly, since the committee is then automatically discharged from further consideration of the matter.”

**235.** `[C0732 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a committee is discharged from further consideration of a question which was pending at the time of its referral and which was referred by means of the subsidiary motion to _____, the question comes before the assembly automatically at that time (unless the committee is discharged by means of a motion that includes the specification of a later time for considering it).

   *Answer:* Commit — “When a committee is discharged from further consideration of a question which was pending at the time of its referral and which was referred by means of the subsidiary motion to Commit, the question comes before the assembly automatically at that time (unless the committee is discharged by means of a motion that includes the specification of a later time for considering it).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:10 — “When a committee is discharged from further consideration of a question which was pending at the time of its referral and which was referred by means of the subsidiary motion to Commit, the question comes before the assembly automatically at that time (unless the committee is discharged by means of a motion that includes the specification of a later time for considering it).”

**236.** `[C0732 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a committee is discharged from further consideration of a question which was pending at the time of its referral and which was referred by means of the subsidiary motion to _____, the question comes before the assembly automatically at that time (unless the committee is discharged by means of a motion that includes the specification of a later time for considering it).
   A. decision
   B. amends
   C. extending limits
   D. Commit

   *Answer:* D) Commit. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:10 — “When a committee is discharged from further consideration of a question which was pending at the time of its referral and which was referred by means of the subsidiary motion to Commit, the question comes before the assembly automatically at that time (unless the committee is discharged by means of a motion that includes the specification of a later time for considering it).”

**237.** `[C0732 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a motion to _____ specifies a later time for considering the question and does not make it a special order, the question comes up under the same conditions as if postponed to that time without making it a special order—that is, it is a general order for the time named.

   *Answer:* Discharge a Committee — “If a motion to Discharge a Committee specifies a later time for considering the question and does not make it a special order, the question comes up under the same conditions as if postponed to that time without making it a special order—that is, it is a general order for the time named.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:10 — “If a motion to Discharge a Committee specifies a later time for considering the question and does not make it a special order, the question comes up under the same conditions as if postponed to that time without making it a special order—that is, it is a general order for the time named.”

**238.** `[C0732 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a motion to _____ specifies a later time for considering the question and does not make it a special order, the question comes up under the same conditions as if postponed to that time without making it a special order—that is, it is a general order for the time named.
   A. Ordered
   B. Meeting
   C. Discharge a Committee
   D. and

   *Answer:* C) Discharge a Committee. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:10 — “If a motion to Discharge a Committee specifies a later time for considering the question and does not make it a special order, the question comes up under the same conditions as if postponed to that time without making it a special order—that is, it is a general order for the time named.”

**239.** `[C0732 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a motion to _____ specifies a later time for considering the question and does not make it a special order, the question comes up under the same conditions as if postponed to that time without making it a special order—that is, it is a general order for the time named.

   *Answer:* Discharge a Committee — “If a motion to Discharge a Committee specifies a later time for considering the question and does not make it a special order, the question comes up under the same conditions as if postponed to that time without making it a special order—that is, it is a general order for the time named.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:10 — “If a motion to Discharge a Committee specifies a later time for considering the question and does not make it a special order, the question comes up under the same conditions as if postponed to that time without making it a special order—that is, it is a general order for the time named.”

**240.** `[C0733 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: On the other hand, a motion to discharge a committee from further consideration of a subject that was referred to the committee by means of a _____ motion is a particular case of the motion to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35).

   *Answer:* main — “On the other hand, a motion to discharge a committee from further consideration of a subject that was referred to the committee by means of a main motion is a particular case of the motion to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:11 — “On the other hand, a motion to discharge a committee from further consideration of a subject that was referred to the committee by means of a main motion is a particular case of the motion to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35).”

**241.** `[C0733 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: On the other hand, a motion to discharge a committee from further consideration of a subject that was referred to the committee by means of a _____ motion is a particular case of the motion to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35).
   A. question again before the assembly
   B. something to
   C. main
   D. society

   *Answer:* C) main. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:11 — “On the other hand, a motion to discharge a committee from further consideration of a subject that was referred to the committee by means of a main motion is a particular case of the motion to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35).”

**242.** `[C0733 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: On the other hand, a motion to discharge a committee from further consideration of a subject that was referred to the committee by means of a main motion is a particular case of the motion to Rescind or to _____ Adopted (35).

   *Answer:* Amend Something Previously — “On the other hand, a motion to discharge a committee from further consideration of a subject that was referred to the committee by means of a main motion is a particular case of the motion to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:11 — “On the other hand, a motion to discharge a committee from further consideration of a subject that was referred to the committee by means of a main motion is a particular case of the motion to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35).”

**243.** `[C0733 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: On the other hand, a motion to discharge a committee from further consideration of a subject that was referred to the committee by means of a main motion is a particular case of the motion to Rescind or to _____ Adopted (35).
   A. pro tem
   B. involve
   C. Amend Something Previously
   D. quasi committee of the whole

   *Answer:* C) Amend Something Previously. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:11 — “On the other hand, a motion to discharge a committee from further consideration of a subject that was referred to the committee by means of a main motion is a particular case of the motion to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35).”

**244.** `[C0733 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: On the other hand, a motion to discharge a committee from further consideration of a subject that was referred to the committee by means of a main motion is a particular case of the motion to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously _____ (35).

   *Answer:* Adopted — “On the other hand, a motion to discharge a committee from further consideration of a subject that was referred to the committee by means of a main motion is a particular case of the motion to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:11 — “On the other hand, a motion to discharge a committee from further consideration of a subject that was referred to the committee by means of a main motion is a particular case of the motion to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35).”

**245.** `[C0734 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The form used in making this motion, as applied to a question being considered by a _____, may be:

   *Answer:* standing committee — “The form used in making this motion, as applied to a question being considered by a standing committee, may be:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:12 — “The form used in making this motion, as applied to a question being considered by a standing committee, may be:”

   A. resolutions
   B. pending
   C. standing committee
   D. parliamentary law

**247.** `[C0736 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the motion to discharge the committee is adopted and includes no provision for consideration at a later time, and if the question was referred while pending (by means of the subsidiary motion to _____), the chair announces the result and immediately states the question brought out of committee.

   *Answer:* Commit — “If the motion to discharge the committee is adopted and includes no provision for consideration at a later time, and if the question was referred while pending (by means of the subsidiary motion to Commit), the chair announces the result and immediately states the question brought out of committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:15 — “If the motion to discharge the committee is adopted and includes no provision for consideration at a later time, and if the question was referred while pending (by means of the subsidiary motion to Commit), the chair announces the result and immediately states the question brought out of committee.”

**248.** `[C0736 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the motion to discharge the committee is adopted and includes no provision for consideration at a later time, and if the question was referred while pending (by means of the subsidiary motion to _____), the chair announces the result and immediately states the question brought out of committee.
   A. interrupted
   B. Commit
   C. amend
   D. Time to Which to Adjourn

   *Answer:* B) Commit. See RONR (12th ed.) §36:15 — “If the motion to discharge the committee is adopted and includes no provision for consideration at a later time, and if the question was referred while pending (by means of the subsidiary motion to Commit), the chair announces the result and immediately states the question brought out of committee.”

**249.** `[C0737 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____—a motion of American origin—enables a majority in an assembly, within a limited time and without notice, to bring back for further consideration a motion that has already been voted on.

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “Reconsider—a motion of American origin—enables a majority in an assembly, within a limited time and without notice, to bring back for further consideration a motion that has already been voted on.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:1 — “Reconsider—a motion of American origin—enables a majority in an assembly, within a limited time and without notice, to bring back for further consideration a motion that has already been voted on.”

**250.** `[C0737 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____—a motion of American origin—enables a majority in an assembly, within a limited time and without notice, to bring back for further consideration a motion that has already been voted on.
   A. voice
   B. Reconsider
   C. Incidental
   D. settlement

   *Answer:* B) Reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:1 — “Reconsider—a motion of American origin—enables a majority in an assembly, within a limited time and without notice, to bring back for further consideration a motion that has already been voted on.”

**251.** `[C0739 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The motion to _____ can, in general, be applied to a vote that either adopted or rejected a motion, and it proposes no specific change in a decision but simply that the original question be reopened.

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “The motion to Reconsider can, in general, be applied to a vote that either adopted or rejected a motion, and it proposes no specific change in a decision but simply that the original question be reopened.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:3 — “The motion to Reconsider can, in general, be applied to a vote that either adopted or rejected a motion, and it proposes no specific change in a decision but simply that the original question be reopened.”

**252.** `[C0739 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The motion to _____ can, in general, be applied to a vote that either adopted or rejected a motion, and it proposes no specific change in a decision but simply that the original question be reopened.
   A. Consideration of a Question
   B. of Privilege
   C. was yielded
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* D) Reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:3 — “The motion to Reconsider can, in general, be applied to a vote that either adopted or rejected a motion, and it proposes no specific change in a decision but simply that the original question be reopened.”

**253.** `[C0743 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Third, if the motion to _____ is adopted, the effect is— to the extent practicable, and with certain exceptions—to place before the assembly again the question on which the vote has been reconsidered, in the exact position it occupied the moment before it was voted on originally.

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “Third, if the motion to Reconsider is adopted, the effect is— to the extent practicable, and with certain exceptions—to place before the assembly again the question on which the vote has been reconsidered, in the exact position it occupied the moment before it was voted on originally.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:7 — “Third, if the motion to Reconsider is adopted, the effect is— to the extent practicable, and with certain exceptions—to place before the assembly again the question on which the vote has been reconsidered, in the exact position it occupied the moment before it was voted on originally.”

**254.** `[C0743 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Third, if the motion to _____ is adopted, the effect is— to the extent practicable, and with certain exceptions—to place before the assembly again the question on which the vote has been reconsidered, in the exact position it occupied the moment before it was voted on originally.
   A. of the second degree
   B. Form and Example
   C. Reconsider
   D. temporarily, but not

   *Answer:* C) Reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:7 — “Third, if the motion to Reconsider is adopted, the effect is— to the extent practicable, and with certain exceptions—to place before the assembly again the question on which the vote has been reconsidered, in the exact position it occupied the moment before it was voted on originally.”

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “Third, if the motion to Reconsider is adopted, the effect is— to the extent practicable, and with certain exceptions—to place before the assembly again the question on which the vote has been reconsidered, in the exact position it occupied the moment before it was voted on originally.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:7 — “Third, if the motion to Reconsider is adopted, the effect is— to the extent practicable, and with certain exceptions—to place before the assembly again the question on which the vote has been reconsidered, in the exact position it occupied the moment before it was voted on originally.”

**256.** `[C0743 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Third, if the motion to _____ is adopted, the effect is— to the extent practicable, and with certain exceptions—to place before the assembly again the question on which the vote has been reconsidered, in the exact position it occupied the moment before it was voted on originally.
   A. resolutions
   B. immediately pending question
   C. Reconsider
   D. substitute

   *Answer:* C) Reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:7 — “Third, if the motion to Reconsider is adopted, the effect is— to the extent practicable, and with certain exceptions—to place before the assembly again the question on which the vote has been reconsidered, in the exact position it occupied the moment before it was voted on originally.”

**257.** `[C0743 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Third, if the motion to _____ is adopted, the effect is— to the extent practicable, and with certain exceptions—to place before the assembly again the question on which the vote has been reconsidered, in the exact position it occupied the moment before it was voted on originally.

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “Third, if the motion to Reconsider is adopted, the effect is— to the extent practicable, and with certain exceptions—to place before the assembly again the question on which the vote has been reconsidered, in the exact position it occupied the moment before it was voted on originally.”. **278.** `[C0751 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Although any member can call up the motion to _____ as just described, usually no one but the mover of the reconsideration calls it up on the day the motion is made—at least in cases where the session is to last beyond that day and there is no need for immediate action.

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “Although any member can call up the motion to Reconsider as just described, usually no one but the mover of the reconsideration calls it up on the day the motion is made—at least in cases where the session is to last beyond that day and there is no need for immediate action.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §37:16 — “Although any member can call up the motion to Reconsider as just described, usually no one but the mover of the reconsideration calls it up on the day the motion is made—at least in cases where the session is to last beyond that day and there is no need for immediate action.”
