**85.** `[C0067 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Business is brought before an assembly by the _____ of a member.

   *Answer:* motion — “Business is brought before an assembly by the motion of a member.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:21 — “Business is brought before an assembly by the motion of a member.”

**86.** `[C0067 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Business is brought before an assembly by the _____ of a member.
   A. motion
   B. Withdraw or Modify a Motion
   C. not pending
   D. Table

   *Answer:* A) motion. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:21 — “Business is brought before an assembly by the motion of a member.”

**87.** `[C0069 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: While all of these motions propose some form of action and while all of them are said to be brought “before the assembly” when they are placed under consideration, most of them do not _____ before it in the sense described above—as a main motion does.

   *Answer:* bring business — “While all of these motions propose some form of action and while all of them are said to be brought “before the assembly” when they are placed under consideration, most of them do not bring business before it in the sense described above—as a main motion does.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:23 — “While all of these motions propose some form of action and while all of them are said to be brought “before the assembly” when they are placed under consideration, most of them do not bring business before it in the sense described above—as a main motion does.”

**88.** `[C0069 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: While all of these motions propose some form of action and while all of them are said to be brought “before the assembly” when they are placed under consideration, most of them do not _____ before it in the sense described above—as a main motion does.
   A. main
   B. bring business
   C. Consideration by Paragraph or Seriatim
   D. Standard

   *Answer:* B) bring business. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:23 — “While all of these motions propose some form of action and while all of them are said to be brought “before the assembly” when they are placed under consideration, most of them do not bring business before it in the sense described above—as a main motion does.”

**89.** `[C0069 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The basic form of motion—the only one whose introduction brings business before the assembly—is a _____.

   *Answer:* main motion — “The basic form of motion—the only one whose introduction brings business before the assembly—is a main motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:23 — “The basic form of motion—the only one whose introduction brings business before the assembly—is a main motion.”

**90.** `[C0069 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The basic form of motion—the only one whose introduction brings business before the assembly—is a _____.
   A. has
   B. representatives
   C. of managers,
   D. main motion

   *Answer:* D) main motion. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:23 — “The basic form of motion—the only one whose introduction brings business before the assembly—is a main motion.”

**91.** `[C0073 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is not customary to make a motion to _____ a communication or a committee report, which means only to permit or cause such a paper to be read.

   *Answer:* receive — “It is not customary to make a motion to receive a communication or a committee report, which means only to permit or cause such a paper to be read.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:27 — “It is not customary to make a motion to receive a communication or a committee report, which means only to permit or cause such a paper to be read.”

**92.** `[C0073 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is not customary to make a motion to _____ a communication or a committee report, which means only to permit or cause such a paper to be read.
   A. original main motion
   B. Definitely
   C. receive
   D. special

   *Answer:* C) receive. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:27 — “It is not customary to make a motion to receive a communication or a committee report, which means only to permit or cause such a paper to be read.”

**93.** `[C0076 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Before a member in an assembly can make a motion or speak in _____—the parliamentary name given to any form of discussion of the merits of a motion—he must obtain the floor;

   *Answer:* debate — “Before a member in an assembly can make a motion or speak in debate—the parliamentary name given to any form of discussion of the merits of a motion—he must obtain the floor;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:30 — “Before a member in an assembly can make a motion or speak in debate—the parliamentary name given to any form of discussion of the merits of a motion—he must obtain the floor;”

**94.** `[C0076 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Before a member in an assembly can make a motion or speak in _____—the parliamentary name given to any form of discussion of the merits of a motion—he must obtain the floor;
   A. Objection to
   B. stand at ease
   C. debate
   D. committees

   *Answer:* C) debate. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:30 — “Before a member in an assembly can make a motion or speak in debate—the parliamentary name given to any form of discussion of the merits of a motion—he must obtain the floor;”

**95.** `[C0076 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: that is, he must be _____ by the chair as having the exclusive right to be heard at that time.

   *Answer:* recognized — “that is, he must be recognized by the chair as having the exclusive right to be heard at that time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:30 — “that is, he must be recognized by the chair as having the exclusive right to be heard at that time.”

**96.** `[C0076 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: that is, he must be _____ by the chair as having the exclusive right to be heard at that time.
   A. applied
   B. rank
   C. five
   D. recognized

   *Answer:* D) recognized. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:30 — “that is, he must be recognized by the chair as having the exclusive right to be heard at that time.”

**97.** `[C0076 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Before a member in an assembly can make a motion or speak in debate—the parliamentary name given to any form of discussion of the merits of a motion—he must _____;

   *Answer:* obtain the floor — “Before a member in an assembly can make a motion or speak in debate—the parliamentary name given to any form of discussion of the merits of a motion—he must obtain the floor;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:30 — “Before a member in an assembly can make a motion or speak in debate—the parliamentary name given to any form of discussion of the merits of a motion—he must obtain the floor;”

**98.** `[C0080 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If at any time the chair makes a mistake, however, and assigns the floor to the wrong person—when preference in recognition was timely claimed or in any other case—his attention can be called to it by raising a _____ (23), and he must immediately correct the error.

   *Answer:* Point of Order — “If at any time the chair makes a mistake, however, and assigns the floor to the wrong person—when preference in recognition was timely claimed or in any other case—his attention can be called to it by raising a Point of Order (23), and he must immediately correct the error.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:34 — “If at any time the chair makes a mistake, however, and assigns the floor to the wrong person—when preference in recognition was timely claimed or in any other case—his attention can be called to it by raising a Point of Order (23), and he must immediately correct the error.”

**99.** `[C0080 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If at any time the chair makes a mistake, however, and assigns the floor to the wrong person—when preference in recognition was timely claimed or in any other case—his attention can be called to it by raising a _____ (23), and he must immediately correct the error.
   A. specification
   B. Assembly
   C. Point of Order
   D. Motion to Adopt an Entire Report,

   *Answer:* C) Point of Order. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:34 — “If at any time the chair makes a mistake, however, and assigns the floor to the wrong person—when preference in recognition was timely claimed or in any other case—his attention can be called to it by raising a Point of Order (23), and he must immediately correct the error.”

**100.** `[C0080 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A member cannot rise for the purpose of claiming _____ (as this right is called in all of the above cases) after the chair has recognized another member.

   *Answer:* preference in being recognized — “A member cannot rise for the purpose of claiming preference in being recognized (as this right is called in all of the above cases) after the chair has recognized another member.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:34 — “A member cannot rise for the purpose of claiming preference in being recognized (as this right is called in all of the above cases) after the chair has recognized another member.”

**101.** `[C0080 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A member cannot rise for the purpose of claiming _____ (as this right is called in all of the above cases) after the chair has recognized another member.
   A. assembly
   B. preference in being recognized
   C. motion
   D. Unfinished

   *Answer:* B) preference in being recognized. See RONR (12th ed.) §3:34 — “A member cannot rise for the purpose of claiming preference in being recognized (as this right is called in all of the above cases) after the chair has recognized another member.”

**102.** `[C0082 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the ordinary case, especially under new business, there are six essential steps—three by which the motion is _____, and three in the consideration of the motion.

   *Answer:* brought before the assembly — “In the ordinary case, especially under new business, there are six essential steps—three by which the motion is brought before the assembly, and three in the consideration of the motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:1 — “In the ordinary case, especially under new business, there are six essential steps—three by which the motion is brought before the assembly, and three in the consideration of the motion.”

**103.** `[C0082 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the ordinary case, especially under new business, there are six essential steps—three by which the motion is _____, and three in the consideration of the motion.
   A. Adjourned Meeting
   B. after
   C. brought before the assembly
   D. except

   *Answer:* C) brought before the assembly. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:1 — “In the ordinary case, especially under new business, there are six essential steps—three by which the motion is brought before the assembly, and three in the consideration of the motion.”

**104.** `[C0082 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the ordinary case, especially under new business, there are six essential steps—three by which the motion is brought before the assembly, and three in the _____ of the motion.

   *Answer:* consideration — “In the ordinary case, especially under new business, there are six essential steps—three by which the motion is brought before the assembly, and three in the consideration of the motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:1 — “In the ordinary case, especially under new business, there are six essential steps—three by which the motion is brought before the assembly, and three in the consideration of the motion.”

**105.** `[C0082 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the ordinary case, especially under new business, there are six essential steps—three by which the motion is brought before the assembly, and three in the _____ of the motion.
   A. consideration
   B. committee
   C. For Permission (or Leave) to
   D. Debate

   *Answer:* A) consideration. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:1 — “In the ordinary case, especially under new business, there are six essential steps—three by which the motion is brought before the assembly, and three in the consideration of the motion.”

**106.** `[C0083 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the assembly decides to do what a motion proposes, it adopts the motion, or the motion is _____;

   *Answer:* carried — “If the assembly decides to do what a motion proposes, it adopts the motion, or the motion is carried;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:3 — “If the assembly decides to do what a motion proposes, it adopts the motion, or the motion is carried;”

**107.** `[C0083 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the assembly decides to do what a motion proposes, it adopts the motion, or the motion is _____;
   A. carried
   B. Standing
   C. what the accused is alleged to have
   D. Point

   *Answer:* A) carried. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:3 — “If the assembly decides to do what a motion proposes, it adopts the motion, or the motion is carried;”

**108.** `[C0083 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the chair has stated the question, the motion is _____, that is, “on the floor.” It is then open to debate (if it is a main motion or one of several other debatable parliamentary motions, which are described in later chapters).

   *Answer:* pending — “When the chair has stated the question, the motion is pending, that is, “on the floor.” It is then open to debate (if it is a main motion or one of several other debatable parliamentary motions, which are described in later chapters).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:3 — “When the chair has stated the question, the motion is pending, that is, “on the floor.” It is then open to debate (if it is a main motion or one of several other debatable parliamentary motions, which are described in later chapters).”

**109.** `[C0083 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the chair has stated the question, the motion is _____, that is, “on the floor.” It is then open to debate (if it is a main motion or one of several other debatable parliamentary motions, which are described in later chapters).
   A. pending
   B. from office
   C. mass meeting
   D. right

   *Answer:* A) pending. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:3 — “When the chair has stated the question, the motion is pending, that is, “on the floor.” It is then open to debate (if it is a main motion or one of several other debatable parliamentary motions, which are described in later chapters).”

**110.** `[C0083 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the assembly decides to do what a motion proposes, it _____ the motion, or the motion is carried;

   *Answer:* adopts — “If the assembly decides to do what a motion proposes, it adopts the motion, or the motion is carried;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:3 — “If the assembly decides to do what a motion proposes, it adopts the motion, or the motion is carried;”

**111.** `[C0084 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The member then makes his motion, in simple cases by saying, “I move that…[announcing what he proposes in a wording intended to become the assembly’s official statement of the action taken].” For more important or complex questions, or when greater formality is desired, he presents the motion in the form of a _____.

   *Answer:* resolution — “The member then makes his motion, in simple cases by saying, “I move that…[announcing what he proposes in a wording intended to become the assembly’s official statement of the action taken].” For more important or complex questions, or when greater formality is desired, he presents the motion in the form of a resolution.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:4 — “The member then makes his motion, in simple cases by saying, “I move that…[announcing what he proposes in a wording intended to become the assembly’s official statement of the action taken].” For more important or complex questions, or when greater formality is desired, he presents the motion in the form of a resolution.”

**112.** `[C0084 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The member then makes his motion, in simple cases by saying, “I move that…[announcing what he proposes in a wording intended to become the assembly’s official statement of the action taken].” For more important or complex questions, or when greater formality is desired, he presents the motion in the form of a _____.
   A. Robert’s Rules of Order Newly Revised
   B. Amend Something
   C. resolution
   D. Objection to

   *Answer:* C) resolution. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:4 — “The member then makes his motion, in simple cases by saying, “I move that…[announcing what he proposes in a wording intended to become the assembly’s official statement of the action taken].” For more important or complex questions, or when greater formality is desired, he presents the motion in the form of a resolution.”

**113.** `[C0084 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The usual wording then is, “I move the adoption of the following resolution: ‘_____, That…’”;

   *Answer:* Resolved — “The usual wording then is, “I move the adoption of the following resolution: ‘Resolved, That…’”;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:4 — “The usual wording then is, “I move the adoption of the following resolution: ‘Resolved, That…’”;”

**114.** `[C0084 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The usual wording then is, “I move the adoption of the following resolution: ‘_____, That…’”;
   A. Postpone Indefinitely
   B. settlement
   C. amend
   D. Resolved

   *Answer:* D) Resolved. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:4 — “The usual wording then is, “I move the adoption of the following resolution: ‘Resolved, That…’”;”

**115.** `[C0084 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The usual wording then is, “I move the adoption of the following resolution: ‘_____, That…’”;

   *Answer:* Resolved — “The usual wording then is, “I move the adoption of the following resolution: ‘Resolved, That…’”;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:4 — “The usual wording then is, “I move the adoption of the following resolution: ‘Resolved, That…’”;”

**116.** `[C0091 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A member may second a motion (even if using the word “support” as indicated above) because he would like to see the assembly go on record as _____ the proposal, if he believes a vote on the motion would have such a result.

   *Answer:* rejecting — “A member may second a motion (even if using the word “support” as indicated above) because he would like to see the assembly go on record as rejecting the proposal, if he believes a vote on the motion would have such a result.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:11 — “A member may second a motion (even if using the word “support” as indicated above) because he would like to see the assembly go on record as rejecting the proposal, if he believes a vote on the motion would have such a result.”

**117.** `[C0091 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A member may second a motion (even if using the word “support” as indicated above) because he would like to see the assembly go on record as _____ the proposal, if he believes a vote on the motion would have such a result.
   A. amends
   B. society
   C. rejecting
   D. impose

   *Answer:* C) rejecting. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:11 — “A member may second a motion (even if using the word “support” as indicated above) because he would like to see the assembly go on record as rejecting the proposal, if he believes a vote on the motion would have such a result.”

**118.** `[C0091 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A second merely implies that the seconder agrees that the motion should _____ and not that he necessarily favors the motion.

   *Answer:* come before the meeting — “A second merely implies that the seconder agrees that the motion should come before the meeting and not that he necessarily favors the motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:11 — “A second merely implies that the seconder agrees that the motion should come before the meeting and not that he necessarily favors the motion.”

**119.** `[C0091 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A second merely implies that the seconder agrees that the motion should _____ and not that he necessarily favors the motion.
   A. specification
   B. come before the meeting
   C. Standing rules
   D. over

   *Answer:* B) come before the meeting. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:11 — “A second merely implies that the seconder agrees that the motion should come before the meeting and not that he necessarily favors the motion.”

**120.** `[C0095 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a motion that is in order has been made and seconded, the chair formally places it before the assembly by _____;

   *Answer:* stating the question — “When a motion that is in order has been made and seconded, the chair formally places it before the assembly by stating the question;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:15 — “When a motion that is in order has been made and seconded, the chair formally places it before the assembly by stating the question;”

**121.** `[C0095 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a motion that is in order has been made and seconded, the chair formally places it before the assembly by _____;
   A. stating the question
   B. less
   C. agenda
   D. Limit or

   *Answer:* A) stating the question. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:15 — “When a motion that is in order has been made and seconded, the chair formally places it before the assembly by stating the question;”

**122.** `[C0097 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a _____ who has legitimately obtained the floor offers a motion which is not in order, the chair may be able, in certain instances, to suggest an alternative motion which would be in order and would carry out the desired intent to the satisfaction of the maker.

   *Answer:* member — “When a member who has legitimately obtained the floor offers a motion which is not in order, the chair may be able, in certain instances, to suggest an alternative motion which would be in order and would carry out the desired intent to the satisfaction of the maker.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:17 — “When a member who has legitimately obtained the floor offers a motion which is not in order, the chair may be able, in certain instances, to suggest an alternative motion which would be in order and would carry out the desired intent to the satisfaction of the maker.”

**123.** `[C0097 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a _____ who has legitimately obtained the floor offers a motion which is not in order, the chair may be able, in certain instances, to suggest an alternative motion which would be in order and would carry out the desired intent to the satisfaction of the maker.
   A. adopt a statement
   B. member
   C. recorder,
   D. Until the chair states the question

   *Answer:* B) member. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:17 — “When a member who has legitimately obtained the floor offers a motion which is not in order, the chair may be able, in certain instances, to suggest an alternative motion which would be in order and would carry out the desired intent to the satisfaction of the maker.”

**124.** `[C0097 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a member who has legitimately obtained the floor offers a motion which is not in order, the chair may be able, in certain instances, to suggest an alternative motion which _____ be in order and would carry out the desired intent to the satisfaction of the maker.

   *Answer:* would — “When a member who has legitimately obtained the floor offers a motion which is not in order, the chair may be able, in certain instances, to suggest an alternative motion which would be in order and would carry out the desired intent to the satisfaction of the maker.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:17 — “When a member who has legitimately obtained the floor offers a motion which is not in order, the chair may be able, in certain instances, to suggest an alternative motion which would be in order and would carry out the desired intent to the satisfaction of the maker.”

**125.** `[C0097 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a member who has legitimately obtained the floor offers a motion which is not in order, the chair may be able, in certain instances, to suggest an alternative motion which _____ be in order and would carry out the desired intent to the satisfaction of the maker.
   A. division
   B. would
   C. Standard
   D. body of people

   *Answer:* B) would. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:17 — “When a member who has legitimately obtained the floor offers a motion which is not in order, the chair may be able, in certain instances, to suggest an alternative motion which would be in order and would carry out the desired intent to the satisfaction of the maker.”

**126.** `[C0097 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (For procedure regarding _____, see 24.)

   *Answer:* Appeal — “(For procedure regarding Appeal, see 24.)”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:17 — “(For procedure regarding Appeal, see 24.)”

**127.** `[C0098 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a motion is offered in a wording that is not clear or that requires smoothing _____ it can be recorded in the minutes, it is the duty of the chair to see that the motion is put into suitable form—preserving the content to the satisfaction of the mover— before the question is stated.

   *Answer:* before — “If a motion is offered in a wording that is not clear or that requires smoothing before it can be recorded in the minutes, it is the duty of the chair to see that the motion is put into suitable form—preserving the content to the satisfaction of the mover— before the question is stated.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:18 — “If a motion is offered in a wording that is not clear or that requires smoothing before it can be recorded in the minutes, it is the duty of the chair to see that the motion is put into suitable form—preserving the content to the satisfaction of the mover— before the question is stated.”

**128.** `[C0098 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a motion is offered in a wording that is not clear or that requires smoothing _____ it can be recorded in the minutes, it is the duty of the chair to see that the motion is put into suitable form—preserving the content to the satisfaction of the mover— before the question is stated.
   A. exhausted his right to debate
   B. before
   C. unanimous consent
   D. deliberative assembly

   *Answer:* B) before. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:18 — “If a motion is offered in a wording that is not clear or that requires smoothing before it can be recorded in the minutes, it is the duty of the chair to see that the motion is put into suitable form—preserving the content to the satisfaction of the mover— before the question is stated.”

**129.** `[C0099 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but while the motion is pending the assembly can change the wording of the motion by the process of _____ (12) before acting upon it.

   *Answer:* amendment — “but while the motion is pending the assembly can change the wording of the motion by the process of amendment (12) before acting upon it.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:19 — “but while the motion is pending the assembly can change the wording of the motion by the process of amendment (12) before acting upon it.”

**130.** `[C0099 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: but while the motion is pending the assembly can change the wording of the motion by the process of _____ (12) before acting upon it.
   A. debate
   B. request
   C. substitute
   D. amendment

   *Answer:* D) amendment. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:19 — “but while the motion is pending the assembly can change the wording of the motion by the process of amendment (12) before acting upon it.”

**131.** `[C0099 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____, the maker has the right to modify his motion as he pleases or to withdraw it entirely.

   *Answer:* Until the chair states the question — “Until the chair states the question, the maker has the right to modify his motion as he pleases or to withdraw it entirely.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:19 — “Until the chair states the question, the maker has the right to modify his motion as he pleases or to withdraw it entirely.”

**132.** `[C0099 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____, the maker has the right to modify his motion as he pleases or to withdraw it entirely.
   A. entitled to vote
   B. Until the chair states the question
   C. exhausted his right to debate
   D. illegal votes

   *Answer:* B) Until the chair states the question. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:19 — “Until the chair states the question, the maker has the right to modify his motion as he pleases or to withdraw it entirely.”

**133.** `[C0101 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but if a modification is accepted _____ another member—either before or after the motion has been seconded—the suggester has in effect seconded the modified motion, so that no other second is necessary.

   *Answer:* as suggested by — “but if a modification is accepted as suggested by another member—either before or after the motion has been seconded—the suggester has in effect seconded the modified motion, so that no other second is necessary.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:21 — “but if a modification is accepted as suggested by another member—either before or after the motion has been seconded—the suggester has in effect seconded the modified motion, so that no other second is necessary.”

**134.** `[C0101 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: but if a modification is accepted _____ another member—either before or after the motion has been seconded—the suggester has in effect seconded the modified motion, so that no other second is necessary.
   A. In Brief
   B. as suggested by
   C. involve
   D. Substitute

   *Answer:* B) as suggested by. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:21 — “but if a modification is accepted as suggested by another member—either before or after the motion has been seconded—the suggester has in effect seconded the modified motion, so that no other second is necessary.”

**135.** `[C0101 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but if a modification is accepted as suggested by _____—either before or after the motion has been seconded—the suggester has in effect seconded the modified motion, so that no other second is necessary.

   *Answer:* another member — “but if a modification is accepted as suggested by another member—either before or after the motion has been seconded—the suggester has in effect seconded the modified motion, so that no other second is necessary.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:21 — “but if a modification is accepted as suggested by another member—either before or after the motion has been seconded—the suggester has in effect seconded the modified motion, so that no other second is necessary.”

**136.** `[C0101 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: but if a modification is accepted as suggested by _____—either before or after the motion has been seconded—the suggester has in effect seconded the modified motion, so that no other second is necessary.
   A. Standing committees
   B. rank
   C. privileged
   D. another member

   *Answer:* D) another member. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:21 — “but if a modification is accepted as suggested by another member—either before or after the motion has been seconded—the suggester has in effect seconded the modified motion, so that no other second is necessary.”

**137.** `[C0108 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A member who has spoken twice on a particular question on the same day has _____ that question for that day.

   *Answer:* exhausted his right to debate — “A member who has spoken twice on a particular question on the same day has exhausted his right to debate that question for that day.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:28 — “A member who has spoken twice on a particular question on the same day has exhausted his right to debate that question for that day.”

**138.** `[C0108 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A member who has spoken twice on a particular question on the same day has _____ that question for that day.
   A. adopt a statement
   B. ruling
   C. Incidental
   D. exhausted his right to debate

   *Answer:* D) exhausted his right to debate. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:28 — “A member who has spoken twice on a particular question on the same day has exhausted his right to debate that question for that day.”

**139.** `[C0114 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The vote on a motion is normally taken by voice (or _____ voce), unless, under certain conditions, it is taken by rising or —sometimes in committees, or in small boards, or other very small assemblies—by a show of hands.

   *Answer:* viva — “The vote on a motion is normally taken by voice (or viva voce), unless, under certain conditions, it is taken by rising or —sometimes in committees, or in small boards, or other very small assemblies—by a show of hands.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:35 — “The vote on a motion is normally taken by voice (or viva voce), unless, under certain conditions, it is taken by rising or —sometimes in committees, or in small boards, or other very small assemblies—by a show of hands.”

**140.** `[C0114 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The vote on a motion is normally taken by voice (or _____ voce), unless, under certain conditions, it is taken by rising or —sometimes in committees, or in small boards, or other very small assemblies—by a show of hands.
   A. viva
   B. require
   C. Call of the House,
   D. takes effect

   *Answer:* A) viva. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:35 — “The vote on a motion is normally taken by voice (or viva voce), unless, under certain conditions, it is taken by rising or —sometimes in committees, or in small boards, or other very small assemblies—by a show of hands.”

**141.** `[C0114 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The vote on a motion is normally taken by voice (or viva voce), unless, under certain conditions, it is taken by rising or —sometimes in committees, or in small boards, or other very small assemblies—by a _____.

   *Answer:* show of hands — “The vote on a motion is normally taken by voice (or viva voce), unless, under certain conditions, it is taken by rising or —sometimes in committees, or in small boards, or other very small assemblies—by a show of hands.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:35 — “The vote on a motion is normally taken by voice (or viva voce), unless, under certain conditions, it is taken by rising or —sometimes in committees, or in small boards, or other very small assemblies—by a show of hands.”

**142.** `[C0114 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The vote on a motion is normally taken by voice (or viva voce), unless, under certain conditions, it is taken by rising or —sometimes in committees, or in small boards, or other very small assemblies—by a _____.
   A. counted
   B. essential to the
   C. germane
   D. show of hands

   *Answer:* D) show of hands. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:35 — “The vote on a motion is normally taken by voice (or viva voce), unless, under certain conditions, it is taken by rising or —sometimes in committees, or in small boards, or other very small assemblies—by a show of hands.”

**143.** `[C0114 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The vote on a motion is normally taken by voice (or viva voce), unless, under certain conditions, it is taken by _____ or —sometimes in committees, or in small boards, or other very small assemblies—by a show of hands.

   *Answer:* rising — “The vote on a motion is normally taken by voice (or viva voce), unless, under certain conditions, it is taken by rising or —sometimes in committees, or in small boards, or other very small assemblies—by a show of hands.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:35 — “The vote on a motion is normally taken by voice (or viva voce), unless, under certain conditions, it is taken by rising or —sometimes in committees, or in small boards, or other very small assemblies—by a show of hands.”

**144.** `[C0116 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Those in favor of the motion, say _____.

   *Answer:* aye — “Those in favor of the motion, say aye.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:37 — “Those in favor of the motion, say aye.”

   A. unanimous consent,

   D. puts

**146.** `[C0116 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Those in favor of the motion, say _____.

   *Answer:* aye — “Those in favor of the motion, say aye.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:37 — “Those in favor of the motion, say aye.”

**147.** `[C0116 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Those in favor of the motion, say _____.
   A. recommit
   B. present,
   C. aye
   D. Fix the Time to

**148.** `[C0117 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When only a majority is required, however, time may sometimes be saved by taking a rising vote initially, if the chair believes in advance that a voice vote _____ inconclusive.

   *Answer:* will be — “When only a majority is required, however, time may sometimes be saved by taking a rising vote initially, if the chair believes in advance that a voice vote will be inconclusive.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:38 — “When only a majority is required, however, time may sometimes be saved by taking a rising vote initially, if the chair believes in advance that a voice vote will be inconclusive.”

**149.** `[C0117 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When only a majority is required, however, time may sometimes be saved by taking a rising vote initially, if the chair believes in advance that a voice vote _____ inconclusive.
   A. will be
   B. Making a Motion
   C. germane
   D. amendment

   *Answer:* A) will be. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:38 — “When only a majority is required, however, time may sometimes be saved by taking a rising vote initially, if the chair believes in advance that a voice vote will be inconclusive.”

**150.** `[C0119 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As an alternative to voting by voice, a vote by show of hands can be used as the basic voting method in small boards or in committees, and it is so used in some _____.

   *Answer:* assemblies — “As an alternative to voting by voice, a vote by show of hands can be used as the basic voting method in small boards or in committees, and it is so used in some assemblies.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:40 — “As an alternative to voting by voice, a vote by show of hands can be used as the basic voting method in small boards or in committees, and it is so used in some assemblies.”

**151.** `[C0119 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As an alternative to voting by voice, a vote by show of hands can be used as the basic voting method in small boards or in committees, and it is so used in some _____.
   A. and Example: 1(a), 1(b)
   B. Robert’s Rules
   C. assemblies
   D. preferential voting

   *Answer:* C) assemblies. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:40 — “As an alternative to voting by voice, a vote by show of hands can be used as the basic voting method in small boards or in committees, and it is so used in some assemblies.”

**152.** `[C0125 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The form of the third point—which is usually needed only for a vote that has caused a motion to be _____—is determined by the particular motion.

   *Answer:* adopted — “The form of the third point—which is usually needed only for a vote that has caused a motion to be adopted—is determined by the particular motion.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:46 — “The form of the third point—which is usually needed only for a vote that has caused a motion to be adopted—is determined by the particular motion.”

**153.** `[C0125 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The form of the third point—which is usually needed only for a vote that has caused a motion to be _____—is determined by the particular motion.
   A. disturb the assembly
   B. adopted
   C. refrain
   D. of the board

   *Answer:* B) adopted. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:46 — “The form of the third point—which is usually needed only for a vote that has caused a motion to be adopted—is determined by the particular motion.”

**154.** `[C0129 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the chair feels that members may question a somewhat close result of which he is reasonably convinced, he can first say, “The ayes [or “the noes”] seem to have it.” The chair then pauses, and any member who doubts the result is thus invited to demand verification of the vote by a _____, as explained below.

   *Answer:* division — “If the chair feels that members may question a somewhat close result of which he is reasonably convinced, he can first say, “The ayes [or “the noes”] seem to have it.” The chair then pauses, and any member who doubts the result is thus invited to demand verification of the vote by a division, as explained below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:50 — “If the chair feels that members may question a somewhat close result of which he is reasonably convinced, he can first say, “The ayes [or “the noes”] seem to have it.” The chair then pauses, and any member who doubts the result is thus invited to demand verification of the vote by a division, as explained below.”

**155.** `[C0129 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the chair feels that members may question a somewhat close result of which he is reasonably convinced, he can first say, “The ayes [or “the noes”] seem to have it.” The chair then pauses, and any member who doubts the result is thus invited to demand verification of the vote by a _____, as explained below.
   A. calling the meeting to order
   B. division
   C. adopt a statement
   D. Fix

   *Answer:* B) division. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:50 — “If the chair feels that members may question a somewhat close result of which he is reasonably convinced, he can first say, “The ayes [or “the noes”] seem to have it.” The chair then pauses, and any member who doubts the result is thus invited to demand verification of the vote by a division, as explained below.”

   *Answer:* seem — “If the chair feels that members may question a somewhat close result of which he is reasonably convinced, he can first say, “The ayes [or “the noes”] seem to have it.” The chair then pauses, and any member who doubts the result is thus invited to demand verification of the vote by a division, as explained below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:50 — “If the chair feels that members may question a somewhat close result of which he is reasonably convinced, he can first say, “The ayes [or “the noes”] seem to have it.” The chair then pauses, and any member who doubts the result is thus invited to demand verification of the vote by a division, as explained below.”

**157.** `[C0129 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the chair feels that members may question a somewhat close result of which he is reasonably convinced, he can first say, “The ayes [or “the noes”] _____ to have it.” The chair then pauses, and any member who doubts the result is thus invited to demand verification of the vote by a division, as explained below.
   A. from office
   B. house of delegates,
   C. one fifth of
   D. seem

   *Answer:* D) seem. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:50 — “If the chair feels that members may question a somewhat close result of which he is reasonably convinced, he can first say, “The ayes [or “the noes”] seem to have it.” The chair then pauses, and any member who doubts the result is thus invited to demand verification of the vote by a division, as explained below.”

**158.** `[C0130 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If, after a vote has been retaken as an un_____ rising vote, the chair finds himself still unable to determine the result, he must take the vote a third time as a counted rising vote.

   *Answer:* counted — “If, after a vote has been retaken as an uncounted rising vote, the chair finds himself still unable to determine the result, he must take the vote a third time as a counted rising vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:51 — “If, after a vote has been retaken as an uncounted rising vote, the chair finds himself still unable to determine the result, he must take the vote a third time as a counted rising vote.”

**159.** `[C0130 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If, after a vote has been retaken as an un_____ rising vote, the chair finds himself still unable to determine the result, he must take the vote a third time as a counted rising vote.
   A. counted
   B. during a meeting
   C. Consideration of a Question
   D. Principles Governing the

   *Answer:* A) counted. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:51 — “If, after a vote has been retaken as an uncounted rising vote, the chair finds himself still unable to determine the result, he must take the vote a third time as a counted rising vote.”

**160.** `[C0131 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____.

   *Answer:* Division of the Assembly — “Division of the Assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:52 — “Division of the Assembly.”

**161.** `[C0131 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____.
   A. Division of the Assembly
   B. regular
   C. privileged motions
   D. executive secretary,

   *Answer:* A) Division of the Assembly. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:52 — “Division of the Assembly.”

**162.** `[C0132 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If such a motion is made and is seconded, the chair states it, and puts the question (by a voice vote, or by an _____ rising vote or show of hands) on whether a count shall be ordered.

   *Answer:* uncounted — “If such a motion is made and is seconded, the chair states it, and puts the question (by a voice vote, or by an uncounted rising vote or show of hands) on whether a count shall be ordered.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:53 — “If such a motion is made and is seconded, the chair states it, and puts the question (by a voice vote, or by an uncounted rising vote or show of hands) on whether a count shall be ordered.”

**163.** `[C0132 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If such a motion is made and is seconded, the chair states it, and puts the question (by a voice vote, or by an _____ rising vote or show of hands) on whether a count shall be ordered.
   A. uncounted
   B. stating the question
   C. incidental main motions
   D. Example

   *Answer:* A) uncounted. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:53 — “If such a motion is made and is seconded, the chair states it, and puts the question (by a voice vote, or by an uncounted rising vote or show of hands) on whether a count shall be ordered.”

**168.** `[C0135 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the presiding officer is a member of the assembly or voting body, he has the same voting _____ as any other member.

   *Answer:* right — “If the presiding officer is a member of the assembly or voting body, he has the same voting right as any other member.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:56 — “If the presiding officer is a member of the assembly or voting body, he has the same voting right as any other member.”

**169.** `[C0135 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the presiding officer is a member of the assembly or voting body, he has the same voting _____ as any other member.

   D. as suggested by

   *Answer:* A) right. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:56 — “If the presiding officer is a member of the assembly or voting body, he has the same voting right as any other member.”

**170.** `[C0136 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In cases where there seems to be no opposition in routine business or on questions of little importance, time can often be saved by the procedure of unanimous consent, or as it was formerly also called, _____.

   *Answer:* general consent — “In cases where there seems to be no opposition in routine business or on questions of little importance, time can often be saved by the procedure of unanimous consent, or as it was formerly also called, general consent.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:58 — “In cases where there seems to be no opposition in routine business or on questions of little importance, time can often be saved by the procedure of unanimous consent, or as it was formerly also called, general consent.”

**171.** `[C0136 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In cases where there seems to be no opposition in routine business or on questions of little importance, time can often be saved by the procedure of unanimous consent, or as it was formerly also called, _____.
   A. Motions Relating to Methods of Voting,
   B. general consent
   C. Requests and Inquiries
   D. the chair

   *Answer:* B) general consent. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:58 — “In cases where there seems to be no opposition in routine business or on questions of little importance, time can often be saved by the procedure of unanimous consent, or as it was formerly also called, general consent.”

**172.** `[C0136 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In cases where there seems to be no opposition in routine business or on questions of little importance, time can often be saved by the procedure of _____, or as it was formerly also called, general consent.

See RONR (12th ed.) §4:58 — “In cases where there seems to be no opposition in routine business or on questions of little importance, time can often be saved by the procedure of unanimous consent, or as it was formerly also called, general consent.”

**173.** `[C0136 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In cases where there seems to be no opposition in routine business or on questions of little importance, time can often be saved by the procedure of _____, or as it was formerly also called, general consent.
   A. select,
   B. unanimous consent
   C. the one
   D. the Time to Which to Adjourn

   *Answer:* B) unanimous consent. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:58 — “In cases where there seems to be no opposition in routine business or on questions of little importance, time can often be saved by the procedure of unanimous consent, or as it was formerly also called, general consent.”

**174.** `[C0142 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As already noted, the foregoing initial description of the handling of motions refers principally to the _____—the basic form of motion by which business is brought up and by which the assembly takes substantive action.

See RONR (12th ed.) §4:64 — “As already noted, the foregoing initial description of the handling of motions refers principally to the main motion—the basic form of motion by which business is brought up and by which the assembly takes substantive action.”

**175.** `[C0142 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As already noted, the foregoing initial description of the handling of motions refers principally to the _____—the basic form of motion by which business is brought up and by which the assembly takes substantive action.
   A. main motion
   B. Regular
   C. after
   D. of Order

   *Answer:* A) main motion. See RONR (12th ed.) §4:64 — “As already noted, the foregoing initial description of the handling of motions refers principally to the main motion—the basic form of motion by which business is brought up and by which the assembly takes substantive action.”

**180.** `[C0146 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The concept of _____ motions serves as a starting point for the division of motions into the classes shown.

   D. session

   *Answer:* A) standard descriptive characteristics. See RONR (12th ed.) §7:1 — “Many of the most important rules governing the use of the individual motions described in this chapter reduce to eight standard descriptive characteristics.”

**195.** `[C0185 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but the complete unit of engagement in proceedings by the assembly is a _____, which (in the general case covering all types of assemblies) consists of one or more connected meetings.

See RONR (12th ed.) §8:1 — “but the complete unit of engagement in proceedings by the assembly is a session, which (in the general case covering all types of assemblies) consists of one or more connected meetings.”

**208.** `[C0197 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The application of the same principle to the case of _____ (which, as the term is understood in this book, do not deal with parliamentary procedure;

   *Answer:* standing rules — “The application of the same principle to the case of standing rules (which, as the term is understood in this book, do not deal with parliamentary procedure;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:13 — “The application of the same principle to the case of standing rules (which, as the term is understood in this book, do not deal with parliamentary procedure;”

**209.** `[C0197 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The application of the same principle to the case of _____ (which, as the term is understood in this book, do not deal with parliamentary procedure;
   A. assembly of an organized society
   B. proxy
   C. beyond
   D. standing rules

   *Answer:* D) standing rules. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:13 — “The application of the same principle to the case of standing rules (which, as the term is understood in this book, do not deal with parliamentary procedure;”

**210.** `[C0198 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____, on the other hand—and special rules of order, which do deal with parliamentary procedure—contain the provisions that are expected to have stability from session to session, and to represent the judgment of the whole society as distinguished from the members voting at any particular session.

   *Answer:* Bylaws — “Bylaws, on the other hand—and special rules of order, which do deal with parliamentary procedure—contain the provisions that are expected to have stability from session to session, and to represent the judgment of the whole society as distinguished from the members voting at any particular session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:14 — “Bylaws, on the other hand—and special rules of order, which do deal with parliamentary procedure—contain the provisions that are expected to have stability from session to session, and to represent the judgment of the whole society as distinguished from the members voting at any particular session.”

   A. other than the reporting officer
   B. Reference Committee
   C. Bylaws
   D. and Example

   *Answer:* C) Bylaws. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:14 — “Bylaws, on the other hand—and special rules of order, which do deal with parliamentary procedure—contain the provisions that are expected to have stability from session to session, and to represent the judgment of the whole society as distinguished from the members voting at any particular session.”

   *Answer:* special rules of order — “Bylaws, on the other hand—and special rules of order, which do deal with parliamentary procedure—contain the provisions that are expected to have stability from session to session, and to represent the judgment of the whole society as distinguished from the members voting at any particular session.”. **214.** `[C0199 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The conditions under which a motion can be _____—that is, can be introduced as if new after having previously been made and disposed of without adoption—are closely related to the freedom of each new session, and to the distinction between a meeting and a session.

   *Answer:* renewed — “The conditions under which a motion can be renewed—that is, can be introduced as if new after having previously been made and disposed of without adoption—are closely related to the freedom of each new session, and to the distinction between a meeting and a session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:15 — “The conditions under which a motion can be renewed—that is, can be introduced as if new after having previously been made and disposed of without adoption—are closely related to the freedom of each new session, and to the distinction between a meeting and a session.”

**215.** `[C0199 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The conditions under which a motion can be _____—that is, can be introduced as if new after having previously been made and disposed of without adoption—are closely related to the freedom of each new session, and to the distinction between a meeting and a session.
   A. Robert’s Rules
   B. standing rule of
   C. renewed
   D. those present,

   *Answer:* C) renewed. See RONR (12th ed.) §8:15 — “The conditions under which a motion can be renewed—that is, can be introduced as if new after having previously been made and disposed of without adoption—are closely related to the freedom of each new session, and to the distinction between a meeting and a session.”

**216.** `[C0201 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The term regular meeting (or _____) refers to the periodic business meeting of a permanent society, local branch, or board, held at weekly, monthly, quarterly, or similar intervals, for which the day (as, “the first Tuesday of each month”) should be prescribed by the bylaws and the hour and place should be fixed by a standing rule.

   *Answer:* stated meeting — “The term regular meeting (or stated meeting) refers to the periodic business meeting of a permanent society, local branch, or board, held at weekly, monthly, quarterly, or similar intervals, for which the day (as, “the first Tuesday of each month”) should be prescribed by the bylaws and the hour and place should be fixed by a standing rule.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:1 — “The term regular meeting (or stated meeting) refers to the periodic business meeting of a permanent society, local branch, or board, held at weekly, monthly, quarterly, or similar intervals, for which the day (as, “the first Tuesday of each month”) should be prescribed by the bylaws and the hour and place should be fixed by a standing rule.”

**217.** `[C0201 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The term regular meeting (or _____) refers to the periodic business meeting of a permanent society, local branch, or board, held at weekly, monthly, quarterly, or similar intervals, for which the day (as, “the first Tuesday of each month”) should be prescribed by the bylaws and the hour and place should be fixed by a standing rule.
   A. Board of Directors
   B. stated meeting
   C. of the day
   D. Carrying Over Unfinished Business

   *Answer:* B) stated meeting. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:1 — “The term regular meeting (or stated meeting) refers to the periodic business meeting of a permanent society, local branch, or board, held at weekly, monthly, quarterly, or similar intervals, for which the day (as, “the first Tuesday of each month”) should be prescribed by the bylaws and the hour and place should be fixed by a standing rule.”

   *Answer:* regular meeting — “The term regular meeting (or stated meeting) refers to the periodic business meeting of a permanent society, local branch, or board, held at weekly, monthly, quarterly, or similar intervals, for which the day (as, “the first Tuesday of each month”) should be prescribed by the bylaws and the hour and place should be fixed by a standing rule.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:1 — “The term regular meeting (or stated meeting) refers to the periodic business meeting of a permanent society, local branch, or board, held at weekly, monthly, quarterly, or similar intervals, for which the day (as, “the first Tuesday of each month”) should be prescribed by the bylaws and the hour and place should be fixed by a standing rule.”

See RONR (12th ed.) §9:1 — “The term regular meeting (or stated meeting) refers to the periodic business meeting of a permanent society, local branch, or board, held at weekly, monthly, quarterly, or similar intervals, for which the day (as, “the first Tuesday of each month”) should be prescribed by the bylaws and the hour and place should be fixed by a standing rule.”

**220.** `[C0207 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Important rules relating to the continuance of a question from one session to the next depend on whether _____ quarterly time interval intervenes between the two sessions.

   *Answer:* no more than a — “Important rules relating to the continuance of a question from one session to the next depend on whether no more than a quarterly time interval intervenes between the two sessions.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:7 — “Important rules relating to the continuance of a question from one session to the next depend on whether no more than a quarterly time interval intervenes between the two sessions.”

**221.** `[C0207 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Important rules relating to the continuance of a question from one session to the next depend on whether _____ quarterly time interval intervenes between the two sessions.
   A. finally, disposed of
   B. no more than a
   C. Objection to the Consideration of a
   D. question again before the assembly

   *Answer:* B) no more than a. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:7 — “Important rules relating to the continuance of a question from one session to the next depend on whether no more than a quarterly time interval intervenes between the two sessions.”

**222.** `[C0207 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Important rules relating to the continuance of a question from one session to the next depend on whether no more than a _____ intervenes between the two sessions.

**223.** `[C0207 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Important rules relating to the continuance of a question from one session to the next depend on whether no more than a _____ intervenes between the two sessions.
   A. deliberative assembly
   B. Debate
   C. quarterly time interval

   *Answer:* C) quarterly time interval. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:7 — “Important rules relating to the continuance of a question from one session to the next depend on whether no more than a quarterly time interval intervenes between the two sessions.”

**224.** `[C0208 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If two business sessions are separated by more than a _____—or if the term of a specified portion of the membership expires before the start of the later session (as may happen in an elected legislative assembly or in a board)— then business can go over from the earlier session to the later one only by means of referral to a committee (13).

   *Answer:* quarterly time interval — “If two business sessions are separated by more than a quarterly time interval—or if the term of a specified portion of the membership expires before the start of the later session (as may happen in an elected legislative assembly or in a board)— then business can go over from the earlier session to the later one only by means of referral to a committee (13).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:8 — “If two business sessions are separated by more than a quarterly time interval—or if the term of a specified portion of the membership expires before the start of the later session (as may happen in an elected legislative assembly or in a board)— then business can go over from the earlier session to the later one only by means of referral to a committee (13).”

**225.** `[C0208 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If two business sessions are separated by more than a _____—or if the term of a specified portion of the membership expires before the start of the later session (as may happen in an elected legislative assembly or in a board)— then business can go over from the earlier session to the later one only by means of referral to a committee (13).
   A. recognized
   B. quarterly time interval
   C. Parliamentary Inquiry
   D. in the office of president

   *Answer:* B) quarterly time interval. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:8 — “If two business sessions are separated by more than a quarterly time interval—or if the term of a specified portion of the membership expires before the start of the later session (as may happen in an elected legislative assembly or in a board)— then business can go over from the earlier session to the later one only by means of referral to a committee (13).”

   *Answer:* more than a — “If two business sessions are separated by more than a quarterly time interval—or if the term of a specified portion of the membership expires before the start of the later session (as may happen in an elected legislative assembly or in a board)— then business can go over from the earlier session to the later one only by means of referral to a committee (13).”. **228.** `[C0209 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If two consecutive regular business sessions are separated by _____, then—provided that there is no specified portion of the membership whose term expires before the start of the later session—there are several ways in which business can go over from the earlier session to the later one:

   *Answer:* no more than a quarterly time interval — “If two consecutive regular business sessions are separated by no more than a quarterly time interval, then—provided that there is no specified portion of the membership whose term expires before the start of the later session—there are several ways in which business can go over from the earlier session to the later one:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:9 — “If two consecutive regular business sessions are separated by no more than a quarterly time interval, then—provided that there is no specified portion of the membership whose term expires before the start of the later session—there are several ways in which business can go over from the earlier session to the later one:”

**229.** `[C0209 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If two consecutive regular business sessions are separated by _____, then—provided that there is no specified portion of the membership whose term expires before the start of the later session—there are several ways in which business can go over from the earlier session to the later one:
   A. Extend Limits of Debate
   B. no more than a quarterly time interval
   C. Designating the Committee Chairman
   D. Methods of Voting

**230.** `[C0210 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The only way for business to be carried over directly from one session to some later regular session _____ the next regular business session is by being referred to a committee that will report at that later session.

   *Answer:* beyond — “The only way for business to be carried over directly from one session to some later regular session beyond the next regular business session is by being referred to a committee that will report at that later session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:10 — “The only way for business to be carried over directly from one session to some later regular session beyond the next regular business session is by being referred to a committee that will report at that later session.”

**231.** `[C0210 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The only way for business to be carried over directly from one session to some later regular session _____ the next regular business session is by being referred to a committee that will report at that later session.
   A. amend
   B. beyond
   C. one fifth of
   D. class

   *Answer:* B) beyond. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:10 — “The only way for business to be carried over directly from one session to some later regular session beyond the next regular business session is by being referred to a committee that will report at that later session.”

**232.** `[C0211 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a question is carried over from one session to another by any of the above processes, it remains within the _____ as a question that has been temporarily, but not finally, disposed of.

   *Answer:* control of the assembly — “When a question is carried over from one session to another by any of the above processes, it remains within the control of the assembly as a question that has been temporarily, but not finally, disposed of.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:11 — “When a question is carried over from one session to another by any of the above processes, it remains within the control of the assembly as a question that has been temporarily, but not finally, disposed of.”

**233.** `[C0211 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a question is carried over from one session to another by any of the above processes, it remains within the _____ as a question that has been temporarily, but not finally, disposed of.

   B. control of the assembly
   C. temporarily, but not
   D. Platform Committee

   *Answer:* B) control of the assembly. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:11 — “When a question is carried over from one session to another by any of the above processes, it remains within the control of the assembly as a question that has been temporarily, but not finally, disposed of.”

   *Answer:* within the — “When a question is carried over from one session to another by any of the above processes, it remains within the control of the assembly as a question that has been temporarily, but not finally, disposed of.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:11 — “When a question is carried over from one session to another by any of the above processes, it remains within the control of the assembly as a question that has been temporarily, but not finally, disposed of.”

**235.** `[C0211 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a question is carried over from one session to another by any of the above processes, it remains _____ control of the assembly as a question that has been temporarily, but not finally, disposed of.
   A. was yielded
