   D. Division

   *Answer:* B) within the. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:11 — “When a question is carried over from one session to another by any of the above processes, it remains within the control of the assembly as a question that has been temporarily, but not finally, disposed of.”

**236.** `[C0213 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A special meeting (or _____) is a separate session of a society held at a time different from that of any regular meeting, and convened only to consider one or more items of business specified in the call of the meeting.

   *Answer:* called meeting — “A special meeting (or called meeting) is a separate session of a society held at a time different from that of any regular meeting, and convened only to consider one or more items of business specified in the call of the meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:13 — “A special meeting (or called meeting) is a separate session of a society held at a time different from that of any regular meeting, and convened only to consider one or more items of business specified in the call of the meeting.”

**237.** `[C0213 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A special meeting (or _____) is a separate session of a society held at a time different from that of any regular meeting, and convened only to consider one or more items of business specified in the call of the meeting.
   A. called meeting
   B. refrain
   C. Privilege
   D. session

   *Answer:* A) called meeting. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:13 — “A special meeting (or called meeting) is a separate session of a society held at a time different from that of any regular meeting, and convened only to consider one or more items of business specified in the call of the meeting.”

**238.** `[C0213 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____ (or called meeting) is a separate session of a society held at a time different from that of any regular meeting, and convened only to consider one or more items of business specified in the call of the meeting.

   *Answer:* special meeting — “A special meeting (or called meeting) is a separate session of a society held at a time different from that of any regular meeting, and convened only to consider one or more items of business specified in the call of the meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:13 — “A special meeting (or called meeting) is a separate session of a society held at a time different from that of any regular meeting, and convened only to consider one or more items of business specified in the call of the meeting.”

**239.** `[C0213 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A _____ (or called meeting) is a separate session of a society held at a time different from that of any regular meeting, and convened only to consider one or more items of business specified in the call of the meeting.
   A. special meeting
   B. general parliamentary law
   C. adopt, accept,
   D. Call of the House,

   *Answer:* A) special meeting. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:13 — “A special meeting (or called meeting) is a separate session of a society held at a time different from that of any regular meeting, and convened only to consider one or more items of business specified in the call of the meeting.”

**240.** `[C0217 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An _____ is a meeting in continuation of the session of the immediately preceding regular or special meeting.

   *Answer:* adjourned meeting — “An adjourned meeting is a meeting in continuation of the session of the immediately preceding regular or special meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:17 — “An adjourned meeting is a meeting in continuation of the session of the immediately preceding regular or special meeting.”

**241.** `[C0217 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An _____ is a meeting in continuation of the session of the immediately preceding regular or special meeting.
   A. adjourned meeting
   B. the Minutes
   C. Withdraw or Modify a Motion
   D. as they stand at the time

   *Answer:* A) adjourned meeting. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:17 — “An adjourned meeting is a meeting in continuation of the session of the immediately preceding regular or special meeting.”

**242.** `[C0217 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An _____ is a meeting in continuation of the session of the immediately preceding regular or special meeting.

   *Answer:* adjourned meeting — “An adjourned meeting is a meeting in continuation of the session of the immediately preceding regular or special meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:17 — “An adjourned meeting is a meeting in continuation of the session of the immediately preceding regular or special meeting.”

**243.** `[C0217 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An _____ is a meeting in continuation of the session of the immediately preceding regular or special meeting.
   A. society
   B. adjourned meeting
   C. all
   D. Commit

   *Answer:* B) adjourned meeting. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:17 — “An adjourned meeting is a meeting in continuation of the session of the immediately preceding regular or special meeting.”

**244.** `[C0218 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When common expressions such as “regular [or “stated”] _____,” “special [or “called”] meeting,” and “annual meeting” (see below) are used in the bylaws, rules, or resolutions adopted by an organization, the word meeting is understood to mean session in the parliamentary sense, and therefore covers all adjourned meetings.

   *Answer:* meeting — “When common expressions such as “regular [or “stated”] meeting,” “special [or “called”] meeting,” and “annual meeting” (see below) are used in the bylaws, rules, or resolutions adopted by an organization, the word meeting is understood to mean session in the parliamentary sense, and therefore covers all adjourned meetings.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:18 — “When common expressions such as “regular [or “stated”] meeting,” “special [or “called”] meeting,” and “annual meeting” (see below) are used in the bylaws, rules, or resolutions adopted by an organization, the word meeting is understood to mean session in the parliamentary sense, and therefore covers all adjourned meetings.”

**245.** `[C0218 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When common expressions such as “regular [or “stated”] _____,” “special [or “called”] meeting,” and “annual meeting” (see below) are used in the bylaws, rules, or resolutions adopted by an organization, the word meeting is understood to mean session in the parliamentary sense, and therefore covers all adjourned meetings.
   A. meeting
   B. Conditions for
   C. rights
   D. Adjourn

   *Answer:* A) meeting. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:18 — “When common expressions such as “regular [or “stated”] meeting,” “special [or “called”] meeting,” and “annual meeting” (see below) are used in the bylaws, rules, or resolutions adopted by an organization, the word meeting is understood to mean session in the parliamentary sense, and therefore covers all adjourned meetings.”

**246.** `[C0218 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When common expressions such as “regular [or “stated”] meeting,” “special [or “called”] meeting,” and “annual meeting” (see below) are used in the bylaws, rules, or resolutions adopted by an organization, the word meeting is understood to mean _____ in the parliamentary sense, and therefore covers all adjourned meetings.

   *Answer:* session — “When common expressions such as “regular [or “stated”] meeting,” “special [or “called”] meeting,” and “annual meeting” (see below) are used in the bylaws, rules, or resolutions adopted by an organization, the word meeting is understood to mean session in the parliamentary sense, and therefore covers all adjourned meetings.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:18 — “When common expressions such as “regular [or “stated”] meeting,” “special [or “called”] meeting,” and “annual meeting” (see below) are used in the bylaws, rules, or resolutions adopted by an organization, the word meeting is understood to mean session in the parliamentary sense, and therefore covers all adjourned meetings.”

**247.** `[C0218 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When common expressions such as “regular [or “stated”] meeting,” “special [or “called”] meeting,” and “annual meeting” (see below) are used in the bylaws, rules, or resolutions adopted by an organization, the word meeting is understood to mean _____ in the parliamentary sense, and therefore covers all adjourned meetings.
   A. of the board
   B. session
   C. Up Business out of Its Proper Order
   D. Commit

   *Answer:* B) session. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:18 — “When common expressions such as “regular [or “stated”] meeting,” “special [or “called”] meeting,” and “annual meeting” (see below) are used in the bylaws, rules, or resolutions adopted by an organization, the word meeting is understood to mean session in the parliamentary sense, and therefore covers all adjourned meetings.”

**248.** `[C0223 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An _____ in general parliamentary usage has come to mean any meeting of a deliberative assembly, or a portion of a meeting, at which the proceedings are secret.

   *Answer:* executive session — “An executive session in general parliamentary usage has come to mean any meeting of a deliberative assembly, or a portion of a meeting, at which the proceedings are secret.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:24 — “An executive session in general parliamentary usage has come to mean any meeting of a deliberative assembly, or a portion of a meeting, at which the proceedings are secret.”

**249.** `[C0223 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An _____ in general parliamentary usage has come to mean any meeting of a deliberative assembly, or a portion of a meeting, at which the proceedings are secret.
   A. variations
   B. executive session
   C. standing rule
   D. first paragraph

   *Answer:* B) executive session. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:24 — “An executive session in general parliamentary usage has come to mean any meeting of a deliberative assembly, or a portion of a meeting, at which the proceedings are secret.”

**250.** `[C0225 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the assembly wishes to further lift the secrecy of action taken in an executive session, it may adopt a motion to do so, which is a motion to Amend Something _____ (35).

   *Answer:* Previously Adopted — “If the assembly wishes to further lift the secrecy of action taken in an executive session, it may adopt a motion to do so, which is a motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:26 — “If the assembly wishes to further lift the secrecy of action taken in an executive session, it may adopt a motion to do so, which is a motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35).”

**251.** `[C0225 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the assembly wishes to further lift the secrecy of action taken in an executive session, it may adopt a motion to do so, which is a motion to Amend Something _____ (35).
   A. same
   B. within the control of the
   C. Previously Adopted
   D. president

   *Answer:* C) Previously Adopted. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:26 — “If the assembly wishes to further lift the secrecy of action taken in an executive session, it may adopt a motion to do so, which is a motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35).”

**252.** `[C0225 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the assembly wishes to further lift the secrecy of action taken in an executive session, it may adopt a motion to do so, which is a motion to _____ Previously Adopted (35).

   *Answer:* Amend Something — “If the assembly wishes to further lift the secrecy of action taken in an executive session, it may adopt a motion to do so, which is a motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:26 — “If the assembly wishes to further lift the secrecy of action taken in an executive session, it may adopt a motion to do so, which is a motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35).”

**253.** `[C0225 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the assembly wishes to further lift the secrecy of action taken in an executive session, it may adopt a motion to do so, which is a motion to _____ Previously Adopted (35).
   A. during a meeting
   B. raising
   C. order of precedence
   D. Amend Something

   *Answer:* D) Amend Something. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:26 — “If the assembly wishes to further lift the secrecy of action taken in an executive session, it may adopt a motion to do so, which is a motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35).”

**254.** `[C0230 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Among some organizations, there is an increasing preference, especially in the case of a relatively small board or other assembly, to transact business at _____— that is, at meetings at which, rather than all participating members being physically present in one room or area as in traditional (or “face-to-face”) meetings, some or all of them communicate with the others through electronic means such as the Internet or by telephone.

   *Answer:* electronic meetings — “Among some organizations, there is an increasing preference, especially in the case of a relatively small board or other assembly, to transact business at electronic meetings— that is, at meetings at which, rather than all participating members being physically present in one room or area as in traditional (or “face-to-face”) meetings, some or all of them communicate with the others through electronic means such as the Internet or by telephone.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:31 — “Among some organizations, there is an increasing preference, especially in the case of a relatively small board or other assembly, to transact business at electronic meetings— that is, at meetings at which, rather than all participating members being physically present in one room or area as in traditional (or “face-to-face”) meetings, some or all of them communicate with the others through electronic means such as the Internet or by telephone.”

**255.** `[C0230 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Among some organizations, there is an increasing preference, especially in the case of a relatively small board or other assembly, to transact business at _____— that is, at meetings at which, rather than all participating members being physically present in one room or area as in traditional (or “face-to-face”) meetings, some or all of them communicate with the others through electronic means such as the Internet or by telephone.
   A. electronic meetings
   B. dilatory
   C. Methods of Voting
   D. strike out an entire paragraph

   *Answer:* A) electronic meetings. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:31 — “Among some organizations, there is an increasing preference, especially in the case of a relatively small board or other assembly, to transact business at electronic meetings— that is, at meetings at which, rather than all participating members being physically present in one room or area as in traditional (or “face-to-face”) meetings, some or all of them communicate with the others through electronic means such as the Internet or by telephone.”

**256.** `[C0235 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If an organization authorizes its assembly, boards, or committees to hold electronic meetings, such a provision should indicate whether members who are not present in person have the _____ to participate by electronic means, or whether the body may choose to allow or disallow such participation;

   *Answer:* right — “If an organization authorizes its assembly, boards, or committees to hold electronic meetings, such a provision should indicate whether members who are not present in person have the right to participate by electronic means, or whether the body may choose to allow or disallow such participation;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:36 — “If an organization authorizes its assembly, boards, or committees to hold electronic meetings, such a provision should indicate whether members who are not present in person have the right to participate by electronic means, or whether the body may choose to allow or disallow such participation;”

**257.** `[C0235 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If an organization authorizes its assembly, boards, or committees to hold electronic meetings, such a provision should indicate whether members who are not present in person have the _____ to participate by electronic means, or whether the body may choose to allow or disallow such participation;
   A. at a meeting
   B. of the
   C. closing
   D. right

   *Answer:* D) right. See RONR (12th ed.) §9:36 — “If an organization authorizes its assembly, boards, or committees to hold electronic meetings, such a provision should indicate whether members who are not present in person have the right to participate by electronic means, or whether the body may choose to allow or disallow such participation;”

**258.** `[C0785 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a motion is made and disposed of without being adopted, and is later allowed to come before the assembly after being made again by any member in essentially the same connection, the motion is said to be _____.

   *Answer:* renewed — “If a motion is made and disposed of without being adopted, and is later allowed to come before the assembly after being made again by any member in essentially the same connection, the motion is said to be renewed.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:1 — “If a motion is made and disposed of without being adopted, and is later allowed to come before the assembly after being made again by any member in essentially the same connection, the motion is said to be renewed.”

**259.** `[C0785 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a motion is made and disposed of without being adopted, and is later allowed to come before the assembly after being made again by any member in essentially the same connection, the motion is said to be _____.
   A. Conditions for
   B. renewed
   C. original
   D. of the entire membership

   *Answer:* B) renewed. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:1 — “If a motion is made and disposed of without being adopted, and is later allowed to come before the assembly after being made again by any member in essentially the same connection, the motion is said to be renewed.”

**260.** `[C0789 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Following are two cases of motions which cannot be renewed at the same _____, but which may have become different questions—and consequently are renewable —at another meeting of the same session (see 8):

   *Answer:* meeting — “Following are two cases of motions which cannot be renewed at the same meeting, but which may have become different questions—and consequently are renewable —at another meeting of the same session (see 8):”. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:6 — “Following are two cases of motions which cannot be renewed at the same meeting, but which may have become different questions—and consequently are renewable —at another meeting of the same session (see 8):”

**261.** `[C0789 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Following are two cases of motions which cannot be renewed at the same _____, but which may have become different questions—and consequently are renewable —at another meeting of the same session (see 8):
   A. Conditions for
   B. Question
   C. meeting
   D. Withdraw or Modify a Motion

   *Answer:* C) meeting. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:6 — “Following are two cases of motions which cannot be renewed at the same meeting, but which may have become different questions—and consequently are renewable —at another meeting of the same session (see 8):”

**262.** `[C0791 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such exceptions occur only through one of the processes by which, from one session to another, a main motion can remain within the control of the assembly (that is, _____ finally, disposed of), so that the same motion can be considered at the later session.

   *Answer:* temporarily, but not — “Such exceptions occur only through one of the processes by which, from one session to another, a main motion can remain within the control of the assembly (that is, temporarily, but not finally, disposed of), so that the same motion can be considered at the later session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:8 — “Such exceptions occur only through one of the processes by which, from one session to another, a main motion can remain within the control of the assembly (that is, temporarily, but not finally, disposed of), so that the same motion can be considered at the later session.”

**263.** `[C0791 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such exceptions occur only through one of the processes by which, from one session to another, a main motion can remain within the control of the assembly (that is, _____ finally, disposed of), so that the same motion can be considered at the later session.
   A. privileged motions
   B. Call
   C. temporarily, but not
   D. Limit or Extend Limits of

   *Answer:* C) temporarily, but not. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:8 — “Such exceptions occur only through one of the processes by which, from one session to another, a main motion can remain within the control of the assembly (that is, temporarily, but not finally, disposed of), so that the same motion can be considered at the later session.”

**264.** `[C0791 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such exceptions occur only through one of the processes by which, from one session to another, a main motion can remain within the control of the assembly (that is, temporarily, but not finally, disposed of), so that _____ motion can be considered at the later session.

   *Answer:* the same — “Such exceptions occur only through one of the processes by which, from one session to another, a main motion can remain within the control of the assembly (that is, temporarily, but not finally, disposed of), so that the same motion can be considered at the later session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:8 — “Such exceptions occur only through one of the processes by which, from one session to another, a main motion can remain within the control of the assembly (that is, temporarily, but not finally, disposed of), so that the same motion can be considered at the later session.”

**265.** `[C0791 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such exceptions occur only through one of the processes by which, from one session to another, a main motion can remain within the control of the assembly (that is, temporarily, but not finally, disposed of), so that _____ motion can be considered at the later session.
   A. Only one question
   B. the same
   C. no more than a
   D. that was referred

   *Answer:* B) the same. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:8 — “Such exceptions occur only through one of the processes by which, from one session to another, a main motion can remain within the control of the assembly (that is, temporarily, but not finally, disposed of), so that the same motion can be considered at the later session.”

**266.** `[C0791 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such exceptions occur only through one of the processes by which, from one session to another, a main motion can remain within the control of the assembly (that is, temporarily, but not _____), so that the same motion can be considered at the later session.

   *Answer:* finally, disposed of — “Such exceptions occur only through one of the processes by which, from one session to another, a main motion can remain within the control of the assembly (that is, temporarily, but not finally, disposed of), so that the same motion can be considered at the later session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:8 — “Such exceptions occur only through one of the processes by which, from one session to another, a main motion can remain within the control of the assembly (that is, temporarily, but not finally, disposed of), so that the same motion can be considered at the later session.”

**267.** `[C0792 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Nonrenewability of Unsustained Objection to _____.

   *Answer:* the Consideration of a Question — “Nonrenewability of Unsustained Objection to the Consideration of a Question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:9 — “Nonrenewability of Unsustained Objection to the Consideration of a Question.”

**268.** `[C0792 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Nonrenewability of Unsustained Objection to _____.
   A. the Consideration of a Question
   B. at a point established in the
   C. opinion
   D. Filling Blanks

   *Answer:* A) the Consideration of a Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:9 — “Nonrenewability of Unsustained Objection to the Consideration of a Question.”

**269.** `[C0792 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Nonrenewability of Unsustained _____ the Consideration of a Question.

   *Answer:* Objection to — “Nonrenewability of Unsustained Objection to the Consideration of a Question.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:9 — “Nonrenewability of Unsustained Objection to the Consideration of a Question.”

**270.** `[C0792 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Nonrenewability of Unsustained _____ the Consideration of a Question.
   A. the chair
   B. member
   C. Objection to
   D. adopt, accept,

   *Answer:* C) Objection to. See RONR (12th ed.) §38:9 — “Nonrenewability of Unsustained Objection to the Consideration of a Question.”

**271.** `[C0793 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A motion is _____ if it seeks to obstruct or thwart the will of the assembly as clearly indicated by the existing parliamentary situation.

   *Answer:* dilatory — “A motion is dilatory if it seeks to obstruct or thwart the will of the assembly as clearly indicated by the existing parliamentary situation.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:1 — “A motion is dilatory if it seeks to obstruct or thwart the will of the assembly as clearly indicated by the existing parliamentary situation.”

**272.** `[C0793 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A motion is _____ if it seeks to obstruct or thwart the will of the assembly as clearly indicated by the existing parliamentary situation.
   A. Types of Deliberative
   B. dilatory
   C. motions
   D. Standing rules

   *Answer:* B) dilatory. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:1 — “A motion is dilatory if it seeks to obstruct or thwart the will of the assembly as clearly indicated by the existing parliamentary situation.”

**273.** `[C0796 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Whenever the chair becomes convinced that one or more members are repeatedly using parliamentary forms for dilatory purposes, he should either not recognize these members or he should rule that such motions are not in order —but he should never adopt such a course merely to _____ business, and he should never permit his personal feelings to affect his judgment in such cases.

   *Answer:* speed up — “Whenever the chair becomes convinced that one or more members are repeatedly using parliamentary forms for dilatory purposes, he should either not recognize these members or he should rule that such motions are not in order —but he should never adopt such a course merely to speed up business, and he should never permit his personal feelings to affect his judgment in such cases.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:4 — “Whenever the chair becomes convinced that one or more members are repeatedly using parliamentary forms for dilatory purposes, he should either not recognize these members or he should rule that such motions are not in order —but he should never adopt such a course merely to speed up business, and he should never permit his personal feelings to affect his judgment in such cases.”

**274.** `[C0796 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Whenever the chair becomes convinced that one or more members are repeatedly using parliamentary forms for dilatory purposes, he should either not recognize these members or he should rule that such motions are not in order —but he should never adopt such a course merely to _____ business, and he should never permit his personal feelings to affect his judgment in such cases.
   A. speed up
   B. aye
   C. not commend
   D. constitution

   *Answer:* A) speed up. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:4 — “Whenever the chair becomes convinced that one or more members are repeatedly using parliamentary forms for dilatory purposes, he should either not recognize these members or he should rule that such motions are not in order —but he should never adopt such a course merely to speed up business, and he should never permit his personal feelings to affect his judgment in such cases.”

**275.** `[C0796 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the chair only _____ that a motion is not made in good faith, he should give the maker of the motion the benefit of the doubt.

   *Answer:* suspects — “If the chair only suspects that a motion is not made in good faith, he should give the maker of the motion the benefit of the doubt.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:4 — “If the chair only suspects that a motion is not made in good faith, he should give the maker of the motion the benefit of the doubt.”

**276.** `[C0796 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the chair only _____ that a motion is not made in good faith, he should give the maker of the motion the benefit of the doubt.
   A. characteristics
   B. Reconsider and Enter on
   C. suspects
   D. show of hands

   *Answer:* C) suspects. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:4 — “If the chair only suspects that a motion is not made in good faith, he should give the maker of the motion the benefit of the doubt.”

**277.** `[C0797 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Likewise, apart from motions to _____ or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), motions are not in order if they conflict with one or more motions previously adopted at any time and still in force.

   *Answer:* Rescind — “Likewise, apart from motions to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), motions are not in order if they conflict with one or more motions previously adopted at any time and still in force.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:5 — “Likewise, apart from motions to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), motions are not in order if they conflict with one or more motions previously adopted at any time and still in force.”

**278.** `[C0797 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Likewise, apart from motions to _____ or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), motions are not in order if they conflict with one or more motions previously adopted at any time and still in force.
   A. amends
   B. Debate
   C. from the primary amendment
   D. Rescind

   *Answer:* D) Rescind. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:5 — “Likewise, apart from motions to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), motions are not in order if they conflict with one or more motions previously adopted at any time and still in force.”

   *Answer:* Previously Adopted — “Likewise, apart from motions to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), motions are not in order if they conflict with one or more motions previously adopted at any time and still in force.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:5 — “Likewise, apart from motions to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), motions are not in order if they conflict with one or more motions previously adopted at any time and still in force.”

**280.** `[C0797 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Likewise, apart from motions to Rescind or to Amend Something _____ (35), motions are not in order if they conflict with one or more motions previously adopted at any time and still in force.
   A. Request to Be Excused from a Duty,
   B. Previously Adopted
   C. Raise a Question
   D. variations

**281.** `[C0797 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Likewise, apart from motions to Rescind or to _____ Previously Adopted (35), motions are not in order if they conflict with one or more motions previously adopted at any time and still in force.

   *Answer:* Amend Something — “Likewise, apart from motions to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), motions are not in order if they conflict with one or more motions previously adopted at any time and still in force.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §39:5 — “Likewise, apart from motions to Rescind or to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), motions are not in order if they conflict with one or more motions previously adopted at any time and still in force.”

**282.** `[C0806 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Subsidiary and incidental motions, questions of privilege, motions to Raise a Question of _____ or Call for the Orders of the Day, and other motions may also be considered if they are related to these motions or to the conduct of the meeting while it remains without a quorum.

   *Answer:* Privilege — “Subsidiary and incidental motions, questions of privilege, motions to Raise a Question of Privilege or Call for the Orders of the Day, and other motions may also be considered if they are related to these motions or to the conduct of the meeting while it remains without a quorum.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:7 — “Subsidiary and incidental motions, questions of privilege, motions to Raise a Question of Privilege or Call for the Orders of the Day, and other motions may also be considered if they are related to these motions or to the conduct of the meeting while it remains without a quorum.”

**283.** `[C0806 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Subsidiary and incidental motions, questions of privilege, motions to Raise a Question of _____ or Call for the Orders of the Day, and other motions may also be considered if they are related to these motions or to the conduct of the meeting while it remains without a quorum.
   A. extending limits
   B. Annul
   C. standing rule
   D. Privilege

   *Answer:* D) Privilege. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:7 — “Subsidiary and incidental motions, questions of privilege, motions to Raise a Question of Privilege or Call for the Orders of the Day, and other motions may also be considered if they are related to these motions or to the conduct of the meeting while it remains without a quorum.”

**284.** `[C0806 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Subsidiary and incidental motions, questions of privilege, motions to _____ Privilege or Call for the Orders of the Day, and other motions may also be considered if they are related to these motions or to the conduct of the meeting while it remains without a quorum.

   *Answer:* Raise a Question of — “Subsidiary and incidental motions, questions of privilege, motions to Raise a Question of Privilege or Call for the Orders of the Day, and other motions may also be considered if they are related to these motions or to the conduct of the meeting while it remains without a quorum.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:7 — “Subsidiary and incidental motions, questions of privilege, motions to Raise a Question of Privilege or Call for the Orders of the Day, and other motions may also be considered if they are related to these motions or to the conduct of the meeting while it remains without a quorum.”

**285.** `[C0806 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Subsidiary and incidental motions, questions of privilege, motions to _____ Privilege or Call for the Orders of the Day, and other motions may also be considered if they are related to these motions or to the conduct of the meeting while it remains without a quorum.
   A. assembly of an organized society
   B. amendment to the main question
   C. rights
   D. Raise a Question of

   *Answer:* D) Raise a Question of. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:7 — “Subsidiary and incidental motions, questions of privilege, motions to Raise a Question of Privilege or Call for the Orders of the Day, and other motions may also be considered if they are related to these motions or to the conduct of the meeting while it remains without a quorum.”

**286.** `[C0806 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Subsidiary and incidental motions, questions of privilege, motions to Raise a Question of Privilege or _____, and other motions may also be considered if they are related to these motions or to the conduct of the meeting while it remains without a quorum.

   *Answer:* Call for the Orders of the Day — “Subsidiary and incidental motions, questions of privilege, motions to Raise a Question of Privilege or Call for the Orders of the Day, and other motions may also be considered if they are related to these motions or to the conduct of the meeting while it remains without a quorum.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:7 — “Subsidiary and incidental motions, questions of privilege, motions to Raise a Question of Privilege or Call for the Orders of the Day, and other motions may also be considered if they are related to these motions or to the conduct of the meeting while it remains without a quorum.”

**287.** `[C0807 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Motions to obtain a quorum are similar to a _____ which can be ordered in assemblies having the power to compel attendance (see below).

   *Answer:* Call of the House, — “Motions to obtain a quorum are similar to a Call of the House, which can be ordered in assemblies having the power to compel attendance (see below).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:8 — “Motions to obtain a quorum are similar to a Call of the House, which can be ordered in assemblies having the power to compel attendance (see below).”

**288.** `[C0807 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Motions to obtain a quorum are similar to a _____ which can be ordered in assemblies having the power to compel attendance (see below).
   A. Call of the House,
   B. stating the question
   C. yields
   D. has

   *Answer:* A) Call of the House,. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:8 — “Motions to obtain a quorum are similar to a Call of the House, which can be ordered in assemblies having the power to compel attendance (see below).”

**289.** `[C0811 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ on a question already pending can be allowed to continue at length after a quorum is no longer present, however, until a member raises the point.

   *Answer:* Debate — “Debate on a question already pending can be allowed to continue at length after a quorum is no longer present, however, until a member raises the point.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:12 — “Debate on a question already pending can be allowed to continue at length after a quorum is no longer present, however, until a member raises the point.”

**290.** `[C0811 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ on a question already pending can be allowed to continue at length after a quorum is no longer present, however, until a member raises the point.
   A. Business and General Orders
   B. two-thirds vote
   C. Debate
   D. presentation

   *Answer:* C) Debate. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:12 — “Debate on a question already pending can be allowed to continue at length after a quorum is no longer present, however, until a member raises the point.”

**291.** `[C0812 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In legislative bodies or other assemblies that have legal power to compel the attendance of their members, a procedure that can be used to obtain a quorum, if necessary, is the motion for a _____.

   *Answer:* Call of the House — “In legislative bodies or other assemblies that have legal power to compel the attendance of their members, a procedure that can be used to obtain a quorum, if necessary, is the motion for a Call of the House.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:13 — “In legislative bodies or other assemblies that have legal power to compel the attendance of their members, a procedure that can be used to obtain a quorum, if necessary, is the motion for a Call of the House.”

**292.** `[C0812 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In legislative bodies or other assemblies that have legal power to compel the attendance of their members, a procedure that can be used to obtain a quorum, if necessary, is the motion for a _____.
   A. come before the meeting
   B. Call of the House
   C. strike out an entire paragraph
   D. other than

   *Answer:* B) Call of the House. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:13 — “In legislative bodies or other assemblies that have legal power to compel the attendance of their members, a procedure that can be used to obtain a quorum, if necessary, is the motion for a Call of the House.”

**293.** `[C0812 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In legislative bodies or other assemblies that have legal power to compel the attendance of their members, a procedure that can be used to obtain a quorum, if necessary, is the motion for a _____.

   *Answer:* Call of the House — “In legislative bodies or other assemblies that have legal power to compel the attendance of their members, a procedure that can be used to obtain a quorum, if necessary, is the motion for a Call of the House.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:13 — “In legislative bodies or other assemblies that have legal power to compel the attendance of their members, a procedure that can be used to obtain a quorum, if necessary, is the motion for a Call of the House.”

**294.** `[C0812 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In legislative bodies or other assemblies that have legal power to compel the attendance of their members, a procedure that can be used to obtain a quorum, if necessary, is the motion for a _____.
   A. of a Question
   B. Suspend the Rules
   C. Call of the House
   D. proposed to be reconsidered

   *Answer:* C) Call of the House. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:13 — “In legislative bodies or other assemblies that have legal power to compel the attendance of their members, a procedure that can be used to obtain a quorum, if necessary, is the motion for a Call of the House.”

**295.** `[C0813 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Assemblies in which there may be occasion to order a Call _____ should adopt a rule governing this motion and providing that if one third, one fifth, or some other number less than a majority of the members or members-elect are present, they can order a Call of the House by a majority vote.

   *Answer:* of the House — “Assemblies in which there may be occasion to order a Call of the House should adopt a rule governing this motion and providing that if one third, one fifth, or some other number less than a majority of the members or members-elect are present, they can order a Call of the House by a majority vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:14 — “Assemblies in which there may be occasion to order a Call of the House should adopt a rule governing this motion and providing that if one third, one fifth, or some other number less than a majority of the members or members-elect are present, they can order a Call of the House by a majority vote.”

**296.** `[C0813 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Assemblies in which there may be occasion to order a Call _____ should adopt a rule governing this motion and providing that if one third, one fifth, or some other number less than a majority of the members or members-elect are present, they can order a Call of the House by a majority vote.
   A. in the brief interval
   B. of the House
   C. Form
   D. Requests and Inquiries

   *Answer:* B) of the House. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:14 — “Assemblies in which there may be occasion to order a Call of the House should adopt a rule governing this motion and providing that if one third, one fifth, or some other number less than a majority of the members or members-elect are present, they can order a Call of the House by a majority vote.”

**297.** `[C0813 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Assemblies in which there may be occasion to order a _____ of the House should adopt a rule governing this motion and providing that if one third, one fifth, or some other number less than a majority of the members or members-elect are present, they can order a Call of the House by a majority vote.

   *Answer:* Call — “Assemblies in which there may be occasion to order a Call of the House should adopt a rule governing this motion and providing that if one third, one fifth, or some other number less than a majority of the members or members-elect are present, they can order a Call of the House by a majority vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:14 — “Assemblies in which there may be occasion to order a Call of the House should adopt a rule governing this motion and providing that if one third, one fifth, or some other number less than a majority of the members or members-elect are present, they can order a Call of the House by a majority vote.”

**298.** `[C0813 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Assemblies in which there may be occasion to order a _____ of the House should adopt a rule governing this motion and providing that if one third, one fifth, or some other number less than a majority of the members or members-elect are present, they can order a Call of the House by a majority vote.
   A. Call
   B. specification
   C. attend
   D. ad hoc

   *Answer:* A) Call. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:14 — “Assemblies in which there may be occasion to order a Call of the House should adopt a rule governing this motion and providing that if one third, one fifth, or some other number less than a majority of the members or members-elect are present, they can order a Call of the House by a majority vote.”

**299.** `[C0813 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the rule allows the call to be moved while a quorum is actually present (for the purpose of obtaining a _____ attendance), the motion at such times should rank only with questions of privilege, should require a majority vote for adoption, and, if rejected, should not be allowed to be renewed while a quorum is present.

   *Answer:* greater — “If the rule allows the call to be moved while a quorum is actually present (for the purpose of obtaining a greater attendance), the motion at such times should rank only with questions of privilege, should require a majority vote for adoption, and, if rejected, should not be allowed to be renewed while a quorum is present.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:14 — “If the rule allows the call to be moved while a quorum is actually present (for the purpose of obtaining a greater attendance), the motion at such times should rank only with questions of privilege, should require a majority vote for adoption, and, if rejected, should not be allowed to be renewed while a quorum is present.”

**300.** `[C0814 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a _____ is ordered, the clerk calls the roll of the members, then calls again the names of the absentees —in whose behalf explanations of absence can be made and excuses can be requested.

   *Answer:* Call of the House — “When a Call of the House is ordered, the clerk calls the roll of the members, then calls again the names of the absentees —in whose behalf explanations of absence can be made and excuses can be requested.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:15 — “When a Call of the House is ordered, the clerk calls the roll of the members, then calls again the names of the absentees —in whose behalf explanations of absence can be made and excuses can be requested.”

**301.** `[C0814 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a _____ is ordered, the clerk calls the roll of the members, then calls again the names of the absentees —in whose behalf explanations of absence can be made and excuses can be requested.
   A. assuming
   B. Amend
   C. ordinary committees
   D. Call of the House

   *Answer:* D) Call of the House. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:15 — “When a Call of the House is ordered, the clerk calls the roll of the members, then calls again the names of the absentees —in whose behalf explanations of absence can be made and excuses can be requested.”

**302.** `[C0815 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After a _____ has been ordered, no motion is in order, even by unanimous consent, except motions relating to the call.

   *Answer:* Call of the House — “After a Call of the House has been ordered, no motion is in order, even by unanimous consent, except motions relating to the call.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:16 — “After a Call of the House has been ordered, no motion is in order, even by unanimous consent, except motions relating to the call.”

**303.** `[C0815 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After a _____ has been ordered, no motion is in order, even by unanimous consent, except motions relating to the call.
   A. right
   B. scribe
   C. Call of the House
   D. request

   *Answer:* C) Call of the House. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:16 — “After a Call of the House has been ordered, no motion is in order, even by unanimous consent, except motions relating to the call.”

**304.** `[C0815 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After a _____ has been ordered, no motion is in order, even by unanimous consent, except motions relating to the call.

   *Answer:* Call of the House — “After a Call of the House has been ordered, no motion is in order, even by unanimous consent, except motions relating to the call.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:16 — “After a Call of the House has been ordered, no motion is in order, even by unanimous consent, except motions relating to the call.”

**305.** `[C0815 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After a _____ has been ordered, no motion is in order, even by unanimous consent, except motions relating to the call.
   A. Division of
   B. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate
   C. Resolved
   D. Call of the House

   *Answer:* D) Call of the House. See RONR (12th ed.) §40:16 — “After a Call of the House has been ordered, no motion is in order, even by unanimous consent, except motions relating to the call.”

**306.** `[C0816 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The terms order of business, orders of the day, agenda, and _____ refer to closely related concepts having to do with the order in which business is taken up in a session (8) and the scheduling of particular business.

   *Answer:* program — “The terms order of business, orders of the day, agenda, and program refer to closely related concepts having to do with the order in which business is taken up in a session (8) and the scheduling of particular business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:1 — “The terms order of business, orders of the day, agenda, and program refer to closely related concepts having to do with the order in which business is taken up in a session (8) and the scheduling of particular business.”

**307.** `[C0816 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The terms order of business, orders of the day, agenda, and _____ refer to closely related concepts having to do with the order in which business is taken up in a session (8) and the scheduling of particular business.
   A. unanimous consent,
   B. program
   C. secondary amendment to strike out
   D. guilty

   *Answer:* B) program. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:1 — “The terms order of business, orders of the day, agenda, and program refer to closely related concepts having to do with the order in which business is taken up in a session (8) and the scheduling of particular business.”

**308.** `[C0817 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An _____ of business is any established sequence in which it may be prescribed that business shall be taken up at a session of a given assembly.

   *Answer:* order — “An order of business is any established sequence in which it may be prescribed that business shall be taken up at a session of a given assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:2 — “An order of business is any established sequence in which it may be prescribed that business shall be taken up at a session of a given assembly.”

**309.** `[C0817 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An _____ of business is any established sequence in which it may be prescribed that business shall be taken up at a session of a given assembly.
   A. standard descriptive characteristics
   B. order
   C. advance
   D. quarterly time interval

   *Answer:* B) order. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:2 — “An order of business is any established sequence in which it may be prescribed that business shall be taken up at a session of a given assembly.”

**310.** `[C0817 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Although the terms _____ and program relate primarily to the business of an entire session, the same terms are also applied to a part of the whole, in speaking of “the order of business,” “the agenda,” or “the program” of a meeting within a session.

   *Answer:* order of business, agenda, — “Although the terms order of business, agenda, and program relate primarily to the business of an entire session, the same terms are also applied to a part of the whole, in speaking of “the order of business,” “the agenda,” or “the program” of a meeting within a session.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:2 — “Although the terms order of business, agenda, and program relate primarily to the business of an entire session, the same terms are also applied to a part of the whole, in speaking of “the order of business,” “the agenda,” or “the program” of a meeting within a session.”

**311.** `[C0817 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Although the terms _____ and program relate primarily to the business of an entire session, the same terms are also applied to a part of the whole, in speaking of “the order of business,” “the agenda,” or “the program” of a meeting within a session.
   A. incidental main motion
   B. after
   C. the same
   D. order of business, agenda,

   *Answer:* D) order of business, agenda,. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:2 — “Although the terms order of business, agenda, and program relate primarily to the business of an entire session, the same terms are also applied to a part of the whole, in speaking of “the order of business,” “the agenda,” or “the program” of a meeting within a session.”

**312.** `[C0817 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An _____ is any established sequence in which it may be prescribed that business shall be taken up at a session of a given assembly.

   *Answer:* order of business — “An order of business is any established sequence in which it may be prescribed that business shall be taken up at a session of a given assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:2 — “An order of business is any established sequence in which it may be prescribed that business shall be taken up at a session of a given assembly.”

**313.** `[C0824 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair says, “The Secretary will read the minutes.” However, in organizations where copies of the minutes of each previous meeting as prepared by the secretary are sent to all members in advance, the chair announces that this has been done, and the actual reading of them aloud is omitted unless any member then requests th_____y be read.

   *Answer:* at the — “The chair says, “The Secretary will read the minutes.” However, in organizations where copies of the minutes of each previous meeting as prepared by the secretary are sent to all members in advance, the chair announces that this has been done, and the actual reading of them aloud is omitted unless any member then requests that they be read.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:9 — “The chair says, “The Secretary will read the minutes.” However, in organizations where copies of the minutes of each previous meeting as prepared by the secretary are sent to all members in advance, the chair announces that this has been done, and the actual reading of them aloud is omitted unless any member then requests that they be read.”

**314.** `[C0824 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair says, “The Secretary will read the minutes.” However, in organizations where copies of the minutes of each previous meeting as prepared by the secretary are sent to all members in advance, the chair announces that this has been done, and the actual reading of them aloud is omitted unless any member then requests th_____y be read.
   A. at the
   B. at a meeting
   C. required
   D. germane

   *Answer:* A) at the. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:9 — “The chair says, “The Secretary will read the minutes.” However, in organizations where copies of the minutes of each previous meeting as prepared by the secretary are sent to all members in advance, the chair announces that this has been done, and the actual reading of them aloud is omitted unless any member then requests that they be read.”

**315.** `[C0827 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The secretary’s draft of _____ minutes is often sent to all members in advance of the meeting at which those minutes are to be approved, usually with the meeting notice.

   *Answer:* the — “The secretary’s draft of the minutes is often sent to all members in advance of the meeting at which those minutes are to be approved, usually with the meeting notice.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:12 — “The secretary’s draft of the minutes is often sent to all members in advance of the meeting at which those minutes are to be approved, usually with the meeting notice.”

**316.** `[C0827 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The secretary’s draft of _____ minutes is often sent to all members in advance of the meeting at which those minutes are to be approved, usually with the meeting notice.
   A. Main Motions,
   B. the
   C. order of the day,
   D. general orders

   *Answer:* B) the. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:12 — “The secretary’s draft of the minutes is often sent to all members in advance of the meeting at which those minutes are to be approved, usually with the meeting notice.”

**317.** `[C0828 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In most societies it is customary to hear reports from all officers (47, 48), boards (49), and standing _____ (50) only at annual meetings.

   *Answer:* committees — “In most societies it is customary to hear reports from all officers (47, 48), boards (49), and standing committees (50) only at annual meetings.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:13 — “In most societies it is customary to hear reports from all officers (47, 48), boards (49), and standing committees (50) only at annual meetings.”

**318.** `[C0828 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In most societies it is customary to hear reports from all officers (47, 48), boards (49), and standing _____ (50) only at annual meetings.
   A. while another
   B. Insert, or Add
   C. minutes,
   D. committees

   *Answer:* D) committees. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:13 — “In most societies it is customary to hear reports from all officers (47, 48), boards (49), and standing committees (50) only at annual meetings.”

**320.** `[C0828 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Reports of Officers, Boards, and _____ Committees.

   B. Standing

   D. Postpone Definitely

See RONR (12th ed.) §41:18 — “Under this heading (referring to the explanation of Orders of the Day in 41:40ff.) are taken up the following in the order listed:”

**323.** `[C0834 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The resolution is as follows: ‘_____ That… [reading it].’ The question is on the adoption of the resolution.”

   *Answer:* Resolved, — “The resolution is as follows: ‘Resolved, That… [reading it].’ The question is on the adoption of the resolution.””. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:19 — “The resolution is as follows: ‘Resolved, That… [reading it].’ The question is on the adoption of the resolution.””

**324.** `[C0834 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The resolution is as follows: ‘_____ That… [reading it].’ The question is on the adoption of the resolution.”
   A. mass meeting
   B. incidental motions
   C. Resolved,
   D. standing rules

   *Answer:* C) Resolved,. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:19 — “The resolution is as follows: ‘Resolved, That… [reading it].’ The question is on the adoption of the resolution.””

**325.** `[C0835 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Matters that the bylaws require to be considered at a particular meeting, such as the nomination and election of officers, may be regarded as special orders for the meeting and be considered under the heading of _____ in the order of business.

   *Answer:* Special Orders — “Matters that the bylaws require to be considered at a particular meeting, such as the nomination and election of officers, may be regarded as special orders for the meeting and be considered under the heading of Special Orders in the order of business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:20 — “Matters that the bylaws require to be considered at a particular meeting, such as the nomination and election of officers, may be regarded as special orders for the meeting and be considered under the heading of Special Orders in the order of business.”

**326.** `[C0835 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Matters that the bylaws require to be considered at a particular meeting, such as the nomination and election of officers, may be regarded as special orders for the meeting and be considered under the heading of _____ in the order of business.
   A. in the bylaws
   B. Special Orders
   C. Division of a Question
   D. decision

   *Answer:* B) Special Orders. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:20 — “Matters that the bylaws require to be considered at a particular meeting, such as the nomination and election of officers, may be regarded as special orders for the meeting and be considered under the heading of Special Orders in the order of business.”

**327.** `[C0837 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A general order (as explained under _____ below) is any question which, usually by postponement, has been made an order of the day without being made a special order.

See RONR (12th ed.) §41:22 — “A general order (as explained under Orders of the Day, below) is any question which, usually by postponement, has been made an order of the day without being made a special order.”

   B. general consent
   C. Orders of the Day,
   D. Call for the Orders of the Day

   *Answer:* C) Orders of the Day,. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:22 — “A general order (as explained under Orders of the Day, below) is any question which, usually by postponement, has been made an order of the day without being made a special order.”

**336.** `[C0839 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair should not announce the heading of Unfinished _____ unless the minutes show that there is some business to come up under it.
   A. mass meeting,
   B. Business and General Orders
   C. session
   D. are

   *Answer:* B) Business and General Orders. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:24 — “The chair should not announce the heading of Unfinished Business and General Orders unless the minutes show that there is some business to come up under it.”

**337.** `[C0839 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The resolution is as follows: ‘_____ That… [reading the resolution].’ The question is on the adoption of the resolution.”

   *Answer:* Resolved, — “The resolution is as follows: ‘Resolved, That… [reading the resolution].’ The question is on the adoption of the resolution.””. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:24 — “The resolution is as follows: ‘Resolved, That… [reading the resolution].’ The question is on the adoption of the resolution.””

**338.** `[C0844 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After the call to order and before the reading of the _____ the next two headings may be included:

   *Answer:* minutes, — “After the call to order and before the reading of the minutes, the next two headings may be included:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:29 — “After the call to order and before the reading of the minutes, the next two headings may be included:”

**339.** `[C0844 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After the call to order and before the reading of the _____ the next two headings may be included:
   A. minutes,
   B. Point
   C. of managers,
   D. temporarily, but not

   *Answer:* A) minutes,. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:29 — “After the call to order and before the reading of the minutes, the next two headings may be included:”

**340.** `[C0844 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: After the call to order and _____ minutes, the next two headings may be included:

   *Answer:* before the reading of the — “After the call to order and before the reading of the minutes, the next two headings may be included:”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:29 — “After the call to order and before the reading of the minutes, the next two headings may be included:”

**341.** `[C0844 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: After the call to order and _____ minutes, the next two headings may be included:
   A. before the reading of the
   B. body of people
   C. temporarily, but not
   D. Board of Managers, Board

   *Answer:* A) before the reading of the. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:29 — “After the call to order and before the reading of the minutes, the next two headings may be included:”

**342.** `[C0847 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This calendar is called over periodically at a point established in the agenda by special rule of order, at least preceding standing _____.

   *Answer:* committee reports — “This calendar is called over periodically at a point established in the agenda by special rule of order, at least preceding standing committee reports.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:32 — “This calendar is called over periodically at a point established in the agenda by special rule of order, at least preceding standing committee reports.”

**343.** `[C0847 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This calendar is called over periodically at a point established in the agenda by special rule of order, at least preceding standing _____.
   A. as thus amended,
   B. before
   C. recognized
   D. committee reports

   *Answer:* D) committee reports. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:32 — “This calendar is called over periodically at a point established in the agenda by special rule of order, at least preceding standing committee reports.”

**344.** `[C0847 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: This calendar is called over periodically _____ agenda by special rule of order, at least preceding standing committee reports.

   *Answer:* at a point established in the — “This calendar is called over periodically at a point established in the agenda by special rule of order, at least preceding standing committee reports.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:32 — “This calendar is called over periodically at a point established in the agenda by special rule of order, at least preceding standing committee reports.”

**345.** `[C0847 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: This calendar is called over periodically _____ agenda by special rule of order, at least preceding standing committee reports.
   A. at a point established in the
   B. Insert, or Add
   C. the Consideration of a Question
   D. Point of Information

   *Answer:* A) at a point established in the. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:32 — “This calendar is called over periodically at a point established in the agenda by special rule of order, at least preceding standing committee reports.”

**346.** `[C0847 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Legislatures, city, town, or county councils, or other assemblies which have a heavy work load including a large number of routine or noncontroversial matters may find a _____ a useful tool for disposing of such items of business.

   *Answer:* consent calendar — “Legislatures, city, town, or county councils, or other assemblies which have a heavy work load including a large number of routine or noncontroversial matters may find a consent calendar a useful tool for disposing of such items of business.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:32 — “Legislatures, city, town, or county councils, or other assemblies which have a heavy work load including a large number of routine or noncontroversial matters may find a consent calendar a useful tool for disposing of such items of business.”

**347.** `[C0848 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____—that is, when no one claims the floor to make a motion in response to the chair’s query, “Is there any further new business?”—the chair may proceed to one or more of the following headings, in an order that may be subject to variation determined by the practice of the organization.

   *Answer:* After the completion of new business — “After the completion of new business—that is, when no one claims the floor to make a motion in response to the chair’s query, “Is there any further new business?”—the chair may proceed to one or more of the following headings, in an order that may be subject to variation determined by the practice of the organization.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:33 — “After the completion of new business—that is, when no one claims the floor to make a motion in response to the chair’s query, “Is there any further new business?”—the chair may proceed to one or more of the following headings, in an order that may be subject to variation determined by the practice of the organization.”

**348.** `[C0848 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____—that is, when no one claims the floor to make a motion in response to the chair’s query, “Is there any further new business?”—the chair may proceed to one or more of the following headings, in an order that may be subject to variation determined by the practice of the organization.
   A. functioning of a society or assembly
   B. After the completion of new business
   C. control of the assembly
   D. question is pending

   *Answer:* B) After the completion of new business. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:33 — “After the completion of new business—that is, when no one claims the floor to make a motion in response to the chair’s query, “Is there any further new business?”—the chair may proceed to one or more of the following headings, in an order that may be subject to variation determined by the practice of the organization.”

**349.** `[C0853 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is not in order to lay on the table or postpone a _____ of questions, like committee reports, or anything but the question that is actually before the assembly.

   *Answer:* class — “It is not in order to lay on the table or postpone a class of questions, like committee reports, or anything but the question that is actually before the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:38 — “It is not in order to lay on the table or postpone a class of questions, like committee reports, or anything but the question that is actually before the assembly.”

**350.** `[C0853 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is not in order to lay on the table or postpone a _____ of questions, like committee reports, or anything but the question that is actually before the assembly.
   A. class
   B. of a Question
   C. notice
   D. of Order Newly Revised

   *Answer:* A) class. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:38 — “It is not in order to lay on the table or postpone a class of questions, like committee reports, or anything but the question that is actually before the assembly.”

**351.** `[C0854 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: or (for obtaining unanimous consent), “If there is no objection, the chair proposes at this time to proceed to take up…” (see also illustration under the heading _____ above).

   *Answer:* Program — “or (for obtaining unanimous consent), “If there is no objection, the chair proposes at this time to proceed to take up…” (see also illustration under the heading Program above).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:39 — “or (for obtaining unanimous consent), “If there is no objection, the chair proposes at this time to proceed to take up…” (see also illustration under the heading Program above).”

**352.** `[C0854 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: or (for obtaining unanimous consent), “If there is no objection, the chair proposes at this time to proceed to take up…” (see also illustration under the heading _____ above).
   A. Business and General Orders
   B. Program
   C. voice
   D. Consider Seriatim

   *Answer:* B) Program. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:39 — “or (for obtaining unanimous consent), “If there is no objection, the chair proposes at this time to proceed to take up…” (see also illustration under the heading Program above).”

**353.** `[C0856 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Orders of the day are divided into the classes of general _____ and special orders.

   *Answer:* orders — “Orders of the day are divided into the classes of general orders and special orders.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:41 — “Orders of the day are divided into the classes of general orders and special orders.”

   B. Platform Committee
   C. orders
   D. delegates

   *Answer:* C) orders. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:41 — “Orders of the day are divided into the classes of general orders and special orders.”

   *Answer:* general — “Orders of the day are divided into the classes of general orders and special orders.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:41 — “Orders of the day are divided into the classes of general orders and special orders.”

   A. Debate

   D. Lay on the Table

See RONR (12th ed.) §41:44 — “It can be offered in this form: “I move that the following resolution be made a special order for the next meeting: ‘Resolved, That…’”;”

   A. Resolved,

   C. aye
   D. Postpone

   *Answer:* A) Resolved,. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:44 — “It can be offered in this form: “I move that the following resolution be made a special order for the next meeting: ‘Resolved, That…’”;”

**362.** `[C0858 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a question that is _____ is made an order of the day, it is usually made a special order.

   *Answer:* not pending — “When a question that is not pending is made an order of the day, it is usually made a special order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:44 — “When a question that is not pending is made an order of the day, it is usually made a special order.”

**363.** `[C0867 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: But when _____ designated hour arrives, the special order automatically interrupts any business that may be pending except: (a) a motion relating to adjournment or recess;

   *Answer:* the — “But when the designated hour arrives, the special order automatically interrupts any business that may be pending except: (a) a motion relating to adjournment or recess;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:53 — “But when the designated hour arrives, the special order automatically interrupts any business that may be pending except: (a) a motion relating to adjournment or recess;”

**364.** `[C0867 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: But when _____ designated hour arrives, the special order automatically interrupts any business that may be pending except: (a) a motion relating to adjournment or recess;
   A. Rules
   B. of the board
   C. functioning of a society or assembly
   D. the

   *Answer:* D) the. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:53 — “But when the designated hour arrives, the special order automatically interrupts any business that may be pending except: (a) a motion relating to adjournment or recess;”

**365.** `[C0871 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When it is desired to devote an entire meeting to a subject, or as much of _____ meeting as may be necessary, the matter can be made the special order for the meeting (as distinguished from a special order for the meeting;

   *Answer:* the — “When it is desired to devote an entire meeting to a subject, or as much of the meeting as may be necessary, the matter can be made the special order for the meeting (as distinguished from a special order for the meeting;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:57 — “When it is desired to devote an entire meeting to a subject, or as much of the meeting as may be necessary, the matter can be made the special order for the meeting (as distinguished from a special order for the meeting;”

**366.** `[C0871 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When it is desired to devote an entire meeting to a subject, or as much of _____ meeting as may be necessary, the matter can be made the special order for the meeting (as distinguished from a special order for the meeting;
   A. takes precedence of
   B. the Table
   C. order of business, agenda,
   D. the

   *Answer:* D) the. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:57 — “When it is desired to devote an entire meeting to a subject, or as much of the meeting as may be necessary, the matter can be made the special order for the meeting (as distinguished from a special order for the meeting;”

   B. Lay on the

**376.** `[C0880 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (However, if the _____ Question has already been ordered on one or more pending motions when the hour for a recess or adjournment arrives, there usually will be no objection to the chair’s putting them all to a vote in succession before declaring the assembly to be in recess or adjourned.)

   *Answer:* Previous — “(However, if the Previous Question has already been ordered on one or more pending motions when the hour for a recess or adjournment arrives, there usually will be no objection to the chair’s putting them all to a vote in succession before declaring the assembly to be in recess or adjourned.)”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:66 — “(However, if the Previous Question has already been ordered on one or more pending motions when the hour for a recess or adjournment arrives, there usually will be no objection to the chair’s putting them all to a vote in succession before declaring the assembly to be in recess or adjourned.)”

**377.** `[C0880 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (However, if the _____ Question has already been ordered on one or more pending motions when the hour for a recess or adjournment arrives, there usually will be no objection to the chair’s putting them all to a vote in succession before declaring the assembly to be in recess or adjourned.)
   A. executive committee
   B. Example
   C. part
   D. Previous

   *Answer:* D) Previous. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:66 — “(However, if the Previous Question has already been ordered on one or more pending motions when the hour for a recess or adjournment arrives, there usually will be no objection to the chair’s putting them all to a vote in succession before declaring the assembly to be in recess or adjourned.)”

**378.** `[C0880 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: (However, if the Previous _____ has already been ordered on one or more pending motions when the hour for a recess or adjournment arrives, there usually will be no objection to the chair’s putting them all to a vote in succession before declaring the assembly to be in recess or adjourned.)

   *Answer:* Question — “(However, if the Previous Question has already been ordered on one or more pending motions when the hour for a recess or adjournment arrives, there usually will be no objection to the chair’s putting them all to a vote in succession before declaring the assembly to be in recess or adjourned.)”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:66 — “(However, if the Previous Question has already been ordered on one or more pending motions when the hour for a recess or adjournment arrives, there usually will be no objection to the chair’s putting them all to a vote in succession before declaring the assembly to be in recess or adjourned.)”

**379.** `[C0880 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: (However, if the Previous _____ has already been ordered on one or more pending motions when the hour for a recess or adjournment arrives, there usually will be no objection to the chair’s putting them all to a vote in succession before declaring the assembly to be in recess or adjourned.)
   A. Question
   B. journal
   C. said
   D. decision

   *Answer:* A) Question.    *Answer:* Carrying Over Unfinished Business — “In the case of an adjournment, see Carrying Over Unfinished Business below.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:68 — “In the case of an adjournment, see Carrying Over Unfinished Business below.”

   A. Carrying Over Unfinished Business
