   D. main

   *Answer:* A) Carrying Over Unfinished Business. See RONR (12th ed.) §41:68 — “In the case of an adjournment, see Carrying Over Unfinished Business below.”

**382.** `[C0889 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: for example, although a member may have begun by debating a pending motion, he may conclude by moving any secondary motion, including the _____ (16), that is in order at the time.

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “for example, although a member may have begun by debating a pending motion, he may conclude by moving any secondary motion, including the Previous Question (16), that is in order at the time.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:5 — “for example, although a member may have begun by debating a pending motion, he may conclude by moving any secondary motion, including the Previous Question (16), that is in order at the time.”

**383.** `[C0889 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: for example, although a member may have begun by debating a pending motion, he may conclude by moving any secondary motion, including the _____ (16), that is in order at the time.
   A. ruling
   B. Objection to
   C. Previous Question
   D. Objection to the Consideration of a

   *Answer:* C) Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:5 — “for example, although a member may have begun by debating a pending motion, he may conclude by moving any secondary motion, including the Previous Question (16), that is in order at the time.”

**384.** `[C0896 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When an undebatable motion that can be amended is reconsidered for that announced purpose, however, the maker of the motion to _____ (37) is entitled to preference in recognition, as explained in the preceding paragraph.

   *Answer:* Reconsider — “When an undebatable motion that can be amended is reconsidered for that announced purpose, however, the maker of the motion to Reconsider (37) is entitled to preference in recognition, as explained in the preceding paragraph.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:12 — “When an undebatable motion that can be amended is reconsidered for that announced purpose, however, the maker of the motion to Reconsider (37) is entitled to preference in recognition, as explained in the preceding paragraph.”

**385.** `[C0896 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When an undebatable motion that can be amended is reconsidered for that announced purpose, however, the maker of the motion to _____ (37) is entitled to preference in recognition, as explained in the preceding paragraph.
   A. said
   B. Taking
   C. first paragraph
   D. Reconsider

   *Answer:* D) Reconsider. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:12 — “When an undebatable motion that can be amended is reconsidered for that announced purpose, however, the maker of the motion to Reconsider (37) is entitled to preference in recognition, as explained in the preceding paragraph.”

**386.** `[C0899 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If at any time the chair makes a mistake and assigns the floor to the wrong person when more than one member rose and addressed the chair promptly, a _____ can be raised.

   *Answer:* Point of Order — “If at any time the chair makes a mistake and assigns the floor to the wrong person when more than one member rose and addressed the chair promptly, a Point of Order can be raised.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:15 — “If at any time the chair makes a mistake and assigns the floor to the wrong person when more than one member rose and addressed the chair promptly, a Point of Order can be raised.”

**387.** `[C0899 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If at any time the chair makes a mistake and assigns the floor to the wrong person when more than one member rose and addressed the chair promptly, a _____ can be raised.
   A. Insert, or Add
   B. Point of Order
   C. question is pending
   D. adjourn

   *Answer:* B) Point of Order. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:15 — “If at any time the chair makes a mistake and assigns the floor to the wrong person when more than one member rose and addressed the chair promptly, a Point of Order can be raised.”

**388.** `[C0900 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the _____ or a motion to limit debate is moved, members who have been waiting in line cannot validly protest;

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “If the Previous Question or a motion to limit debate is moved, members who have been waiting in line cannot validly protest;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:16 — “If the Previous Question or a motion to limit debate is moved, members who have been waiting in line cannot validly protest;”

**389.** `[C0900 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the _____ or a motion to limit debate is moved, members who have been waiting in line cannot validly protest;
   A. Previous Question
   B. Only one question
   C. by article or section
   D. After the completion of new business

   *Answer:* A) Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §42:16 — “If the Previous Question or a motion to limit debate is moved, members who have been waiting in line cannot validly protest;”

**390.** `[C0907 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In a _____ assembly, this term applies to discussion on the merits of a pending question—that is, whether the proposal under consideration should, or should not, be agreed to.

   *Answer:* deliberative — “In a deliberative assembly, this term applies to discussion on the merits of a pending question—that is, whether the proposal under consideration should, or should not, be agreed to.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:1 — “In a deliberative assembly, this term applies to discussion on the merits of a pending question—that is, whether the proposal under consideration should, or should not, be agreed to.”

**391.** `[C0907 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In a _____ assembly, this term applies to discussion on the merits of a pending question—that is, whether the proposal under consideration should, or should not, be agreed to.
   A. deliberative
   B. secretary
   C. come before the meeting
   D. Subsidiary motions

   *Answer:* A) deliberative. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:1 — “In a deliberative assembly, this term applies to discussion on the merits of a pending question—that is, whether the proposal under consideration should, or should not, be agreed to.”

**392.** `[C0907 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ rightly understood, is an essential element in the making of rational decisions of consequence by intelligent people.

   *Answer:* Debate, — “Debate, rightly understood, is an essential element in the making of rational decisions of consequence by intelligent people.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:1 — “Debate, rightly understood, is an essential element in the making of rational decisions of consequence by intelligent people.”

**393.** `[C0907 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ rightly understood, is an essential element in the making of rational decisions of consequence by intelligent people.
   A. Resolved
   B. meetings
   C. Debate,
   D. vote in the negative

   *Answer:* C) Debate,. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:1 — “Debate, rightly understood, is an essential element in the making of rational decisions of consequence by intelligent people.”

**394.** `[C0911 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A member may both speak in debate and conclude by offering a secondary motion, which is a particular application of the principle that a member having been recognized for any legitimate purpose has the floor for _____ legitimate purposes.

   *Answer:* all — “A member may both speak in debate and conclude by offering a secondary motion, which is a particular application of the principle that a member having been recognized for any legitimate purpose has the floor for all legitimate purposes.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:5 — “A member may both speak in debate and conclude by offering a secondary motion, which is a particular application of the principle that a member having been recognized for any legitimate purpose has the floor for all legitimate purposes.”

**395.** `[C0911 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A member may both speak in debate and conclude by offering a secondary motion, which is a particular application of the principle that a member having been recognized for any legitimate purpose has the floor for _____ legitimate purposes.
   A. all
   B. even if it is
   C. For Information
   D. Order of Business

   *Answer:* A) all. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:5 — “A member may both speak in debate and conclude by offering a secondary motion, which is a particular application of the principle that a member having been recognized for any legitimate purpose has the floor for all legitimate purposes.”

**396.** `[C0911 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A member may both speak in debate and conclude by offering a secondary motion, which is a particular application of the principle that a member having been recognized for _____ legitimate purpose has the floor for all legitimate purposes.

   *Answer:* any — “A member may both speak in debate and conclude by offering a secondary motion, which is a particular application of the principle that a member having been recognized for any legitimate purpose has the floor for all legitimate purposes.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:5 — “A member may both speak in debate and conclude by offering a secondary motion, which is a particular application of the principle that a member having been recognized for any legitimate purpose has the floor for all legitimate purposes.”

**397.** `[C0911 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A member may both speak in debate and conclude by offering a secondary motion, which is a particular application of the principle that a member having been recognized for _____ legitimate purpose has the floor for all legitimate purposes.
   A. rank
   B. Particular Types of Committee Reports
   C. any
   D. consent calendar

   *Answer:* C) any. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:5 — “A member may both speak in debate and conclude by offering a secondary motion, which is a particular application of the principle that a member having been recognized for any legitimate purpose has the floor for all legitimate purposes.”

**398.** `[C0918 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Unless the assembly has a special rule providing otherwise, no member can speak more than twice to the same question on the same day—except that in the case of an _____ (24), only the presiding officer can speak twice (the second time at the close of the debate), all other members being limited to one speech.

   *Answer:* Appeal — “Unless the assembly has a special rule providing otherwise, no member can speak more than twice to the same question on the same day—except that in the case of an Appeal (24), only the presiding officer can speak twice (the second time at the close of the debate), all other members being limited to one speech.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:12 — “Unless the assembly has a special rule providing otherwise, no member can speak more than twice to the same question on the same day—except that in the case of an Appeal (24), only the presiding officer can speak twice (the second time at the close of the debate), all other members being limited to one speech.”

**399.** `[C0918 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Unless the assembly has a special rule providing otherwise, no member can speak more than twice to the same question on the same day—except that in the case of an _____ (24), only the presiding officer can speak twice (the second time at the close of the debate), all other members being limited to one speech.
   A. limiting
   B. to a Certain Time
   C. Appeal
   D. adopt,

   *Answer:* C) Appeal. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:12 — “Unless the assembly has a special rule providing otherwise, no member can speak more than twice to the same question on the same day—except that in the case of an Appeal (24), only the presiding officer can speak twice (the second time at the close of the debate), all other members being limited to one speech.”

**400.** `[C0922 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In a convention—where the limits of debate generally need to be stricter than in a local society—such a modification is usually adopted in the form of a _____ the convention (59), requiring a two-thirds vote in such a case.

   *Answer:* standing rule of — “In a convention—where the limits of debate generally need to be stricter than in a local society—such a modification is usually adopted in the form of a standing rule of the convention (59), requiring a two-thirds vote in such a case.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:16 — “In a convention—where the limits of debate generally need to be stricter than in a local society—such a modification is usually adopted in the form of a standing rule of the convention (59), requiring a two-thirds vote in such a case.”

**401.** `[C0922 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In a convention—where the limits of debate generally need to be stricter than in a local society—such a modification is usually adopted in the form of a _____ the convention (59), requiring a two-thirds vote in such a case.
   A. standing rule of
   B. obtain the floor
   C. obtained the floor
   D. recognized

   *Answer:* A) standing rule of. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:16 — “In a convention—where the limits of debate generally need to be stricter than in a local society—such a modification is usually adopted in the form of a standing rule of the convention (59), requiring a two-thirds vote in such a case.”

**402.** `[C0922 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In a convention—where the limits of debate generally need to be stricter than in a local society—such a modification is usually adopted in the form of a standing rule of _____ (59), requiring a two-thirds vote in such a case.

   *Answer:* the convention — “In a convention—where the limits of debate generally need to be stricter than in a local society—such a modification is usually adopted in the form of a standing rule of the convention (59), requiring a two-thirds vote in such a case.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:16 — “In a convention—where the limits of debate generally need to be stricter than in a local society—such a modification is usually adopted in the form of a standing rule of the convention (59), requiring a two-thirds vote in such a case.”

**403.** `[C0922 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In a convention—where the limits of debate generally need to be stricter than in a local society—such a modification is usually adopted in the form of a standing rule of _____ (59), requiring a two-thirds vote in such a case.
   A. offense
   B. primary amendment
   C. the convention
   D. postponed

   *Answer:* C) the convention. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:16 — “In a convention—where the limits of debate generally need to be stricter than in a local society—such a modification is usually adopted in the form of a standing rule of the convention (59), requiring a two-thirds vote in such a case.”

**404.** `[C0923 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If two thirds of those voting wish to close debate immediately, they can do so by adopting the motion for the _____ (16).

   *Answer:* Previous Question — “If two thirds of those voting wish to close debate immediately, they can do so by adopting the motion for the Previous Question (16).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:17 — “If two thirds of those voting wish to close debate immediately, they can do so by adopting the motion for the Previous Question (16).”

**405.** `[C0923 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If two thirds of those voting wish to close debate immediately, they can do so by adopting the motion for the _____ (16).
   A. whether to order
   B. Previous Question
   C. greater
   D. first paragraph

   *Answer:* B) Previous Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:17 — “If two thirds of those voting wish to close debate immediately, they can do so by adopting the motion for the Previous Question (16).”

**406.** `[C0923 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: While a debatable question is immediately pending, the allowed length or number of speeches can be reduced or increased, for that question only, by means of the subsidiary motion to _____ (15), adopted by a two-thirds vote.

   *Answer:* Limit or Extend Limits of Debate — “While a debatable question is immediately pending, the allowed length or number of speeches can be reduced or increased, for that question only, by means of the subsidiary motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate (15), adopted by a two-thirds vote.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:17 — “While a debatable question is immediately pending, the allowed length or number of speeches can be reduced or increased, for that question only, by means of the subsidiary motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate (15), adopted by a two-thirds vote.”

**407.** `[C0923 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: While a debatable question is immediately pending, the allowed length or number of speeches can be reduced or increased, for that question only, by means of the subsidiary motion to _____ (15), adopted by a two-thirds vote.
   A. Only one question
   B. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate
   C. Postpone to a Certain Time
   D. Viva-Voce Election,

   *Answer:* B) Limit or Extend Limits of Debate. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:17 — “While a debatable question is immediately pending, the allowed length or number of speeches can be reduced or increased, for that question only, by means of the subsidiary motion to Limit or Extend Limits of Debate (15), adopted by a two-thirds vote.”

**408.** `[C0934 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: During debate, during remarks by the presiding officer to the assembly, and during voting, no member should be permitted to _____ by whispering, walking across the floor, or in any other way.

   *Answer:* disturb the assembly — “During debate, during remarks by the presiding officer to the assembly, and during voting, no member should be permitted to disturb the assembly by whispering, walking across the floor, or in any other way.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:28 — “During debate, during remarks by the presiding officer to the assembly, and during voting, no member should be permitted to disturb the assembly by whispering, walking across the floor, or in any other way.”

**409.** `[C0934 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: During debate, during remarks by the presiding officer to the assembly, and during voting, no member should be permitted to _____ by whispering, walking across the floor, or in any other way.
   A. unanimous consent,
   B. preferential voting
   C. disturb the assembly
   D. Point of Information

   *Answer:* C) disturb the assembly. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:28 — “During debate, during remarks by the presiding officer to the assembly, and during voting, no member should be permitted to disturb the assembly by whispering, walking across the floor, or in any other way.”

**410.** `[C0935 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the presiding officer is a member of the society, he has— as an individual—the same _____ in debate as any other member;

   *Answer:* rights — “If the presiding officer is a member of the society, he has— as an individual—the same rights in debate as any other member;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:29 — “If the presiding officer is a member of the society, he has— as an individual—the same rights in debate as any other member;”

**411.** `[C0935 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the presiding officer is a member of the society, he has— as an individual—the same _____ in debate as any other member;
   A. immediately
   B. rights
   C. in some way
   D. Fix the Time to Which to Adjourn

   *Answer:* B) rights. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:29 — “If the presiding officer is a member of the society, he has— as an individual—the same rights in debate as any other member;”

**412.** `[C0940 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In general, for a member to speak when no question is pending, without promptly leading to a motion, implies an unusual circumstance and requires permission of the _____.

   *Answer:* assembly — “In general, for a member to speak when no question is pending, without promptly leading to a motion, implies an unusual circumstance and requires permission of the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:34 — “In general, for a member to speak when no question is pending, without promptly leading to a motion, implies an unusual circumstance and requires permission of the assembly.”

**413.** `[C0940 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In general, for a member to speak when no question is pending, without promptly leading to a motion, implies an unusual circumstance and requires permission of the _____.
   A. Improper Amendments
   B. recognized
   C. voce
   D. assembly

   *Answer:* D) assembly. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:34 — “In general, for a member to speak when no question is pending, without promptly leading to a motion, implies an unusual circumstance and requires permission of the assembly.”

**414.** `[C0940 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In general, for a member to speak when no question is pending, without promptly leading to a motion, implies an unusual circumstance and requires permission _____ assembly.

   *Answer:* of the — “In general, for a member to speak when no question is pending, without promptly leading to a motion, implies an unusual circumstance and requires permission of the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:34 — “In general, for a member to speak when no question is pending, without promptly leading to a motion, implies an unusual circumstance and requires permission of the assembly.”

**415.** `[C0940 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In general, for a member to speak when no question is pending, without promptly leading to a motion, implies an unusual circumstance and requires permission _____ assembly.
   A. Division of a

   C. question of privilege
   D. of the

   *Answer:* D) of the. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:34 — “In general, for a member to speak when no question is pending, without promptly leading to a motion, implies an unusual circumstance and requires permission of the assembly.”

**416.** `[C0944 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____ such as a resignation, may require some discussion for its proper decision, and for this reason it is debatable.

   *Answer:* Request to Be Excused from a Duty, — “A Request to Be Excused from a Duty, such as a resignation, may require some discussion for its proper decision, and for this reason it is debatable.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:39 — “A Request to Be Excused from a Duty, such as a resignation, may require some discussion for its proper decision, and for this reason it is debatable.”

**417.** `[C0944 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A _____ such as a resignation, may require some discussion for its proper decision, and for this reason it is debatable.
   A. substitute
   B. Request to Be Excused from a Duty,
   C. made by secondary amendment
   D. in the brief interval

   *Answer:* B) Request to Be Excused from a Duty,. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:39 — “A Request to Be Excused from a Duty, such as a resignation, may require some discussion for its proper decision, and for this reason it is debatable.”

**418.** `[C0944 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the case of an _____ that relates to indecorum, the rules of debate, or the priority of business, it is assumed that debate would be a hindrance to business, as it would be if the appeal were made when an undebatable question is immediately pending or involved in the appeal.

   *Answer:* Appeal — “In the case of an Appeal that relates to indecorum, the rules of debate, or the priority of business, it is assumed that debate would be a hindrance to business, as it would be if the appeal were made when an undebatable question is immediately pending or involved in the appeal.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:39 — “In the case of an Appeal that relates to indecorum, the rules of debate, or the priority of business, it is assumed that debate would be a hindrance to business, as it would be if the appeal were made when an undebatable question is immediately pending or involved in the appeal.”

**419.** `[C0944 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the case of an _____ that relates to indecorum, the rules of debate, or the priority of business, it is assumed that debate would be a hindrance to business, as it would be if the appeal were made when an undebatable question is immediately pending or involved in the appeal.
   A. strike out
   B. Appeal
   C. rejected
   D. secretary

   *Answer:* B) Appeal. See RONR (12th ed.) §43:39 — “In the case of an Appeal that relates to indecorum, the rules of debate, or the priority of business, it is assumed that debate would be a hindrance to business, as it would be if the appeal were made when an undebatable question is immediately pending or involved in the appeal.”

See RONR (12th ed.) §47:5 — “The presiding officer of an assembly ordinarily is called the chairman when no special title has been assigned, or in a body not permanently organized, such as a mass meeting (53).”

   B. confirm
   C. the chair
   D. all

   *Answer:* C) the chair. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:5 — “The presiding officer of an assembly ordinarily is called the chairman when no special title has been assigned, or in a body not permanently organized, such as a mass meeting (53).”

See RONR (12th ed.) §47:5 — “In organized societies the presiding officer’s title is usually prescribed by the bylaws, that of president being most common.”

   D. Retaking a Vote

   *Answer:* C) president. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:5 — “In organized societies the presiding officer’s title is usually prescribed by the bylaws, that of president being most common.”

**425.** `[C1092 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A good first step is to read through _____ In Brief—a concise introductory guide prepared by the authors of this book and fully compatible with it—paying particular attention to Chapter 15 (“President or Vice-President”).

See RONR (12th ed.) §47:15 — “A good first step is to read through Robert’s Rules of Order Newly Revised In Brief—a concise introductory guide prepared by the authors of this book and fully compatible with it—paying particular attention to Chapter 15 (“President or Vice-President”).”

   C. standard descriptive characteristics
   D. from the primary amendment

   *Answer:* A) Robert’s Rules of Order Newly Revised. **429.** `[C1094 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The chair should take special care to make sure that the members always understand what is the immediately pending business—the _____ question to be voted on the next time a vote is taken.

   *Answer:* exact — “The chair should take special care to make sure that the members always understand what is the immediately pending business—the exact question to be voted on the next time a vote is taken.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:17 — “The chair should take special care to make sure that the members always understand what is the immediately pending business—the exact question to be voted on the next time a vote is taken.”

**430.** `[C1094 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The chair should take special care to make sure that the members always understand what is the immediately pending business—the _____ question to be voted on the next time a vote is taken.
   A. division
   B. exact
   C. Again
   D. approve

   *Answer:* B) exact. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:17 — “The chair should take special care to make sure that the members always understand what is the immediately pending business—the exact question to be voted on the next time a vote is taken.”

**431.** `[C1098 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Some organizations desire to elect their president one entire term in advance, and in such cases, during the term following the election, the person chosen is called the _____.

   *Answer:* D) president-elect. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:21 — “Some organizations desire to elect their president one entire term in advance, and in such cases, during the term following the election, the person chosen is called the president-elect.”

**440.** `[C1115 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In such cases some organizations have, in addition to the treasurer, a _____—an officer whose usual duties are to bill members for their dues and to receive payment of them, to maintain a ledger of each member’s account, and to turn over to the treasurer and obtain his receipt for moneys received.

   *Answer:* financial secretary — “In such cases some organizations have, in addition to the treasurer, a financial secretary—an officer whose usual duties are to bill members for their dues and to receive payment of them, to maintain a ledger of each member’s account, and to turn over to the treasurer and obtain his receipt for moneys received.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:39 — “In such cases some organizations have, in addition to the treasurer, a financial secretary—an officer whose usual duties are to bill members for their dues and to receive payment of them, to maintain a ledger of each member’s account, and to turn over to the treasurer and obtain his receipt for moneys received.”

**441.** `[C1115 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In such cases some organizations have, in addition to the treasurer, a _____—an officer whose usual duties are to bill members for their dues and to receive payment of them, to maintain a ledger of each member’s account, and to turn over to the treasurer and obtain his receipt for moneys received.
   A. chairman pro tem
   B. financial secretary
   C. Methods of Voting
   D. within

   *Answer:* B) financial secretary. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:39 — “In such cases some organizations have, in addition to the treasurer, a financial secretary—an officer whose usual duties are to bill members for their dues and to receive payment of them, to maintain a ledger of each member’s account, and to turn over to the treasurer and obtain his receipt for moneys received.”

**442.** `[C1119 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The term _____ or executive director, is usually applied to a salaried officer who devotes full time to the position of administrative officer and general manager of an organization, especially at the national, regional, or state level;

   *Answer:* executive secretary, — “The term executive secretary, or executive director, is usually applied to a salaried officer who devotes full time to the position of administrative officer and general manager of an organization, especially at the national, regional, or state level;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:43 — “The term executive secretary, or executive director, is usually applied to a salaried officer who devotes full time to the position of administrative officer and general manager of an organization, especially at the national, regional, or state level;”

**443.** `[C1119 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The term _____ or executive director, is usually applied to a salaried officer who devotes full time to the position of administrative officer and general manager of an organization, especially at the national, regional, or state level;
   A. preference in being recognized
   B. and Example: 1(a), 1(b)
   C. the assembly or any of its members
   D. executive secretary,

   *Answer:* D) executive secretary,. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:43 — “The term executive secretary, or executive director, is usually applied to a salaried officer who devotes full time to the position of administrative officer and general manager of an organization, especially at the national, regional, or state level;”

**444.** `[C1119 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The term executive secretary, or _____ is usually applied to a salaried officer who devotes full time to the position of administrative officer and general manager of an organization, especially at the national, regional, or state level;

   *Answer:* executive director, — “The term executive secretary, or executive director, is usually applied to a salaried officer who devotes full time to the position of administrative officer and general manager of an organization, especially at the national, regional, or state level;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:43 — “The term executive secretary, or executive director, is usually applied to a salaried officer who devotes full time to the position of administrative officer and general manager of an organization, especially at the national, regional, or state level;”

**445.** `[C1119 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The term executive secretary, or _____ is usually applied to a salaried officer who devotes full time to the position of administrative officer and general manager of an organization, especially at the national, regional, or state level;
   A. executive director,
   B. whether to order
   C. are
   D. Limit or Extend Limits of

   *Answer:* A) executive director,. See RONR (12th ed.) §47:43 — “The term executive secretary, or executive director, is usually applied to a salaried officer who devotes full time to the position of administrative officer and general manager of an organization, especially at the national, regional, or state level;”

**446.** `[C1135 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The official record of the proceedings of a deliberative assembly is usually called the minutes, or sometimes— particularly in legislative bodies—the _____.

   *Answer:* journal — “The official record of the proceedings of a deliberative assembly is usually called the minutes, or sometimes— particularly in legislative bodies—the journal.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:1 — “The official record of the proceedings of a deliberative assembly is usually called the minutes, or sometimes— particularly in legislative bodies—the journal.”

**447.** `[C1135 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The official record of the proceedings of a deliberative assembly is usually called the minutes, or sometimes— particularly in legislative bodies—the _____.
   A. beyond
   B. rules
   C. Special Orders
   D. journal

   *Answer:* D) journal. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:1 — “The official record of the proceedings of a deliberative assembly is usually called the minutes, or sometimes— particularly in legislative bodies—the journal.”

**448.** `[C1135 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The official record of the proceedings of a deliberative assembly is usually called the _____ or sometimes— particularly in legislative bodies—the journal.

   *Answer:* minutes, — “The official record of the proceedings of a deliberative assembly is usually called the minutes, or sometimes— particularly in legislative bodies—the journal.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:1 — “The official record of the proceedings of a deliberative assembly is usually called the minutes, or sometimes— particularly in legislative bodies—the journal.”

**449.** `[C1135 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The official record of the proceedings of a deliberative assembly is usually called the _____ or sometimes— particularly in legislative bodies—the journal.
   A. minutes,
   B. debatable
   C. consent
   D. that are pending

   *Answer:* A) minutes,. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:1 — “The official record of the proceedings of a deliberative assembly is usually called the minutes, or sometimes— particularly in legislative bodies—the journal.”

**450.** `[C1136 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In an ordinary society, the minutes should contain mainly a record of what was done at the meeting, not what was _____ by the members.

   *Answer:* said — “In an ordinary society, the minutes should contain mainly a record of what was done at the meeting, not what was said by the members.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:2 — “In an ordinary society, the minutes should contain mainly a record of what was done at the meeting, not what was said by the members.”

**451.** `[C1136 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In an ordinary society, the minutes should contain mainly a record of what was done at the meeting, not what was _____ by the members.
   A. Adjourned Meeting
   B. before
   C. said
   D. the Time to Which to Adjourn

   *Answer:* C) said. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:2 — “In an ordinary society, the minutes should contain mainly a record of what was done at the meeting, not what was said by the members.”

**452.** `[C1136 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In an ordinary society, the minutes should contain mainly a record of what was _____ at the meeting, not what was said by the members.

   *Answer:* done — “In an ordinary society, the minutes should contain mainly a record of what was done at the meeting, not what was said by the members.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:2 — “In an ordinary society, the minutes should contain mainly a record of what was done at the meeting, not what was said by the members.”

**453.** `[C1136 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In an ordinary society, the minutes should contain mainly a record of what was _____ at the meeting, not what was said by the members.
   A. Something Previously Adopted
   B. Subsidiary motions
   C. done
   D. secondary amendment

   *Answer:* C) done. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:2 — “In an ordinary society, the minutes should contain mainly a record of what was done at the meeting, not what was said by the members.”

**454.** `[C1138 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ of the minutes should contain the following information (which need not, however, be divided into numbered or separated items directly corresponding to those below):

   *Answer:* first paragraph — “The first paragraph of the minutes should contain the following information (which need not, however, be divided into numbered or separated items directly corresponding to those below):”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:4 — “The first paragraph of the minutes should contain the following information (which need not, however, be divided into numbered or separated items directly corresponding to those below):”

**455.** `[C1138 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ of the minutes should contain the following information (which need not, however, be divided into numbered or separated items directly corresponding to those below):
   A. first paragraph
   B. Rescind
   C. question again before the assembly
   D. the Minutes

   *Answer:* A) first paragraph. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:4 — “The first paragraph of the minutes should contain the following information (which need not, however, be divided into numbered or separated items directly corresponding to those below):”

**456.** `[C1140 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The words _____—although occasionally used —represent an older practice that is not essential in signing the minutes.

   *Answer:* Respectfully submitted — “The words Respectfully submitted—although occasionally used —represent an older practice that is not essential in signing the minutes.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:7 — “The words Respectfully submitted—although occasionally used —represent an older practice that is not essential in signing the minutes.”

**457.** `[C1140 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The words _____—although occasionally used —represent an older practice that is not essential in signing the minutes.
   A. Respectfully submitted
   B. the Day
   C. main
   D. Taking

   *Answer:* A) Respectfully submitted. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:7 — “The words Respectfully submitted—although occasionally used —represent an older practice that is not essential in signing the minutes.”

**458.** `[C1144 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the assembly does not wish to carry out the reading and approval of the minutes at the regular time, it may, by majority vote without debate, “dispense with the reading of the minutes.” The minutes can then be taken up by majority vote without debate at any later time during the meeting while no business is pending, under the same rules as those governing _____ (34).

   *Answer:* Take from the Table — “If the assembly does not wish to carry out the reading and approval of the minutes at the regular time, it may, by majority vote without debate, “dispense with the reading of the minutes.” The minutes can then be taken up by majority vote without debate at any later time during the meeting while no business is pending, under the same rules as those governing Take from the Table (34).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:11 — “If the assembly does not wish to carry out the reading and approval of the minutes at the regular time, it may, by majority vote without debate, “dispense with the reading of the minutes.” The minutes can then be taken up by majority vote without debate at any later time during the meeting while no business is pending, under the same rules as those governing Take from the Table (34).”

**459.** `[C1144 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the assembly does not wish to carry out the reading and approval of the minutes at the regular time, it may, by majority vote without debate, “dispense with the reading of the minutes.” The minutes can then be taken up by majority vote without debate at any later time during the meeting while no business is pending, under the same rules as those governing _____ (34).
   A. mass meeting
   B. adjourned meeting
   C. Take from the Table
   D. ordinary committees

   *Answer:* C) Take from the Table. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:11 — “If the assembly does not wish to carry out the reading and approval of the minutes at the regular time, it may, by majority vote without debate, “dispense with the reading of the minutes.” The minutes can then be taken up by majority vote without debate at any later time during the meeting while no business is pending, under the same rules as those governing Take from the Table (34).”

**460.** `[C1147 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the minutes are approved, the word _____, with the secretary’s initials (or the signature of the chairman of the approving committee) and the date, should be written below them.

   *Answer:* Approved — “When the minutes are approved, the word Approved, with the secretary’s initials (or the signature of the chairman of the approving committee) and the date, should be written below them.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:14 — “When the minutes are approved, the word Approved, with the secretary’s initials (or the signature of the chairman of the approving committee) and the date, should be written below them.”

**461.** `[C1147 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the minutes are approved, the word _____, with the secretary’s initials (or the signature of the chairman of the approving committee) and the date, should be written below them.
   A. rules of order
   B. speak in debate
   C. Approved
   D. Order

   *Answer:* C) Approved. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:14 — “When the minutes are approved, the word Approved, with the secretary’s initials (or the signature of the chairman of the approving committee) and the date, should be written below them.”

**462.** `[C1148 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the existence of an error or material omission in the minutes becomes reasonably established after their approval —even many years later—the minutes can then be corrected by means of the motion to Amend Something Previously _____ (35), which requires a two-thirds vote, or a majority vote with notice, or the vote of a majority of the entire membership, or unanimous consent.

   *Answer:* Adopted — “If the existence of an error or material omission in the minutes becomes reasonably established after their approval —even many years later—the minutes can then be corrected by means of the motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), which requires a two-thirds vote, or a majority vote with notice, or the vote of a majority of the entire membership, or unanimous consent.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:15 — “If the existence of an error or material omission in the minutes becomes reasonably established after their approval —even many years later—the minutes can then be corrected by means of the motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), which requires a two-thirds vote, or a majority vote with notice, or the vote of a majority of the entire membership, or unanimous consent.”

**463.** `[C1148 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the existence of an error or material omission in the minutes becomes reasonably established after their approval —even many years later—the minutes can then be corrected by means of the motion to Amend Something Previously _____ (35), which requires a two-thirds vote, or a majority vote with notice, or the vote of a majority of the entire membership, or unanimous consent.
   A. Adopted
   B. variations
   C. said
   D. Executive Board

   *Answer:* A) Adopted. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:15 — “If the existence of an error or material omission in the minutes becomes reasonably established after their approval —even many years later—the minutes can then be corrected by means of the motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), which requires a two-thirds vote, or a majority vote with notice, or the vote of a majority of the entire membership, or unanimous consent.”

**464.** `[C1148 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the existence of an error or material omission in the minutes becomes reasonably established after their approval —even many years later—the minutes can then be corrected by means of the motion to _____ Adopted (35), which requires a two-thirds vote, or a majority vote with notice, or the vote of a majority of the entire membership, or unanimous consent.

   *Answer:* Amend Something Previously — “If the existence of an error or material omission in the minutes becomes reasonably established after their approval —even many years later—the minutes can then be corrected by means of the motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), which requires a two-thirds vote, or a majority vote with notice, or the vote of a majority of the entire membership, or unanimous consent.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:15 — “If the existence of an error or material omission in the minutes becomes reasonably established after their approval —even many years later—the minutes can then be corrected by means of the motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), which requires a two-thirds vote, or a majority vote with notice, or the vote of a majority of the entire membership, or unanimous consent.”

**465.** `[C1148 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the existence of an error or material omission in the minutes becomes reasonably established after their approval —even many years later—the minutes can then be corrected by means of the motion to _____ Adopted (35), which requires a two-thirds vote, or a majority vote with notice, or the vote of a majority of the entire membership, or unanimous consent.
   A. stand at ease
   B. Amend Something Previously
   C. Postpone Indefinitely
   D. Optional Headings

   *Answer:* B) Amend Something Previously. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:15 — “If the existence of an error or material omission in the minutes becomes reasonably established after their approval —even many years later—the minutes can then be corrected by means of the motion to Amend Something Previously Adopted (35), which requires a two-thirds vote, or a majority vote with notice, or the vote of a majority of the entire membership, or unanimous consent.”

**466.** `[C1152 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Motions to adopt or implement any recommendations should be made from the floor by a member _____.

   *Answer:* other than the reporting officer — “Motions to adopt or implement any recommendations should be made from the floor by a member other than the reporting officer.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:19 — “Motions to adopt or implement any recommendations should be made from the floor by a member other than the reporting officer.”

**467.** `[C1152 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Motions to adopt or implement any recommendations should be made from the floor by a member _____.
   A. calling the meeting to order
   B. other than the reporting officer
   C. undebatable
   D. immediately

   *Answer:* B) other than the reporting officer. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:19 — “Motions to adopt or implement any recommendations should be made from the floor by a member other than the reporting officer.”

**468.** `[C1158 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the _____ report consists only of an endorsement on the financial report —to the effect that it has been found correct, as shown in the model above—the treasurer can simply read out this certification as he concludes the presentation of his own report.

   *Answer:* auditors’ — “If the auditors’ report consists only of an endorsement on the financial report —to the effect that it has been found correct, as shown in the model above—the treasurer can simply read out this certification as he concludes the presentation of his own report.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:25 — “If the auditors’ report consists only of an endorsement on the financial report —to the effect that it has been found correct, as shown in the model above—the treasurer can simply read out this certification as he concludes the presentation of his own report.”

**469.** `[C1158 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If the _____ report consists only of an endorsement on the financial report —to the effect that it has been found correct, as shown in the model above—the treasurer can simply read out this certification as he concludes the presentation of his own report.
   A. Form and Example
   B. Limit or Extend Limits of
   C. auditors’
   D. in the office of president

   *Answer:* C) auditors’. See RONR (12th ed.) §48:25 — “If the auditors’ report consists only of an endorsement on the financial report —to the effect that it has been found correct, as shown in the model above—the treasurer can simply read out this certification as he concludes the presentation of his own report.”

---

## Domain 5: Voting, Nominations, and Elections

**1.** `[C0948 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____—when the term is unqualified—means at least two thirds of the votes cast by persons entitled to vote, excluding blanks or abstentions, at a regular or properly called meeting.

   *Answer:* two-thirds vote — “A two-thirds vote—when the term is unqualified—means at least two thirds of the votes cast by persons entitled to vote, excluding blanks or abstentions, at a regular or properly called meeting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:3 — “A two-thirds vote—when the term is unqualified—means at least two thirds of the votes cast by persons entitled to vote, excluding blanks or abstentions, at a regular or properly called meeting.”

**2.** `[C0948 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A _____—when the term is unqualified—means at least two thirds of the votes cast by persons entitled to vote, excluding blanks or abstentions, at a regular or properly called meeting.
   A. two-thirds vote
   B. Time to Which to Adjourn
   C. standing rule of
   D. Request for Information,

   *Answer:* A) two-thirds vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:3 — “A two-thirds vote—when the term is unqualified—means at least two thirds of the votes cast by persons entitled to vote, excluding blanks or abstentions, at a regular or properly called meeting.”

**3.** `[C0951 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In an assembly that has no special rule permitting a small fraction (that is, a specified fraction somewhat less than one third) of the voters to require a twothirds vote to be counted, the chair, in judging whether to obtain a count of the vote at his own instance, must be particularly c_____ful to leave no room for anyone to doubt the result in cases where he finds that there are two thirds on the side that thereby prevails.

   *Answer:* are — “In an assembly that has no special rule permitting a small fraction (that is, a specified fraction somewhat less than one third) of the voters to require a twothirds vote to be counted, the chair, in judging whether to obtain a count of the vote at his own instance, must be particularly careful to leave no room for anyone to doubt the result in cases where he finds that there are two thirds on the side that thereby prevails.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:6 — “In an assembly that has no special rule permitting a small fraction (that is, a specified fraction somewhat less than one third) of the voters to require a twothirds vote to be counted, the chair, in judging whether to obtain a count of the vote at his own instance, must be particularly careful to leave no room for anyone to doubt the result in cases where he finds that there are two thirds on the side that thereby prevails.”

**4.** `[C0951 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In an assembly that has no special rule permitting a small fraction (that is, a specified fraction somewhat less than one third) of the voters to require a twothirds vote to be counted, the chair, in judging whether to obtain a count of the vote at his own instance, must be particularly c_____ful to leave no room for anyone to doubt the result in cases where he finds that there are two thirds on the side that thereby prevails.
   A. are
   B. quorum
   C. speed up
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* A) are. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:6 — “In an assembly that has no special rule permitting a small fraction (that is, a specified fraction somewhat less than one third) of the voters to require a twothirds vote to be counted, the chair, in judging whether to obtain a count of the vote at his own instance, must be particularly careful to leave no room for anyone to doubt the result in cases where he finds that there are two thirds on the side that thereby prevails.”

**5.** `[C0952 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and (2) the set of members to which the proportion applies—which (a) when not stated, is always the number of members _____, but (b) can be specified by rule as the number of members present, the total membership, or some other grouping.

   *Answer:* present and voting — “and (2) the set of members to which the proportion applies—which (a) when not stated, is always the number of members present and voting, but (b) can be specified by rule as the number of members present, the total membership, or some other grouping.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:7 — “and (2) the set of members to which the proportion applies—which (a) when not stated, is always the number of members present and voting, but (b) can be specified by rule as the number of members present, the total membership, or some other grouping.”

**6.** `[C0952 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: and (2) the set of members to which the proportion applies—which (a) when not stated, is always the number of members _____, but (b) can be specified by rule as the number of members present, the total membership, or some other grouping.
   A. variations
   B. the Consideration of a Question
   C. present and voting
   D. Bylaws

   *Answer:* C) present and voting. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:7 — “and (2) the set of members to which the proportion applies—which (a) when not stated, is always the number of members present and voting, but (b) can be specified by rule as the number of members present, the total membership, or some other grouping.”

**7.** `[C0955 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Whatever voting basis is used, it is also possible to include a requirement of _____ for specified types of action.

   *Answer:* previous notice — “Whatever voting basis is used, it is also possible to include a requirement of previous notice for specified types of action.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:10 — “Whatever voting basis is used, it is also possible to include a requirement of previous notice for specified types of action.”

**8.** `[C0955 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Whatever voting basis is used, it is also possible to include a requirement of _____ for specified types of action.
   A. motion
   B. society,
   C. previous notice
   D. After the completion of new business

   *Answer:* C) previous notice. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:10 — “Whatever voting basis is used, it is also possible to include a requirement of previous notice for specified types of action.”

**9.** `[C0956 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____ is the largest number of votes to be given any candidate or proposition when three or more choices are possible;

   *Answer:* plurality vote — “A plurality vote is the largest number of votes to be given any candidate or proposition when three or more choices are possible;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:11 — “A plurality vote is the largest number of votes to be given any candidate or proposition when three or more choices are possible;”

**10.** `[C0956 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A _____ is the largest number of votes to be given any candidate or proposition when three or more choices are possible;
   A. Refer
   B. Standing rules
   C. plurality vote
   D. standard descriptive characteristics

   *Answer:* C) plurality vote. See RONR (12th ed.) §44:11 — “A plurality vote is the largest number of votes to be given any candidate or proposition when three or more choices are possible;”

**11.** `[C0969 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Also described in Chapter II is the procedure of action by _____.

   *Answer:* unanimous consent — “Also described in Chapter II is the procedure of action by unanimous consent.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:12 — “Also described in Chapter II is the procedure of action by unanimous consent.”

**12.** `[C0969 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Also described in Chapter II is the procedure of action by _____.
   A. Form
   B. extend the limits
   C. For Information
   D. unanimous consent

   *Answer:* D) unanimous consent. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:12 — “Also described in Chapter II is the procedure of action by unanimous consent.”

**13.** `[C0970 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A characteristic that the three methods of voting listed above have in common is that in each case the chair calls first for those voting in the affirmative to indicate the fact in a specified manner (“say _____” “rise,” or “raise the right hand”), after which he calls for the negative vote, then judges and declares which side prevails.

   *Answer:* aye, — “A characteristic that the three methods of voting listed above have in common is that in each case the chair calls first for those voting in the affirmative to indicate the fact in a specified manner (“say aye,” “rise,” or “raise the right hand”), after which he calls for the negative vote, then judges and declares which side prevails.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:13 — “A characteristic that the three methods of voting listed above have in common is that in each case the chair calls first for those voting in the affirmative to indicate the fact in a specified manner (“say aye,” “rise,” or “raise the right hand”), after which he calls for the negative vote, then judges and declares which side prevails.”

**14.** `[C0970 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A characteristic that the three methods of voting listed above have in common is that in each case the chair calls first for those voting in the affirmative to indicate the fact in a specified manner (“say _____” “rise,” or “raise the right hand”), after which he calls for the negative vote, then judges and declares which side prevails.
   A. Any Other Privilege
   B. does
   C. Indefinitely
   D. aye,

   *Answer:* D) aye,. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:13 — “A characteristic that the three methods of voting listed above have in common is that in each case the chair calls first for those voting in the affirmative to indicate the fact in a specified manner (“say aye,” “rise,” or “raise the right hand”), after which he calls for the negative vote, then judges and declares which side prevails.”

**15.** `[C0971 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If this motion is seconded, the question as to whether a count shall be ordered is voted on by voice vote, or by an _____ rising vote or show of hands.

   *Answer:* uncounted — “If this motion is seconded, the question as to whether a count shall be ordered is voted on by voice vote, or by an uncounted rising vote or show of hands.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:14 — “If this motion is seconded, the question as to whether a count shall be ordered is voted on by voice vote, or by an uncounted rising vote or show of hands.”

**16.** `[C0971 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If this motion is seconded, the question as to whether a count shall be ordered is voted on by voice vote, or by an _____ rising vote or show of hands.
   A. no more than a
   B. board of directors, board
   C. uncounted
   D. Recess

   *Answer:* C) uncounted. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:14 — “If this motion is seconded, the question as to whether a count shall be ordered is voted on by voice vote, or by an uncounted rising vote or show of hands.”

**17.** `[C0971 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Any member, by demanding a _____ (29), can require a voice vote or a vote by show of hands to be retaken as a rising vote —but no individual member can compel it to be counted.

   *Answer:* Division — “Any member, by demanding a Division (29), can require a voice vote or a vote by show of hands to be retaken as a rising vote —but no individual member can compel it to be counted.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:14 — “Any member, by demanding a Division (29), can require a voice vote or a vote by show of hands to be retaken as a rising vote —but no individual member can compel it to be counted.”

**18.** `[C0971 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Any member, by demanding a _____ (29), can require a voice vote or a vote by show of hands to be retaken as a rising vote —but no individual member can compel it to be counted.
   A. assuming
   B. Division
   C. standing rules
   D. raising

   *Answer:* B) Division. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:14 — “Any member, by demanding a Division (29), can require a voice vote or a vote by show of hands to be retaken as a rising vote —but no individual member can compel it to be counted.”

**19.** `[C0975 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Voting by _____ (also known as secret ballot) is used when secrecy of the members’ votes is desired.

   *Answer:* ballot — “Voting by ballot (also known as secret ballot) is used when secrecy of the members’ votes is desired.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:18 — “Voting by ballot (also known as secret ballot) is used when secrecy of the members’ votes is desired.”

**20.** `[C0975 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Voting by _____ (also known as secret ballot) is used when secrecy of the members’ votes is desired.
   A. to a Certain Time
   B. made by secondary amendment
   C. ballot
   D. the one

   *Answer:* C) ballot. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:18 — “Voting by ballot (also known as secret ballot) is used when secrecy of the members’ votes is desired.”

**21.** `[C0975 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Voting by ballot (also known as _____ ballot) is used when secrecy of the members’ votes is desired.

   *Answer:* secret — “Voting by ballot (also known as secret ballot) is used when secrecy of the members’ votes is desired.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:18 — “Voting by ballot (also known as secret ballot) is used when secrecy of the members’ votes is desired.”

**22.** `[C0975 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Voting by ballot (also known as _____ ballot) is used when secrecy of the members’ votes is desired.
   A. secret
   B. execution
   C. and Example
   D. recorder,

   *Answer:* A) secret. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:18 — “Voting by ballot (also known as secret ballot) is used when secrecy of the members’ votes is desired.”

**23.** `[C0975 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Voting by _____ (also known as secret ballot) is used when secrecy of the members’ votes is desired.

   *Answer:* ballot — “Voting by ballot (also known as secret ballot) is used when secrecy of the members’ votes is desired.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:18 — “Voting by ballot (also known as secret ballot) is used when secrecy of the members’ votes is desired.”

**24.** `[C0989 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Unintelligible ballots or ballots cast for an unidentifiable or ineligible candidate are treated instead as _____—that is, they are counted as votes cast but are not credited to any candidate or choice.

   *Answer:* illegal votes — “Unintelligible ballots or ballots cast for an unidentifiable or ineligible candidate are treated instead as illegal votes—that is, they are counted as votes cast but are not credited to any candidate or choice.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:32 — “Unintelligible ballots or ballots cast for an unidentifiable or ineligible candidate are treated instead as illegal votes—that is, they are counted as votes cast but are not credited to any candidate or choice.”

**25.** `[C0989 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Unintelligible ballots or ballots cast for an unidentifiable or ineligible candidate are treated instead as _____—that is, they are counted as votes cast but are not credited to any candidate or choice.
   A. in some way
   B. Debate
   C. sustaining
   D. illegal votes

   *Answer:* D) illegal votes. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:32 — “Unintelligible ballots or ballots cast for an unidentifiable or ineligible candidate are treated instead as illegal votes—that is, they are counted as votes cast but are not credited to any candidate or choice.”

**26.** `[C0999 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Taking a vote by roll call (or by _____ as it is also called) has the effect of placing on the record how each member, or sometimes each delegation, votes;

   *Answer:* yeas and nays, — “Taking a vote by roll call (or by yeas and nays, as it is also called) has the effect of placing on the record how each member, or sometimes each delegation, votes;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:45 — “Taking a vote by roll call (or by yeas and nays, as it is also called) has the effect of placing on the record how each member, or sometimes each delegation, votes;”

**27.** `[C0999 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Taking a vote by roll call (or by _____ as it is also called) has the effect of placing on the record how each member, or sometimes each delegation, votes;
   A. ratify
   B. are
   C. strike out
   D. yeas and nays,

   *Answer:* D) yeas and nays,. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:45 — “Taking a vote by roll call (or by yeas and nays, as it is also called) has the effect of placing on the record how each member, or sometimes each delegation, votes;”

**28.** `[C0999 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Taking a vote by _____ (or by yeas and nays, as it is also called) has the effect of placing on the record how each member, or sometimes each delegation, votes;

   *Answer:* roll call — “Taking a vote by roll call (or by yeas and nays, as it is also called) has the effect of placing on the record how each member, or sometimes each delegation, votes;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:45 — “Taking a vote by roll call (or by yeas and nays, as it is also called) has the effect of placing on the record how each member, or sometimes each delegation, votes;”

**29.** `[C0999 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Taking a vote by _____ (or by yeas and nays, as it is also called) has the effect of placing on the record how each member, or sometimes each delegation, votes;
   A. right
   B. Insert, or Add
   C. roll call
   D. rising

   *Answer:* C) roll call. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:45 — “Taking a vote by roll call (or by yeas and nays, as it is also called) has the effect of placing on the record how each member, or sometimes each delegation, votes;”

**30.** `[C1000 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is in order, as one of the _____ however, to move “that a signed ballot be taken by tellers”;

   *Answer:* Motions Relating to Methods of Voting, — “It is in order, as one of the Motions Relating to Methods of Voting, however, to move “that a signed ballot be taken by tellers”;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:46 — “It is in order, as one of the Motions Relating to Methods of Voting, however, to move “that a signed ballot be taken by tellers”;”

**31.** `[C1000 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is in order, as one of the _____ however, to move “that a signed ballot be taken by tellers”;
   A. Motions Relating to Methods of Voting,
   B. Indefinitely
   C. privileged motions
   D. or local law

   *Answer:* A) Motions Relating to Methods of Voting,. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:46 — “It is in order, as one of the Motions Relating to Methods of Voting, however, to move “that a signed ballot be taken by tellers”;”

**32.** `[C1000 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In a representative body, if there is no legal or constitutional provision specifying the size of the minority that can order a roll-call vote, the body should adopt a rule fixing the size of such a minority—for example, one fifth of _____ as in Congress, or some other portion of those present that is less than a majority.

   *Answer:* those present, — “In a representative body, if there is no legal or constitutional provision specifying the size of the minority that can order a roll-call vote, the body should adopt a rule fixing the size of such a minority—for example, one fifth of those present, as in Congress, or some other portion of those present that is less than a majority.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:46 — “In a representative body, if there is no legal or constitutional provision specifying the size of the minority that can order a roll-call vote, the body should adopt a rule fixing the size of such a minority—for example, one fifth of those present, as in Congress, or some other portion of those present that is less than a majority.”

**33.** `[C1000 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In a representative body, if there is no legal or constitutional provision specifying the size of the minority that can order a roll-call vote, the body should adopt a rule fixing the size of such a minority—for example, one fifth of _____ as in Congress, or some other portion of those present that is less than a majority.
   A. Viva-Voce Election,
   B. Dilatory Motions
   C. business
   D. those present,

   *Answer:* D) those present,. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:46 — “In a representative body, if there is no legal or constitutional provision specifying the size of the minority that can order a roll-call vote, the body should adopt a rule fixing the size of such a minority—for example, one fifth of those present, as in Congress, or some other portion of those present that is less than a majority.”

   *Answer:* one fifth of — “In a representative body, if there is no legal or constitutional provision specifying the size of the minority that can order a roll-call vote, the body should adopt a rule fixing the size of such a minority—for example, one fifth of those present, as in Congress, or some other portion of those present that is less than a majority.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:46 — “In a representative body, if there is no legal or constitutional provision specifying the size of the minority that can order a roll-call vote, the body should adopt a rule fixing the size of such a minority—for example, one fifth of those present, as in Congress, or some other portion of those present that is less than a majority.”

**35.** `[C1002 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If he does not wish to vote, he answers _____ (or abstain).

   *Answer:* present — “If he does not wish to vote, he answers present (or abstain).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:48 — “If he does not wish to vote, he answers present (or abstain).”

**36.** `[C1002 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If he does not wish to vote, he answers _____ (or abstain).
   A. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate
   B. Raise a Question of Privilege
   C. involve
   D. present

   *Answer:* D) present. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:48 — “If he does not wish to vote, he answers present (or abstain).”

**37.** `[C1002 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If he does not wish to vote, he answers present (or _____).

   *Answer:* abstain — “If he does not wish to vote, he answers present (or abstain).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:48 — “If he does not wish to vote, he answers present (or abstain).”

**38.** `[C1002 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If he does not wish to vote, he answers present (or _____).
   A. takes precedence
   B. assuming

   D. original main motion

   *Answer:* C) abstain. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:48 — “If he does not wish to vote, he answers present (or abstain).”

**39.** `[C1002 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If he is not ready to vote, but wishes to be called on again after the roll has been completely called, he answers _____.

**40.** `[C1003 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: and those answering _____ are tallied in a third column, to the far right or left.

   *Answer:* present — “and those answering present are tallied in a third column, to the far right or left.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:49 — “and those answering present are tallied in a third column, to the far right or left.”

**41.** `[C1003 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: and those answering _____ are tallied in a third column, to the far right or left.
   A. Limit or Extend
   B. in the bylaws
   C. present
   D. presiding

   *Answer:* C) present. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:49 — “and those answering present are tallied in a third column, to the far right or left.”

   *Answer:* present — “The secretary gives the final number of those voting on each side, and the number answering present, to the chair, who announces these figures and declares the result.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:51 — “The secretary gives the final number of those voting on each side, and the number answering present, to the chair, who announces these figures and declares the result.”

**43.** `[C1005 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The secretary gives the final number of those voting on each side, and the number answering _____, to the chair, who announces these figures and declares the result.

   C. Reconsider
   D. any

**44.** `[C1005 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The secretary gives the final number of those voting on each side, and the number answering _____ to the chair, who announces these figures and declares the result.

   *Answer:* present, — “The secretary gives the final number of those voting on each side, and the number answering present, to the chair, who announces these figures and declares the result.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:51 — “The secretary gives the final number of those voting on each side, and the number answering present, to the chair, who announces these figures and declares the result.”

**45.** `[C1005 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The secretary gives the final number of those voting on each side, and the number answering _____ to the chair, who announces these figures and declares the result.
