   C. Adjourned Meeting
   D. Optional Headings

   *Answer:* A) present,. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:51 — “The secretary gives the final number of those voting on each side, and the number answering present, to the chair, who announces these figures and declares the result.”

**46.** `[C1016 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The term _____ refers to any of a number of voting methods by which, on a single ballot when there are more than two possible choices, the second or less-preferred choices of voters can be taken into account if no candidate or proposition attains a majority.

   *Answer:* preferential voting — “The term preferential voting refers to any of a number of voting methods by which, on a single ballot when there are more than two possible choices, the second or less-preferred choices of voters can be taken into account if no candidate or proposition attains a majority.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:62 — “The term preferential voting refers to any of a number of voting methods by which, on a single ballot when there are more than two possible choices, the second or less-preferred choices of voters can be taken into account if no candidate or proposition attains a majority.”

**47.** `[C1016 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The term _____ refers to any of a number of voting methods by which, on a single ballot when there are more than two possible choices, the second or less-preferred choices of voters can be taken into account if no candidate or proposition attains a majority.
   A. from office
   B. preferential voting
   C. words
   D. rejected

   *Answer:* B) preferential voting. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:62 — “The term preferential voting refers to any of a number of voting methods by which, on a single ballot when there are more than two possible choices, the second or less-preferred choices of voters can be taken into account if no candidate or proposition attains a majority.”

**48.** `[C1024 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A _____ is a power of attorney given by one person to another to vote in his stead;

   *Answer:* proxy — “A proxy is a power of attorney given by one person to another to vote in his stead;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:70 — “A proxy is a power of attorney given by one person to another to vote in his stead;”

**49.** `[C1024 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A _____ is a power of attorney given by one person to another to vote in his stead;
   A. original
   B. takes precedence
   C. viva voce
   D. proxy

   *Answer:* D) proxy. See RONR (12th ed.) §45:70 — “A proxy is a power of attorney given by one person to another to vote in his stead;”

**50.** `[C1070 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: For ballot or roll-call elections of boards, committees, delegates, or other positions held by more than one individual, the bylaws may provide for _____.

   *Answer:* cumulative voting — “For ballot or roll-call elections of boards, committees, delegates, or other positions held by more than one individual, the bylaws may provide for cumulative voting.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:43 — “For ballot or roll-call elections of boards, committees, delegates, or other positions held by more than one individual, the bylaws may provide for cumulative voting.”

**51.** `[C1070 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: For ballot or roll-call elections of boards, committees, delegates, or other positions held by more than one individual, the bylaws may provide for _____.
   A. rank
   B. accept
   C. presents
   D. cumulative voting

   *Answer:* D) cumulative voting. See RONR (12th ed.) §46:43 — “For ballot or roll-call elections of boards, committees, delegates, or other positions held by more than one individual, the bylaws may provide for cumulative voting.”

---

## Domain 6: Being and Serving as a Professional Parliamentarian and Teaching Parliamentary Procedure

**1.** `[C1583 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In addition, even when improper conduct occurs at a meeting, in order for disciplinary action to be taken other than _____, charges must be preferred and a formal trial held.

   *Answer:* promptly after the breach occurs — “In addition, even when improper conduct occurs at a meeting, in order for disciplinary action to be taken other than promptly after the breach occurs, charges must be preferred and a formal trial held.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:22 — “In addition, even when improper conduct occurs at a meeting, in order for disciplinary action to be taken other than promptly after the breach occurs, charges must be preferred and a formal trial held.”

**2.** `[C1583 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In addition, even when improper conduct occurs at a meeting, in order for disciplinary action to be taken other than _____, charges must be preferred and a formal trial held.
   A. of Order
   B. promptly after the breach occurs
   C. in the office of president
   D. Discharge a

   *Answer:* B) promptly after the breach occurs. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:22 — “In addition, even when improper conduct occurs at a meeting, in order for disciplinary action to be taken other than promptly after the breach occurs, charges must be preferred and a formal trial held.”

**3.** `[C1583 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In addition, even when improper conduct occurs at a meeting, in order for disciplinary action to be taken _____ promptly after the breach occurs, charges must be preferred and a formal trial held.

   *Answer:* other than — “In addition, even when improper conduct occurs at a meeting, in order for disciplinary action to be taken other than promptly after the breach occurs, charges must be preferred and a formal trial held.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:22 — “In addition, even when improper conduct occurs at a meeting, in order for disciplinary action to be taken other than promptly after the breach occurs, charges must be preferred and a formal trial held.”

**4.** `[C1583 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In addition, even when improper conduct occurs at a meeting, in order for disciplinary action to be taken _____ promptly after the breach occurs, charges must be preferred and a formal trial held.
   A. Debatability of Motions,
   B. over
   C. illegal votes
   D. other than

   *Answer:* D) other than. See RONR (12th ed.) §61:22 — “In addition, even when improper conduct occurs at a meeting, in order for disciplinary action to be taken other than promptly after the breach occurs, charges must be preferred and a formal trial held.”

**5.** `[C1585 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Making and Enforcing Points of _____ and Appeals.

   *Answer:* Order — “Making and Enforcing Points of Order and Appeals.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:2 — “Making and Enforcing Points of Order and Appeals.”

**6.** `[C1585 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Making and Enforcing Points of _____ and Appeals.
   A. temporarily, but not
   B. the board
   C. Order
   D. pending

   *Answer:* C) Order. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:2 — “Making and Enforcing Points of Order and Appeals.”

**7.** `[C1585 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In attempting to remedy any incorrect or abusive act by the chair at a meeting, the first step is for a member to raise a _____ Order as soon as the breach of the rules occurs.

   *Answer:* Point of — “In attempting to remedy any incorrect or abusive act by the chair at a meeting, the first step is for a member to raise a Point of Order as soon as the breach of the rules occurs.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:2 — “In attempting to remedy any incorrect or abusive act by the chair at a meeting, the first step is for a member to raise a Point of Order as soon as the breach of the rules occurs.”

**8.** `[C1585 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In attempting to remedy any incorrect or abusive act by the chair at a meeting, the first step is for a member to raise a _____ Order as soon as the breach of the rules occurs.
   A. authorizes
   B. Amend
   C. Point of
   D. rising

   *Answer:* C) Point of. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:2 — “In attempting to remedy any incorrect or abusive act by the chair at a meeting, the first step is for a member to raise a Point of Order as soon as the breach of the rules occurs.”

**9.** `[C1587 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: So, if the chair ignores a member seeking the floor while entitled to it, ignores a properly made nondilatory motion, “gavels through” a motion in an improper attempt to cut off debate, or violates any other rule, any member may immediately raise a _____.

   *Answer:* Point of Order — “So, if the chair ignores a member seeking the floor while entitled to it, ignores a properly made nondilatory motion, “gavels through” a motion in an improper attempt to cut off debate, or violates any other rule, any member may immediately raise a Point of Order.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:4 — “So, if the chair ignores a member seeking the floor while entitled to it, ignores a properly made nondilatory motion, “gavels through” a motion in an improper attempt to cut off debate, or violates any other rule, any member may immediately raise a Point of Order.”

**10.** `[C1587 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: So, if the chair ignores a member seeking the floor while entitled to it, ignores a properly made nondilatory motion, “gavels through” a motion in an improper attempt to cut off debate, or violates any other rule, any member may immediately raise a _____.
   A. to that
   B. within
   C. sustaining
   D. Point of Order

   *Answer:* D) Point of Order. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:4 — “So, if the chair ignores a member seeking the floor while entitled to it, ignores a properly made nondilatory motion, “gavels through” a motion in an improper attempt to cut off debate, or violates any other rule, any member may immediately raise a Point of Order.”

**11.** `[C1588 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: To raise a _____, a member rises and, without waiting to be recognized, immediately addresses the chair, saying, “I rise to a point of order,” or simply, “Point of order!” The chair must then ask the member to state the point;

   *Answer:* Point of Order — “To raise a Point of Order, a member rises and, without waiting to be recognized, immediately addresses the chair, saying, “I rise to a point of order,” or simply, “Point of order!” The chair must then ask the member to state the point;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:5 — “To raise a Point of Order, a member rises and, without waiting to be recognized, immediately addresses the chair, saying, “I rise to a point of order,” or simply, “Point of order!” The chair must then ask the member to state the point;”

**12.** `[C1588 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: To raise a _____, a member rises and, without waiting to be recognized, immediately addresses the chair, saying, “I rise to a point of order,” or simply, “Point of order!” The chair must then ask the member to state the point;
   A. amendment to the amendment
   B. no more than a quarterly time interval
   C. Indefinitely
   D. Point of Order

   *Answer:* D) Point of Order. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:5 — “To raise a Point of Order, a member rises and, without waiting to be recognized, immediately addresses the chair, saying, “I rise to a point of order,” or simply, “Point of order!” The chair must then ask the member to state the point;”

**13.** `[C1589 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Immediately after the chair makes a ruling, any member who disagrees with it may then move an _____ (unless another Appeal is already pending or an appeal would be dilatory because there cannot possibly be two reasonable opinions on the question).

   *Answer:* Appeal — “Immediately after the chair makes a ruling, any member who disagrees with it may then move an Appeal (unless another Appeal is already pending or an appeal would be dilatory because there cannot possibly be two reasonable opinions on the question).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:6 — “Immediately after the chair makes a ruling, any member who disagrees with it may then move an Appeal (unless another Appeal is already pending or an appeal would be dilatory because there cannot possibly be two reasonable opinions on the question).”

**14.** `[C1589 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Immediately after the chair makes a ruling, any member who disagrees with it may then move an _____ (unless another Appeal is already pending or an appeal would be dilatory because there cannot possibly be two reasonable opinions on the question).
   A. Reconsider and Enter on
   B. raising
   C. Appeal
   D. event

   *Answer:* C) Appeal. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:6 — “Immediately after the chair makes a ruling, any member who disagrees with it may then move an Appeal (unless another Appeal is already pending or an appeal would be dilatory because there cannot possibly be two reasonable opinions on the question).”

**15.** `[C1589 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Immediately after the chair makes a ruling, any member who disagrees with it may then move an _____ (unless another Appeal is already pending or an appeal would be dilatory because there cannot possibly be two reasonable opinions on the question).

   *Answer:* Appeal — “Immediately after the chair makes a ruling, any member who disagrees with it may then move an Appeal (unless another Appeal is already pending or an appeal would be dilatory because there cannot possibly be two reasonable opinions on the question).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:6 — “Immediately after the chair makes a ruling, any member who disagrees with it may then move an Appeal (unless another Appeal is already pending or an appeal would be dilatory because there cannot possibly be two reasonable opinions on the question).”

**16.** `[C1589 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Immediately after the chair makes a ruling, any member who disagrees with it may then move an _____ (unless another Appeal is already pending or an appeal would be dilatory because there cannot possibly be two reasonable opinions on the question).
   A. expressly
   B. pending
   C. Appeal
   D. Parliamentary Inquiry

   *Answer:* C) Appeal. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:6 — “Immediately after the chair makes a ruling, any member who disagrees with it may then move an Appeal (unless another Appeal is already pending or an appeal would be dilatory because there cannot possibly be two reasonable opinions on the question).”

**17.** `[C1589 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Immediately after the chair makes a ruling, any member who disagrees with it may then move an _____ (unless another Appeal is already pending or an appeal would be dilatory because there cannot possibly be two reasonable opinions on the question).

   *Answer:* Appeal — “Immediately after the chair makes a ruling, any member who disagrees with it may then move an Appeal (unless another Appeal is already pending or an appeal would be dilatory because there cannot possibly be two reasonable opinions on the question).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:6 — “Immediately after the chair makes a ruling, any member who disagrees with it may then move an Appeal (unless another Appeal is already pending or an appeal would be dilatory because there cannot possibly be two reasonable opinions on the question).”

**18.** `[C1590 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: These procedures enable the majority to ensure enforcement of the rules unless the chair fails to respond to and resolve the _____, fails to respond appropriately to any Appeal, or fails to act in accordance with the assembly’s decision on the Appeal (or on a Point of Order submitted by the chair to the assembly).

   *Answer:* Point of Order — “These procedures enable the majority to ensure enforcement of the rules unless the chair fails to respond to and resolve the Point of Order, fails to respond appropriately to any Appeal, or fails to act in accordance with the assembly’s decision on the Appeal (or on a Point of Order submitted by the chair to the assembly).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:7 — “These procedures enable the majority to ensure enforcement of the rules unless the chair fails to respond to and resolve the Point of Order, fails to respond appropriately to any Appeal, or fails to act in accordance with the assembly’s decision on the Appeal (or on a Point of Order submitted by the chair to the assembly).”

**19.** `[C1590 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: These procedures enable the majority to ensure enforcement of the rules unless the chair fails to respond to and resolve the _____, fails to respond appropriately to any Appeal, or fails to act in accordance with the assembly’s decision on the Appeal (or on a Point of Order submitted by the chair to the assembly).
   A. Point of Order
   B. Ordered
   C. within the
   D. house of

   *Answer:* A) Point of Order. See RONR (12th ed.) §62:7 — “These procedures enable the majority to ensure enforcement of the rules unless the chair fails to respond to and resolve the Point of Order, fails to respond appropriately to any Appeal, or fails to act in accordance with the assembly’s decision on the Appeal (or on a Point of Order submitted by the chair to the assembly).”

**20.** `[C1590 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: These procedures enable the majority to ensure enforcement of the rules unless the chair fails to respond to and resolve the Point of Order, fails to respond appropriately to any _____, or fails to act in accordance with the assembly’s decision on the Appeal (or on a Point of Order submitted by the chair to the assembly).

   *Answer:* Appeal — “These procedures enable the majority to ensure enforcement of the rules unless the chair fails to respond to and resolve the Point of Order, fails to respond appropriately to any Appeal, or fails to act in accordance with the assembly’s decision on the Appeal (or on a Point of Order submitted by the chair to the assembly).”.    *Answer:* Appeal — “These procedures enable the majority to ensure enforcement of the rules unless the chair fails to respond to and resolve the Point of Order, fails to respond appropriately to any Appeal, or fails to act in accordance with the assembly’s decision on the Appeal (or on a Point of Order submitted by the chair to the assembly).”. **49.** `[C1631 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If the trial has been held before a trial committee instead of the assembly of the society, this committee reports to the assembly in executive session (9) the results of its trial of the case, with resolutions—in cases where its finding is one of _____—covering the penalty it recommends that the society impose.

   *Answer:* guilty — “If the trial has been held before a trial committee instead of the assembly of the society, this committee reports to the assembly in executive session (9) the results of its trial of the case, with resolutions—in cases where its finding is one of guilty—covering the penalty it recommends that the society impose.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:35 — “If the trial has been held before a trial committee instead of the assembly of the society, this committee reports to the assembly in executive session (9) the results of its trial of the case, with resolutions—in cases where its finding is one of guilty—covering the penalty it recommends that the society impose.”

   A. speed up
   B. informal consideration,
   C. guilty
   D. adjournment

**51.** `[C1635 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: It is generally best not to empower the committee to _____ a penalty, however, but to require it to report its recommended disciplinary measures to the society for action, just as in the case of a special committee to hear a trial.

   *Answer:* impose — “It is generally best not to empower the committee to impose a penalty, however, but to require it to report its recommended disciplinary measures to the society for action, just as in the case of a special committee to hear a trial.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:39 — “It is generally best not to empower the committee to impose a penalty, however, but to require it to report its recommended disciplinary measures to the society for action, just as in the case of a special committee to hear a trial.”

**52.** `[C1635 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: It is generally best not to empower the committee to _____ a penalty, however, but to require it to report its recommended disciplinary measures to the society for action, just as in the case of a special committee to hear a trial.
   A. quasi committee of the whole
   B. the Assembly
   C. impose
   D. speed up

   *Answer:* C) impose. See RONR (12th ed.) §63:39 — “It is generally best not to empower the committee to impose a penalty, however, but to require it to report its recommended disciplinary measures to the society for action, just as in the case of a special committee to hear a trial.”

---

## Domain 7: Boards and Committees, and Writing and Interpreting Bylaws

**1.** `[C1163 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such a board is usually known as the executive board, or—in organizations where there is an executive committee within and subordinate to the board as described below—the board of directors, board of managers, or _____

   *Answer:* board of trustees. — “Such a board is usually known as the executive board, or—in organizations where there is an executive committee within and subordinate to the board as described below—the board of directors, board of managers, or board of trustees.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:3 — “Such a board is usually known as the executive board, or—in organizations where there is an executive committee within and subordinate to the board as described below—the board of directors, board of managers, or board of trustees.”

**2.** `[C1163 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such a board is usually known as the executive board, or—in organizations where there is an executive committee within and subordinate to the board as described below—the board of directors, board of managers, or _____
   A. something to
   B. the
   C. board of trustees.
   D. Objection to the Consideration

   *Answer:* C) board of trustees.. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:3 — “Such a board is usually known as the executive board, or—in organizations where there is an executive committee within and subordinate to the board as described below—the board of directors, board of managers, or board of trustees.”

**3.** `[C1163 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such a board is usually known as the _____ or—in organizations where there is an executive committee within and subordinate to the board as described below—the board of directors, board of managers, or board of trustees.

   *Answer:* executive board, — “Such a board is usually known as the executive board, or—in organizations where there is an executive committee within and subordinate to the board as described below—the board of directors, board of managers, or board of trustees.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:3 — “Such a board is usually known as the executive board, or—in organizations where there is an executive committee within and subordinate to the board as described below—the board of directors, board of managers, or board of trustees.”

**4.** `[C1163 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such a board is usually known as the _____ or—in organizations where there is an executive committee within and subordinate to the board as described below—the board of directors, board of managers, or board of trustees.
   A. what the accused is alleged to have
   B. temporarily, but not
   C. executive board,
   D. Platform Committee

   *Answer:* C) executive board,. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:3 — “Such a board is usually known as the executive board, or—in organizations where there is an executive committee within and subordinate to the board as described below—the board of directors, board of managers, or board of trustees.”

**5.** `[C1163 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such a board is usually known as the executive board, or—in organizations where there is an executive committee within and subordinate to the board as described below—the board of directors, board _____ or board of trustees.

   *Answer:* of managers, — “Such a board is usually known as the executive board, or—in organizations where there is an executive committee within and subordinate to the board as described below—the board of directors, board of managers, or board of trustees.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:3 — “Such a board is usually known as the executive board, or—in organizations where there is an executive committee within and subordinate to the board as described below—the board of directors, board of managers, or board of trustees.”

**6.** `[C1171 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As a general principle, a board cannot delegate its authority —that is, it cannot empower a subordinate group to act independently in its name—except as may be authorized by the bylaws (of the society) or other instrument under which _____ is constituted;

   *Answer:* the board — “As a general principle, a board cannot delegate its authority —that is, it cannot empower a subordinate group to act independently in its name—except as may be authorized by the bylaws (of the society) or other instrument under which the board is constituted;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:12 — “As a general principle, a board cannot delegate its authority —that is, it cannot empower a subordinate group to act independently in its name—except as may be authorized by the bylaws (of the society) or other instrument under which the board is constituted;”

**7.** `[C1171 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As a general principle, a board cannot delegate its authority —that is, it cannot empower a subordinate group to act independently in its name—except as may be authorized by the bylaws (of the society) or other instrument under which _____ is constituted;
   A. Appeal
   B. Consideration of the Question
   C. the board
   D. Withdraw or Modify a Motion

   *Answer:* C) the board. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:12 — “As a general principle, a board cannot delegate its authority —that is, it cannot empower a subordinate group to act independently in its name—except as may be authorized by the bylaws (of the society) or other instrument under which the board is constituted;”

**8.** `[C1171 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such committees _____ always report to the board.

   *Answer:* of the board — “Such committees of the board always report to the board.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:12 — “Such committees of the board always report to the board.”

**9.** `[C1171 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such committees _____ always report to the board.
   A. resolution
   B. the
   C. of the board
   D. Ordered

   *Answer:* C) of the board. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:12 — “Such committees of the board always report to the board.”

**10.** `[C1171 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As a general principle, a board cannot delegate its authority —that is, it cannot empower a subordinate group to act independently in its name—except as may be authorized by the bylaws (of the _____) or other instrument under which the board is constituted;

   *Answer:* society — “As a general principle, a board cannot delegate its authority —that is, it cannot empower a subordinate group to act independently in its name—except as may be authorized by the bylaws (of the society) or other instrument under which the board is constituted;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:12 — “As a general principle, a board cannot delegate its authority —that is, it cannot empower a subordinate group to act independently in its name—except as may be authorized by the bylaws (of the society) or other instrument under which the board is constituted;”

**11.** `[C1172 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In a society where the board is large or its members must travel from a distance to meet, it is usual for the bylaws to establish an _____ composed of a specified number of board members, which shall have all or much of the power of the board between meetings (just as the board has all or much of the power of the society between the society’s meetings), but which cannot alter any decision made by the board (just as the board cannot alter any decision made by the society).

   *Answer:* executive committee — “In a society where the board is large or its members must travel from a distance to meet, it is usual for the bylaws to establish an executive committee composed of a specified number of board members, which shall have all or much of the power of the board between meetings (just as the board has all or much of the power of the society between the society’s meetings), but which cannot alter any decision made by the board (just as the board cannot alter any decision made by the society).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:13 — “In a society where the board is large or its members must travel from a distance to meet, it is usual for the bylaws to establish an executive committee composed of a specified number of board members, which shall have all or much of the power of the board between meetings (just as the board has all or much of the power of the society between the society’s meetings), but which cannot alter any decision made by the board (just as the board cannot alter any decision made by the society).”

**12.** `[C1172 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In a society where the board is large or its members must travel from a distance to meet, it is usual for the bylaws to establish an _____ composed of a specified number of board members, which shall have all or much of the power of the board between meetings (just as the board has all or much of the power of the society between the society’s meetings), but which cannot alter any decision made by the board (just as the board cannot alter any decision made by the society).
   A. executive committee
   B. essential to the
   C. aye.
   D. Dilatory Motions

   *Answer:* A) executive committee. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:13 — “In a society where the board is large or its members must travel from a distance to meet, it is usual for the bylaws to establish an executive committee composed of a specified number of board members, which shall have all or much of the power of the board between meetings (just as the board has all or much of the power of the society between the society’s meetings), but which cannot alter any decision made by the board (just as the board cannot alter any decision made by the society).”

**13.** `[C1173 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In such cases the committees are genuinely subordinate to the board and must ordinarily report back to it for authority to act (in contrast to an executive committee, which usually has power to act as the board, and in contrast to standing committees of the _____ which are not subordinate to the board unless made so by a provision in the bylaws).

   *Answer:* society, — “In such cases the committees are genuinely subordinate to the board and must ordinarily report back to it for authority to act (in contrast to an executive committee, which usually has power to act as the board, and in contrast to standing committees of the society, which are not subordinate to the board unless made so by a provision in the bylaws).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:14 — “In such cases the committees are genuinely subordinate to the board and must ordinarily report back to it for authority to act (in contrast to an executive committee, which usually has power to act as the board, and in contrast to standing committees of the society, which are not subordinate to the board unless made so by a provision in the bylaws).”

**14.** `[C1173 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In such cases the committees are genuinely subordinate to the board and must ordinarily report back to it for authority to act (in contrast to an executive committee, which usually has power to act as the board, and in contrast to standing committees of the _____ which are not subordinate to the board unless made so by a provision in the bylaws).
   A. society,
   B. who should be engaged well in
   C. obtained the floor
   D. secondary amendment

   *Answer:* A) society,. See RONR (12th ed.) §49:14 — “In such cases the committees are genuinely subordinate to the board and must ordinarily report back to it for authority to act (in contrast to an executive committee, which usually has power to act as the board, and in contrast to standing committees of the society, which are not subordinate to the board unless made so by a provision in the bylaws).”

**15.** `[C1183 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: An assembly can also designate all of its members present to act as a committee, which is called a _____ and is distinguished from an ordinary committee.

   *Answer:* committee of the whole — “An assembly can also designate all of its members present to act as a committee, which is called a committee of the whole and is distinguished from an ordinary committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:2 — “An assembly can also designate all of its members present to act as a committee, which is called a committee of the whole and is distinguished from an ordinary committee.”

**16.** `[C1183 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: An assembly can also designate all of its members present to act as a committee, which is called a _____ and is distinguished from an ordinary committee.
   A. recognized
   B. committee of the whole
   C. Any Other Privilege
   D. Unfinished Business and General Orders

   *Answer:* B) committee of the whole. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:2 — “An assembly can also designate all of its members present to act as a committee, which is called a committee of the whole and is distinguished from an ordinary committee.”

**17.** `[C1183 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Although the term committee commonly implies a relatively small number of persons appointed to give a task more detailed attention than is possible in a body the size of the assembly, this characteristic more accurately describes what are known as _____.

   *Answer:* ordinary committees — “Although the term committee commonly implies a relatively small number of persons appointed to give a task more detailed attention than is possible in a body the size of the assembly, this characteristic more accurately describes what are known as ordinary committees.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:2 — “Although the term committee commonly implies a relatively small number of persons appointed to give a task more detailed attention than is possible in a body the size of the assembly, this characteristic more accurately describes what are known as ordinary committees.”

**18.** `[C1183 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Although the term committee commonly implies a relatively small number of persons appointed to give a task more detailed attention than is possible in a body the size of the assembly, this characteristic more accurately describes what are known as _____.
   A. ordinary committees
   B. stated meeting
   C. Limit or Extend Limits of Debate
   D. of a Question

   *Answer:* A) ordinary committees. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:2 — “Although the term committee commonly implies a relatively small number of persons appointed to give a task more detailed attention than is possible in a body the size of the assembly, this characteristic more accurately describes what are known as ordinary committees.”

**19.** `[C1183 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Although the term _____ commonly implies a relatively small number of persons appointed to give a task more detailed attention than is possible in a body the size of the assembly, this characteristic more accurately describes what are known as ordinary committees.

   *Answer:* committee — “Although the term committee commonly implies a relatively small number of persons appointed to give a task more detailed attention than is possible in a body the size of the assembly, this characteristic more accurately describes what are known as ordinary committees.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:2 — “Although the term committee commonly implies a relatively small number of persons appointed to give a task more detailed attention than is possible in a body the size of the assembly, this characteristic more accurately describes what are known as ordinary committees.”

**20.** `[C1184 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Ordinary committees are of two types—standing committees (which have a continuing existence) and _____ committees (which go out of existence as soon as they have completed a specified task).

   *Answer:* special — “Ordinary committees are of two types—standing committees (which have a continuing existence) and special committees (which go out of existence as soon as they have completed a specified task).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:3 — “Ordinary committees are of two types—standing committees (which have a continuing existence) and special committees (which go out of existence as soon as they have completed a specified task).”

**21.** `[C1184 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Ordinary committees are of two types—standing committees (which have a continuing existence) and _____ committees (which go out of existence as soon as they have completed a specified task).
   A. Point of Order
   B. Appeal
   C. insert
   D. special

   *Answer:* D) special. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:3 — “Ordinary committees are of two types—standing committees (which have a continuing existence) and special committees (which go out of existence as soon as they have completed a specified task).”

**22.** `[C1184 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Ordinary committees are of two types—_____ committees (which have a continuing existence) and special committees (which go out of existence as soon as they have completed a specified task).

   *Answer:* standing — “Ordinary committees are of two types—standing committees (which have a continuing existence) and special committees (which go out of existence as soon as they have completed a specified task).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:3 — “Ordinary committees are of two types—standing committees (which have a continuing existence) and special committees (which go out of existence as soon as they have completed a specified task).”

**23.** `[C1184 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Ordinary committees are of two types—_____ committees (which have a continuing existence) and special committees (which go out of existence as soon as they have completed a specified task).
   A. representatives
   B. standing
   C. Annul
   D. secretary

   *Answer:* B) standing. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:3 — “Ordinary committees are of two types—standing committees (which have a continuing existence) and special committees (which go out of existence as soon as they have completed a specified task).”

   *Answer:* committees — “Ordinary committees are of two types—standing committees (which have a continuing existence) and special committees (which go out of existence as soon as they have completed a specified task).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:3 — “Ordinary committees are of two types—standing committees (which have a continuing existence) and special committees (which go out of existence as soon as they have completed a specified task).”

**25.** `[C1185 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Generally the term _____ implies that, within the area of its assigned responsibilities, the committee has less authority to act independently for the society (or other constituting power) than a board is usually understood to have.

   *Answer:* committee — “Generally the term committee implies that, within the area of its assigned responsibilities, the committee has less authority to act independently for the society (or other constituting power) than a board is usually understood to have.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:4 — “Generally the term committee implies that, within the area of its assigned responsibilities, the committee has less authority to act independently for the society (or other constituting power) than a board is usually understood to have.”

**26.** `[C1185 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Generally the term _____ implies that, within the area of its assigned responsibilities, the committee has less authority to act independently for the society (or other constituting power) than a board is usually understood to have.
   A. at the
   B. committee
   C. secretary
   D. standing committee

   *Answer:* B) committee. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:4 — “Generally the term committee implies that, within the area of its assigned responsibilities, the committee has less authority to act independently for the society (or other constituting power) than a board is usually understood to have.”

**27.** `[C1188 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: _____ are constituted to perform a continuing function, and remain in existence permanently or for the life of the assembly that establishes them.

   *Answer:* Standing committees — “Standing committees are constituted to perform a continuing function, and remain in existence permanently or for the life of the assembly that establishes them.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:7 — “Standing committees are constituted to perform a continuing function, and remain in existence permanently or for the life of the assembly that establishes them.”

**28.** `[C1188 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: _____ are constituted to perform a continuing function, and remain in existence permanently or for the life of the assembly that establishes them.
   A. Standing committees
   B. will be
   C. adhere
   D. the Time to Which to Adjourn

   *Answer:* A) Standing committees. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:7 — “Standing committees are constituted to perform a continuing function, and remain in existence permanently or for the life of the assembly that establishes them.”

**29.** `[C1191 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A special (select, or _____) committee is a committee appointed, as the need arises, to carry out a specified task, at the completion of which—that is, on presentation of its final report to the assembly—it automatically ceases to exist.

   *Answer:* ad hoc — “A special (select, or ad hoc) committee is a committee appointed, as the need arises, to carry out a specified task, at the completion of which—that is, on presentation of its final report to the assembly—it automatically ceases to exist.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:10 — “A special (select, or ad hoc) committee is a committee appointed, as the need arises, to carry out a specified task, at the completion of which—that is, on presentation of its final report to the assembly—it automatically ceases to exist.”

**30.** `[C1191 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A special (select, or _____) committee is a committee appointed, as the need arises, to carry out a specified task, at the completion of which—that is, on presentation of its final report to the assembly—it automatically ceases to exist.
   A. previous notice,
   B. ad hoc
   C. revision
   D. as suggested by

   *Answer:* B) ad hoc. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:10 — “A special (select, or ad hoc) committee is a committee appointed, as the need arises, to carry out a specified task, at the completion of which—that is, on presentation of its final report to the assembly—it automatically ceases to exist.”

   *Answer:* select, — “A special (select, or ad hoc) committee is a committee appointed, as the need arises, to carry out a specified task, at the completion of which—that is, on presentation of its final report to the assembly—it automatically ceases to exist.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:10 — “A special (select, or ad hoc) committee is a committee appointed, as the need arises, to carry out a specified task, at the completion of which—that is, on presentation of its final report to the assembly—it automatically ceases to exist.”

**32.** `[C1191 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A special (_____ or ad hoc) committee is a committee appointed, as the need arises, to carry out a specified task, at the completion of which—that is, on presentation of its final report to the assembly—it automatically ceases to exist.
   A. house of delegates

**33.** `[C1191 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A special (select, or ad hoc) _____ is a committee appointed, as the need arises, to carry out a specified task, at the completion of which—that is, on presentation of its final report to the assembly—it automatically ceases to exist.

See RONR (12th ed.) §50:10 — “A special (select, or ad hoc) committee is a committee appointed, as the need arises, to carry out a specified task, at the completion of which—that is, on presentation of its final report to the assembly—it automatically ceases to exist.”

**34.** `[C1203 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When a special committee has finished with the business assigned to it, a motion is made for the committee to “rise”—which is equivalent to the motion to adjourn _____ (or without day)—and for the chairman or some other member to make its report to the assembly.

   *Answer:* sine die — “When a special committee has finished with the business assigned to it, a motion is made for the committee to “rise”—which is equivalent to the motion to adjourn sine die (or without day)—and for the chairman or some other member to make its report to the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:23 — “When a special committee has finished with the business assigned to it, a motion is made for the committee to “rise”—which is equivalent to the motion to adjourn sine die (or without day)—and for the chairman or some other member to make its report to the assembly.”

**35.** `[C1203 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When a special committee has finished with the business assigned to it, a motion is made for the committee to “rise”—which is equivalent to the motion to adjourn _____ (or without day)—and for the chairman or some other member to make its report to the assembly.
   A. electronic meetings
   B. sine die
   C. voice
   D. receives

   *Answer:* B) sine die. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:23 — “When a special committee has finished with the business assigned to it, a motion is made for the committee to “rise”—which is equivalent to the motion to adjourn sine die (or without day)—and for the chairman or some other member to make its report to the assembly.”

**36.** `[C1207 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Such a meeting is usually called a _____.

   *Answer:* hearing — “Such a meeting is usually called a hearing.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:27 — “Such a meeting is usually called a hearing.”

**37.** `[C1207 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Such a meeting is usually called a _____.
   A. hearing
   B. Fix the
   C. assemblies
   D. adjourned meeting

   *Answer:* A) hearing. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:27 — “Such a meeting is usually called a hearing.”

**38.** `[C1209 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Since members of standing _____s in ordinary societies are appointed for a term corresponding to that of the officers, such a committee is generally required to report at least once a year, usually at the annual meeting, on its activities and everything referred to it during the year.

   *Answer:* committee — “Since members of standing committees in ordinary societies are appointed for a term corresponding to that of the officers, such a committee is generally required to report at least once a year, usually at the annual meeting, on its activities and everything referred to it during the year.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:29 — “Since members of standing committees in ordinary societies are appointed for a term corresponding to that of the officers, such a committee is generally required to report at least once a year, usually at the annual meeting, on its activities and everything referred to it during the year.”

**39.** `[C1209 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Since members of standing _____s in ordinary societies are appointed for a term corresponding to that of the officers, such a committee is generally required to report at least once a year, usually at the annual meeting, on its activities and everything referred to it during the year.
   A. resolution
   B. committee
   C. vote
   D. on the Table

   *Answer:* B) committee. See RONR (12th ed.) §50:29 — “Since members of standing committees in ordinary societies are appointed for a term corresponding to that of the officers, such a committee is generally required to report at least once a year, usually at the annual meeting, on its activities and everything referred to it during the year.”

**40.** `[C1214 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Although it is possible for a report, in the circumstances just described, to present recommendations which are not in the form of resolutions or motions, the “adoption” of such recommendations by the parent assembly may, depending on their wording or that of the motion to adopt, lead to confusion as to whether their adoption authorizes _____ or only has the force of a declaration of intent (requiring the adoption of subsequent resolutions for implementation).

   *Answer:* action, — “Although it is possible for a report, in the circumstances just described, to present recommendations which are not in the form of resolutions or motions, the “adoption” of such recommendations by the parent assembly may, depending on their wording or that of the motion to adopt, lead to confusion as to whether their adoption authorizes action, or only has the force of a declaration of intent (requiring the adoption of subsequent resolutions for implementation).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:4 — “Although it is possible for a report, in the circumstances just described, to present recommendations which are not in the form of resolutions or motions, the “adoption” of such recommendations by the parent assembly may, depending on their wording or that of the motion to adopt, lead to confusion as to whether their adoption authorizes action, or only has the force of a declaration of intent (requiring the adoption of subsequent resolutions for implementation).”

**41.** `[C1214 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Although it is possible for a report, in the circumstances just described, to present recommendations which are not in the form of resolutions or motions, the “adoption” of such recommendations by the parent assembly may, depending on their wording or that of the motion to adopt, lead to confusion as to whether their adoption authorizes _____ or only has the force of a declaration of intent (requiring the adoption of subsequent resolutions for implementation).
   A. Order of Business
   B. Previous
   C. stating the question
   D. action,

   *Answer:* D) action,. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:4 — “Although it is possible for a report, in the circumstances just described, to present recommendations which are not in the form of resolutions or motions, the “adoption” of such recommendations by the parent assembly may, depending on their wording or that of the motion to adopt, lead to confusion as to whether their adoption authorizes action, or only has the force of a declaration of intent (requiring the adoption of subsequent resolutions for implementation).”

**42.** `[C1214 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Although it is possible for a report, in the circumstances just described, to present recommendations which are not in the form of resolutions or motions, the “adoption” of such recommendations by the parent assembly may, depending on their wording or that of the motion to adopt, lead to confusion as to whether their adoption _____ action, or only has the force of a declaration of intent (requiring the adoption of subsequent resolutions for implementation).

   *Answer:* authorizes — “Although it is possible for a report, in the circumstances just described, to present recommendations which are not in the form of resolutions or motions, the “adoption” of such recommendations by the parent assembly may, depending on their wording or that of the motion to adopt, lead to confusion as to whether their adoption authorizes action, or only has the force of a declaration of intent (requiring the adoption of subsequent resolutions for implementation).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:4 — “Although it is possible for a report, in the circumstances just described, to present recommendations which are not in the form of resolutions or motions, the “adoption” of such recommendations by the parent assembly may, depending on their wording or that of the motion to adopt, lead to confusion as to whether their adoption authorizes action, or only has the force of a declaration of intent (requiring the adoption of subsequent resolutions for implementation).”

**43.** `[C1214 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Although it is possible for a report, in the circumstances just described, to present recommendations which are not in the form of resolutions or motions, the “adoption” of such recommendations by the parent assembly may, depending on their wording or that of the motion to adopt, lead to confusion as to whether their adoption _____ action, or only has the force of a declaration of intent (requiring the adoption of subsequent resolutions for implementation).
   A. secondary amendment by inserting
   B. authorizes
   C. clerk
   D. committee of the whole

   *Answer:* B) authorizes. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:4 — “Although it is possible for a report, in the circumstances just described, to present recommendations which are not in the form of resolutions or motions, the “adoption” of such recommendations by the parent assembly may, depending on their wording or that of the motion to adopt, lead to confusion as to whether their adoption authorizes action, or only has the force of a declaration of intent (requiring the adoption of subsequent resolutions for implementation).”

**44.** `[C1219 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A reporting member _____ or presents a report on behalf of a board or a committee when, having been assigned the floor for such a purpose in a meeting, he does one of the following things (depending on the nature of the report and other conditions): (a) renders the report orally, if it is not in writing;

   *Answer:* makes — “A reporting member makes or presents a report on behalf of a board or a committee when, having been assigned the floor for such a purpose in a meeting, he does one of the following things (depending on the nature of the report and other conditions): (a) renders the report orally, if it is not in writing;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:9 — “A reporting member makes or presents a report on behalf of a board or a committee when, having been assigned the floor for such a purpose in a meeting, he does one of the following things (depending on the nature of the report and other conditions): (a) renders the report orally, if it is not in writing;”

**45.** `[C1219 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: A reporting member _____ or presents a report on behalf of a board or a committee when, having been assigned the floor for such a purpose in a meeting, he does one of the following things (depending on the nature of the report and other conditions): (a) renders the report orally, if it is not in writing;
   A. makes
   B. subsequently
   C. limit
   D. select,

   *Answer:* A) makes. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:9 — “A reporting member makes or presents a report on behalf of a board or a committee when, having been assigned the floor for such a purpose in a meeting, he does one of the following things (depending on the nature of the report and other conditions): (a) renders the report orally, if it is not in writing;”

**46.** `[C1219 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: When the assembly hears the report thus read or orally rendered, it _____ the report.

   *Answer:* receives — “When the assembly hears the report thus read or orally rendered, it receives the report.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:9 — “When the assembly hears the report thus read or orally rendered, it receives the report.”

**47.** `[C1219 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: When the assembly hears the report thus read or orally rendered, it _____ the report.
   A. viva voce
   B. closing
   C. in good standing
   D. receives

   *Answer:* D) receives. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:9 — “When the assembly hears the report thus read or orally rendered, it receives the report.”

**48.** `[C1219 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: A reporting member makes or _____ a report on behalf of a board or a committee when, having been assigned the floor for such a purpose in a meeting, he does one of the following things (depending on the nature of the report and other conditions): (a) renders the report orally, if it is not in writing;

   *Answer:* presents — “A reporting member makes or presents a report on behalf of a board or a committee when, having been assigned the floor for such a purpose in a meeting, he does one of the following things (depending on the nature of the report and other conditions): (a) renders the report orally, if it is not in writing;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:9 — “A reporting member makes or presents a report on behalf of a board or a committee when, having been assigned the floor for such a purpose in a meeting, he does one of the following things (depending on the nature of the report and other conditions): (a) renders the report orally, if it is not in writing;”

**49.** `[C1222 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Or, when the proper motion is a matter of clear-cut procedure and must necessarily be introduced to resolve the case, the chair may sometimes expedite matters by _____ the motion—that is, stating the question on it without waiting for it to be made.

   *Answer:* assuming — “Or, when the proper motion is a matter of clear-cut procedure and must necessarily be introduced to resolve the case, the chair may sometimes expedite matters by assuming the motion—that is, stating the question on it without waiting for it to be made.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:12 — “Or, when the proper motion is a matter of clear-cut procedure and must necessarily be introduced to resolve the case, the chair may sometimes expedite matters by assuming the motion—that is, stating the question on it without waiting for it to be made.”

**50.** `[C1222 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Or, when the proper motion is a matter of clear-cut procedure and must necessarily be introduced to resolve the case, the chair may sometimes expedite matters by _____ the motion—that is, stating the question on it without waiting for it to be made.
   A. Raise a
   B. assuming
   C. stated meeting
   D. to a Certain Time

   *Answer:* B) assuming. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:12 — “Or, when the proper motion is a matter of clear-cut procedure and must necessarily be introduced to resolve the case, the chair may sometimes expedite matters by assuming the motion—that is, stating the question on it without waiting for it to be made.”

**51.** `[C1224 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As applied to an assembly’s action with respect to board or committee reports or any of their contents, the expressions adopt, accept, and _____ are all equivalent—that is, the text adopted becomes in effect the act or statement of the assembly.

   *Answer:* agree to — “As applied to an assembly’s action with respect to board or committee reports or any of their contents, the expressions adopt, accept, and agree to are all equivalent—that is, the text adopted becomes in effect the act or statement of the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:14 — “As applied to an assembly’s action with respect to board or committee reports or any of their contents, the expressions adopt, accept, and agree to are all equivalent—that is, the text adopted becomes in effect the act or statement of the assembly.”

**52.** `[C1224 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As applied to an assembly’s action with respect to board or committee reports or any of their contents, the expressions adopt, accept, and _____ are all equivalent—that is, the text adopted becomes in effect the act or statement of the assembly.
   A. agree to
   B. confirm
   C. main motion
   D. legislative body

   *Answer:* A) agree to. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:14 — “As applied to an assembly’s action with respect to board or committee reports or any of their contents, the expressions adopt, accept, and agree to are all equivalent—that is, the text adopted becomes in effect the act or statement of the assembly.”

**53.** `[C1224 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As applied to an assembly’s action with respect to board or committee reports or any of their contents, the expressions _____ and agree to are all equivalent—that is, the text adopted becomes in effect the act or statement of the assembly.

   *Answer:* adopt, accept, — “As applied to an assembly’s action with respect to board or committee reports or any of their contents, the expressions adopt, accept, and agree to are all equivalent—that is, the text adopted becomes in effect the act or statement of the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:14 — “As applied to an assembly’s action with respect to board or committee reports or any of their contents, the expressions adopt, accept, and agree to are all equivalent—that is, the text adopted becomes in effect the act or statement of the assembly.”

**54.** `[C1224 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: As applied to an assembly’s action with respect to board or committee reports or any of their contents, the expressions _____ and agree to are all equivalent—that is, the text adopted becomes in effect the act or statement of the assembly.
   A. standing committee
   B. Nominations by the Chair,
   C. adopt, accept,
   D. at the

   *Answer:* C) adopt, accept,. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:14 — “As applied to an assembly’s action with respect to board or committee reports or any of their contents, the expressions adopt, accept, and agree to are all equivalent—that is, the text adopted becomes in effect the act or statement of the assembly.”

**55.** `[C1224 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: As applied to an assembly’s action with respect to board or committee reports or any of their contents, the expressions _____ accept, and agree to are all equivalent—that is, the text adopted becomes in effect the act or statement of the assembly.

   *Answer:* adopt, — “As applied to an assembly’s action with respect to board or committee reports or any of their contents, the expressions adopt, accept, and agree to are all equivalent—that is, the text adopted becomes in effect the act or statement of the assembly.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:14 — “As applied to an assembly’s action with respect to board or committee reports or any of their contents, the expressions adopt, accept, and agree to are all equivalent—that is, the text adopted becomes in effect the act or statement of the assembly.”

**56.** `[C1226 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: Another error—less common, but dangerous—is to move, after the report has been read (or even _____ the reading), that it “be accepted,” when the actual intent is that of the mistaken motion to receive, as just explained, or of a legitimate motion to receive made before the report is read.

   *Answer:* before — “Another error—less common, but dangerous—is to move, after the report has been read (or even before the reading), that it “be accepted,” when the actual intent is that of the mistaken motion to receive, as just explained, or of a legitimate motion to receive made before the report is read.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:16 — “Another error—less common, but dangerous—is to move, after the report has been read (or even before the reading), that it “be accepted,” when the actual intent is that of the mistaken motion to receive, as just explained, or of a legitimate motion to receive made before the report is read.”

**57.** `[C1226 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: Another error—less common, but dangerous—is to move, after the report has been read (or even _____ the reading), that it “be accepted,” when the actual intent is that of the mistaken motion to receive, as just explained, or of a legitimate motion to receive made before the report is read.
   A. of the second degree
   B. before
   C. Table
   D. parliamentary authority

   *Answer:* B) before. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:16 — “Another error—less common, but dangerous—is to move, after the report has been read (or even before the reading), that it “be accepted,” when the actual intent is that of the mistaken motion to receive, as just explained, or of a legitimate motion to receive made before the report is read.”

**58.** `[C1228 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: In the foregoing connection it should be noted that the consideration of a matter _____ to a board or committee cannot be objected to (26) when it is reported on— regardless of whether the matter was referred as a pending question (13) or as a subject on which no question was pending.

   *Answer:* that was referred — “In the foregoing connection it should be noted that the consideration of a matter that was referred to a board or committee cannot be objected to (26) when it is reported on— regardless of whether the matter was referred as a pending question (13) or as a subject on which no question was pending.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:18 — “In the foregoing connection it should be noted that the consideration of a matter that was referred to a board or committee cannot be objected to (26) when it is reported on— regardless of whether the matter was referred as a pending question (13) or as a subject on which no question was pending.”

**59.** `[C1228 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: In the foregoing connection it should be noted that the consideration of a matter _____ to a board or committee cannot be objected to (26) when it is reported on— regardless of whether the matter was referred as a pending question (13) or as a subject on which no question was pending.
   A. orders
   B. that was referred
   C. receives
   D. Motion to Adopt an Entire Report,

   *Answer:* B) that was referred. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:18 — “In the foregoing connection it should be noted that the consideration of a matter that was referred to a board or committee cannot be objected to (26) when it is reported on— regardless of whether the matter was referred as a pending question (13) or as a subject on which no question was pending.”

**60.** `[C1228 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The reason is that an Objection to the Consideration _____ can be raised only against an original main motion at the time of its introduction;

   *Answer:* of a Question — “The reason is that an Objection to the Consideration of a Question can be raised only against an original main motion at the time of its introduction;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:18 — “The reason is that an Objection to the Consideration of a Question can be raised only against an original main motion at the time of its introduction;”

**61.** `[C1228 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The reason is that an Objection to the Consideration _____ can be raised only against an original main motion at the time of its introduction;
   A. preferential voting
   B. are
   C. Limits of Debate
   D. of a Question

   *Answer:* D) of a Question. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:18 — “The reason is that an Objection to the Consideration of a Question can be raised only against an original main motion at the time of its introduction;”

**62.** `[C1228 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The reason is that an _____ of a Question can be raised only against an original main motion at the time of its introduction;

   *Answer:* Objection to the Consideration — “The reason is that an Objection to the Consideration of a Question can be raised only against an original main motion at the time of its introduction;”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:18 — “The reason is that an Objection to the Consideration of a Question can be raised only against an original main motion at the time of its introduction;”

**63.** `[C1250 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The question is always stated and put on the _____ of the resolution (that is, so that a vote of aye is a vote in favor of it).

   *Answer:* adoption — “The question is always stated and put on the adoption of the resolution (that is, so that a vote of aye is a vote in favor of it).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:41 — “The question is always stated and put on the adoption of the resolution (that is, so that a vote of aye is a vote in favor of it).”

**64.** `[C1250 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The question is always stated and put on the _____ of the resolution (that is, so that a vote of aye is a vote in favor of it).
   A. ballot
   B. Example
   C. adoption
   D. sine die

   *Answer:* C) adoption. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:41 — “The question is always stated and put on the adoption of the resolution (that is, so that a vote of aye is a vote in favor of it).”

**65.** `[C1250 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The question is always stated and put on the adoption of the resolution (that is, so that a vote of _____ is a vote in favor of it).

   *Answer:* aye — “The question is always stated and put on the adoption of the resolution (that is, so that a vote of aye is a vote in favor of it).”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:41 — “The question is always stated and put on the adoption of the resolution (that is, so that a vote of aye is a vote in favor of it).”

**66.** `[C1250 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The question is always stated and put on the adoption of the resolution (that is, so that a vote of _____ is a vote in favor of it).
   A. Regular
   B. aye
   C. resolutions
   D. present,

   *Answer:* B) aye. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:41 — “The question is always stated and put on the adoption of the resolution (that is, so that a vote of aye is a vote in favor of it).”

**67.** `[C1253 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: If a resolution or other main question is referred to a committee while a motion to postpone it indefinitely is pending, that motion to _____ (11) is ignored by the committee and by the assembly when the committee reports.

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely — “If a resolution or other main question is referred to a committee while a motion to postpone it indefinitely is pending, that motion to Postpone Indefinitely (11) is ignored by the committee and by the assembly when the committee reports.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:44 — “If a resolution or other main question is referred to a committee while a motion to postpone it indefinitely is pending, that motion to Postpone Indefinitely (11) is ignored by the committee and by the assembly when the committee reports.”

**68.** `[C1253 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a resolution or other main question is referred to a committee while a motion to postpone it indefinitely is pending, that motion to _____ (11) is ignored by the committee and by the assembly when the committee reports.

   B. special orders
   C. Postpone Indefinitely
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* C) Postpone Indefinitely. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:44 — “If a resolution or other main question is referred to a committee while a motion to postpone it indefinitely is pending, that motion to Postpone Indefinitely (11) is ignored by the committee and by the assembly when the committee reports.”

   *Answer:* Postpone Indefinitely — “If a resolution or other main question is referred to a committee while a motion to postpone it indefinitely is pending, that motion to Postpone Indefinitely (11) is ignored by the committee and by the assembly when the committee reports.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:44 — “If a resolution or other main question is referred to a committee while a motion to postpone it indefinitely is pending, that motion to Postpone Indefinitely (11) is ignored by the committee and by the assembly when the committee reports.”

**70.** `[C1253 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: If a resolution or other main question is referred to a committee while a motion to postpone it indefinitely is pending, that motion to _____ (11) is ignored by the committee and by the assembly when the committee reports.

   D. over

**71.** `[C1260 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: but if the motion to substitute is adopted, the resolution thus substituted can be amended only by _____.

   *Answer:* adding — “but if the motion to substitute is adopted, the resolution thus substituted can be amended only by adding.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:51 — “but if the motion to substitute is adopted, the resolution thus substituted can be amended only by adding.”

**72.** `[C1260 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: but if the motion to substitute is adopted, the resolution thus substituted can be amended only by _____.
   A. Resolutions Committee,
   B. adding
   C. Optional Headings
   D. business

   *Answer:* B) adding. See RONR (12th ed.) §51:51 — “but if the motion to substitute is adopted, the resolution thus substituted can be amended only by adding.”

**73.** `[C1278 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The _____ and its two alternate forms, the quasi committee of the whole (or consideration as if in committee of the whole) and informal consideration, are devices that enable the full assembly to give detailed consideration to a matter under conditions of freedom somewhat like those of a committee.

   *Answer:* committee of the whole — “The committee of the whole and its two alternate forms, the quasi committee of the whole (or consideration as if in committee of the whole) and informal consideration, are devices that enable the full assembly to give detailed consideration to a matter under conditions of freedom somewhat like those of a committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:1 — “The committee of the whole and its two alternate forms, the quasi committee of the whole (or consideration as if in committee of the whole) and informal consideration, are devices that enable the full assembly to give detailed consideration to a matter under conditions of freedom somewhat like those of a committee.”

**74.** `[C1278 · Applied multiple-choice]` Fill in the blank: The _____ and its two alternate forms, the quasi committee of the whole (or consideration as if in committee of the whole) and informal consideration, are devices that enable the full assembly to give detailed consideration to a matter under conditions of freedom somewhat like those of a committee.
   A. rank
   B. Discharge a Committee
   C. committee of the whole
   D. resolution

   *Answer:* C) committee of the whole. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:1 — “The committee of the whole and its two alternate forms, the quasi committee of the whole (or consideration as if in committee of the whole) and informal consideration, are devices that enable the full assembly to give detailed consideration to a matter under conditions of freedom somewhat like those of a committee.”

**75.** `[C1278 · Cloze recall]` Fill the blank: The committee of the whole and its two alternate forms, the quasi committee of the whole (or _____ committee of the whole) and informal consideration, are devices that enable the full assembly to give detailed consideration to a matter under conditions of freedom somewhat like those of a committee.

   *Answer:* consideration as if in — “The committee of the whole and its two alternate forms, the quasi committee of the whole (or consideration as if in committee of the whole) and informal consideration, are devices that enable the full assembly to give detailed consideration to a matter under conditions of freedom somewhat like those of a committee.”. See RONR (12th ed.) §52:1 — “The committee of the whole and its two alternate forms, the quasi committee of the whole (or consideration as if in committee of the whole) and informal consideration, are devices that enable the full assembly to give detailed consideration to a matter under conditions of freedom somewhat like those of a committee.”
